LanguageData.VFEPD_AppleTree.description: A fake tree bearing glossy, red apples, while a myriad of different varieties exist this particular type favors warm summers and mild winters.
LanguageData.VFEPD_AppleTree.label: apple tree
LanguageData.VFEPD_BananaTree.description: A fake, broad-leafed, tall tropical tree adorned with a cluster of yellow, elongated berries succinctly known as bananas.
LanguageData.VFEPD_BananaTree.label: banana tree
LanguageData.VFEPD_BearBerryBush.description: A low-lying bush speckled with pink berries that thrives in poor, even sandy soil. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_BearBerryBush.label: bearberry bush
LanguageData.VFEPD_Beet.description: A fake herb-like vegetable widely cultivated for humans and livestock alike, notably nutritious and historically used in dye making.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Beet.label: beet
LanguageData.VFEPD_Cabbage.description: A fake somewhat hardy, leafy green vegetable, often chopped and then fermented or stewed.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Cabbage.label: cabbage
LanguageData.VFEPD_CherryTree.description: A fake tree bearing tiny purple-red sweet cherries.
LanguageData.VFEPD_CherryTree.label: cherry tree
LanguageData.VFEPD_Eggplant.description: A fake bulbous purple fruit of the nightshade family. Eggplant is rarely served as a side, generally filling the role of the main component of the dish.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Eggplant.label: eggplant
LanguageData.VFEPD_Onion.description: A fake edible bulb of pungent, savory taste and smell with almost as many varieties as cooking applications.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Onion.label: onion
LanguageData.VFEPD_Opuntia.description: More often referred to as the “prickly pear” cactus, this plant is known to be quite bountiful even in hot, dry deserts. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Opuntia.label: opuntia
LanguageData.VFEPD_OrangeTree.description: A fake, bushy, semi-tropical tree dotted with orange fruits well equipped for growing in humid environments and in wet soil.
LanguageData.VFEPD_OrangeTree.label: orange tree
LanguageData.VFEPD_PeachTree.description: A fake deciduous tree bearing fuzzy, stone fruit known as peaches, peach trees often require full sunlight and warm temperatures.
LanguageData.VFEPD_PeachTree.label: peach tree
LanguageData.VFEPD_PearTree.description: A fake stout tree bearing crispy green pears, pear trees are notable for requiring high amounts of water and cool weather, and also doing quite well in shade.
LanguageData.VFEPD_PearTree.label: pear tree
LanguageData.VFEPD_Peas.description: Fake spherical green seeds tucked safely away in a fleshy pod. Pea plants are known to be quite thirsty, and will benefit greatly from rainfall.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Peas.label: pea
LanguageData.VFEPD_Pepper.description: Fake, tiny, spicy, crimson pods often used in sauces, relishes, or powders.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Pepper.label: pepper
LanguageData.VFEPD_PlumTree.description: A fake bushy tree bearing a smooth-skinned purple fruit.
LanguageData.VFEPD_PlumTree.label: plum tree
LanguageData.VFEPD_Pumpkin.description: Technically a giant berry, pumpkins are renowned for being trivial to both sow and harvest. These ones are fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Pumpkin.label: pumpkin
LanguageData.VFEPD_Tomato.description: A series of juicy, glossy red fruits attached by vine, used commonly as a vegetable. These ones are fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Tomato.label: tomato
LanguageData.VFEPD_AloeVera.description: A herb with succulent leaves that are arranged in a rosette. The leaves are grey to green and sometimes have white spots on their surfaces. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_AloeVera.label: aloe vera
LanguageData.VFEPD_Barley.description: A cereal grain that people can use in bread, beverages, stews, and other dishes. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Barley.label: barley
LanguageData.VFEPD_Beans.description: A herbaceous bush with a long history of cultivation. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Beans.label: beans
LanguageData.VFEPD_BellPepper.description: Also known as sweet peppers, bell peppers are technically nightshades. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_BellPepper.label: bell pepper
LanguageData.VFEPD_Blueberry.description: A shrub prized for its sweet edible fruits. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Blueberry.label: blueberry
LanguageData.VFEPD_BrusselsSprout.description: A form of cabbage grown for its edible buds called "sprouts". This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_BrusselsSprout.label: brussels sprout
LanguageData.VFEPD_Buckwheat.description: A pseudocereal cultivated for its grain-like seeds. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Buckwheat.label: buckwheat
LanguageData.VFEPD_ButternutSquash.description: A vine plant that produces a vegetable with a sweet, nutty taste similar to that of a pumpkin. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_ButternutSquash.label: butternut squash
LanguageData.VFEPD_Canola.description: A bright-yellow flowering variety of the rapeseed, cultivated mainly for its oil-rich seed. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Canola.label: canola
LanguageData.VFEPD_Carrot.description: A herbaceous plant of the Apiaceae family that produces an edible taproot. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Carrot.label: carrot
LanguageData.VFEPD_Cassava.description: A tuberous edible plant also called manioc, mandioca, or yuca. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Cassava.label: cassava
LanguageData.VFEPD_Celery.description: A vegetable consisting of a long fibrous stalk tapering into leaves. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Celery.label: celery
LanguageData.VFEPD_Chickpea.description: One of the earliest cultivated legumes, widely grown for its nutritious seeds. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Chickpea.label: chickpea
LanguageData.VFEPD_CoconutPalm.description: A palm tree cultivated extensively in tropical areas for its edible fruit, the coconut. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_CoconutPalm.label: coconut palm
LanguageData.VFEPD_Cucumber.description: A sprawling vine with large leaves and curling tendrils, triangular prickly hairy leaves and yellow flowers. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Cucumber.label: cucumber
LanguageData.VFEPD_Garlic.description: A plant with long, flat grasslike leaves forming a stalk. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Garlic.label: garlic
LanguageData.VFEPD_Healshroom.description: A slow-growing mushroom which yields herbal medicine when harvested. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Healshroom.label: healshroom
LanguageData.VFEPD_Lettuce.description: A leaf vegetable commonly eaten fresh and served as the base of green salads. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Lettuce.label: lettuce
LanguageData.VFEPD_Lotus.description: Sometimes colloquially called a water lily, lotus plants are adapted to grow in the flood plains of slow-moving rivers and delta areas. This one is fake. Intended to be placed over water. Or wherever you want, I'm not your mom!
LanguageData.VFEPD_Lotus.label: lotus
LanguageData.VFEPD_Melon.description: A plant producing a sweet, edible, and fleshy fruit of considerable size. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Melon.label: melon
LanguageData.VFEPD_Moss.description: A nutrient-rich moss that can be used to feed cave animals, but not humans. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Moss.label: cave moss
LanguageData.VFEPD_Oats.description: The oat, sometimes called the common oat, is a species of cereal grain grown for its seed, which is known by the same name. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Oats.label: oats
LanguageData.VFEPD_Okra.description: A flowering plant in the mallow family valued for its edible green seed pods. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Okra.label: okra
LanguageData.VFEPD_Peanut.description: A plant that produces seeds with an oblong shape and a papery seed coat. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Peanut.label: peanut
LanguageData.VFEPD_Pineapple.description: A sweet tropical fruit with a tough leathery skin and spiky leaves on top. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Pineapple.label: pineapple
LanguageData.VFEPD_Radish.description: A tuberous edible plant also called manioc, mandioca, or yuca. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Radish.label: radish
LanguageData.VFEPD_Rye.description: A plant that grows well in much poorer soils than those necessary for most cereal grains. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Rye.label: rye
LanguageData.VFEPD_Sorghum.description: A drought resistant cereal used for food, animal fodder, the production of alcoholic beverages, and biofuels. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Sorghum.label: sorghum
LanguageData.VFEPD_Sunflower.description: A large crop used for its edible oil and edible fruits. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Sunflower.label: sunflower
LanguageData.VFEPD_SweetPotato.description: A tuberous edible plant also called manioc, mandioca, or yuca. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_SweetPotato.label: sweet potato
LanguageData.VFEPD_Taro.description: A tropical plant grown primarily for its edible corm, a root vegetable. This one is fake. Intended to be placed over water. Or wherever you want, I'm not your mom!
LanguageData.VFEPD_Taro.label: taro
LanguageData.VFEPD_Tinctshroom.description: A genetically-engineered mushroom which secretes a self-altering dye. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Tinctshroom.label: tinctshroom
LanguageData.VFEPD_TreeAlmond.description: A deciduous tree cultivated for its fruit, almonds. The sweet almond can be eaten plain or be used as ingredient in desserts and confectioneries. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_TreeAlmond.label: almond tree
LanguageData.VFEPD_TreeAvocado.description: An evergreen tree with thick, leathery leaves that produces perfect white, ivory to yellow flowers. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_TreeAvocado.label: avocado tree
LanguageData.VFEPD_TreeMango.description: A deciduous tree cultivated for its fruit, almonds. The sweet almond can be eaten plain or be used as ingredient in desserts and confectioneries. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_TreeMango.label: mango tree
LanguageData.VFEPD_TreeOlive.description: A subtropical broad-leaved evergreen tree. Its leaves, leathery and lance-shaped, are dark green above and silvery on the underside and are paired opposite each other on the twig. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_TreeOlive.label: olive tree
LanguageData.VFEPD_WaterChestnut.description: An aquatic vegetable that grows in marshes, under water, in the mud. This one is fake. Intended to be placed over water. Or wherever you want, I'm not your mom!
LanguageData.VFEPD_WaterChestnut.label: water chestnut
LanguageData.VFEPD_Watercress.description: Watercress is a rapidly growing, perennial plant. It is one of the oldest known leaf vegetables consumed by humans. This one is fake. Intended to be placed over water. Or wherever you want, I'm not your mom!
LanguageData.VFEPD_Watercress.label: watercress
LanguageData.VFEPD_YellowBellPepper.description: Also known as sweet peppers, bell peppers are technically nightshades. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_YellowBellPepper.label: yellow bell pepper
LanguageData.VFEPD_Bileceps.description: Bileceps is a peculiar, coral-like mushroom that thrives on the bile of decaying bodies. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Bileceps.label: bileceps
LanguageData.VFEPD_Blisscap.description: A long, light-blue mushroom that offers more than just sustenance. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Blisscap.label: blisscap
LanguageData.VFEPD_Foampuffer.description: An ugly, thick mushroom with a cap that smells of muffalo fecal matter. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Foampuffer.label: foampuffer
LanguageData.VFEPD_Glowcap.description: A small blue mushroom typically found in caves. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Glowcap.label: glowcap
LanguageData.VFEPD_Healtruffle.description: Healtruffle is a mushroom that grows slowly by using nutrients from rotstink. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Healtruffle.label: healtruffle
LanguageData.VFEPD_Lullabloom.description: A special kind of mushroom that emits calming spores, ideal for planting next to a bed. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Lullabloom.label: lullabloom
LanguageData.VFEPD_MycothreadMushroom.description: This mushroom has been cultivated for ages by tunneler mining colonies. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_MycothreadMushroom.label: mycothread mushroom
LanguageData.VFEPD_Redcap.description: A cultivated mushroom with a beautiful red cap and a somewhat short lifespan. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Redcap.label: redcap
LanguageData.VFEPD_Stinker.description: A small, fast-growing mushroom that forms large patches. It produces rotstink through a chemical process. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Stinker.label: stinker
LanguageData.VFEPD_Thermoregulus.description: A large, bulbous, beige mushroom that is known to produce small amounts of electricity as it grows. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Thermoregulus.label: thermoregulus
LanguageData.VFEPD_Timbercap_VE.description: A short, brown mushroom with a hard stem. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Timbercap_VE.label: timbercap
LanguageData.VFEPD_Zapcap.description: A large, bulbous, beige mushroom that is known to produce small amounts of electricity as it grows. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Zapcap.label: zapcap
LanguageData.VFEPD_AloeVera_Succulent.description: A cultivated succulent with thick, greenish, fleshy leaves that fan out from the short stem. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_AloeVera_Succulent.label: aloe vera
LanguageData.VFEPD_BunnyEarsCactus.description: A cultivated succulent with pads resembling ears of a bunny, and spines resembling fuzzy fur. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_BunnyEarsCactus.label: bunny ears cactus
LanguageData.VFEPD_Echeveria.description: A cultivated succulent forming a rosette, native to arid shrublands. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Echeveria.label: echeveria
LanguageData.VFEPD_FairyWashboard.description: A cultivated succulent with rosette forming long triangular leaves. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_FairyWashboard.label: fairy washboard
LanguageData.VFEPD_JadePlant.description: A cultivated succulent with thick, woody stems and oval-shaped leaves. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_JadePlant.label: jade
LanguageData.VFEPD_PeyotePlant.description: A cultivated succulent resembling thick buttons. Whilst not as beautiful as flowers, this plant has a very long lifespan. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_PeyotePlant.label: peyote
LanguageData.VFEPD_PincushionPlant.description: A cultivated succulent of cactus variety, with pointy spikes covering its exterior. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_PincushionPlant.label: pincushion
LanguageData.VFEPD_Schlumbergera.description: A cultivated succulent with bell-shaped flowers. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Schlumbergera.label: schlumbergera
LanguageData.VFEPD_SnakePlant.description: A cultivated succulent with stiff, green banded leaves with a yellow border. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_SnakePlant.label: snake plant
LanguageData.VFEPD_SweetheartPlant.description: A cultivated succulent with heart-shaped leaves on a tall stem. This one is fake.
LanguageData.VFEPD_SweetheartPlant.label: sweetheart
LanguageData.VFEPD_BangBus.description: A basic passenger transport, the Bangbus is a relatively cheap piece of machinery to construct to transport people across roads of flat terrain. Non-functional: all its internal wiring and its motor have been removed.
LanguageData.VFEPD_BangBus.label: bang bus
LanguageData.VFEPD_Bulldog.description: A general main battle tank, the Bulldog is an improved combat vehicle that performs well in any engagement. Non-functional: all its internal wiring and its motor have been removed.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Bulldog.label: bulldog
LanguageData.VFEPD_Bunsen.description: A basic light tank, the Bunsen is a modified armoured vehicle suited for taking on enemy fortifications and exposed infantry. Non-functional: all its internal wiring and its motor have been removed.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Bunsen.label: bunsen
LanguageData.VFEPD_Charley.description: A general motorcycle, the Charley is a very effective single-person vehicle with a small cargo capacity. Non-functional: all its internal wiring and its motor have been removed.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Charley.label: charley
LanguageData.VFEPD_Dinghy.description: A very basic motorised boat, the Dingy is an incredibly cheap and discrete method of transportation over water. Non-functional: all its internal wiring and its motor have been removed.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Dinghy.label: dinghy
LanguageData.VFEPD_Dirtbike.description: A basic off-road motorcycle, the Dirtbike is a very cheap vehicle that allows a single user to travel quickly, efficiently and quietly. Non-functional: all its internal wiring and its motor have been removed.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Dirtbike.label: dirtbike
LanguageData.VFEPD_Frog.description: A basic reusable transport pod, the Frog is a relatively cheap vehicle that is the first option when it comes to flying without crashing into the ground. Non-functional: all its internal wiring and its motor have been removed.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Frog.label: frog
LanguageData.VFEPD_Highwayman.description: A basic infantry fighting vehicle, the Highwayman is the cheapest weaponized option for transporting three people. Non-functional: all its internal wiring and its motor have been removed.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Highwayman.label: highwayman
LanguageData.VFEPD_Mosquito.description: A general-purpose aircraft, the Mosquito is an extremely fast and mobile mode of transportation. Non-functional: all its internal wiring and its motor have been removed.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Mosquito.label: mosquito
LanguageData.VFEPD_Mule.description: A basic cargo transport, the Mule is a relatively cheap vehicle that can carry a large amount of cargo across smooth roads of flat terrain. Non-functional: all its internal wiring and its motor have been removed.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Mule.label: mule
LanguageData.VFEPD_Prowler.description: A basic off-road exploration vehicle, the Prowler is relatively cheap to construct and is an effective all-weather option for efficient travel over steep terrain. Non-functional: all its internal wiring and its motor have been removed.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Prowler.label: prowler
LanguageData.VFEPD_Roadkill.description: A basic medium tank, the Roadkill is a comparatively cheap armoured vehicle suited for short engagements. Non-functional: all its internal wiring and its motor have been removed.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Roadkill.label: roadkill
LanguageData.VFEPD_Roadrunner.description: A general cargo transporter, the Roadrunner is an excellent vehicle for getting a lot of material from one location to another. Non-functional: all its internal wiring and its motor have been removed.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Roadrunner.label: roadrunner
LanguageData.VFEPD_Scytheman.description: A juryrigged multiple rocket launcher system, the Scytheman is a repurposed farming vehicle intended to saturate a location with inaccurate volleys of explosives. Non-functional: all its internal wiring and its motor have been removed.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Scytheman.label: scytheman
LanguageData.VFEPD_Smuggler.description: A general-purpose aircraft, the Smuggler is an extremely fast and effective mode of transportation. Non-functional: all its internal wiring and its motor have been removed.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Smuggler.label: smuggler
LanguageData.VFEPD_Snatcher.description: A general passenger transport, the Snatcher is a solid vehicle for transporting people to different locations. Non-functional: all its internal wiring and its motor have been removed.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Snatcher.label: snatcher
LanguageData.VFEPD_Tango.description: A general purpose armoured personnel carrier, the Tango is a slow but hardy troop transport. Non-functional: all its internal wiring and its motor have been removed.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Tango.label: tango
LanguageData.VFEPD_Toad.description: A squad and mortar deployment pod, the Toad is a somewhat expensive vehicle to quickly mount a siege on a nearby locale without exposing your gunners to danger. Non-functional: all its internal wiring and its motor have been removed.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Toad.label: toad
LanguageData.VFEPD_Traveller.description: A basic road vehicle suited for flat terrain, the Traveller is a relatively cheap and fast option that still performs quite amicably in cold weather and snowy conditions. Non-functional: all its internal wiring and its motor have been removed.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Traveller.label: traveller
LanguageData.VFEPD_Trawler.description: A general-purpose boat, the Trawler is an effective long distance transportation across open waters. Non-functional: all its internal wiring and its motor have been removed.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Trawler.label: trawler
LanguageData.VFEPD_Wagon.description: A general all-rounder vehicle, the Wagon performs well in all categories with a good cargo and passenger capacity. Non-functional: all its internal wiring and its motor have been removed.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Wagon.label: wagon
LanguageData.VFEPD_Warbird.description: A general-purpose fighter plane, the Warbird is an incredibly fast and effective attack craft. Non-functional: all its internal wiring and its motor have been removed.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Warbird.label: warbird
LanguageData.VFEPD_Wisent.description: A military-grade all-purpose transporter, the Wisent is a powerful truck capable of transporting both plentiful cargo and soldiers into hostile territory. Non-functional: all its internal wiring and its motor have been removed.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Wisent.label: wisent
LanguageData.VFEPD_ArtilleryWreck.description: An ancient, broken self propelled artillery. Everything that isn't rusted away was looted long ago.
LanguageData.VFEPD_ArtilleryWreck.label: artillery wreck
LanguageData.VFEPD_Badger.description: A tracked, armoured fighting vehicle, the Badger is designed to transport scouts and infantry while providing covering fire to suppress enemy troops. Non-functional: all its internal wiring and its motor have been removed.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Badger.label: badger
LanguageData.VFEPD_BattleTankWreck.description: An ancient, broken battle tank. Everything that isn't rusted away was looted long ago.
LanguageData.VFEPD_BattleTankWreck.label: battle tank wreck
LanguageData.VFEPD_BigRig.description: A dedicated cargo transporter, the Big Rig is the epitome of bulk material transportation. Non-functional: all its internal wiring and its motor have been removed.
LanguageData.VFEPD_BigRig.label: big rig
LanguageData.VFEPD_Burya.description: A dedicated multiple launch rocket system platform, the Burya is a long-range siege and area denial weapon that can be operated by a two-man crew for faster loading speeds. Non-functional: all its internal wiring and its motor have been removed.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Burya.label: burya
LanguageData.VFEPD_CargoHelicopterWreck.description: An ancient, crashed cargo helicopter. Everything that isn't rusted away was looted long ago.
LanguageData.VFEPD_CargoHelicopterWreck.label: cargo helicopter wreck
LanguageData.VFEPD_ClassicCarWreck.description: An ancient, broken classic car. Everything that isn't rusted away was looted long ago.
LanguageData.VFEPD_ClassicCarWreck.label: classical car wreck
LanguageData.VFEPD_Goliath.description: More of a movable mini-base than a deployment pod, Goliath is an expensive but powerful solution to any immovable enemy object. Non-functional: all its internal wiring and its motor have been removed.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Goliath.label: goliath
LanguageData.VFEPD_HelicopterWreck.description: An ancient, broken helicopter. Everything that isn't rusted away was looted long ago.
LanguageData.VFEPD_HelicopterWreck.label: helicopter wreck
LanguageData.VFEPD_Hermano.description: A dedicated refrigeration vehicle, the Hermano is a decent cargo transporter with a specific niche in ferrying perishable goods. Non-functional: all its internal wiring and its motor have been removed.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Hermano.label: hermano
LanguageData.VFEPD_InfantryFightingVehicleWreck.descr: An ancient, broken infantry fighting vehicle. Everything that isn't rusted away was looted long ago.
LanguageData.VFEPD_InfantryFightingVehicleWreck.label: infantry fighting vehicle wreck
LanguageData.VFEPD_LightArmoredVehicleWreck.descripti: An ancient, broken light armored vehicle. Everything that isn't rusted away was looted long ago.
LanguageData.VFEPD_LightArmoredVehicleWreck.label: light armored vehicle wreck
LanguageData.VFEPD_Lightning.description: A fast general purpose transporter, the Lightning is an excellent road vehicle for getting an appreciable amount of cargo in a very small time frame. Non-functional: all its internal wiring and its motor have been removed.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Lightning.label: lightning
LanguageData.VFEPD_Louie.description: An armoured transport aircraft, the Louie is a robust and agile mobile mode of transportation. Non-functional: all its internal wiring and its motor have been removed.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Louie.label: louie
LanguageData.VFEPD_Marshal.description: A general main battle tank, the Marshal is an improved combat vehicle that performs well in any engagement. Non-functional: all its internal wiring and its motor have been removed.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Marshal.label: marshal
LanguageData.VFEPD_Nomad.description: An armoured all-rounder vehicle, the Nomad performs remarkably well in all categories with an excellent cargo and passenger capacity. Non-functional: all its internal wiring and its motor have been removed.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Nomad.label: nomad
LanguageData.VFEPD_OffroadJeepWreck.description: An ancient, broken offroad jeep. Everything that isn't rusted away was looted long ago.
LanguageData.VFEPD_OffroadJeepWreck.label: offroad jeep wreck
LanguageData.VFEPD_Paladin.description: A general purpose armoured personnel carrier, the Paladin is a fast and hardy troop transport. Non-functional: all its internal wiring and its motor have been removed.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Paladin.label: paladin
LanguageData.VFEPD_RefrigeratedTruckWreck.description: An ancient, broken refrigerated truck. Everything that isn't rusted away was looted long ago.
LanguageData.VFEPD_RefrigeratedTruckWreck.label: refrigerated truck wreck
LanguageData.VFEPD_RocketArtilleryWreck.description: An ancient, broken rocket artillery truck. Everything that isn't rusted away was looted long ago.
LanguageData.VFEPD_RocketArtilleryWreck.label: rocket artillery wreck
LanguageData.VFEPD_SiegePodWreck.description: An ancient, destroyed siege pod. Everything that isn't rusted away was looted long ago.
LanguageData.VFEPD_SiegePodWreck.label: siege pod wreck
LanguageData.VFEPD_Springbok.description: A general all-rounder vehicle, the Springbok performs well in all categories with a good cargo and passenger capacity. Non-functional: all its internal wiring and its motor have been removed.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Springbok.label: springbok
LanguageData.VFEPD_SupercarWreck.description: An ancient, broken supercar. Everything that isn't rusted away was looted long ago.
LanguageData.VFEPD_SupercarWreck.label: supercar wreck
LanguageData.VFEPD_TruckWreck.description: An ancient, broken truck. Everything that isn't rusted away was looted long ago.
LanguageData.VFEPD_TruckWreck.label: truck wreck
LanguageData.VFEPD_Udar.description: A dedicated artillery unit, the Udar is a long-range siege weapon that can be operated by a two-man crew for faster loading speeds. Non-functional: all its internal wiring and its motor have been removed.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Udar.label: udar
LanguageData.VFEPD_CommsTerminal.description: A compact version of a comms console that allows radio contact with other factions and orbital traders. Inactive.
LanguageData.VFEPD_CommsTerminal.label: comms terminal
LanguageData.VFEPD_CopilotConsole.description: An auxiliary interface that allows a copilot to assist in launching and piloting a gravship. Inactive.
LanguageData.VFEPD_CopilotConsole.label: copilot console
LanguageData.VFEPD_DestroyedGravExtender.description: This device once expanded a gravship’s build area, but it is now shattered.
LanguageData.VFEPD_DestroyedGravExtender.label: destroyed grav field extender
LanguageData.VFEPD_DestroyedLargeThruster.description: This powerful thruster once pushed gravships across space, but now it lies broken.
LanguageData.VFEPD_DestroyedLargeThruster.label: destroyed large thruster
LanguageData.VFEPD_DestroyedSmallAstrofuelTank.descri: This tank once stored astrofuel for thrusters, but it has been destroyed.
LanguageData.VFEPD_DestroyedSmallAstrofuelTank.label: destroyed small astrofuel tank
LanguageData.VFEPD_DestroyedSmallHeatsink.description: This heatsink once cooled the gravship’s systems, but it has burned out.
LanguageData.VFEPD_DestroyedSmallHeatsink.label: destroyed small heatsink
LanguageData.VFEPD_DestroyedSmallOxygenTank.descripti: This tank once supplied oxygen to the gravship, but it’s now broken and useless.
LanguageData.VFEPD_DestroyedSmallOxygenTank.label: destroyed small oxygen tank
LanguageData.VFEPD_DestroyedSmallThruster.description: This thruster once powered the gravship, but it is now wrecked beyond use.
LanguageData.VFEPD_DestroyedSmallThruster.label: destroyed small thruster
LanguageData.VFEPD_FauxPlant.description: An artificial plant in a sturdy metal pot. It requires no light, water, or air, making it perfect for adding beauty to even the most inhospitable gravship environments.
LanguageData.VFEPD_FauxPlant.label: faux plant
LanguageData.VFEPD_GravFieldAmplifier.description: An advanced ultratech device that dramatically increases the size of a gravship. Inactive.
LanguageData.VFEPD_GravFieldAmplifier.label: grav field amplifier
LanguageData.VFEPD_GravshipShelf.description: A reinforced storage shelf designed for use aboard gravships. Made out of cardboard, can't hold any items.
LanguageData.VFEPD_GravshipShelf.label: gravship shelf
LanguageData.VFEPD_PilotBridge.description: An interface that allows launching and piloting a gravhulk. Inactive.
LanguageData.VFEPD_PilotBridge.label: pilot bridge
LanguageData.VFEPD_PilotCockpit.description: An interface that allows launching and piloting a gravjumper. Inactive.
LanguageData.VFEPD_PilotCockpit.label: pilot cockpit
LanguageData.VFEPD_SealantPopper.label: sealant popper
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_Agrocell.description: An enclosed, space-efficient growing unit designed for use on gravships. Upon closer inspection, it's just a decal!
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_Agrocell.label: agrocell
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_AmmoBox_GaussShell.description: A small-sized box containing large fake uranium shells.
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_AmmoBox_GaussShell.label: gauss shell ammo box
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_AstrofuelDrain.description: A hopper-like storage structure meant to input astrofuel into the network, filling up connected tanks.
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_AstrofuelDrain.label: astrofuel drain
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_AstrofuelGenerator.description: Produces power by consuming astrofuel. Much more efficient than chemfuel generators.
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_AstrofuelGenerator.label: astrofuel generator
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_AstrofuelPipe.description: Steel pipes. These do not transport anything.
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_AstrofuelPipe.label: astrofuel pipe
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_AstrofuelSynthesizer.descripti: A specialized structure that refines chemfuel into high-grade astrofuel. It must be connected to the astrofuel pipe network and linked to at least one astrofuel tank to operate. Once connected, it automatically converts inserted chemfuel into astrofuel for use in engines and systems.
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_AstrofuelSynthesizer.label: astrofuel synthesizer
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_AstrofuelTap.description: A hopper-like storage device meant to hold dispensed astrofuel for use later.
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_AstrofuelTap.label: astrofuel tap
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_Astropurifier.description: A gravtech unit that enhances the precision and stability of astrofuel refinement. When placed next to an astrofuel synthesizer, it enables perfect 1:1 conversion from chemfuel to astrofuel, though the process takes twice as long.
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_Astropurifier.label: astropurifier
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_CapacitorHarmonizer.descriptio: A specialized device that balances and synchronizes energy flow between nearby capacitors. This one is non-functional.
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_CapacitorHarmonizer.label: capacitor harmonizer
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_CompactDrugLab.description: A space-efficient, gravship-powered drug lab designed for use aboard gravships. This one seems to be non-functional.
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_CompactDrugLab.label: compact drug lab
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_CompactFabBench.description: A space-efficient, gravship-powered fabrication bench designed for use aboard gravships. This one seems to be non-functional.
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_CompactFabBench.label: compact fabrication bench
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_CompactMachiningTable.descript: A space-efficient, gravship-powered machining table designed for use aboard gravships. This one seems to be non-functional.
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_CompactMachiningTable.label: compact machining table
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_CompactRefinery.description: A space-efficient, gravship-powered biofuel refinery designed for use aboard gravships. This one seems to be non-functional.
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_CompactRefinery.label: compact biofuel refinery
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_CompactSmithy.description: A space-efficient, gravship-powered smithy designed for use aboard gravships. This one seems to be non-functional.
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_CompactSmithy.label: compact smithy
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_CompactStove.description: A space-efficient, gravship-powered stove designed for use aboard gravships. This one seems to be non-functional.
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_CompactStove.label: compact stove
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_CompactTailor.description: A space-efficient, gravship-powered tailor bench designed for use aboard gravships. This one seems to be non-functional.
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_CompactTailor.label: compact tailor bench
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_EngineeringConsole.description: An interface used to monitor the maintenance status of all gravship components. It draws power directly from the grav engine. Non-functional.
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_EngineeringConsole.label: engineering console
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_FlightComputer.description: An advanced computational unit that optimizes data collection during gravship travel. Non-functional.
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_FlightComputer.label: flight computer
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_GiantAstrofuelTank.description: A monumental astrofuel storage tank that supplies fuel for thrusters on a gravship. Sealed and empty
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_GiantAstrofuelTank.label: giant astrofuel tank
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_GiantThruster.description:
A massive, reinforced astrofuel thruster built with advanced alloys and ultra-dense vacstone plating. It massively increases the range and maximum size of the gravship. Thrusters require an unobstructed area free of large buildings and gravship substructure.

Requires vacstone blocks, which can be obtained from orbital asteroids.

A gravship can support up to four giant thrusters.
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_GiantThruster.label: giant thruster
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_GravcorePowerCoil.description: A massive gravtech reactor that channels and amplifies gravitational wave energy into immense electrical output. This one is non-functional.
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_GravcorePowerCoil.label: gravcore power coil
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_GravheatAbsorber.description: A specialized thermal sink that draws all residual heat from the grav engine, instantly clearing its cooldown. The absorbed heat leaves the gravheat absorber inoperable for an extended period while it safely dissipates the stored energy. Non-functional.
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_GravheatAbsorber.label: gravheat absorber
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_Gravlift.description: A complex vertical launch and stabilization platform integrated into the gravship’s core structure. This one is non-functional.
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_Gravlift.label: gravlift
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_GravshipBlackBox.description: An advanced recorder integrated into the gravship’s systems. The only thing this one would do in an accident is be blown to pieces.
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_GravshipBlackBox.label: gravship black box
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_GravshipCapacitor.description: Stores high amounts of electricity for later use. Or it would, if it wasn't so fake!
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_GravshipCapacitor.label: gravship capacitor
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_GravtechConsole.description: An interface used to analyze gravdata gathered from gravship launches and travel. Inert.
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_GravtechConsole.label: gravtech console
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_GunnerSubpersonaCore.descripti: A low-grade subpersona specialized in gravship combat. This one lacks the brain, so it can post on Reddit.
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_GunnerSubpersonaCore.label: gunner subpersona core
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_IndustrialAstrofuelGenerator.d: Produces a large amount of power by consuming astrofuel. Much more efficient than chemfuel generators.
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_LargeHeatsink.description: A large thermal regulator that absorbs heat from gravship travel and slowly converts part of it into usable power while preventing system overheating and decreasing the grav engine cooldown duration. Non-functional.
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_LargeHeatsink.label: large heatsink
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_LargeMechanoidThruster.descrip: A massive astrofuel thruster built by the mechanoid core to propel their orbital platforms. This one is non-functional.
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_LargeMechanoidThruster.label: large mechanoid thruster
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_LargeOxygenTank.description: A large oxygen tank that supplies oxygen to pumps throughout the gravship.
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_LargeOxygenTank.label: large oxygen tank
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_LifeSupportSystem.description: A vital device that processes harvested oxyalgae into breathable oxygen. Converts biomass into gas and pumps it into the oxygen network to supply pressurized rooms and equipment.
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_LifeSupportSystem.label: algae oxygenator
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_MaintenanceHub.description: A centralized system that optimizes and automates routine upkeep across the gravship. This one is non-functional.
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_MaintenanceHub.label: maintenance hub
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_MechanoidAstrofuelNode.descrip: A compact fuel reservoir integrated into mechanoid platforms. Empty and sealed.
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_MechanoidAstrofuelNode.label: astrofuel node
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_MechanoidGravEngine.descriptio: The cold, artificial heart of a mechanoid orbital platform. This one seems inert.
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_MechanoidGravEngine.label: mechanoid grav engine
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_MechanoidPodLauncher.descripti: A mechanized launch bay that periodically fires drop pods packed with mechanoids. This one is non-functional.
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_MechanoidPodLauncher.label: mechanoid pod launcher
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_MechanoidResourceProcessor.des: A machine-built structure that sustains mechanoid platforms by breaking down cosmic debris and wreckage into usable resources. This one is non-functional.
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_MechanoidTempShield.descriptio: A compact defense unit that projects a localized shield around itself. This one is non-functional.
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_MechanoidTempShield.label: mechanoid temp shield
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_OxyalgaePlant.description: A fast-growing aquatic plant cultivated in hydroponics and used to generate oxygen when converted in algae oxygenator. This one seems to be a plastic plants.
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_OxyalgaePlant.label: oxyalgae plant
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_OxygenHarvester.description: A device that draws in breathable oxygen from the surrounding atmosphere and stores it in the oxygen network. It functions only on planetary surfaces with an atmosphere and will not operate in vacuum or low-oxygen environments.
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_OxygenHarvester.label: oxygen harvester
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_OxygenPipe.description: Steel pipes. These do not transport anything.
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_OxygenPipe.label: oxygen pipe
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_OxygenReclaimer.description: A self-contained atmospheric unit that extracts trace oxygen from surrounding gases. It produces oxygen at a slow, steady rate, but requires no input materials. Needs to be connected to an oxygen tank via oxygen pipes to work.
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_OxygenReclaimer.label: oxygen reclaimer
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_SmallHeatsink.description: A compact thermal regulator that absorbs heat from gravship travel and slowly converts part of it into usable power while preventing system overheating and decreasing the grav engine cooldown duration. Non-functional.
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_SmallHeatsink.label: small heatsink
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_SmallOxygenTank.description: An oxygen tank that supplies oxygen to pumps throughout the gravship.
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_SmallOxygenTank.label: small oxygen tank
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_SolarPanelling.description: Fake solar panelling built out of cardboard. This will probably win a high school science fair or something. We don't have those where I live, so I have only seen them in movies.
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_SolarPanelling.label: solar panelling
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_TargetingTerminal.description: A control station dedicated to operating a single gravship artillery piece. Out of order.
LanguageData.VFEPD_VGE_TargetingTerminal.label: targeting terminal
LanguageData.VFEPD_WallFirefoamPopper.description: A wall-mounted variant of the firefoam popper. It is empty.
LanguageData.VFEPD_WallFirefoamPopper.label: wall firefoam popper
LanguageData.VFEPD_WallOrbitalTradeBeacon.description: A wall-mounted version of an orbital trade beacon. Inactive.
LanguageData.VFEPD_WallOrbitalTradeBeacon.label: wall orbital trade beacon
LanguageData.VFEPD_CarDeliveryBlack.label: black delivery truck
LanguageData.VFEPD_CarDeliveryBlue.label: blue delivery truck
LanguageData.VFEPD_CarDeliveryRed.label: red delivery truck
LanguageData.VFEPD_CarDeliverySilver.label: silver delivery truck
LanguageData.VFEPD_CarDeliveryWhite.label: white delivery truck

LanguageData.VFEPD_CarFamilyBlue.label: blue family car
LanguageData.VFEPD_CarFamilyRed.label: red family car
LanguageData.VFEPD_CarFamilySilver.label: silver family car
LanguageData.VFEPD_CarFamilyWhite.label: white family car
LanguageData.VFEPD_CarForklift.label: forklift
LanguageData.VFEPD_CarPickupBlack.label: black pickup truck
LanguageData.VFEPD_CarPickupBlue.label: blue pickup truck
LanguageData.VFEPD_CarPickupRed.label: red pickup truck
LanguageData.VFEPD_CarPickupSilver.label: silver pickup truck
LanguageData.VFEPD_CarPickupWhite.label: white pickup truck
LanguageData.VFEPD_CarVintageBlack.label: black vintage car
LanguageData.VFEPD_CarVintageBlue.label: blue vintage car
LanguageData.VFEPD_CarVintageRed.label: red vintage car
LanguageData.VFEPD_CarVintageSilver.label: silver vintage car
LanguageData.VFEPD_CarVintageWhite.label: white vintage car
LanguageData.VFEP_ShortWalls.description: A passable chest-height wall. Cannot support roofs.
LanguageData.VFEP_ShortWalls.label: short wall
LanguageData.VFEPD_AdjustableWrench.description: A small adjustable wrench. Intended to be placed over an empty workbench. Just for decorative purposes.
LanguageData.VFEPD_AdjustableWrench.label: adjustable wrench
LanguageData.VFEPD_Alembic.description: An alembic. Intended to be placed over an empty workbench. Just for decorative purposes.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Alembic.label: alembic
LanguageData.VFEPD_AssortedVials.description: Assorted vials. Intended to be placed over an empty workbench. Just for decorative purposes.
LanguageData.VFEPD_AssortedVials.label: assorted vials
LanguageData.VFEPD_AssortedVialsB.description: Assorted vials. Intended to be placed over an empty workbench. Just for decorative purposes.
LanguageData.VFEPD_AssortedVialsB.label: assorted vials
LanguageData.VFEPD_AssortedVialsC.description: Assorted vials. Intended to be placed over an empty workbench. Just for decorative purposes.
LanguageData.VFEPD_AssortedVialsC.label: assorted vials
LanguageData.VFEPD_Beakers.description: Assorted beakers. Intended to be placed over an empty workbench. Just for decorative purposes.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Beakers.label: beakers
LanguageData.VFEPD_BioRefineryLargeBarrel.description: The large barrel of a biorefinery. Intended to be placed alongside an empty workbench. Just for decorative purposes.
LanguageData.VFEPD_BioRefineryLargeBarrel.label: biorefinery large barrel
LanguageData.VFEPD_BioRefineryLargeBarrelHorizontal.d: The large barrel of a biorefinery. Intended to be placed alongside an empty workbench. Just for decorative purposes.
LanguageData.VFEPD_BioRefineryMediumBarrel.descriptio: The medium barrel of a biorefinery. Intended to be placed alongside an empty workbench. Just for decorative purposes.
LanguageData.VFEPD_BioRefineryMediumBarrel.label: biorefinery medium barrel
The medium barrel of a biorefinery. Intended to be placed alongside an empty workbench. Just for decorative purposes.
LanguageData.VFEPD_BioRefinerySmallBarrel.description: The small barrel of a biorefinery. Intended to be placed alongside an empty workbench. Just for decorative purposes.
LanguageData.VFEPD_BioRefinerySmallBarrel.label: biorefinery small barrel
LanguageData.VFEPD_BlocksPileA.description: A pile of blocks in the process of being chiseled. Intended to be placed alongside an empty workbench. Just for decorative purposes.
LanguageData.VFEPD_BlocksPileA.label: pile of blocks
LanguageData.VFEPD_BlocksPileB.description: A pile of blocks in the process of being chiseled. Intended to be placed alongside an empty workbench. Just for decorative purposes.
LanguageData.VFEPD_BlocksPileB.label: pile of blocks
LanguageData.VFEPD_BlocksPileC.description: A pile of blocks in the process of being chiseled. Intended to be placed alongside an empty workbench. Just for decorative purposes.
LanguageData.VFEPD_BlocksPileC.label: pile of blocks
LanguageData.VFEPD_BloodSplatter.description: Blood splatter from a butcher table. Intended to be placed alongside an empty workbench. Just for decorative purposes.
LanguageData.VFEPD_BloodSplatter.label: blood splatter
LanguageData.VFEPD_BreweryBarrel.description: A small brewery barrel. Intended to be placed over an empty workbench. Just for decorative purposes.
LanguageData.VFEPD_BreweryBarrel.label: brewery barrel
LanguageData.VFEPD_BreweryTablet.description: A brewery mat to process the plants into must. Or something. Don't look at me, I'm not a beer maker. Intended to be placed alongside an empty workbench. Just for decorative purposes.
LanguageData.VFEPD_BreweryTablet.label: brewery mat
LanguageData.VFEPD_BrownSmelterGizmo.description: A small smelter gizmo. God knows what this does. Intended to be placed over an empty workbench. Just for decorative purposes.
LanguageData.VFEPD_BrownSmelterGizmo.label: smelter gizmo, grey
LanguageData.VFEPD_ChemfuelTankSmall.description: A small chemfuel tank. Intended to be placed over an empty workbench. Just for decorative purposes.
LanguageData.VFEPD_ChemfuelTankSmall.label: chemfuel tank
LanguageData.VFEPD_Chisel.description: A small chisel. Intended to be placed over an empty workbench. Just for decorative purposes.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Chisel.label: chisel
LanguageData.VFEPD_Cleaver.description: A small cleaver. Intended to be placed over an empty workbench. Just for decorative purposes.
LanguageData.VFEPD_Cleaver.label: cleaver
LanguageData.VFEPD_ConeWrench.description: A small cone wrench. Intended to be placed over an empty workbench. Just for decorative purposes.
LanguageData.VFEPD_ConeWrench.label: cone wrench
LanguageData.VFEPD_ConveyorInput.description: A small conveyor input. Intended to be placed over an empty workbench. Just for decorative purposes.
LanguageData.VFEPD_ConveyorInput.label: conveyor input
LanguageData.VFEPD_CuttingTableWithKnife.description: A cutting table with a sharpened knife. Intended to be placed over an empty workbench. Just for decorative purposes.
LanguageData.VFEPD_CuttingTableWithKnife.label: cutting table, knife

LanguageData.VPEPD_EmptyWorkbench.description: A working surface to fill with production stuff. Non-functional for crafting purposes.

LanguageData.VCE_CanningMachine.description: A canning machine is an appliance to store preserved food inside metallic cans. Each canning operation needs a certain amount of Steel.
LanguageData.VCE_CanningMachine.label: canning machine
LanguageData.VCE_CheesePress.description: A cheese press is a device for pressing whey from curds when making cheese. Pressing influences the cheese's hardness and texture and will also determine the shape of the block or wheel of cheese. This press also works as a ripening chamber, so the longer the cheese is left ripening, the higher its quality will be.
LanguageData.VCE_CheesePress.label: cheese press
LanguageData.VCE_CondimentPrepTable.description: A working table to cut, slice and prepare condiments used in gourmet recipes.
LanguageData.VCE_CondimentPrepTable.label: condiment prep table
LanguageData.VCE_DeepFrier.description: A deep fryer is a kitchen appliance used for deep frying. Deep frying is a method of cooking by submerging food into oil at high heat, typically between temperatures of 350 °F and 375 °F (175 °C to 190 °C).
LanguageData.VCE_DeepFrier.label: deep fryer
LanguageData.VCE_ElectricPot.description: An electric pot, also called a thermo pot, is a small appliance used for boiling water and maintaining it at a constant temperature. It is typically used to simmer soup over an extended period of time. Ingredients are submerged in water and left to cook over an extended period, allowing the flavors of the ingredients to gradually deepen and blend over time.\n\nSoup on an unpowered electric pot will spoil extremely rapidly. Beware of solar flares!
LanguageData.VCE_ElectricPot.label: electric pot
LanguageData.VCE_Grill.description: A grill is a device that cooks food by applying heat from below. This is a wood fired grill that needs wood as fuel to work.
LanguageData.VCE_Grill.label: grill
LanguageData.VCE_FineBake.description: A complex bake assembled with care from a wide selection of ingredients and flour.
LanguageData.VCE_FineBake.label: fine bake
LanguageData.VCE_GourmetBake.description: A culinary work of art, characterized by refined, even elaborate preparation and presentation of an aesthetically balanced, albeit low quantity bake.
LanguageData.VCE_GourmetBake.label: gourmet bake
LanguageData.VCE_LavishBake.description: A masterpiece of the culinary arts, this bake nourishes the body, mind, and soul.
LanguageData.VCE_LavishBake.label: lavish bake
LanguageData.VCE_SimpleBake.description: A simple bake quickly cooked from a few ingredients and flour.
LanguageData.VCE_SimpleBake.label: simple bake
LanguageData.VCE_CannedAP.description: Boiled eggs vacuum sealed and secured in a steel can effectively extending shelf life indefinitely. While edible cold right out of the can it's far more nourishing mixed into a meal and heated.\n\nCanned goods are intended as a way to preserve food and use it an an ingredient later, not as a final meal.
LanguageData.VCE_CannedAP.label: canned eggs
LanguageData.VCE_CannedFish.description: Fish vacuum sealed and secured in a steel can effectively extending shelf life indefinitely. While edible cold right out of the can it's far more nourishing mixed into a meal and heated.\n\nCanned goods are intended as a way to preserve food and use it an an ingredient later, not as a final meal.
LanguageData.VCE_CannedFish.label: canned fish
LanguageData.VCE_CannedFruit.description:
Cooked and preserved fruit in a hermetically sealed, durable container. Preserved food lasts indefinitely without refrigeration.

Fruit differs from produce in that it is specifically required in the production of desserts.

Canned goods are intended as a way to preserve food and use it an an ingredient later, not as a final meal.
LanguageData.VCE_CannedFruit.label: canned fruit
LanguageData.VCE_CannedMeat.description: Meat vacuum sealed and secured in a steel can effectively extending shelf life indefinitely. While edible cold right out of the can it's far more nourishing mixed into a meal and heated.\n\nCanned goods are intended as a way to preserve food and use it an an ingredient later, not as a final meal.
LanguageData.VCE_CannedMeat.label: canned meat
LanguageData.VCE_CannedProduce.description: Produce vacuum sealed and secured in a steel can effectively extending shelf life indefinitely. While edible cold right out of the can it's far more nourishing mixed into a meal and heated.\n\nCanned goods are intended as a way to preserve food and use it an an ingredient later, not as a final meal.
LanguageData.VCE_CannedProduce.label: canned produce
LanguageData.VCE_Cheese.description: Cheese is a dairy product, derived from milk and produced in a wide range of flavors, textures and forms by coagulation of the milk protein casein. Cheese is valued for its portability, long life, and high content of fat, protein, calcium, and phosphorus. It is more compact and has a longer shelf life than milk.
LanguageData.VCE_Cheese.label: cheese
LanguageData.VCE_AgaveNectar.description: A thin and semi-sweet nectar, known for its healing and anti-inflammatory properties. Although healthier than sugar, its high fructose content may be harmful if taken constantly.
LanguageData.VCE_AgaveNectar.label: agave nectar
LanguageData.VCE_ChocolateSyrup.description: A mixture of cocoa, water and sugar, forming a bitter-sweet pourable syrup, perfect for coating desserts. While still not good for you, chocolate syrup is not quite as bad as straight sugar.
LanguageData.VCE_ChocolateSyrup.label: chocolate syrup
LanguageData.VCE_DigestibleResurrectorNanites.descrip: An extremely high tech glitterworld concoction, unsafe to drink directly due to bitter taste and a mild case of intraventricular hemorrhaging. However, when cooked properly into food, these effects inexplicably seem nullified. Any pawns with this present upon death will immediately be revived as if by miracle.
LanguageData.VCE_DigestibleResurrectorNanites.label: digestible resurrector nanites
LanguageData.VCE_InsectJellyPreserves.description: Insect jelly cut with sweetener then sealed as a preserve. Very rich and sweet, while being surprisingly healthy.
LanguageData.VCE_InsectJellyPreserves.label: insect jelly preserves
LanguageData.VCE_Mayo.description: A thick emulsion of egg, oil, and other herbs. Mayonnaise, while terrible for you, adds a pleasing tang and is very calorically dense.
LanguageData.VCE_Mayo.label: mayo
LanguageData.VCE_RawSpices.description: A variety of aromatic and pungent spices, adds several sharp notes on the tongue and puts a bit of a pep in your step.
LanguageData.VCE_RawSpices.label: spices
LanguageData.VCE_RawSugar.description: Simple crystalline cane sugar, adds a much desired sweetness. Sugar is easily converted into energy by the body, which may result in a nice burst of energy.
LanguageData.VCE_RawSugar.label: sugar
LanguageData.VCE_Salt.description: Rock salt, like common salt, enhances the flavor profile of other ingredients it is cooked with.
LanguageData.VCE_Salt.label: salt
LanguageData.VCE_SmokeleafButter.description: A thickened, fatty substance made by churning milk into very thick cream, combining with smokeleaf oil and allowing it to set. Very unhealthy and hits quite a bit harder than a joint.
LanguageData.VCE_SmokeleafButter.label: smokeleaf butter
LanguageData.VCE_HotSauce.description: A potent, spicy sauce made from blending and simmering chilis and other spices. While particularly strong hot sauces hurt at first, they are known to numb pain and work up a warm sweat shortly after consumption.
LanguageData.VCE_HotSauce.label: hot sauce
LanguageData.VCE_Ketchup.description: A thick sauce made from stewing tomatoes, both salty and tangy in tandem with a sweet aftertaste. Perfect for meaty meals or fried foods.
LanguageData.VCE_Ketchup.label: ketchup
LanguageData.VCE_DeepFriedBigMeat.description: Meat tenderized then filleted, then rolled in a thick batter before being submerged in hot oil and fried. The end result is a crunchy, unhealthy yet tasty soul food - Not quite as filling as a meal, but very pleasing to eat. Deep frying meats in this matter reduces shelf life as well unless frozen.
LanguageData.VCE_DeepFriedBigMeat.label: deep fried meat
LanguageData.VCE_DeepFriedFish.description: Fish tenderized then filleted, then rolled in a thick batter before being submerged in hot oil and fried. The end result is a crunchy, unhealthy yet tasty soul food - Not quite as filling as a meal, but very pleasing to eat. Deep frying meats in this matter reduces shelf life as well unless frozen
LanguageData.VCE_DeepFriedFish.label: deep fried fish
LanguageData.VCE_DeepFriedVegetables.description: Vegetables julienned then patted dry before being dunked into hot oil until golden brown. As crispy and pleasing as they are, these deep fried treats are not as filling as a normal meal, nor as healthy. Deep frying vegetables in this matter reduces shelf life as well unless frozen.
LanguageData.VCE_DeepFriedVegetables.label: deep fried vegetables
LanguageData.VCE_FineDessert.description: A complex dessert assembled with care from a wide selection of ingredients.
LanguageData.VCE_FineDessert.label: fine dessert
LanguageData.VCE_GourmetDessert.description: A culinary work of art, characterized by refined, even elaborate preparation and presentation of an aesthetically balanced, albeit low quantity dessert.
LanguageData.VCE_GourmetDessert.label: gourmet dessert
LanguageData.VCE_LavishDessert.description: A masterpiece of the culinary arts, this dessert nourishes the body, mind, and soul.
LanguageData.VCE_LavishDessert.label: lavish dessert
LanguageData.VCE_SimpleDessert.description: A simple dessert quickly made from a few fruits and other ingredients.
LanguageData.VCE_SimpleDessert.label: simple dessert
LanguageData.VCE_MealGourmet.description: A culinary work of art, characterized by refined, even elaborate preparation and presentation of an aesthetically balanced, albeit low quantity meal.
LanguageData.VCE_MealGourmet.label: gourmet meal
LanguageData.VCE_FineGrill.description: A complex grilled meat assembled with care from a wide selection of ingredients. Very filling, grilled meats allow people to not feel hunger for a long time. Freezing them ruins this effect.
LanguageData.VCE_FineGrill.label: fine grill
LanguageData.VCE_GourmetGrill.description: A culinary work of art, characterized by refined, even elaborate preparation and presentation of an aesthetically balanced, albeit low quantity grilled meat. Very filling, grilled meats allow people to not feel hunger for a long time. Freezing them ruins this effect.
LanguageData.VCE_GourmetGrill.label: gourmet grill
LanguageData.VCE_LavishGrill.description: A masterpiece of the culinary arts, this grilled meat nourishes the body, mind, and soul. Very filling, grilled meats allow people to not feel hunger for a long time. Freezing them ruins this effect.
LanguageData.VCE_LavishGrill.label: lavish grill
LanguageData.VCE_RuinedFineGrill.description: A complex grilled meat assembled with care from a wide selection of ingredients. This grill has been frozen, and as such it is still edible but much less filling. It won't provide the "well fed" buff.
LanguageData.VCE_RuinedFineGrill.label: ruined fine grill
LanguageData.VCE_RuinedGourmetGrill.description: A culinary work of art, characterized by refined, even elaborate preparation and presentation of an aesthetically balanced, albeit low quantity grilled meat. This grill has been frozen, and as such it is still edible but much less filling. It won't provide the "well fed" buff.
LanguageData.VCE_RuinedGourmetGrill.label: ruined gourmet grill
LanguageData.VCE_RuinedLavishGrill.description: A masterpiece of the culinary arts, this grilled meat nourishes the body, mind, and soul. This grill has been frozen, and as such it is still edible but much less filling. It won't provide the "well fed" buff.
LanguageData.VCE_RuinedLavishGrill.label: ruined lavish grill
LanguageData.VCE_RuinedSimpleGrill.description: A simple grilled meat cooked from a few ingredients. This grill has been frozen, and as such it is still edible but much less filling. It won't provide the "well fed" buff.
LanguageData.VCE_RuinedSimpleGrill.label: ruined simple grill
LanguageData.VCE_SimpleGrill.description: A simple grilled meat cooked from a few ingredients. Very filling, grilled meats allow people to not feel hunger for a long time. Freezing them ruins this effect.
LanguageData.VCE_SimpleGrill.label: simple grill
LanguageData.VCE_Flour.description: Wheat flour, used in baking. Wheat flour takes a very long time to go bad, if stored in a dry environment. It was produced by wheat that has been genetically engineered to gently break into flour with a mere touch.
LanguageData.VCE_Flour.label: flour
LanguageData.VCE_CookedSoupFine.description: A complex soup assembled with care from a wide selection of ingredients.
LanguageData.VCE_CookedSoupFine.label: fine soup
LanguageData.VCE_CookedSoupGourmet.description: A culinary work of art, characterized by refined, even elaborate preparation and presentation of an aesthetically balanced, albeit low quantity soup.
LanguageData.VCE_CookedSoupGourmet.label: gourmet soup
LanguageData.VCE_CookedSoupLavish.description: A masterpiece of the culinary arts, this soup nourishes the body, mind, and soul.
LanguageData.VCE_CookedSoupLavish.label: lavish soup
LanguageData.VCE_CookedSoupSimple.description: A simple soup slow cooked from a few ingredients.
LanguageData.VCE_CookedSoupSimple.label: simple soup
LanguageData.VCE_UncookedSoupFine.description: An uncooked fine soup. This needs to be cooked in an electric pot to be edible.
LanguageData.VCE_UncookedSoupFine.label: uncooked fine soup
LanguageData.VCE_UncookedSoupGourmet.description: An uncooked gourmet soup. This needs to be cooked in an electric pot to be edible.
LanguageData.VCE_UncookedSoupGourmet.label: uncooked gourmet soup
LanguageData.VCE_UncookedSoupLavish.description: An uncooked lavish soup. This needs to be cooked in an electric pot to be edible.
LanguageData.VCE_UncookedSoupLavish.label: uncooked lavish soup
LanguageData.VCE_UncookedSoupSimple.description: An uncooked simple soup. This needs to be cooked in an electric pot to be edible.
LanguageData.VCE_UncookedSoupSimple.label: uncooked simple soup
LanguageData.VCE_Allspice.description: An anomalous, leafy vine bearing tiny unpalatable green fruits. When ground finely during cooking the fruits release a complex, peppery flavor, perfect for spicing dishes.
LanguageData.VCE_Allspice.label: allspice plant
LanguageData.VCE_Sugarcane.description: A thick, tall, tropical grass with jointed stems. While not exactly the most nutritious thing to eat on its own, this genetically modified sugarcane is easily milled into sugar and then used for a variety of culinary creations - Particularly desserts.
LanguageData.VCE_Sugarcane.label: sugarcane plant
LanguageData.VCE_Wheat.description: An amber cereal plant that takes almost an entire growing season to come to fruition. While fragile and time-consuming, this wheat has been genetically engineered to gently break into flour with a mere touch.
LanguageData.VCE_Wheat.label: wheat plant
LanguageData.VCE_Bearberry.description: These are small, pink berries known as bearberries. While raw bearberries possess an acidic and bland taste, after cooking a natural sweetness comes through and the results can be much more palatable.
LanguageData.VCE_Bearberry.label: bearberries
LanguageData.VCE_RawApple.description: Glossy, red apples. Apples have been used as snacking food, a dessert ingredient, and brewed into cider since early mankind.
LanguageData.VCE_RawApple.label: apples
LanguageData.VCE_RawBanana.description: Yellow, elongated berries, easily peeled to reveal a deeply sweet, nearly mushy flesh inside. They have been a favorite of both humans and other primates across the tropics for millenia.
LanguageData.VCE_RawBanana.label: bananas
LanguageData.VCE_RawCherry.description: Tiny purple-red sweet cherries. Cherries are notable for requiring a brief freezing season, as such they quickly became a favorite among northern occidental cultures.
LanguageData.VCE_RawCherry.label: cherries
LanguageData.VCE_RawOrange.description: Spherical and perforated, oranges are notoriously annoying to peel and quite pulpy, but known as one of the absolute juiciest fruits with a bright tang and sour aftertaste.
LanguageData.VCE_RawOrange.label: oranges
LanguageData.VCE_RawPeach.description: An edible juicy fruit called peach or nectarine. A ripe peach will have a fragrant aroma and be delicious eaten fresh out of hand.
LanguageData.VCE_RawPeach.label: peaches
LanguageData.VCE_RawPear.description: Crispy green pears. While at first similar to apples, pears are slightly more fibrous and less sweet.
LanguageData.VCE_RawPear.label: pears
LanguageData.VCE_RawPlum.description: A smooth-skinned purple fruit. Many cultures dry-age plums into prunes for long-term preservation. Instead of rotting, once the 15 days have passed the plums will dry into prunes, which are considered preserved food.
LanguageData.VCE_RawPlum.label: plums
LanguageData.VCE_RawPricklyPear.description: Pink, melon-like edible fruits, used historically for both snacking as well as medicine by desert natives.
LanguageData.VCE_RawPricklyPear.label: prickly pears
LanguageData.VCE_RawPrunes.description: Dried plums. Prunes take a very long time to deteriorate.
LanguageData.VCE_RawPrunes.label: prunes
LanguageData.VCE_RawBeets.description: An herb-like vegetable widely cultivated for humans and livestock alike, notably nutritious and historically used in dye making.
LanguageData.VCE_RawBeets.label: beets
LanguageData.VCE_RawCabbage.description: A somewhat hardy, leafy green vegetable, often chopped and then fermented or stewed.
LanguageData.VCE_RawCabbage.label: cabbages
LanguageData.VCE_RawEggplant.description: A bulbous purple fruit of the nightshade family. Eggplant is rarely served as a side, generally filling the role of the main component of the dish.
LanguageData.VCE_RawEggplant.label: eggplants
LanguageData.VCE_RawOnion.description: An edible bulb of pungent, savory taste and smell with almost as many varieties as cooking applications.
LanguageData.VCE_RawOnion.label: onions
LanguageData.VCE_RawPeas.description: Spherical green seeds tucked safely away in a fleshy pod. Peas are notable for having potentially long shelf-lives when dried properly.
LanguageData.VCE_RawPeas.label: peas
LanguageData.VCE_RawPepper.description: Tiny, spicy, crimson pods often used in sauces, relishes, or powders. While the flavor itself is mild the capsaicin contained inside means humans find them oddly addicting, if painful when consumed in mass. Animals are known to avoid eating the plant entirely.
LanguageData.VCE_RawPepper.label: peppers
LanguageData.VCE_RawPumpkin.description: Technically a giant berry, pumpkins are renowned for being trivial to both sow and harvest.
LanguageData.VCE_RawPumpkin.label: pumpkins
LanguageData.VCE_RawTomatoes.description: A series of juicy, glossy red fruits attached by vine, used commonly as a vegetable. Strong stewing releases natural sugars and mellows out acids contained within.
LanguageData.VCE_RawTomatoes.label: tomatoes
LanguageData.VCE_Beet.description: An herb-like vegetable widely cultivated for humans and livestock alike, notably nutritious and historically used in dye making. While beets do require rich soil they are quite hearty, and will continue to grow very slowly through winter. They need to be planted during growing season, though.
LanguageData.VCE_Beet.label: beet plant
LanguageData.VCE_Cabbage.description: A somewhat hardy, leafy green vegetable, often chopped and then fermented or stewed. This particular strain seems highly resistant to airborne toxin and radiation.
LanguageData.VCE_Cabbage.label: cabbage plant
LanguageData.VCE_Eggplant.description: A bulbous purple fruit of the nightshade family. Eggplant is rarely served as a side, generally filling the role of the main component of the dish. Flourishes in warm weather.
LanguageData.VCE_Eggplant.label: eggplant
LanguageData.VCE_Grass.description: Cultivated grass. Grows anywhere there is a little light and minimally fertile ground.
LanguageData.VCE_Grass.label: grass
LanguageData.VCE_Onion.description: An edible bulb of pungent, savory taste and smell with almost as many varieties as cooking applications. Cannot suffer the effects of blight.
LanguageData.VCE_Onion.label: onion plant
LanguageData.VCE_Peas.description: Spherical green seeds tucked safely away in a fleshy pod. Pea plants are known to be quite thirsty, and will benefit greatly from rainfall. Peas are also notable for having potentially long shelf-lives when dried properly.
LanguageData.VCE_Peas.label: pea plant
LanguageData.VCE_Pepper.description: Tiny, spicy, crimson pods often used in sauces, relishes, or powders. While the flavor itself is mild the capsaicin contained inside means humans find them oddly addicting, if painful when consumed in mass. Animals are known to avoid eating them entirely.
LanguageData.VCE_Pepper.label: pepper plant
LanguageData.VCE_Pumpkin.description: Technically a giant berry, pumpkins are renowned for being trivial to both sow and harvest. However, their durability means added weight and mass - Too cumbersome for hydroponics basins.
LanguageData.VCE_Pumpkin.label: pumpkin plant
LanguageData.VCE_TallGrass.description: Cultivated tall grass. Grows anywhere there is a little light and minimally fertile ground. Slows down anyone moving over it.
LanguageData.VCE_TallGrass.label: tall grass
LanguageData.VCE_Tomato.description: A series of juicy, glossy red fruits attached by vine, used commonly as a vegetable. Strong stewing releases natural sugars and mellows out acids contained within. Tomatoes are easily removed from the vine without having to uproot the plant.
LanguageData.VCE_Tomato.label: tomato plant
LanguageData.VCE_AppleTree.description: A tree bearing glossy, red apples, while a myriad of different varieties exist this particular type favors warm summers and mild winters. Apples have been used as snacking food, a dessert ingredient, and brewed into cider since early mankind.
LanguageData.VCE_AppleTree.label: apple tree
LanguageData.VCE_BananaTree.description: A broad-leafed, tall tropical tree adorned with a cluster of yellow, elongated berries succinctly known as bananas. Bananas are easily peeled to reveal a deeply sweet, nearly mushy flesh inside and have been a favorite of both humans and other primates across the tropics for millenia. Bananas are easy to harvest, since a whole "hand" can be cut at the same time.
LanguageData.VCE_BananaTree.label: banana tree
LanguageData.VCE_BearBerryBush.description: A low-lying bush speckled with pink berries that thrives in poor, even sandy soil. While raw bearberries possess an acidic and bland taste, after cooking a natural sweetness comes through and the results can be much more palatable. This plant can be grown in pots.
LanguageData.VCE_BearBerryBush.label: bearberry bush
LanguageData.VCE_CherryTree.description: A tree bearing tiny purple-red sweet cherries. Cherry trees are notable for requiring a brief freezing season, as such their fruit quickly became a favorite among northern occidental civilizations. This particular genus is quite beautiful, speckled with pink blossoms throughout, blooming fully each winter.
LanguageData.VCE_CherryTree.label: cherry tree
LanguageData.VCE_Opuntia.description: More often referred to as the “prickly pear” cactus, this plant is known to be quite bountiful even in hot, dry deserts. The pads of this tall, spiny cactus bloom pink, melon-like edible fruits, used historically for both snacking as well as medicine by desert natives.
LanguageData.VCE_Opuntia.label: opuntia
LanguageData.VCE_OrangeTree.description: A bushy, semi-tropical tree dotted with orange fruits well equipped for growing in humid environments and in wet soil. Spherical and perforated, oranges are notoriously annoying to peel and quite pulpy, but known as one of the absolute juiciest fruits with a bright tang and sour aftertaste.
LanguageData.VCE_OrangeTree.label: orange tree
LanguageData.VCE_PeachTree.description: A deciduous tree bearing fuzzy, stone fruit known as peaches, peach trees often require full sunlight and warm temperatures. Ancient oriental cultures cultivated these trees groves at a time, noting its ability to survive in drought conditions.
LanguageData.VCE_PeachTree.label: peach tree
LanguageData.VCE_PearTree.description: A stout tree bearing crispy green pears, pear trees are notable for requiring high amounts of water and cool weather, and also doing quite well in shade. While at first similar to apples, pears are slightly more fibrous and less sweet.
LanguageData.VCE_PearTree.label: pear tree
LanguageData.VCE_PlumTree.description: A bushy tree bearing a smooth-skinned purple fruit. Plums are particularly tart and produced into a variety of jams, jellies, and ciders. Many cultures also dry-age plums into prunes for long-term preservation.
LanguageData.VCE_PlumTree.label: plum tree
LanguageData.VCE_StewPot.description: A wood-fired stove with a top side perfectly indented for a large pot. The lack of need for electricity means this cooking apparatus is perfect for the rugged outdoorsman or medieval peasant alike. Stews are heartier and easier to cook than proper soups. Stews often meld into a singular flavor over long periods of time, perfect for gamey or “less enticing” meat products.
LanguageData.VCE_StewPot.label: stew pot
LanguageData.VCE_CookedStewFine.description: A complex stew assembled with care from a wide selection of ingredients.
LanguageData.VCE_CookedStewFine.label: fine stew
LanguageData.VCE_CookedStewLavish.description: A masterpiece of the culinary arts, this stew nourishes the body, mind, and soul.
LanguageData.VCE_CookedStewLavish.label: lavish stew
LanguageData.VCE_CookedStewSimple.description: A simple stew slow cooked from a few ingredients.
LanguageData.VCE_CookedStewSimple.label: simple stew
LanguageData.VCE_UncookedStewFine.description: An uncooked fine stew. This needs to be cooked in a stew pot to be edible.
LanguageData.VCE_UncookedStewFine.label: uncooked fine stew
LanguageData.VCE_UncookedStewLavish.description: An uncooked lavish stew. This needs to be cooked in a stew pot to be edible.
LanguageData.VCE_UncookedStewLavish.label: uncooked lavish stew
LanguageData.VCE_UncookedStewSimple.description: An uncooked simple stew. This needs to be cooked in a stew pot to be edible.
LanguageData.VCE_UncookedStewSimple.label: uncooked simple stew
LanguageData.VCE_SoyFermenter.description: A soy fermenter is a cilindrical, metallic drum where soybeans are pressed, mixed with aspergillus bacteria and grain, and aged for long time periods. The result is soy sauce of increasing quality, which can be used as a food condiment.
LanguageData.VCE_SoyFermenter.label: soy sauce fermenter
LanguageData.VCE_SushiPrepTable.description: A working table to cut, slice and prepare all kinds of sushi.
LanguageData.VCE_SushiPrepTable.label: sushi prep table
LanguageData.VCE_SoySauce.description: A fermented paste of soybeans, roasted grain, brine, and Aspergillus bacteria. Soy sauce can be added directly to food, and is used as a dip or salt flavor in cooking, as well as providing a rich umami taste.
LanguageData.VCE_SoySauce.label: soy sauce
LanguageData.VCE_WasabiPaste.description: A paste made from ground wasabi rhizomes and used as a pungent condiment for sushi and other foods. It is similar in taste to hot mustard rather than chili peppers in that it stimulates the nose more than the tongue. It loses its flavour and deteriorates extremely fast.
LanguageData.VCE_WasabiPaste.label: wasabi paste
LanguageData.VCE_DeepFriedSushi.description: Sushi is one of the healthiest foods about. So sooner or later someone was going to deep fry it and make it the opposite. The end result is a crunchy, unhealthy yet tasty soul food - Not quite as filling as a meal, but very pleasing to eat. Deep frying sushi reduces shelf life as well unless frozen.
LanguageData.VCE_DeepFriedSushi.label: deep fried sushi
LanguageData.VCE_HumanSurimi.description: A nondistinct paste mimicking the texture of lobster, crab, or other shellfish. Only, this one... is made with human meat. A delicacy for cannibalistic Rim tribes, and a profoundly disgusting and taboo culinary experience for anyone else.
LanguageData.VCE_HumanSurimi.label: human "surimi"
LanguageData.VCE_RawSoybean.description: An edible bean that can be used as a vegetable in a multitude of recipes, and most famously known as the main ingredient in delicious soy sauce.
LanguageData.VCE_RawSoybean.label: soybeans
LanguageData.VCE_RawWasabi.description: A plant of the horseradish family. A paste made from its ground rhizomes is used as a pungent condiment for sushi and other foods. It is similar in taste to hot mustard rather than chili peppers in that it stimulates the nose more than the tongue.
LanguageData.VCE_RawWasabi.label: wasabi
LanguageData.VCE_Chirashizushi.description: A simple chirashizushi bowl made from a few ingredients. In Chirashizushi rice is served in a bowl and topped with a variety of raw fish and vegetable garnishes. It is commonly eaten because it is filling, fast and easy to make.
LanguageData.VCE_Chirashizushi.label: chirashizushi bowl (simple)
LanguageData.VCE_Futomaki.description: A masterpiece of the culinary arts, this sushi nourishes the body, mind, and soul. Futomaki is a large cylindrical piece, usually with nori on the outside. A typical futomaki is five to six centimetres in diameter. They are often made with two, three, or more fillings that are chosen for their complementary tastes and colors.
LanguageData.VCE_Futomaki.label: futomaki (lavish)
LanguageData.VCE_Gunkanmaki.description: A culinary work of art, characterized by refined, even elaborate preparation and presentation of an aesthetically balanced, albeit low quantity sushi. Gunkanmaki is a special type of nigirizushi: an oval, hand-formed clump of sushi rice that has a strip of nori wrapped around its perimeter to form a vessel that is filled with some soft, loose or fine-chopped ingredient that requires the confinement of nori such as roe.
LanguageData.VCE_Gunkanmaki.label: gunkanmaki (gourmet)
LanguageData.VCE_Nigiri.description: Complex nigiri sushi assembled with care from a wide selection of ingredients. Nigirizushi consists of an oblong mound of sushi rice that the chef presses between the palms of the hands to form an oval-shaped ball, and a topping (the neta) draped over the ball.
LanguageData.VCE_Nigiri.label: nigiri (fine)
LanguageData.VCE_Norimaki.description: Simple norimaki sushi made from a few ingredients. Norimaki are a type of sushi wrapped with seaweed.
LanguageData.VCE_Norimaki.label: norimaki (simple)
LanguageData.VCE_Oshizushi.description: A culinary work of art, characterized by refined, even elaborate preparation and presentation of an aesthetically balanced, albeit low quantity sushi. In oshizushi, a block-shaped piece is formed using a wooden mold.
