The chef lines the bottom of the mold with the toppings, covers them with sushi rice, and then presses the lid of the mold down to create a compact, rectilinear block. The block is removed from the mold and then cut into bite-sized pieces.
LanguageData.VCE_Oshizushi.label: oshizushi (gourmet)
LanguageData.VCE_Temaki.description: A masterpiece of the culinary arts, this sushi nourishes the body, mind, and soul. Temaki is a large cone-shaped piece of nori on the outside and the ingredients spilling out the wide end. A typical temaki is about 10 centimetres long, and is eaten with fingers because it is too awkward to pick it up with chopsticks.
LanguageData.VCE_Temaki.label: temaki (lavish)
LanguageData.VCE_Uramaki.description: Complex uramaki sushi assembled with care from a wide selection of ingredients. Uramaki is a medium-sized cylindrical piece with two or more fillings, and it differs from other maki because the rice is on the outside and the nori seaweed inside.
LanguageData.VCE_Uramaki.label: uramaki (fine)
LanguageData.VCE_Soybean.description: A species of legume widely grown for its edible bean, which has numerous uses. Soybean plants grow quite fast, but they are very sensitive to temperature, with optimum growing conditions in mean temperatures of 20 to 30 °C (68 to 86 °F). Temperatures outside that range stunt growth significantly.
LanguageData.VCE_Soybean.label: soybean plant
LanguageData.VCE_Wasabi.description: A plant of the horseradish family. A paste made from its ground rhizomes is used as a pungent condiment for sushi and other foods. It is similar in taste to hot mustard rather than chili peppers in that it stimulates the nose more than the tongue. Wasabi is widely considered to be one of the most difficult plants to grow, since it needs a temperate, extremely humid environment. It will receive a growth penalty if less than 20 tiles of water are found in a 6 tile radius.
LanguageData.VCE_Wasabi.label: wasabi plant
LanguageData.VBE_AmbrandyAwful.label: ambrandy (awful)
LanguageData.VBE_AmbrandyExcellent.label: ambrandy (excellent)
LanguageData.VBE_AmbrandyGood.label: ambrandy (good)
LanguageData.VBE_AmbrandyLegendary.label: ambrandy (legendary)
LanguageData.VBE_AmbrandyMasterwork.label: ambrandy (masterwork)
LanguageData.VBE_AmbrandyNormal.label: ambrandy (normal)
LanguageData.VBE_AmbrandyPoor.label: ambrandy (poor)
LanguageData.VBE_Gin.description: A particularly mild and colorless spirit, aromatically speaking this bottle has a pine-tree like scent as well as subtle fruity notes. This liquor has a reputation for being light-hearted and fun to drink and for causing what is known as “gin drunkenness”, during which one feels increased empathy and understanding.
LanguageData.VBE_Gin.label: gin
LanguageData.VBE_Tequila.description: A fermented liquor made from agave sap, a favorite of wild, rowdy banditos and muffalo wranglers alike. Includes a high-protein snack at the bottom of the bottle believed to bring good luck. Imbibers are known to excel at extended combat.
LanguageData.VBE_Tequila.label: tequila
LanguageData.VBE_Vodka.description: A clear and mostly flavorless alcohol made from fermented potatoes. While vodka is drinkable alone it is mostly known for being a perfect mixing agent and for causing foolishly high levels of boldness in those who choose to drink it.
LanguageData.VBE_Vodka.label: vodka
LanguageData.VBE_DoubleWhiskey.description: The result of whiskey being aged once more in an unused or lightly used secondary barrel. Flavor profiles are stronger and the afterburn in one's throat notably reduced. The increased aging time for fine whiskey generally portrays a sense of mercantilism and class among its consumers.
LanguageData.VBE_DoubleWhiskey.label: double-aged whiskey
LanguageData.VBE_Whiskey.description: A potent liquor distilled from fermented grain. The flavors of the aging barrel tend to permeate this spirit more than others. A woody, almost smokey flavor is pronounced throughout. Simple whiskeys are stereotypically associated with rough manual laborers and hard workers, a common sight in steamworlds across the galaxy.
LanguageData.VBE_Whiskey.label: whiskey
LanguageData.VBE_Bar.description: A somewhat ornate countertop stocked to the brim with different vessels and devices for making a variety of drinks. An ice box, a series of taps, hoses, and carbonators means anybody tending it can brew much quicker than at a rudimentary brewery.
LanguageData.VBE_Bar.label: bar
LanguageData.VBE_HotCoffee.description: A warm, bitter beverage usually complemented with milk and sugar. A favorite among students and office workers across the galaxy due to high caffeine content and pleasing aroma.
LanguageData.VBE_HotCoffee.label: coffee
LanguageData.VBE_IceCoffee.description: An icy, chilled coffee. Chilling coffee is preferred by many in warmer climates, anecdotally it also smooths out bitter flavors and brings out noticeable, albeit subtle nuttiness. Just like its warm counterpart, it is ubiquitous among students and professionals alike.
LanguageData.VBE_IceCoffee.label: iced coffee
LanguageData.VBE_EnergyDrink.description: A sour and fizzy drink finished off with a hint of bitterness. The combination of sugar and caffeine means a burst of energy upfront, but with a crash later. Notable aftertaste of radiation.
LanguageData.VBE_EnergyDrink.label: energy drink
LanguageData.VBE_Juice.description: Juiced fruit, tart and tangy, can be made from a singular fruit or a mixed variety - Also known as a juice medley. Many drinks can be brewed or mixed using juices as base. While no more nutritious than non juiced fruit it is much easier to store and transport.
LanguageData.VBE_Juice.label: juice
LanguageData.VBE_Soda.description: A very sweet and semi-citrusy beverage best enjoyed chilled. Whilst not exactly nutritious the sugar rush is welcomed and the effervescence is quite refreshing.
LanguageData.VBE_Soda.label: soda
LanguageData.VBE_Tea.description: A pleasing, aromatic beverage prepared by steeping a bag of tea leaves in boiling water. Tea can range anywhere from citrusy to bitter to spicy flavor profiles. Tea has been one of humanity's favorite beverages since ancient times and is believed to possess many natural healing properties.
LanguageData.VBE_Tea.label: tea
LanguageData.VBE_AmbrandyDistillery.description: A gilded distillery of very delicate craftsmanship meant for aging ambrosia must into a drinkable form.
LanguageData.VBE_AmbrandyDistillery.label: ambrandy distillery
LanguageData.VBE_GinStill.description: A fully metal industrial distilling device, meant to purify and ferment gin into a drinkable form. The metallic design means aging is possible in a wide range of temperatures and conditions.
LanguageData.VBE_GinStill.label: gin still
LanguageData.VBE_SodaFountain.description: An all-in-one soda manufacturing unit. Syrup is mixed with water thoroughly, carbonated and then deeply chilled before being dispensed into sterilized bottles.
LanguageData.VBE_SodaFountain.label: soda fountain
LanguageData.VBE_TequilaBarrel.description: A small still and fermenter combo unit, perfect for storing behind the counter of a warm saloon, but doesn’t fare very well in rain.
LanguageData.VBE_TequilaBarrel.label: tequila barrel
LanguageData.VBE_VodkaDrum.description: A cheap container meant for storing water retrofitted to be air tight for short-term fermenting.
LanguageData.VBE_VodkaDrum.label: vodka drum
LanguageData.VBE_WhiskeyCask.description: A huge barrel meant for aging a high volume of whiskey at once. Natural woody flavors are imparted into the mixture as the barrel itself absorbs a portion of the liquid during the fermenting process. Any sunlight hitting the barrel may slow down the fermenting process, or even ruin the whole batch.
LanguageData.VBE_WhiskeyCask.label: whiskey cask
LanguageData.VBE_Cider.description: A barely alcoholic beverage meant to be refreshing and fruity. Try as anybody might it is pointless trying to get inebriated by cider alone, thus it is popular among those with prior addiction. Anecdotally, many praise it for supposed antioxidant properties.
LanguageData.VBE_Cider.label: cider
LanguageData.VBE_UnfermentedCider.description: Unfermented cider with a starter in an air-tight bottle. If left alone to develop the proper bacteria the end result will be a weak alcoholic beverage.
LanguageData.VBE_UnfermentedCider.label: unfermented cider
LanguageData.VBE_FineCocktail.description: A fine complex cocktail, mixed precisely from multiple ingredients.
LanguageData.VBE_FineCocktail.label: fine cocktail
LanguageData.VBE_GourmetCocktail.description: A veritable miracle of mixology. This cocktails every factor from temperature of glass, choice of stirring instrument and type of garnish has been precisely calculated for the maximum flavor and body possible.
LanguageData.VBE_GourmetCocktail.label: gourmet cocktail
LanguageData.VBE_LavishCocktail.description: A masterpiece of bartending incarnate, this cocktail was mixed from a wide variety of ingredients and chilled to perfection before meticulously shaken and served.
LanguageData.VBE_LavishCocktail.label: lavish cocktail
LanguageData.VBE_SimpleCocktail.description: A simple cocktail quickly mixed from a duo of ingredients.
LanguageData.VBE_SimpleCocktail.label: simple cocktail
LanguageData.VBE_GoJuiceStock.description: A gelatinized cube of Go-Juice, able to be digested rather than injected. While it can be used in any meal, it is often melted into soups or used as a thickening agent in cocktails. Provides a very diluted version of the drug without risk of addictions.
LanguageData.VBE_GoJuiceStock.label: go-juice stock
LanguageData.VBE_LuciferiumPowder.description: A very finely blended powder made from luciferium. Usually put around rims of alcoholic drinks in bars frequented by the wealthy and powerful. Provides a very diluted version of the drug without risk of addictions.
LanguageData.VBE_LuciferiumPowder.label: luciferium powder
LanguageData.VBE_WakeUpPowder.description: A crushed and powdered form of wake-up, surprisingly sweet and sometimes used as a replacement for sugar in desserts or dissolved into a morning espresso. Provides a very diluted version of the drug without risk of addictions.
LanguageData.VBE_WakeUpPowder.label: wake-up powder
LanguageData.VBE_AmbrandyMush.description: Sliced and pressed ambrosia, can be left alone to ripen into must to be further distilled into an extremely fine liquor.
LanguageData.VBE_AmbrandyMush.label: ambrosia mush
LanguageData.VBE_AmbrandyMust.description: Fully ripened ambrosia liquified into essentially a thick juice and left alone to grow bacteria for fermenting. Ready to be distilled into a drinkable form.
LanguageData.VBE_AmbrandyMust.label: ambrandy must
LanguageData.VBE_GinMush.description: A clear but cloudy mixture of juiced berries and miscellaneous grain, ready to be fermented.
LanguageData.VBE_GinMush.label: gin mush
LanguageData.VBE_SodaSyrup.description: A thick and disgustingly sweet mixture made by boiling citric acid and natural sugars together - Usually derived straight from cane sugar or as corn syrup. Must be cut with water and left to carbonate briefly in a sealed environment before consumption.
LanguageData.VBE_SodaSyrup.label: soda syrup
LanguageData.VBE_TequilaMust.description: A thin mixture of juiced agave sap or prickly cactus pear and insect meat starter, ready to be cut and fermented in a barrel.
LanguageData.VBE_TequilaMust.label: tequila must
LanguageData.VBE_VodkaMash.description: A thick, starchy mash of raw potatoes and a starter, ready to be fermented.
LanguageData.VBE_VodkaMash.label: vodka mash
LanguageData.VBE_WhiskeyMash.description: A starchy and mostly liquid mixture with pulpy chunks strewn throughout. Ready To be aged.
LanguageData.VBE_WhiskeyMash.label: whiskey mash
LanguageData.VBE_RawCoffee.description: The pits of coffee plant berries, known succinctly as “coffee beans”. They can be roasted to bring out a robust and bitter flavor, as well as natural caffeine.
LanguageData.VBE_RawCoffee.label: coffee beans
LanguageData.VBE_RawTea.description: The leaves of the tea plant. They can be boiled into a variety of earthy and soothing beverages.
LanguageData.VBE_RawTea.label: tea leaves
LanguageData.VBE_RawTobacco.description: The leaves of the tobacco plant. Tobacco leaves can be dried quite easily to then be rolled and smoked, providing a mild stimulating effect.
LanguageData.VBE_RawTobacco.label: tobacco leaves
LanguageData.VBE_Cigarettes.description: A small, rolled paper tube stuffed loosely with tobacco leaves. Offers a brief euphoria as well as mental stimulation, and is very addictive. The pungent odor is generally disliked at first, but becomes much more pleasurable as the smoker becomes more addicted. Can be crafted without equipment, at a crafting spot.
LanguageData.VBE_Cigarettes.label: cigarettes
LanguageData.VBE_Cigars.description: A fat, dried tobacco leaf stuffed to the brim with more tobacco leaves and a gold foil wrap around it. Cigars have a historically posh presentation and are usually only affordable by those who mingle with glitterworld elite regularly. Cigars are often flavored with citrus or vanilla to make their flavor and odor less acrid.
LanguageData.VBE_Cigars.label: cigars
LanguageData.VBE_Plant_Coffee.description: A low-lying bush of tropical origin, dotted by small reddish berries when fully grown. The pits of these berries are known succinctly as “coffee beans” and can be roasted to bring out a robust and bitter flavor, as well as natural caffeine. This particular genus has been selectively bred for quick maturing compared to its ancient counterpart.
LanguageData.VBE_Plant_Coffee.label: coffee plant
LanguageData.VBE_Plant_Tea.description: A very leafy shrub that is particularly easy to cultivate. Since early mankind it was discovered the leaves of these plants can be boiled into a variety of earthy and soothing tea. This particular genus has been selectively bred for quick maturing compared to its ancient counterpart.
LanguageData.VBE_Plant_Tea.label: tea grass
LanguageData.VBE_Plant_Tobacco.description: A short, broad-leafed plant that thrives in warm climates. Tobacco leaves can be dried quite easily to then be rolled and smoked, providing a mild stimulating effect.
LanguageData.VBE_Plant_Tobacco.label: tobacco plant
LanguageData.VBE_TypewritersTable.description: A work station with all the necessary equipment to write books. This station is equipped with a typewriter which speeds up the work significantly, but does require connection to power.
LanguageData.VBE_TypewritersTable.label: typewriter table
LanguageData.VBE_WritersTable.description: A work station with all the necessary equipment to write books.
LanguageData.VBE_WritersTable.label: writers table
LanguageData.VBE_Filth_Newspaper.label: old newspaper
LanguageData.VBE_Blueprint.description: A blueprint containing technological secrets. Studying it will provide your colony with full understanding of a given research project.\n\nBlueprints containing too advanced knowledge cannot be read until more research projects are completed.
LanguageData.VBE_Blueprint.label: blueprint
LanguageData.VBE_BookAnimals.label: animals book
LanguageData.VBE_BookArt.label: art book
LanguageData.VBE_BookConstruction.label: construction book
LanguageData.VBE_BookCooking.label: cooking book
LanguageData.VBE_BookCrafting.label: crafting book
LanguageData.VBE_BookGrowing.label: growing book
LanguageData.VBE_BookIntellectual.label: intellectual book
LanguageData.VBE_BookMedical.label: medical book
LanguageData.VBE_BookMelee.label: melee book
LanguageData.VBE_BookMining.label: mining book
LanguageData.VBE_BookShooting.label: shooting book
LanguageData.VBE_BookSocial.label: social book
LanguageData.VBE_Map.description: A paper map containing information about a point of interest. Studying it will unlock a location on the world map.
LanguageData.VBE_Map.label: map
LanguageData.VBE_Newspaper.description: A perishable newspaper. Reading it will provide colonists with joy.\n\nOut of date newspaper cannot be read and needs to be cleaned up.
LanguageData.VBE_Newspaper.label: newspaper
LanguageData.VBE_UnfinishedBook.description: An unfinished book of some sorts.
LanguageData.VBE_UnfinishedBook.label: unfinished book
LanguageData.VBE_UnfinishedNewspaper.label: unfinished newspaper
LanguageData.VCE_Plant_AloeVera.description: A cultivated succulent with thick, greenish, fleshy leaves that fan out from the short stem. Whilst not as beautiful as flowers, this plant has a very long lifespan.
LanguageData.VCE_Plant_AloeVera.label: aloe vera plant
LanguageData.VCE_Plant_BunnyEarsCactus.description: A cultivated succulent with pads resembling ears of a bunny, and spines resembling fuzzy fur. Whilst not as beautiful as flowers, this plant has a very long lifespan.
LanguageData.VCE_Plant_BunnyEarsCactus.label: bunny ears cactus
LanguageData.VCE_Plant_Echeveria.description: A cultivated succulent forming a rosette, native to arid shrublands. Whilst not as beautiful as flowers, this plant has a very long lifespan.
LanguageData.VCE_Plant_Echeveria.label: echeveria plant
LanguageData.VCE_Plant_FairyWashboard.description: A cultivated succulent with rosette forming long triangular leaves. Whilst not as beautiful as flowers, this plant has a very long lifespan.
LanguageData.VCE_Plant_FairyWashboard.label: fairy washboard plant
LanguageData.VCE_Plant_JadePlant.description: A cultivated succulent with thick, woody stems and oval-shaped leaves. Whilst not as beautiful as flowers, this plant has a very long lifespan.
LanguageData.VCE_Plant_JadePlant.label: jade plant
LanguageData.VCE_Plant_PeyotePlant.description: A cultivated succulent resembling thick buttons. Whilst not as beautiful as flowers, this plant has a very long lifespan.
LanguageData.VCE_Plant_PeyotePlant.label: peyote plant
LanguageData.VCE_Plant_PincushionPlant.description: A cultivated succulent of cactus variety, with pointy spikes covering its exterior. Whilst not as beautiful as flowers, this plant has a very long lifespan.
LanguageData.VCE_Plant_PincushionPlant.label: pincushion plant
LanguageData.VCE_Plant_Schlumbergera.description: A cultivated succulent with bell-shaped flowers. Whilst not as beautiful as flowers, this plant has a very long lifespan.
LanguageData.VCE_Plant_Schlumbergera.label: schlumbergera plant
LanguageData.VCE_Plant_SnakePlant.description: A cultivated succulent with stiff, green banded leaves with a yellow border. Whilst not as beautiful as flowers, this plant has a very long lifespan.
LanguageData.VCE_Plant_SnakePlant.label: snake plant
LanguageData.VCE_Plant_SweetheartPlant.description: A cultivated succulent with heart-shaped leaves on a tall stem. Whilst not as beautiful as flowers, this plant has a very long lifespan.
LanguageData.VCE_Plant_SweetheartPlant.label: sweetheart plant
LanguageData.VBE_EspressoMachineBuilding.description: An espresso machine brews coffee by forcing pressurized water near boiling point through a "puck" of ground coffee and a filter in order to produce a thick, concentrated coffee called espresso. This particular machine also has the possibility of brewing tea using a separate system.
LanguageData.VBE_EspressoMachineBuilding.label: espresso machine
LanguageData.VBE_BlackTea.description: A particularly bitter tea cut with creamy milk, wakes one up and helps cure ailments.
LanguageData.VBE_BlackTea.label: black tea
LanguageData.VBE_CannibalCoffee.description: For a very particular… refined coffee drinker.
LanguageData.VBE_CannibalCoffee.label: cannibal coffee
LanguageData.VBE_DalgonaCoffee.description: A trendy, newer drink in the world of coffee rumored to originate from far off steamworlds. Coffee granules are rapidly mixed with sugar to form a thick, luxuriously sweet foam. Afterwards the foam is dispersed over deeply chilled milk before drinking. Arguably the perfect drink to sip on while studying or reading.
LanguageData.VBE_DalgonaCoffee.label: dalgona coffee
LanguageData.VBE_EarlGreyTea.description: A very strong and semi-sweet fruity tea. Floral notes dance around one's pallet at even the most dull scent. People who finish the drink feel strangely urged to colonize.
LanguageData.VBE_EarlGreyTea.label: earl grey tea
LanguageData.VBE_LemonCoffee.description: A particularly strange combination. Already acidic coffee garnished with a wedge of more acidic lemon, meant to be juiced into the coffee before quickly consumed while hot. An acquired taste to be sure but lauded by those who do prefer it. Specially indicated to combat intestinal parasites.
LanguageData.VBE_LemonCoffee.label: espresso romano
LanguageData.VBE_GreenTea.description: A light, citrusy green tea spiked with sugar to create a buttery, almost nutty flavor.
LanguageData.VBE_GreenTea.label: green tea
LanguageData.VBE_IrishCoffee.description: Whiskey and coffee blended thoroughly before forming a head and being topped with a spoonful of whipped cream. Perfect for those who just got out of bed and are already wanting to go back to it.
LanguageData.VBE_IrishCoffee.label: irish coffee
LanguageData.VBE_Kombucha.description: A sugary tea left out to ferment, after fermenting the end product is a sweet and sour, sometimes even naturally fizzy drink known as kombucha. Praised for health benefits, though some remain skeptical. Kombucha acts as a home remedy against the flu, reducing its severity by 5%.
LanguageData.VBE_Kombucha.label: kombucha tea
LanguageData.VBE_LatteCoffee.description: Potent espresso mixed with fresh milk to help ease bitterness before pouring and serving. Lattes have a reputation of being particularly meant for “sophisticated” individuals.
LanguageData.VBE_LatteCoffee.label: latte coffee
LanguageData.VBE_Lemonade.description: A sweet beverage mixed together with lemon juice, sugar, and water.
LanguageData.VBE_Lemonade.label: lemonade
LanguageData.VBE_LemonTea.description: A dark tea cut with water, chilled and garnished with a wedge of bright lemon.
LanguageData.VBE_LemonTea.label: lemon tea
LanguageData.VBE_MasalaChai.description: A musty, warm, almost woody tea crafted by simple leaves and complex spices. Masala chai acts as a home remedy against wound infections, reducing the severity of a random infection by 5%.
LanguageData.VBE_MasalaChai.label: masala chai
LanguageData.VBE_MintCoffee.description: Bitter espresso mixed with cream or milk, then further mixed with mint oil and garnished. Cool and velvety coffee gives way to an even cooler, soothing aftertaste, not to mention great smelling breath.
LanguageData.VBE_MintCoffee.label: mint coffee
LanguageData.VBE_MintTea.description: Black tea leaves mixed with mint leaves and deeply steeped, then garnished with additional mint leaves. Leaves your breath surprisingly fresh.
LanguageData.VBE_MintTea.label: mint tea
LanguageData.VBE_MochaCoffee.description: Coffee mixed with sweet and luxurious chocolate, very fun to drink. Those of snobbish persuasion would argue mocha is more of a dessert than an actual coffee drink.
LanguageData.VBE_MochaCoffee.label: mocha coffee
LanguageData.VBE_NoonChai.description: An ancient Kashmiri tea mixing tea leaves, a pinch of salt and baking soda, which results in an striking pink beverage that is famous for being not only delicious but also healthy for the body. Used for treating muscle parasites.
LanguageData.VBE_NoonChai.label: noon chai
LanguageData.VBE_PumpkinCoffee.description: Cheap, mass produced generic coffee seeped in an array of spices, oddly excluding any actual pumpkin. Thousands of years ago, this type of coffee was stereotypically consumed by red-haired hipsters with man buns.
LanguageData.VBE_PumpkinCoffee.label: pumpkin spice coffee
LanguageData.VBE_Yuenyeung.description: A mixture of coffee and tea. Tea and coffee drinkers respectively set their differences aside while concocting this unholy amalgamation. It is usually served cold, but whether it’s served hot or cold it’s equally confusing to the imbiber.
LanguageData.VBE_Yuenyeung.label: yuenyeung
LanguageData.VBE_MintLeaves.description: Mint leaves are infamous for their cooling effect on one's mouth, as well as ease of seeping into an array of teas or delicately garnishing a dish. Mint leaves rot quite fast.
LanguageData.VBE_MintLeaves.label: mint leaves
LanguageData.VCE_RawLemon.description: An acid and sour tasting fruit. The juice, zest, and pulp are often used in cooking, often on fish and other meat for better taste. Lemon is also used to flavour drinks, such as lemonade, or mixed with tea and coffee.
LanguageData.VCE_RawLemon.label: lemons
LanguageData.VBE_MintPlant.description: A plant sprouting into an array of mint leaves. Mint leaves are infamous for their cooling effect on one's mouth, as well as ease of seeping into an array of teas or delicately garnishing a dish. Flourishes indoors and in plant pots, while being pleasant to look at.
LanguageData.VBE_MintPlant.label: mint plant
LanguageData.VBE_LemonTree.description: A tall tree native to both deserts and tropics alike. Bright, sour lemons grow profusely on this tree, some lemons may even mature early and fall to the ground before the primary harvest is ready.
LanguageData.VBE_LemonTree.label: lemon tree
LanguageData.VIEHAR_Apparel_Beads.description: Large beads made of any variety of material used to signify devotion to one's beliefs.
LanguageData.VIEHAR_Apparel_Beads.label: beads
LanguageData.VIEHAR_Apparel_BishopHat.description: A tall, regal ceremonial headdress meant to adorn the head of leaders of Christian faith.
LanguageData.VIEHAR_Apparel_BishopHat.label: bishop hat
LanguageData.VIEHAR_Apparel_BrimlessHat.description: A small, plain hat meant to rest gently on one’s head. Those who wear it often claim it radiates a certain angelic overtone.
LanguageData.VIEHAR_Apparel_BrimlessHat.label: brimless hat
LanguageData.VIEHAR_Apparel_CommisarHat.description: A distinguished, brimmed hat adorned with a badge of bravery, meant to inspire cooperation in one's subordinates at any cost.
LanguageData.VIEHAR_Apparel_CommisarHat.label: commissar hat
LanguageData.VIEHAR_Apparel_DeerSkull.description: A simple hood with a forward facing mounted animal skull on the front. This piece is commonly seen worn by those of tribal persuasion and those close to nature.
LanguageData.VIEHAR_Apparel_DeerSkull.label: deer skull
LanguageData.VIEHAR_Apparel_Fez.description: A flat-topped conical hat decorated with a black tassel, often worn by desert nobility.
LanguageData.VIEHAR_Apparel_Fez.label: fez
LanguageData.VIEHAR_Apparel_Headbag.description: A cult-like hood meant to be worn for anonymity and collectivism during rituals or worn as a sign of great humility.
LanguageData.VIEHAR_Apparel_Headbag.label: headbag
LanguageData.VIEHAR_Apparel_Headscarf.description: A warm, floppy hat which covers one's ears, often worn by those belonging to alpinian tribes.
LanguageData.VIEHAR_Apparel_Headscarf.label: headscarf
LanguageData.VIEHAR_Apparel_HorseMask.description: A jovial hat that mimics the appearance of an equestrian, party animals far and wide know it to be an epitome of comical relief.
LanguageData.VIEHAR_Apparel_HorseMask.label: horse mask
LanguageData.VIEHAR_Apparel_MilitaryCap.description: A standard rank-and-file military cap meant to be worn during non-combat procedures, though brave officers are known to wear them during combat as well.
LanguageData.VIEHAR_Apparel_MilitaryCap.label: military cap
LanguageData.VIEHAR_Apparel_Skullcap.description: A small, extremely plain hat meant to cover only the crown of one's head. Often worn continuously by the pious and devout alike.
LanguageData.VIEHAR_Apparel_Skullcap.label: skullcap
LanguageData.VIEHAR_Apparel_SpikedSkullcap.descriptio: A halo-shaped headpiece meant to accentuate the wearer as being that of profane standing. Often worn by those who find salvation in suffering.
LanguageData.VIEHAR_Apparel_SpikedSkullcap.label: spiked skullcap
LanguageData.VIEHAR_Apparel_WideHat.description: A very wide-brimmed and rustic hat meant to be worn by sheriffs and lawbringers of far off steamworlds.
LanguageData.VIEHAR_Apparel_WideHat.label: wide hat
LanguageData.VIEHAR_Apparel_Rags.description: A set of rags meant to cover oneself, offers almost no protection or warmth, but meant to convey detachment from life's pleasures and peace with oneself.
LanguageData.VIEHAR_Apparel_Rags.label: rags
LanguageData.VFEArch_FineWall.description: A beautiful, impassable wall. Capable of holding up a roof.
LanguageData.VFEArch_FineWall.label: fine wall
LanguageData.VFEArch_AnimalGate.description: A doorway with a gate easy enough to open so that it can be pushed open by animals. It allows animals to pass, but doesn’t insulate the room. Animal gates are often used as barn entrances in warmer climates.
LanguageData.VFEArch_AnimalGate.label: animal gate
LanguageData.VFEArch_ArmoredDoor.description: Divides rooms. Armored doors must be manually opened, which slows people down. Armored doors are nearly entirely blast resistant and barely take damage from breaching methods.
LanguageData.VFEArch_ArmoredDoor.label: armored door
LanguageData.VFEArch_ArmoredWall.description: An impassable, heavy wall with multiple layers of armor. Nearly completely resistant to explosives and any breaching tools.
LanguageData.VFEArch_ArmoredWall.label: armored wall
LanguageData.VFEArch_BrickFence.description: A brick fence made tall brick pillars and a short fence in between them, used for building pens for certain kinds of animals. Some kinds of animals can get past fences. Predators will not hunt creatures on the other side of a fence, but may still wander inside.
LanguageData.VFEArch_BrickFence.label: brick fence
LanguageData.VFEArch_CellDoor.description: A slow moving door that separates two rooms. Cell doors cannot be attacked by prisoners.
LanguageData.VFEArch_CellDoor.label: cell door
LanguageData.VFEArch_CellWall.description: An impassable wall consisting of prison bars. Cannot be attacked by prisoners.
LanguageData.VFEArch_CellWall.label: cell wall
LanguageData.VFEArch_ChainlinkFence.description: A metal fence made of posts joined by chain link sheets used for building pens for certain kinds of animals. Some kinds of animals can get past fences. Predators will not hunt creatures on the other side of a fence, but may still wander inside.
LanguageData.VFEArch_ChainlinkFence.label: chain link fence
LanguageData.VFEArch_HedgeWall.description: An impassable wall made out of fake hedge. Very flammable.
LanguageData.VFEArch_HedgeWall.label: hedge wall
LanguageData.VFEArch_InvisibleFence.description: An invisible fence, for players wanting to create their own pens without fences, or to make it so that rivers or trees act as fence barriers. Does not have any path cost - thus not slowing down pawns wanting to traverse through it.
LanguageData.VFEArch_InvisibleFence.label: invisible fence
LanguageData.VFEArch_LogWall.description: A wall of horizontally placed wooden logs, for this nice, homely feeling. Capable of holding up a roof.
LanguageData.VFEArch_LogWall.label: log wall
LanguageData.VFEArch_LowFence.description: A fence made of posts joined by crossbars used for building pens for certain kinds of animals. Some kinds of animals can get past fences. Predators will not hunt creatures on the other side of a fence, but may still wander inside.
LanguageData.VFEArch_LowFence.label: low fence
LanguageData.VFEArch_PicketFence.description:
A fence made of posts joined by vertical wooden boards called pickets, used for building pens for certain kinds of animals. Some kinds of animals can get past fences. Predators will not hunt creatures on the other side of a fence, but may still wander inside.
Picket fences are considerably more beautiful than alternatives, and evoke a nice, homely feeling.
LanguageData.VFEArch_PicketFence.label: picket fence
LanguageData.VFEArch_SingleDoor.description: Divides rooms. Simple doors must be manually opened, which slows people down. The amount of slowdown does not depend on what the door is made of.
LanguageData.VFEArch_SingleDoor.label: single door
LanguageData.VFEArch_WoodLog_Birch.label: birch
LanguageData.VFEArch_WoodLog_Mahogany.label: mahogany
LanguageData.VFEArch_WoodLog_Walnut.label: walnut
LanguageData.VME_AncientBarrel.description: An ancient, broken barrel that looks like it once contained waste of some sort. The only thing it contains now is a smear of dried sludge.
LanguageData.VME_AncientBarrel.label: ancient barrel
LanguageData.VME_AncientBed.description: An old, rotted mattress on a rusty frame. Though uncomfortable, it can still be used, but it's too dilapidated to restore.
LanguageData.VME_AncientBed.label: ancient bed
LanguageData.VME_AncientCrate.description: An ancient, broken crate. Any valuables it once contained have degraded to uselessness.
LanguageData.VME_AncientCrate.label: ancient crate
LanguageData.VME_AncientDisplayBank.description: An ancient computer interface. Valuable parts are missing, and everything else is degraded to uselessness.
LanguageData.VME_AncientDisplayBank.label: ancient display bank
LanguageData.VME_AncientEquipmentBlocks.description: A collection of ancient equipment blocks of unknown function. Valuable parts are missing, and everything else is degraded to uselessness.
LanguageData.VME_AncientEquipmentBlocks.label: ancient equipment blocks
LanguageData.VME_AncientGenerator.description: An ancient electrical generator. Its usable parts were smashed or looted long ago, and the rest has rusted into uselessness.
LanguageData.VME_AncientGenerator.label: ancient generator
LanguageData.VME_AncientLamp.description: An ancient self-powered lamp. Its internal power supply is badly depleted, but it still emits a weak glow.
LanguageData.VME_AncientLamp.label: ancient lamp
LanguageData.VME_AncientLockerBank.description: An ancient, rusted bank of lockers. All of the lockers have been forced open with nothing valuable left behind.
LanguageData.VME_AncientLockerBank.label: ancient locker bank
LanguageData.VME_AncientMachine.description: A large ancient machine case filled with unknown machinery. Valuable parts are missing, and everything else is degraded to uselessness.
LanguageData.VME_AncientMachine.label: ancient machine
LanguageData.VME_AncientMilitaryCrate.description: An ancient, military-looking crate. It was broken long ago and any valuables it once contained were looted.
LanguageData.VME_AncientMilitaryCrate.label: ancient military crate
LanguageData.VME_AncientOperatingTable.description: A table once used for surgical operations. It has long since deteriorated into uselessness.
LanguageData.VME_AncientOperatingTable.label: ancient operating table
LanguageData.VME_AncientSpacerCrate.description: An ancient, broken crate. Any valuables it once contained were taken long ago.
LanguageData.VME_AncientSpacerCrate.label: ancient spacer crate
LanguageData.VME_AncientStorageCylinder.description: An ancient device that looks like it once stored some kind of fluid. Valuable parts are missing, and everything else is degraded to uselessness.
LanguageData.VME_AncientStorageCylinder.label: ancient storage cylinder
LanguageData.VME_AncientSystemRack.description: An ancient rack unit packed with unidentifiable devices. Valuable parts are missing, and everything else is degraded to uselessness.
LanguageData.VME_AncientSystemRack.label: ancient system rack
LanguageData.VME_AncientAirConditioner.description: An ancient air conditioning machine. Its internals were smashed long ago and what wasn't looted has rusted away in the time since.
LanguageData.VME_AncientAirConditioner.label: ancient air conditioner
LanguageData.VME_AncientAPC.description: The remains of an ancient armoured troop carrier which was destroyed by some kind of heavy weapon. All the useful components were looted or deteriorated long ago.
LanguageData.VME_AncientAPC.label: ancient ruined APC
LanguageData.VME_AncientATM.description: An ancient ATM. Smashed and rusted, it is completely useless now.\n\nHow it got here is hard to say.
LanguageData.VME_AncientATM.label: ancient ATM
LanguageData.VME_AncientCentipedeShell.description: A shell from a smashed centipede. It has been deteriorating for many years and all the useful components have disintegrated.
LanguageData.VME_AncientCentipedeShell.label: ancient centipede shell
LanguageData.VME_AncientContainer.description: A large container from some ancient freight carrying vehicle. It has long since been looted and deteriorted into uselessness.
LanguageData.VME_AncientContainer.label: ancient container
LanguageData.VME_AncientDropshipEngine.description: An ancient dropship engine. It probably came off a military troop carrier. Its delicate internals are rusted to uselessness, and the valuable parts were looted long ago.
LanguageData.VME_AncientDropshipEngine.label: ancient dropship engine
LanguageData.VME_AncientFence.description: An ancient fence made of reinforced concrete posts joined by concrete panels.
LanguageData.VME_AncientFence.label: ancient fence
LanguageData.VME_AncientGiantWheel.description: A giant wheel which came off some large vehicle many ages ago. The rubber has disintegrated away and the metal is twisted and rusted.
LanguageData.VME_AncientGiantWheel.label: ancient giant wheel
LanguageData.VME_AncientHydrant.description: An ancient hydrant. Heavily rusted, it is completely useless now.\n\nHow it got here is hard to say.
LanguageData.VME_AncientHydrant.label: ancient hydrant
LanguageData.VME_AncientJetEngine.description: An ancient jet engine, probably detached from a large airplane. Its delicate internals are rusted to uselessness, and the valuable parts were looted long ago.
LanguageData.VME_AncientJetEngine.label: ancient jet engine
LanguageData.VME_AncientKitchenSink.description: An ancient kitchen sink made of some non-metallic fiber-sheet material. It is badly deteriorated and there's no way to salvage anything from it.\n\nHow it got here must be a long story.
LanguageData.VME_AncientKitchenSink.label: ancient kitchen sink
LanguageData.VME_AncientLargeCrate.description: A large crate which was abandoned ages ago. It's too rusted to store anything.
LanguageData.VME_AncientLargeCrate.label: ancient large crate
LanguageData.VME_AncientLargeRustedEngineBlock.descri: An large, ancient engine block from some giant vehicle or power source. It's rusted through, and all the useful components are gone. No repair could salvage this.
LanguageData.VME_AncientLargeRustedEngineBlock.label: ancient macro-engine block
LanguageData.VME_AncientLongCrate.description: A long, empty crate which was abandoned ages ago. It's too rusted to store anything.
LanguageData.VME_AncientLongCrate.label: ancient long crate
LanguageData.VME_AncientMechanoidShell.description: A shell from a smashed mechanoid. It has been deteriorating for many years and the useful components have disintegrated.
LanguageData.VME_AncientMechanoidShell.label: ancient mechanoid shell
LanguageData.VME_AncientMechDropBeacon.description: The broken shell of a mech drop beacon. Its useful components are missing and the shell has deteriorated after many years.
LanguageData.VME_AncientMechDropBeacon.label: ancient mech drop beacon
LanguageData.VME_AncientMegaCannonBarrel.description: A barrel from a long-range cannon for artillery or anti-ship use. When it was new, it must have been frighteningly powerful. Now, it's far beyond repair and every useful component has been looted.
LanguageData.VME_AncientMegaCannonBarrel.label: ancient mega-cannon barrel
LanguageData.VME_AncientMegaCannonTripod.description: A mounting platform that once held some sort of cannon. It has been broken for many years and every useful component was looted long ago.
LanguageData.VME_AncientMegaCannonTripod.label: ancient mega-cannon platform
LanguageData.VME_AncientMicrowave.description: An ancient device for heating food. Smashed and rusted, it is completely useless now.\n\nHow it got here is hard to say.
LanguageData.VME_AncientMicrowave.label: ancient microwave
LanguageData.VME_AncientMiniWarwalkerRemains.descript: The shattered remains of an ancient sub-scale warsprinter. Whatever parts weren't ruined when it was destroyed were looted long ago.
LanguageData.VME_AncientMiniWarwalkerRemains.label: ancient warsprinter remains
LanguageData.VME_AncientOven.description: An ancient oven. Smashed and rusted, it is completely useless now.\n\nHow it got here is hard to say.
LanguageData.VME_AncientOven.label: ancient oven
LanguageData.VME_AncientPipelineSection.description: A section from an ancient pipeline. It once carried some valuable fluid over long distances. Now it's been looted and deteriorated to uselessness.
LanguageData.VME_AncientPipelineSection.label: ancient pipeline section
LanguageData.VME_AncientPipes.description: A bunch of rusted old pipes. It is hard to say how these ended up here.
LanguageData.VME_AncientPipes.label: ancient pipes
LanguageData.VME_AncientPodCar.description: An ancient pod car once capable of automated flight. Its valuable parts were looted long ago, and the rest is badly deteriorated.
LanguageData.VME_AncientPodCar.label: ancient pod car
LanguageData.VME_AncientPostbox.description: An ancient postbox, smashed and deteriorated to uselessness. It seems to have been moved here long ago for some unknown reason.
LanguageData.VME_AncientPostbox.label: ancient postbox
LanguageData.VME_AncientRazorWire.description: A piece of ancient razor wire. It has been deteriorating for many years and no longer poses any danger.
LanguageData.VME_AncientRazorWire.label: ancient razor wire
LanguageData.VME_AncientRefrigerator.description: An ancient refrigerator. It was stripped of interesting parts long ago.
LanguageData.VME_AncientRefrigerator.label: ancient refrigerator
LanguageData.VME_AncientRustedCar.description: An ancient, broken car. Everything that isn't rusted away was looted long ago.
LanguageData.VME_AncientRustedCar.label: ancient car
LanguageData.VME_AncientRustedCarFrame.description: An ancient, rusted car frame. The body shell has long since rusted away to nothing.
LanguageData.VME_AncientRustedCarFrame.label: ancient car frame
LanguageData.VME_AncientRustedDropship.description: The remains of an ancient dropship. The valuable parts were looted long ago and the rest has deteriorated to uselessness.
LanguageData.VME_AncientRustedDropship.label: ancient dropship
LanguageData.VME_AncientRustedEngineBlock.description: An ancient engine block that is rusted through. No repair could salvage this.
LanguageData.VME_AncientRustedEngineBlock.label: ancient engine block
LanguageData.VME_AncientRustedJeep.description: An ancient, broken troop carrier. Everything that isn't rusted away was looted long ago.
LanguageData.VME_AncientRustedJeep.label: ancient troop carrier
LanguageData.VME_AncientRustedTruck.description: An ancient, broken truck. Everything useful either rusted away or was looted long ago.
LanguageData.VME_AncientRustedTruck.label: ancient truck
LanguageData.VME_AncientSecurityTurret.description: An ancient, broken security turret. The valuable parts have all been looted or smashed.
LanguageData.VME_AncientSecurityTurret.label: ancient security turret
LanguageData.VME_AncientShipBeacon.description: An ancient, broken ship navigation beacon. Every useful component was looted long ago.
LanguageData.VME_AncientShipBeacon.label: ancient nav beacon
LanguageData.VME_AncientShoppingCart.description: An ancient, rusted shopping cart. How exactly it got here is a mystery, but the story is long and probably quite sad.
LanguageData.VME_AncientShoppingCart.label: ancient shopping cart
LanguageData.VME_AncientSmallCrate.description: A small, empty crate which was abandoned ages ago. It's too rusted to store anything.
LanguageData.VME_AncientSmallCrate.label: ancient small crate
LanguageData.VME_AncientSpaceJunk.description: Ancient chunks of space junk that fell from orbit. Perhaps a relic of an ancient space battle.
LanguageData.VME_AncientSpaceJunk.label: ancient space junk
LanguageData.VME_AncientStove.description: An ancient stove. Smashed and rusted, it is completely useless now.\n\nHow it got here is hard to say.
LanguageData.VME_AncientStove.label: ancient stove
LanguageData.VME_AncientTank.description: The remains of an ancient tank which was destroyed by some kind of heavy weapon. All the useful components were looted or deteriorated long ago.
LanguageData.VME_AncientTank.label: ancient ruined tank
LanguageData.VME_AncientTankTrap.description: An defensive fortification for stopping vehicles. It's been deteriorating for many years.
LanguageData.VME_AncientTankTrap.label: ancient tank trap
LanguageData.VME_AncientToilet.description: An ancient toilet. Smashed long ago, it is completely useless now.\n\nHow it got here is hard to say.
LanguageData.VME_AncientToilet.label: ancient toilet
LanguageData.VME_AncientVendingMachine.description: An ancient vending machine. It was smashed ages ago and generations of looters have taken every conceivably useful part. How it ended up here is a mystery.
LanguageData.VME_AncientVendingMachine.label: ancient vending machine
LanguageData.VME_AncientWarspiderRemains.description: The ruined remains of an ancient mid-scale warspider. It has been picked over many times, and there is nothing valuable left to loot.
LanguageData.VME_AncientWarspiderRemains.label: ancient warspider remains
LanguageData.VME_AncientWarwalkerClaw.description: The assault claw of an ancient mid-scale warwalker. This weapon specialized in tearing through heavy plasteel armor at close range. Its valuable components were looted long ago.
LanguageData.VME_AncientWarwalkerClaw.label: ancient warwalker claw
LanguageData.VME_AncientWarwalkerFoot.description: A massive foot from an ancient warwalker. It seems to have been torn free from its owner by some kind of heavy weapon. All the useful components were looted or deteriorated long ago.
LanguageData.VME_AncientWarwalkerFoot.label: ancient warwalker foot
LanguageData.VME_AncientWarwalkerLeg.description: The shattered leg of a mid-scale warwalker that fell here long ago. It has been picked clean by looters, and no valuable components remain.
LanguageData.VME_AncientWarwalkerLeg.label: ancient warwalker leg
LanguageData.VME_AncientWarwalkerShell.description: Most of the armored shell of a warwalker. It appears to have been torn off by some colossal weapon system. All the valuable parts were looted, and the rest is badly deteriorated.
LanguageData.VME_AncientWarwalkerShell.label: ancient warwalker shell
LanguageData.VME_AncientWarwalkerTorso.description: The torso of a mid-scale warwalker which fell here in ancient days. The valuable parts were looted long ago.
LanguageData.VME_AncientWarwalkerTorso.label: ancient warwalker torso
LanguageData.VME_AncientWashingMachine.description: An ancient device used for washing textiles. Smashed and rusted, it is completely useless now.\n\nHow it got here is hard to say.
LanguageData.VME_AncientWashingMachine.label: ancient washing machine
LanguageData.VME_AncientWheel.description: An ancient wheel from some kind of vehicle. The rubber has deteriorated away and the metal is twisted and rusted.
LanguageData.VME_AncientWheel.label: ancient wheel
LanguageData.VME_Bonfire.description: An incredibly large and bright-burning bonfire made out of a carefully organized pile of logs on a stone base. Used heavily during flame-based rituals and can only be placed outdoors.
LanguageData.VME_Bonfire.label: bonfire
LanguageData.VME_BonfireAfterRitual.description: An incredibly large and bright-burning bonfire made out of a carefully organized pile of logs on a stone base. Used heavily during flame-based rituals and can only be placed outdoors. Will last for at least two quadrums and provide beauty nearby, as well as count for the Fire: Desired precept.
LanguageData.VME_BonfireAfterRitual.label: bonfire
LanguageData.VME_InsectNest.description: A large, swollen hive that seems to be seeping high amounts of bubbling insectoid jelly but not incubating any insectoids themselves.\n\nThe nest will need maintenance by a colonist with the insectoid herder role. If maintenance reaches 0%, the nest will be destroyed.
LanguageData.VME_InsectNest.label: insect nest
LanguageData.VME_MechanoidEffigy.description: A structure assembled from scrapped mechanoid parts. Emits an IFF programming signal capable of confusing ancient mechanoid receptors. Should two exist per colonist enough signal noise will soft reset ancient mechanoid programming, causing an immediate retreat.
LanguageData.VME_MechanoidEffigy.label: mechanoid effigy
LanguageData.VME_Obelisk.description: An ominous-looking obelisk of pure chiseled stone. It seems to radiate an aura of hopelessness and despair.\n\nThe Corruption need of any colonist in an obelisk's radius of effect won't decrease.
LanguageData.VME_Obelisk.label: ominous obelisk
LanguageData.VME_WickerMan.description: A large humanoid figure made out of dry brush, thatch, and wood scraps. Prisoners have to be physically restrained to the figurine then burned alive as part of a trial or ritual.
LanguageData.VME_WickerMan.label: wicker man
LanguageData.VME_LargeFishingTrap.description: A primitive, though quite ingenious barricade made of wooden stick and netting meant to entrap incoming fish and leave them with no way out. Fish traps are eventually destroyed by the currents and struggling fish, and will need replacing.\n\nThis is a large trap that will only catch large sized fish.
LanguageData.VME_LargeFishingTrap.label: large fishing trap
LanguageData.VME_MediumFishingTrap.description: A primitive, though quite ingenious barricade made of wooden stick and netting meant to entrap incoming fish and leave them with no way out. Fish traps are eventually destroyed by the currents and struggling fish, and will need replacing.\n\nThis is a medium trap that will only catch medium sized fish.
LanguageData.VME_MediumFishingTrap.label: medium fishing trap
LanguageData.VME_SmallFishingTrap.description: A primitive, though quite ingenious barricade made of wooden stick and netting meant to entrap incoming fish and leave them with no way out. Fish traps are eventually destroyed by the currents and struggling fish, and will need replacing.\n\nThis is a small trap that will only catch small sized fish.
LanguageData.VME_SmallFishingTrap.label: small fishing trap
LanguageData.VME_EldritchSlab_Broad.description: A stone bas relief of eldritch imagery for decorating a floor.
LanguageData.VME_EldritchSlab_Broad.label: eldritch slab (broad)
LanguageData.VME_EldritchSlab_Medium.description: A stone bas relief of eldritch imagery for decorating a floor.
LanguageData.VME_EldritchSlab_Medium.label: eldritch slab (medium)
LanguageData.VME_EsotericismSlab_Broad.description: A stone bas relief of esoteric imagery for decorating a floor.
LanguageData.VME_EsotericismSlab_Broad.label: esotericism slab (broad)
LanguageData.VME_EsotericismSlab_Medium.description: A stone bas relief of esoteric imagery for decorating a floor.
LanguageData.VME_EsotericismSlab_Medium.label: esotericism slab (medium)
LanguageData.VME_TaoistSlab_Broad.description: A stone bas relief of shintao imagery for decorating a floor.
LanguageData.VME_TaoistSlab_Broad.label: shintao slab (broad)
LanguageData.VME_TaoistSlab_Medium.description: A stone bas relief of shintao imagery for decorating a floor.
LanguageData.VME_TaoistSlab_Medium.label: shintao slab (medium)
LanguageData.VME_FleshcraftedArm.description: A biological human arm, fleshcrafted from a corpse. This can be safely installed in colonists following a Fleshcrafting ideoligion, but will cause infections due to organ rejection in others.
LanguageData.VME_FleshcraftedArm.label: fleshcrafted arm
LanguageData.VME_FleshcraftedEar.description: A biological human ear, fleshcrafted from a corpse. This can be safely installed in colonists following a Fleshcrafting ideoligion, but will cause infections due to organ rejection in others.
LanguageData.VME_FleshcraftedEar.label: fleshcrafted ear
LanguageData.VME_FleshcraftedEye.description: A biological human eye, fleshcrafted from a corpse. This can be safely installed in colonists following a Fleshcrafting ideoligion, but will cause infections due to organ rejection in others.
LanguageData.VME_FleshcraftedEye.label: fleshcrafted eye
LanguageData.VME_FleshcraftedFinger.description: A biological human finger, fleshcrafted from a corpse. This can be safely installed in colonists following a Fleshcrafting ideoligion, but will cause infections due to organ rejection in others.
LanguageData.VME_FleshcraftedFinger.label: fleshcrafted finger
LanguageData.VME_FleshcraftedFoot.description: A biological human foot, fleshcrafted from a corpse. This can be safely installed in colonists following a Fleshcrafting ideoligion, but will cause infections due to organ rejection in others.
LanguageData.VME_FleshcraftedFoot.label: fleshcrafted foot
LanguageData.VME_FleshcraftedHand.description: A biological human hand, fleshcrafted from a corpse. This can be safely installed in colonists following a Fleshcrafting ideoligion, but will cause infections due to organ rejection in others.
LanguageData.VME_FleshcraftedHand.label: fleshcrafted hand
LanguageData.VME_FleshcraftedHeart.description: A biological human heart, fleshcrafted from a corpse. This can be safely installed in colonists following a Fleshcrafting ideoligion, but will cause infections due to organ rejection in others.
LanguageData.VME_FleshcraftedHeart.label: fleshcrafted heart
LanguageData.VME_FleshcraftedJaw.description: A biological human jaw, fleshcrafted from a corpse. This can be safely installed in colonists following a Fleshcrafting ideoligion, but will cause infections due to organ rejection in others.
LanguageData.VME_FleshcraftedJaw.label: fleshcrafted jaw
LanguageData.VME_FleshcraftedKidney.description: A biological human kidney, fleshcrafted from a corpse. This can be safely installed in colonists following a Fleshcrafting ideoligion, but will cause infections due to organ rejection in others.
LanguageData.VME_FleshcraftedKidney.label: fleshcrafted kidney
LanguageData.VME_FleshcraftedLeg.description: A biological human leg, fleshcrafted from a corpse. This can be safely installed in colonists following a Fleshcrafting ideoligion, but will cause infections due to organ rejection in others.
LanguageData.VME_FleshcraftedLeg.label: fleshcrafted leg
LanguageData.VME_FleshcraftedLiver.description: A biological human liver, fleshcrafted from a corpse. This can be safely installed in colonists following a Fleshcrafting ideoligion, but will cause infections due to organ rejection in others.
LanguageData.VME_FleshcraftedLiver.label: fleshcrafted liver
LanguageData.VME_FleshcraftedLung.description: A biological human lung, fleshcrafted from a corpse. This can be safely installed in colonists following a Fleshcrafting ideoligion, but will cause infections due to organ rejection in others.
LanguageData.VME_FleshcraftedLung.label: fleshcrafted lung
LanguageData.VME_FleshcraftedNose.description: A biological human nose, fleshcrafted from a corpse. This can be safely installed in colonists following a Fleshcrafting ideoligion, but will cause infections due to organ rejection in others.
LanguageData.VME_FleshcraftedNose.label: fleshcrafted nose
LanguageData.VME_FleshcraftedSpine.description: A biological human spine, fleshcrafted from a corpse. This can be safely installed in colonists following a Fleshcrafting ideoligion, but will cause infections due to organ rejection in others.
LanguageData.VME_FleshcraftedSpine.label: fleshcrafted spine
LanguageData.VME_FleshcraftedStomach.description: A biological human stomach, fleshcrafted from a corpse. This can be safely installed in colonists following a Fleshcrafting ideoligion, but will cause infections due to organ rejection in others.
LanguageData.VME_FleshcraftedStomach.label: fleshcrafted stomach
LanguageData.VME_FleshcraftedToe.description: A biological human toe, fleshcrafted from a corpse. This can be safely installed in colonists following a Fleshcrafting ideoligion, but will cause infections due to organ rejection in others.
LanguageData.VME_FleshcraftedToe.label: fleshcrafted toe
LanguageData.VME_FleshcraftedTongue.description: A biological human tongue, fleshcrafted from a corpse. This can be safely installed in colonists following a Fleshcrafting ideoligion, but will cause infections due to organ rejection in others.
LanguageData.VME_FleshcraftedTongue.label: fleshcrafted tongue
LanguageData.VME_TackleBox.description: A small metal box filled with different hooks, bobbers and baits meant to entice fish big and small to latch on before being reeled in via line and rod.
LanguageData.VME_TackleBox.label: tackle box
LanguageData.VCEF_ArapaimaItem.description: Raw arapaima. On old Earth, it was among the world's largest freshwater fish, reaching as much as 3 m in length. It was an important food fish.\n\nThis is an adept catch and provides double nutrition than other fish in their size category.
LanguageData.VCEF_ArapaimaItem.label: arapaima (adept catch)
LanguageData.VCEF_ButterFishItem.description: Raw Atlantic butterfish. The Atlantic butterfish is distinguished by its very thin, deep body, resembling a flounder on edge. It earned its buttery name from its high oil content.\n\nThis is an adept catch and provides double nutrition than other fish in their size category.
LanguageData.VCEF_ButterFishItem.label: butterfish (adept catch)
LanguageData.VCEF_CrayfishItem.description: Raw crayfish. Crayfish are freshwater crustaceans resembling small lobsters (to which they are related). Some species are found in brooks and streams, where fresh water is running, while others thrive in swamps, ditches, and paddy fields.\n\nThis is an adept catch and provides double nutrition than other fish in their size category.
LanguageData.VCEF_CrayfishItem.label: crayfish (adept catch)
LanguageData.VCEF_FlyingFishItem.description: Raw flying fish. While they cannot fly in the same way a bird does, flying fish can make powerful, self-propelled leaps out of the water where their long wing-like fins enable gliding for considerable distances above the water's surface.\n\nThis is an adept catch and provides double nutrition than other fish in their size category.
LanguageData.VCEF_FlyingFishItem.label: flying fish (adept catch)
LanguageData.VCEF_FreshwaterStingrayItem.description: Raw freshwater stingray. Freshwater stingrays are one of the only rays inhabiting freshwater rather than saltwater. Though docile animals, they were responsiblein old Earth for more injuries to humans each year than any other Amazonian species.\n\nThis is an adept catch and provides double nutrition than other fish in their size category.
LanguageData.VCEF_FreshwaterStingrayItem.label: freshwater stingray (adept catch)
LanguageData.VCEF_ShortfinMakoSharkItem.description: Raw shortfin mako shark. A large, predatory shark that lives in the open ocean and is often considered the fastest shark. It is known for its incredible leaping ability and can be observed jumping to extreme heights (out of the water) when hunting.\n\nThis is an adept catch and provides double nutrition than other fish in their size category.
LanguageData.VCEF_ShortfinMakoSharkItem.label: shortfin mako shark (adept catch)
LanguageData.VCE_ChocolateBush.description: A low-lying bush that produces chocolate, genetically engineered to sweeten its own product, and be plantable in almost any vessel.
LanguageData.VCE_ChocolateBush.label: cocoa bush
LanguageData.VDE_AcidBolt.label: acid spit
LanguageData.VDE_BarkskinAwakeningCocoon.description: An awakened dryad will emerge from this pod in one day.
LanguageData.VDE_BarkskinAwakeningCocoon.label: barkskin awakening pod
LanguageData.VDE_BerrymakerAwakeningCocoon.descriptio: An awakened dryad will emerge from this pod in one day.
LanguageData.VDE_BerrymakerAwakeningCocoon.label: berrymaker awakening pod
LanguageData.VDE_CarrierAwakeningCocoon.description: An awakened dryad will emerge from this pod in one day.
LanguageData.VDE_CarrierAwakeningCocoon.label: carrier awakening pod
LanguageData.VDE_ClawerAwakeningCocoon.description: An awakened dryad will emerge from this pod in one day.
LanguageData.VDE_ClawerAwakeningCocoon.label: clawer awakening pod
LanguageData.VDE_DryadNest.description: A small nest of logs and twigs created by the awakened woodmaker dryad. Animals can sleep in it, but it can also be deconstructed for precious building materials.
LanguageData.VDE_DryadNest.label: dryad nest
LanguageData.VDE_GaubricmakerAwakeningCocoon.descript: An awakened dryad will emerge from this pod in one day.
LanguageData.VDE_GaubricmakerAwakeningCocoon.label: gaubricmaker awakening pod
LanguageData.VDE_MedicinemakerAwakeningCocoon.descrip: An awakened dryad will emerge from this pod in one day.
LanguageData.VDE_MedicinemakerAwakeningCocoon.label: medicinemaker awakening pod
LanguageData.VDE_NectarmakerAwakeningCocoon.descripti: An awakened dryad will emerge from this pod in one day.
LanguageData.VDE_NectarmakerAwakeningCocoon.label: nectarmaker awakening pod
LanguageData.VDE_SpitterAwakeningCocoon.description: An awakened dryad will emerge from this pod in one day.
LanguageData.VDE_SpitterAwakeningCocoon.label: spitter awakening pod
LanguageData.VDE_StonediggerAwakeningCocoon.descripti: An awakened dryad will emerge from this pod in one day.
LanguageData.VDE_StonediggerAwakeningCocoon.label: stonedigger awakening pod
LanguageData.VDE_WoodmakerAwakeningCocoon.description: An awakened dryad will emerge from this pod in one day.
LanguageData.VDE_WoodmakerAwakeningCocoon.label: woodmaker awakening pod
LanguageData.VDE_Gaubric.description: A fabric produced by dryads that closely resembles thick leather in many of its properties, whilst also staying soft and fluffy to touch.
LanguageData.VDE_Gaubric.label: gaubric
LanguageData.VDE_AmbrosiaPalm.description: A legendary palm tree which yields the ambrosia fruit. Ambrosia fruit is delicious and produces a drug-like mood boost. It’s a very rare sight to see an Ambrosia tree that can be farmed for numerous years, as most common sights of Ambrosia are of the bush variety.\n\nIts rarity, beauty, and desirability are why ambrosia was named after the food of the ancient Greek gods.
LanguageData.VDE_AmbrosiaPalm.label: ambrosia palm
LanguageData.VDE_AwakenedDryad_Barkskin.description: A dryad caste specialized in absorbing hits during combat. Its awakening has created multiple layers of hardened bark and deadened all pain receptors. Can act as a mobile form of cover.\n\nAwakened dryads have both advantages and disadvantages compared to their bound relatives. While more proficient, specialized and unshackled, they can neither morph nor receive nourishment from a queen, resulting in a very limited lifespan.
LanguageData.VDE_AwakenedDryad_Barkskin.label: awakened barkskin dryad
LanguageData.VDE_AwakenedDryad_Barkskin.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.VDE_AwakenedDryad_Barkskin.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.VDE_AwakenedDryad_Barkskin.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.VDE_AwakenedDryad_Berrymaker.description: A dryad caste specialized in food production. Its awakening has accelerated the berry production and instills an instinct to bury parts of its peeling hide that are entirely similar to regular berry bushes.\n\nAwakened dryads have both advantages and disadvantages compared to their bound relatives. While more proficient, specialized and unshackled, they can neither morph nor receive nourishment from a queen, resulting in a very limited lifespan.
LanguageData.VDE_AwakenedDryad_Berrymaker.label: awakened berrymaker dryad
LanguageData.VDE_AwakenedDryad_Carrier.description: A dryad caste specialized in carrying items. Its awakening has improved their size, carrying capacity and added functionality as a pack animal for long-distance hauling.\n\nAwakened dryads have both advantages and disadvantages compared to their bound relatives. While more proficient, specialized and unshackled, they can neither morph nor receive nourishment from a queen, resulting in a very limited lifespan.
LanguageData.VDE_AwakenedDryad_Carrier.label: awakened carrier dryad
LanguageData.VDE_AwakenedDryad_Carrier.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.VDE_AwakenedDryad_Carrier.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.VDE_AwakenedDryad_Carrier.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.VDE_AwakenedDryad_Clawer.description: A dryad caste with huge claws that is specialized in aggression and damage-dealing attacks. Its awakening has improved their combat prowess and strengthened the wings, permitting them to ignore terrain movement penalties.\n\nAwakened dryads have both advantages and disadvantages compared to their bound relatives. While more proficient, specialized and unshackled, they can neither morph nor receive nourishment from a queen, resulting in a very limited lifespan.
LanguageData.VDE_AwakenedDryad_Clawer.label: awakened clawer dryad
LanguageData.VDE_AwakenedDryad_Clawer.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.VDE_AwakenedDryad_Clawer.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.VDE_AwakenedDryad_Clawer.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.VDE_AwakenedDryad_Gaubricmaker.descripti: A caste of dryad which specializes in producing a unique shaggy, leather-like material. Its awakening has accelerated the fabric production and lengthens the shagginess, turning it an adorable, living wonder-mop that cleans up messes.\n\nAwakened dryads have both advantages and disadvantages compared to their bound relatives. While more proficient, specialized and unshackled, they can neither morph nor receive nourishment from a queen, resulting in a very limited lifespan.
LanguageData.VDE_AwakenedDryad_Gaubricmaker.label: awakened gaubricmaker dryad
LanguageData.VDE_AwakenedDryad_Medicinemaker.descript: A dryad caste specialized in medicine production. Its awakening has accelerated the herbal production and gives them a large nodule that slowly accumulated neutroamine, which can be milked when full.\n\nAwakened dryads have both advantages and disadvantages compared to their bound relatives. While more proficient, specialized and unshackled, they can neither morph nor receive nourishment from a queen, resulting in a very limited lifespan.
LanguageData.VDE_AwakenedDryad_Medicinemaker.label: awakened medicinemaker dryad
LanguageData.VDE_AwakenedDryad_Nectarmaker.descriptio: A curious dryad caste specialized in ambrosia production. Its awakening has accelerated the ambrosia production and allows it to permanently root itself, transforming into a hardy Ambrosia Palm tree.\n\nAwakened dryads have both advantages and disadvantages compared to their bound relatives. While more proficient, specialized and unshackled, they can neither morph nor receive nourishment from a queen, resulting in a very limited lifespan.
LanguageData.VDE_AwakenedDryad_Nectarmaker.label: awakened nectarmaker dryad
LanguageData.VDE_AwakenedDryad_Spitter.description: A dryad caste specialized in territorialism and can launch ranged acid projectiles. Its awakening has improved their range and adds large acid reservoirs that explode when the dryad dies.\n\nAwakened dryads have both advantages and disadvantages compared to their bound relatives. While more proficient, specialized and unshackled, they can neither morph nor receive nourishment from a queen, resulting in a very limited lifespan.
LanguageData.VDE_AwakenedDryad_Spitter.label: awakened spitter dryad
LanguageData.VDE_AwakenedDryad_Spitter.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.VDE_AwakenedDryad_Spitter.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.VDE_AwakenedDryad_Spitter.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.VDE_AwakenedDryad_Spitter.verbs.0.label: acidic spit
LanguageData.VDE_AwakenedDryad_Stonedigger.descriptio: An extremely easy-going caste of dryad which specializes in finding stone chunks. Its awakening has instilled a sense of awe and strengthened pincers, drawing envy from stone ruins and carving excavated stone chunks into blocks to build a better shell.\n\nAwakened dryads have both advantages and disadvantages compared to their bound relatives. While more proficient, specialized and unshackled, they can neither morph nor receive nourishment from a queen, resulting in a very limited lifespan.
LanguageData.VDE_AwakenedDryad_Stonedigger.label: awakened stonedigger dryad
LanguageData.VDE_AwakenedDryad_Woodmaker.description: A slow, bumbling caste of dryad which specializes in wood production. Its awakening has accelerated the wood-like material production and instilled a motherly instinct to create nests made of numerous twigs.\n\nAwakened dryads have both advantages and disadvantages compared to their bound relatives. While more proficient, specialized and unshackled, they can neither morph nor receive nourishment from a queen, resulting in a very limited lifespan.
LanguageData.VDE_AwakenedDryad_Woodmaker.label: awakened woodmaker dryad
LanguageData.VDE_Dryad_Gaubricmaker.description: A caste of dryad which specializes in producing a unique shaggy, leather-like material. Together with its Gauranlen tree, this dryad can be sheared for a soft, beautiful, insulative material on an ongoing basis. However, it is slow and ineffective at work or combat.\n\nIn general, dryads are mammal-like creatures that have a symbiotic relationship with the Gauranlen tree. They reproduce together with their tree, which contains a hidden dryad queen. Dryads can morph into various specialized castes.
LanguageData.VDE_Dryad_Gaubricmaker.label: gaubricmaker dryad
LanguageData.VDE_Dryad_Nectarmaker.description: A curious dryad caste specialized in ambrosia production. Together with its Gauranlen tree, this dryad can generate invigorating ambrosia on an ongoing basis. However, it is completely defenseless and requires careful attention against threats.\n\nIn general, dryads are mammal-like creatures that have a symbiotic relationship with the Gauranlen tree. They reproduce together with their tree, which contains a hidden dryad queen. Dryads can morph into various specialized castes.
LanguageData.VDE_Dryad_Nectarmaker.label: nectarmaker dryad
LanguageData.VDE_Dryad_Spitter.description: A dryad caste specialized in territorialism and can launch ranged acid projectiles. Though it can dish out tremendous damage, it is vulnerable in melee due to its oversized mouth.\n\nIn general, dryads are mammal-like creatures that have a symbiotic relationship with the Gauranlen tree. They reproduce together with their tree, which contains a hidden dryad queen. Dryads can morph into various specialized castes.
LanguageData.VDE_Dryad_Spitter.label: spitter dryad
LanguageData.VDE_Dryad_Spitter.verbs.0.label: acidic spit
LanguageData.VDE_Dryad_Stonedigger.description: An extremely easy-going caste of dryad which specializes in finding stone chunks. Absorbs minerals from the ground that help harden its carapace while leaving excessively large pieces aside.\n\nIn general, dryads are mammal-like creatures that have a symbiotic relationship with the Gauranlen tree. They reproduce together with their tree, which contains a hidden dryad queen. Dryads can morph into various specialized castes.
LanguageData.VDE_Dryad_Stonedigger.label: stonedigger dryad
LanguageData.VCE_VegMilkExtractor.description: A small work station containing a presser and an industrial decanter centrifuge to separate vegetable extract from mash. The high G-forces generated by the decanter centrifuge allow you to extract vegetable milk from rice, almonds, coconuts, oats and soybeans.
LanguageData.VCE_VegMilkExtractor.label: vegetable milk extractor
LanguageData.VCE_RawCanola.description: A bright-yellow flowering variety of the rapeseed, cultivated mainly for its oil-rich seed.
LanguageData.VCE_RawCanola.label: canola flowers
LanguageData.VCE_Mangoes.description: An oval fruit, vividly coloured with shades of red and yellow. The single large seed is flattened, and the flesh that surrounds it is yellow to orange in colour, juicy, and of distinctive sweet-spicy flavour.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Mangoes cannot be frozen or they get destroyed. The game won't be smart enough to detect this, so disable them from freezers.
LanguageData.VCE_Mangoes.label: mangoes
LanguageData.VCE_RawAlmonds.description: An oval shaped drupe often eaten on its own, raw or toasted, though it can also be used as a component of various dishes. A wide range of classic sweets feature almonds as a central ingredient.
LanguageData.VCE_RawAlmonds.label: almonds
LanguageData.VCE_RawAvocado.description: A green or nearly black coloured fruit with a large seed or pit in the center. Avocados have a very mild, creamy texture. Hipsters love them.
LanguageData.VCE_RawAvocado.label: avocados
LanguageData.VCE_RawCoconut.description: The edible fruit of the coconut palm. Coconut flesh is high in fat and can be dried or eaten fresh or processed into coconut milk or coconut oil. The liquid of the nut, known as coconut water, is used in beverages.
LanguageData.VCE_RawCoconut.label: coconuts
LanguageData.VCE_RawMelon.description: A sweet, edible, and fleshy fruit of considerable size. Melons are usually cultivated in warm regions.
LanguageData.VCE_RawMelon.label: melons
LanguageData.VCE_RawPineapple.description: A sweet tropical fruit with a tough leathery skin and spiky leaves on top. The word pineapple was first used to mean "pine cone," and the fruit was given this name because of its resemblance to a large pine cone.
LanguageData.VCE_RawPineapple.label: pineapples
LanguageData.VCE_RawBlueberries.description: A sweet, edible fruit hailed as a "superfood", blueberries are commonly eaten fresh as a dessert fruit and can be baked in a variety of pastries.
LanguageData.VCE_RawBlueberries.label: blueberries
LanguageData.VCE_RawBeans.description: Young, unripe fruits of a cultivar of the common bean. They can be steamed, boiled, stir-fried, or baked. They are commonly cooked in other dishes such as soups, stews, and casseroles. Green beans can also be pickled, much like cucumbers are.
LanguageData.VCE_RawBeans.label: green beans
LanguageData.VCE_RawBellPeppers.description: Also known as sweet peppers, bell peppers are technically nightshades and part of the capsicum family, but they are the only chili pepper that carries a recessive gene that stops any capsaicin from being made in the fruit, thus making them not spicy.
LanguageData.VCE_RawBellPeppers.label: red bell peppers
LanguageData.VCE_RawBrusselsSprouts.description: A form of cabbage grown for its edible buds called "sprouts". Brussels sprouts usually are eaten cooked, and the small young sprouts have a more delicate flavour than older ones.
LanguageData.VCE_RawBrusselsSprouts.label: brussels sprouts
LanguageData.VCE_RawBuckwheat.description: A pseudocereal cultivated for its grain-like seeds. Its white flowers are produced in terminal clusters and after pòllination turn into triangular-shaped seeds that are enclosed by a tough dark brown or gray rind.
LanguageData.VCE_RawBuckwheat.label: buckwheat
LanguageData.VCE_RawButternutSquash.description: A vegetable with a sweet, nutty taste similar to that of a pumpkin. It has tan-yellow skin and orange fleshy pulp with a compartment of seeds in the blossom end.
LanguageData.VCE_RawButternutSquash.label: butternut squashes
LanguageData.VCE_RawCarrot.description: A root vegetable, typically orange in color, though purple, black, red, white, and yellow cultivars exist. A versatile food that can be chopped and boiled, fried or steamed, cooked in soups and stews, as well as used for baby and pet foods.
LanguageData.VCE_RawCarrot.label: carrots
LanguageData.VCE_RawCassava.description: A tuberous edible plant also called manioc, mandioca, or yuca. They have a mild nutty taste and are commonly eaten as root vegetables in stews or as side dishes. Cassava roots are a versatile vegetable that can be used to produce flour, breads, tapioca, a laundry starch, and an alcoholic beverage.
LanguageData.VCE_RawCassava.label: cassavas
LanguageData.VCE_RawCelery.description: A vegetable consisting of a long fibrous stalk tapering into leaves. The leaves are strongly flavored and are used less often, either as a flavoring in soups and stews or as a dried herb. Celery is a staple in many soups.
LanguageData.VCE_RawCelery.label: celery
LanguageData.VCE_RawChickpeas.description: One of the earliest cultivated legumes. Chickpeas are high in fibre and protein and are a good source of iron, phosphorus, and folic acid.
LanguageData.VCE_RawChickpeas.label: chickpeas
LanguageData.VCE_RawCucumber.description: A long thin vegetable with hard green skin and wet transparent flesh. Can be eaten raw.
LanguageData.VCE_RawCucumber.label: cucumbers
LanguageData.VCE_RawGarlic.description: A very old crop which is cultivated for its bulb, formed in the base of the leaves. It is highly appreciated in cooking for its pungent and characteristic taste.
LanguageData.VCE_RawGarlic.label: garlic cloves
LanguageData.VCE_RawLettuce.description: A leaf vegetable commonly eaten fresh and served as the base of green salads. Lettuce is generally a rich source of vitamins K and A.
LanguageData.VCE_RawLettuce.label: lettuce
LanguageData.VCE_RawLotus.description: The seeds of the lotus plant. They are packed with nutritional elements which make them an excellent choice for a healthy diet, with a high content of proteins, carbohydrates, and vitamins.
LanguageData.VCE_RawLotus.label: lotus seeds
LanguageData.VCE_RawOkra.description: Edible green seed pods, famously known for their slimy or sticky texture when cut open. Okra is a powerhouse of valuable nutrients.
LanguageData.VCE_RawOkra.label: okra
LanguageData.VCE_RawOlives.description: A small, egg-shaped black or green fruit that is used as food or for making oil. Olives freshly picked from the tree contain oleuropein, which makes the fruit unpalatable for immediate consumption. They must be brined or fermented to be used.
LanguageData.VCE_RawOlives.label: olives
LanguageData.VCE_RawPeanut.description: Seeds with an oblong shape and a papery seed coat. Similar in taste and nutritional profile to tree nuts such as walnuts and almonds, but technically a legume.
LanguageData.VCE_RawPeanut.label: peanuts
LanguageData.VCE_RawRadish.description: An edible root vegetable that is very easy to grow and quick to harvest. Radishes are often planted by novice gardeners.
LanguageData.VCE_RawRadish.label: radishes
LanguageData.VCE_RawSunflower.description: The seeds of the sunflower plant are more commonly eaten as a snack than as part of a meal. They can also be used as garnishes or ingredients in various recipes.
LanguageData.VCE_RawSunflower.label: sunflower seeds
LanguageData.VCE_RawSweetPotatoes.description: Large, starchy, sweet-tasting (tasting similar to pumpkin), tuberous roots that are used as a root vegetable. The sweet potato is distantly related to the common potato.
LanguageData.VCE_RawSweetPotatoes.label: sweet potatoes
LanguageData.VCE_RawTaro.description: A starchy root vegetable. It has a brown outer skin and white flesh with purple specks throughout. When cooked, it has a mildly sweet taste and a texture similar to potato.
LanguageData.VCE_RawTaro.label: taro roots
LanguageData.VCE_RawWaterChestnut.description: Small, rounded corms with a crisp, white flesh that may be eaten raw, slightly boiled, or grilled.
LanguageData.VCE_RawWaterChestnut.label: water chestnuts
LanguageData.VCE_RawWatercress.description: A green leaf vegetable rich in vitamins and minerals and from the same family as kale and broccoli.
LanguageData.VCE_RawWatercress.label: watercress leaves
