LanguageData.VCE_RawYellowBellPeppers.description: Also known as sweet peppers, bell peppers are technically nightshades and part of the capsicum family, but they are the only chili pepper that carries a recessive gene that stops any capsaicin from being made in the fruit, thus making them not spicy.
LanguageData.VCE_RawYellowBellPeppers.label: yellow bell peppers
LanguageData.VCE_RawOats.description: The grain of the oat plant. Oats have numerous uses in foods; most commonly, they are rolled or crushed into oatmeal. Oatmeal is chiefly eaten as porridge, but may also be used in a variety of baked goods. Oats are also used for production of milk substitutes.
LanguageData.VCE_RawOats.label: oats
LanguageData.VCE_RawMoss.description: A nutrient-rich moss that can be used to feed cave animals, but not humans.
LanguageData.VCE_RawMoss.label: cave moss
LanguageData.VCE_AloeVera.description: A herb with succulent leaves that are arranged in a rosette. The leaves are grey to green and sometimes have white spots on their surfaces. They have sharp, pinkish spines along their edges and are the source of a colourless gel that is used in many medicinal products.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Aloe vera is only growable in sandy growing zones, which ignore fertility. These plants will however take a long time to sow and harvest, basically exchanging colonist work for growing plants in a normally unused space. Aloe vera is a source of herbal medicine.
LanguageData.VCE_AloeVera.label: aloe vera plant
LanguageData.VCE_Beans.description: A herbaceous bush with a long history of cultivation. They are distinguished from the many other varieties of beans in that green beans are harvested and consumed with their enclosing pods, before the bean seeds inside have fully matured, similar to peas.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Companion planting: green beans will grow faster if carrots are cultivated in the same map (up to 30% faster if 100 are found).
LanguageData.VCE_Beans.label: green bean plant
LanguageData.VCE_BellPepper.description: Also known as sweet peppers, bell peppers are technically nightshades and part of the capsicum family, but they are the only chili pepper that carries a recessive gene that stops any capsaicin from being made in the fruit, thus making them not spicy.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> On average, 25% of bell peppers will mature into yellow peppers, while 75% will mature into red peppers. The former are more nutritive. If planted in large numbers this results in increased average yield.
LanguageData.VCE_BellPepper.label: bell pepper plant
LanguageData.VCE_BrusselsSprout.description: A form of cabbage grown for its edible buds called "sprouts". Brussels sprouts usually are eaten cooked, and the small young sprouts have a more delicate flavour than older ones.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Contrary to other plants, brussels sprouts thrive in snow, and receive a considerable boost to growth if snowing, AS LONG AS it is also growth season (they won't grow or be able to be planted otherwise). They won't die due to cold.
LanguageData.VCE_BrusselsSprout.label: brussels sprout plant
LanguageData.VCE_Buckwheat.description: A pseudocereal cultivated for its grain-like seeds. Its white flowers are produced in terminal clusters and after pòllination turn into triangular-shaped seeds that are enclosed by a tough dark brown or gray rind.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Buckwheat is very resistant to fire, and this variety has been modified to be completely non-flammable.
LanguageData.VCE_Buckwheat.label: buckwheat plant
LanguageData.VCE_ButternutSquash.description: A vine plant that produces a vegetable with a sweet, nutty taste similar to that of a pumpkin. It has tan-yellow skin and orange fleshy pulp with a compartment of seeds in the blossom end.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Butternut squash is only growable in sandy growing zones, which ignore fertility. These plants will however take a long time to sow and harvest, basically exchanging colonist work for growing plants in a normally unused space. Butternut squash takes a long time to grow.
LanguageData.VCE_ButternutSquash.label: butternut squash plant
LanguageData.VCE_Carrot.description: A herbaceous plant of the Apiaceae family that produces an edible taproot. An erect rosette of doubly compound, finely divided leaves develops above ground, while the edible carrot and attached roots are below.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Companion planting: carrots will grow faster if green beans are cultivated in the same map (up to 30% faster if 100 are found).
LanguageData.VCE_Carrot.label: carrot plant
LanguageData.VCE_Cassava.description: A tuberous edible plant also called manioc, mandioca, or yuca. They have a mild nutty taste and are commonly eaten as root vegetables in stews or as side dishes. Cassava roots are a versatile vegetable that can be used to produce flour, breads, tapioca, a laundry starch, and an alcoholic beverage.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Cassava grows best in tropical, warm and humid climates. As such, it will receive a considerable growth bonus if the weather is foggy.
LanguageData.VCE_Cassava.label: cassava plant
LanguageData.VCE_Celery.description: A vegetable consisting of a long fibrous stalk tapering into leaves. The leaves are strongly flavored and are used less often, either as a flavoring in soups and stews or as a dried herb. Celery is a staple in many soups.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Celery has a very long growth period (almost a full year!). If it can be kept alive all that time, it produces greater yield than corn.
LanguageData.VCE_Celery.label: celery plant
LanguageData.VCE_Chickpea.description: One of the earliest cultivated legumes, widely grown for its nutritious seeds. These are high in fibre and protein and are a good source of iron, phosphorus, and folic acid.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Chickpeas grow quite fast and have good yield, but they require a LOT of space to be stored (this may be negated by stack-increasing mods, you dirty cheater, but it's up to you to decide to mess up the game's balance).
LanguageData.VCE_Chickpea.label: chickpea plant
LanguageData.VCE_Cucumber.description: A sprawling vine with large leaves and curling tendrils, triangular prickly hairy leaves and yellow flowers. Produces a fruit that is a long thin vegetable with hard green skin and wet transparent flesh. \n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Cucumbers are only growable in sandy growing zones, which ignore fertility. These plants will however take a long time to sow and harvest, basically exchanging colonist work for growing plants in a normally unused space. Cucumbers grow quite fast, providing little yield but in a short period. They can be eaten raw.
LanguageData.VCE_Cucumber.label: cucumber plant
LanguageData.VCE_Garlic.description: A plant with long, flat grasslike leaves forming a stalk, which rises directly from the flower bulb, the part of the plant used as food and medicine. The bulb is made up of many smaller cloves covered with a papery skin.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Garlic has very little yield, but can grow in almost any terrain and takes an obscene time to rot, as dried garlic can be used to cook too.
LanguageData.VCE_Garlic.label: garlic plant
LanguageData.VCE_Lettuce.description: A leaf vegetable commonly eaten fresh and served as the base of green salads. Lettuce is generally a rich source of vitamins K and A.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Lettuce can be eaten raw like strawberries, but needs to be grown in shade: outside, but under roof. It won't grow inside.
LanguageData.VCE_Lettuce.label: lettuce plant
LanguageData.VCE_Lotus.description: Sometimes colloquially called a water lily, lotus plants are adapted to grow in the flood plains of slow-moving rivers and delta areas. Lotus rhizomes have a crunchy texture with sweet-tangy flavours and can be deep-fried, stir-fried, or stuffed with meats or preserved fruits.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Lotus is only growable in aquatic growing zones, which ignore fertility. These plants will however take a long time to sow and harvest, basically exchanging colonist work for growing plants in a normally unused space. Lotus takes a long time to grow.
LanguageData.VCE_Lotus.label: lotus plant
LanguageData.VCE_Melon.description: A plant producing a sweet, edible, and fleshy fruit of considerable size. Melons are usually cultivated in warm regions.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Melons are only growable in sandy growing zones, which ignore fertility. These plants will however take a long time to sow and harvest, basically exchanging colonist work for growing plants in a normally unused space. Melons need a lot of space, blocking sowing in adjacent spaces, but harvesting them can't fail
LanguageData.VCE_Melon.label: melon plant
LanguageData.VCE_Okra.description: A flowering plant in the mallow family valued for its edible green seed pods, which are famously known for their slimy or sticky texture when cut open. Okra is a powerhouse of valuable nutrients.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Okra is very, VERY resistant to damage. Okra plants will take less damage from prolonged lack of light, and will regenerate damage caused by any other means.
LanguageData.VCE_Okra.label: okra plant
LanguageData.VCE_Peanut.description: A plant that produces seeds with an oblong shape and a papery seed coat. Similar in taste and nutritional profile to tree nuts such as walnuts and almonds, but technically a legume.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> This genetically modified variety of peanuts is sown by throwing the seeds haphazardly on the growing zone, and as such the plants will automatically be planted in the adjacent tiles 75% of the time, reducing the total amount of sowing work needed. Useful!
LanguageData.VCE_Peanut.label: peanut plant
LanguageData.VCE_PeanutSecondary.description: A plant that produces seeds with an oblong shape and a papery seed coat. Similar in taste and nutritional profile to tree nuts such as walnuts and almonds, but technically a legume.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> This genetically modified variety of peanuts is sown by throwing the seeds haphazardly on the growing zone, and as such the plants will automatically be planted in the adjacent tiles 75% of the time, reducing the total amount of sowing work needed. Useful!
LanguageData.VCE_PeanutSecondary.label: secondary peanut plant
LanguageData.VCE_Pineapple.description: A sweet tropical fruit with a tough leathery skin and spiky leaves on top. The word pineapple was first used to mean "pine cone," and the fruit was given this name because of its resemblance to a large pine cone.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Pineapples have a very long growth period, but they are very nutritive, taking up less space in stockpiles.
LanguageData.VCE_Pineapple.label: pineapple plant
LanguageData.VCE_Radish.description: An edible root vegetable that is very easy to grow and quick to harvest. Radishes are often planted by novice gardeners.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> This genetically modified radish has a very short growth period (less than 2 days). This means it is an effective crop if you need small amounts of food FAST, and the sowing and harvesting work will be the most important factor.
LanguageData.VCE_Radish.label: radish plant
LanguageData.VCE_Sunflower.description: A large crop used for its edible oil and edible fruits. The name sunflower may derive from the flower's head's shape, which resembles the sun.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Sunflowers grow 30% faster in equatorial regions. The size of the equatorial region depends on the planet's overall temperature: below 15º for Normal, below 35º for Warm, below 65º for Hot and all the planet for Very Hot. Planets with below Normal temperature are considered to have no equator.
LanguageData.VCE_Sunflower.label: sunflower plant
LanguageData.VCE_SweetPotato.description: A plant with large, starchy, sweet-tasting (tasting similar to pumpkin), tuberous roots that are used as a root vegetable. The sweet potato is distantly related to the common potato.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Sweet potatoes show a marked preference for rocky soils, flourishing on them. Rich soils are actually detrimental to them. They do not tolerate frost, growing best at an average temperature of 24 °C, and receinving penalties under 20ºC.
LanguageData.VCE_SweetPotato.label: sweet potato plant
LanguageData.VCE_Taro.description: A tropical plant grown primarily for its edible corm, a root vegetable. Taro is one of the few crops that can be grown under flooded conditions, with the base of the plant always under water. This is due to air spaces in the petiole, which permit underwater gaseous exchange with the atmosphere.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Taro is only growable in aquatic growing zones, which ignore fertility. These plants will however take a long time to sow and harvest, basically exchanging colonist work for growing plants in a normally unused space. Taro is the baseline aquatic plant.
LanguageData.VCE_Taro.label: taro plant
LanguageData.VCE_WaterChestnut.description: An aquatic vegetable that grows in marshes, under water, in the mud. It has stem-like, tubular green leaves, and small, rounded corms with a crisp, white flesh that may be eaten raw, slightly boiled, or grilled.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Water chestnut is only growable in aquatic growing zones, which ignore fertility. These plants will however take a long time to sow and harvest, basically exchanging colonist work for growing plants in a normally unused space. Water chestnut grows 30% faster in the heat (37-60ºC).
LanguageData.VCE_WaterChestnut.label: water chestnut plant
LanguageData.VCE_Watercress.description: Watercress is a rapidly growing, perennial plant. It is one of the oldest known leaf vegetables consumed by humans. Watercress and many of its relatives are noteworthy for their piquant flavors.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Watercress is only growable in aquatic growing zones, which ignore fertility. These plants will however take a long time to sow and harvest, basically exchanging colonist work for growing plants in a normally unused space. Watercress grows faster than taro but needs a lot of space, blocking sowing in adjacent spaces.
LanguageData.VCE_Watercress.label: watercress plant
LanguageData.VCE_YellowBellPepper.description: Also known as sweet peppers, bell peppers are technically nightshades and part of the capsicum family, but they are the only chili pepper that carries a recessive gene that stops any capsaicin from being made in the fruit, thus making them not spicy.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> On average, 25% of bell peppers will mature into yellow peppers, while 75% will mature into red peppers. The former are more nutritive. If planted in large numbers this results in increased average yield.
LanguageData.VCE_YellowBellPepper.label: bell pepper plant
LanguageData.VCE_Barley.description: A cereal grain that people can use in bread, beverages, stews, and other dishes. As a whole grain, barley provides fiber, vitamins, and minerals. It has been genetically engineered to gently break into flour with a mere touch.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Barley is a "dual crop", in the sense that it produces both flour and hay.
LanguageData.VCE_Barley.label: barley plant
LanguageData.VCE_Oats.description: The oat, sometimes called the common oat, is a species of cereal grain grown for its seed, which is known by the same name. Oats have numerous uses in gastronomy, and they can also be turned into vegetable milk.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Oat has low yield, but it is very versatile, being a "dual crop", in the sense that it produces both flour and oats which can be either used as a vegetable or turned into milk.
LanguageData.VCE_Oats.label: oat plant
LanguageData.VCE_Rye.description: A plant that grows well in much poorer soils than those necessary for most cereal grains. Thus, it is an especially valuable crop in regions where the soil has sand or peat. It has been genetically engineered to gently break into flour with a mere touch.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Rye is only growable in sandy growing zones, which ignore fertility. These plants will however take a long time to sow and harvest, basically exchanging colonist work for growing plants in a normally unused space. Rye needs to be grown away from large bodies of water (water within 10 tiles of it will stop its growth).
LanguageData.VCE_Rye.label: rye plant
LanguageData.VCE_Sorghum.description: A drought resistant cereal used for food, animal fodder, the production of alcoholic beverages, and biofuels. Sorghum grain is gluten free, high in resistant starch, and more abundant and diverse phenolic compounds compared to other major cereal crops.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Sorghum stalks can grow to up to 2.5 meters in height, and grow very close together. A sorghum field can be used as quite effective cover.
LanguageData.VCE_Sorghum.label: sorghum plant
LanguageData.VCE_Blueberry.description: A shrub prized for its sweet edible fruits. Hailed as a "superfood", blueberries are commonly eaten fresh as a dessert fruit and can be baked in a variety of pastries.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Blueberries are pollinated by bees: they will receive a growth boost if an apiary is found in a radius of 6 tiles. To avoid lag this is only calculated once, when the plant is sown, so apiaries placed after sowing it will have no effect. If there are no apiaries it will behave as a slower strawberry plant.
LanguageData.VCE_Blueberry.label: blueberry plant
LanguageData.VCE_Canola.description: A bright-yellow flowering variety of the rapeseed, cultivated mainly for its oil-rich seed.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Canola, though not directly edible, can be very efficiently converted into chemfuel at a refinery.
LanguageData.VCE_Canola.label: canola plant
LanguageData.VCE_CoconutPalm.description:
A palm tree cultivated extensively in tropical areas for its edible fruit, the coconut. Coconut palms flourish best close to the sea on low-lying areas a few feet above high water.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All fruit trees take about four times as much space as regular crops, take a long time to grow and produce fruits that can be eaten raw. They aren't destroyed after harvesting but have a limited lifespan and will eventually need to be replanted.

Coconut palms are only growable in sandy growing zones, which ignore fertility. These plants will however take a long time to sow and harvest, basically exchanging colonist work for growing plants in a normally unused space.

Coconut palms receive a growth bonus if sea water tiles are found in a 6 tile radius. Coconuts can be used as fruits or processed into milk.
LanguageData.VCE_CoconutPalm.label: coconut palm
LanguageData.VCE_TreeAlmond.description:
A deciduous tree cultivated for its fruit, almonds. The sweet almond can be eaten plain or be used as ingredient in desserts and confectioneries. The trees are strikingly beautiful when in flower; they produce fragrant, five-petaled, light pink to white flowers.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All fruit trees take about four times as much space as regular crops, take a long time to grow and produce fruits that can be eaten raw. They aren't destroyed after harvesting but have a limited lifespan and will eventually need to be replanted.

Almond trees are beautiful, but they require water to be present in a 6 tile radius to grow properly. Almonds can be used as fruits or processed into milk.
LanguageData.VCE_TreeAlmond.label: almond tree
LanguageData.VCE_TreeAvocado.description: An evergreen tree with thick, leathery leaves that produces perfect white, ivory to yellow flowers. The fruit has a large seed or pit in the center and may be green or nearly black.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Avocado trees take a notoriously long period to mature and produce their fruits. If they can be kept alive all that time, they produce greater yield than other fruit trees. Plus if you install the Coffees and Teas mod and pair them with Pumpkin Spice Coffee you can have your dream RimWorld Hipster experience!
LanguageData.VCE_TreeAvocado.label: avocado tree
LanguageData.VCE_TreeMango.description:
A tropical evergreen tree that produces oval fruits, vividly coloured with shades of red and yellow. The single large seed is flattened, and the flesh that surrounds it is yellow to orange in colour, juicy, and of distinctive sweet-spicy flavour.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All fruit trees take about four times as much space as regular crops, take a long time to grow and produce fruits that can be eaten raw. They aren't destroyed after harvesting but have a limited lifespan and will eventually need to be replanted.

Mango trees have very good yield, but mangoes cannot be frozen or they get destroyed. The game won't be smart enough to detect this, so disable them from freezers.
LanguageData.VCE_TreeMango.label: mango tree
LanguageData.VCE_TreeOlive.description: A subtropical broad-leaved evergreen tree. Its leaves, leathery and lance-shaped, are dark green above and silvery on the underside and are paired opposite each other on the twig. The wood is resistant to decay. If the top dies back, a new trunk will often arise from the roots.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Interestingly, olive trees show a marked preference for rocky soils, flourishing on them. Rich soils are actually detrimental to them. They will also live for a very, very long time, far longer than other produce trees. Contrary to other tree produce, olives can't be eaten raw.
LanguageData.VCE_TreeOlive.label: olive tree
LanguageData.VCE_Healshroom.description: A slow-growing mushroom which yields herbal medicine when harvested. Sowing and harvesting healshroom are both very labor-intensive tasks because of its fragility. It can grow on fungal gravel, but only in darkness - exposing it to any kind of light, including darklights, will kill it.
LanguageData.VCE_Healshroom.label: healshroom
LanguageData.VCE_Moss.description: A nutrient-rich moss that can be used to feed cave animals, but not humans. It can grow on fungal gravel, but only in darkness - exposing it to any kind of light, including darklights, will kill it.
LanguageData.VCE_Moss.label: cave moss
LanguageData.VCE_Tinctshroom.description: A genetically-engineered mushroom which secretes a self-altering dye. This dye can be coaxed into different colors and used to dye apparel. It can grow on fungal gravel, but only in darkness - exposing it to any kind of light, including darklights, will kill it.
LanguageData.VCE_Tinctshroom.label: tinctshroom
LanguageData.PS_ChemfuelTank.description: An industrial liquid storage tank meant for storing chemfuel delivered via chemfuel pipe network. Tanks can be designated to be siphoned for usable chemfuel as needed. If damaged even lightly the escaping fumes will very swiftly create a gaseous fireball, the size of the resulting explosion will depend on the amount stored within.
LanguageData.PS_ChemfuelTank.label: chemfuel tank
LanguageData.VCHE_ChemfuelDrain.description: A hopper-like storage basin meant to input chemfuel into the network, filling up connected tanks.
LanguageData.VCHE_ChemfuelDrain.label: chemfuel drain
LanguageData.VCHE_ChemfuelPipe.description: Steel pipes designed to transport usable chemfuel from deepchem refineries to chemfuel tanks for storage or to chemfuel-consuming structures for automatic refilling.
LanguageData.VCHE_ChemfuelPipe.label: chemfuel pipe
LanguageData.VCHE_ChemfuelTap.description: A hopper-like storage basin meant to hold dispensed chemfuel from tanks periodically for use later.
LanguageData.VCHE_ChemfuelTap.label: chemfuel tap
LanguageData.VCHE_ChemfuelValve.label: chemfuel valve
LanguageData.VCHE_UndergroundChemfuelPipe.description: Subterranean steel pipes designed to transport usable chemfuel from deepchem refineries to chemfuel tanks for storage or to chemfuel-consuming structures for automatic refilling.
LanguageData.VCHE_UndergroundChemfuelPipe.label: subterranean chemfuel pipe
LanguageData.PS_DeepchemRefinery.description: A complex machine capable of refining deepchem delivered via deepchem pipes into usable liquid chemfuel. If no output is detected fuel will automatically be dispensed into cans. If output is connected to a tank or tap via chemfuel pipes the finished product will be transported as such for storage or dispensing.
LanguageData.PS_DeepchemRefinery.label: deepchem refinery
LanguageData.PS_DeepchemTank.description: A heavy industrial sludge storage tank meant for storing deepchem delivered via deepchem pipe network. Tanks can be designated to be siphoned as needed. If damaged even lightly the escaping fumes will very swiftly create a gaseous fireball, the size of the resulting explosion will depend on the amount stored within.
LanguageData.PS_DeepchemTank.label: deepchem tank
LanguageData.VCHE_DeepchemDrain.description: A hopper-like storage basin meant to input deepchem into the network, filling up connected tanks.
LanguageData.VCHE_DeepchemDrain.label: deepchem drain
LanguageData.VCHE_DeepchemPipe.description: Heavy steel pipes for transporting the sludge known as deepchem from pumpjacks to refineries or deepchem tanks for storage.
LanguageData.VCHE_DeepchemPipe.label: deepchem pipe
LanguageData.VCHE_DeepchemPumpjack.description: A deep-crust drilling rig for extracting deepchem from deep under the surface. Use a ground penetrating scanner to detect deposits. Deepchem can then be further refined into usable chemfuel. Can be repositioned freely.
LanguageData.VCHE_DeepchemPumpjack.label: deepchem pumpjack
LanguageData.VCHE_DeepchemTap.description: A hopper-like storage basin meant to hold dispensed deepchem from tanks periodically for use later.
LanguageData.VCHE_DeepchemTap.label: deepchem tap
LanguageData.VCHE_DeepchemValve.label: deepchem valve
LanguageData.VCHE_UndergroundDeepchemPipe.description: Subterranean heavy steel pipes for transporting the sludge known as deepchem from pumpjacks to refineries or deepchem tanks for storage.
LanguageData.VCHE_UndergroundDeepchemPipe.label: subterranean deepchem pipe
LanguageData.VCHE_Deepchem.description: A volatile liquid chemical sludge, extracted from deep underground pockets. It’s too thick to be used to fuel engines and rockets, or to transmute into propellant for projectiles, but it can be refined down into standard chemfuel.
LanguageData.VCHE_Deepchem.label: deepchem
LanguageData.GR_CryoBlast.label: cryo blast
LanguageData.GR_Disintegrator.label: disintegrator beam
LanguageData.GR_Distortion.label: spatial distortion
LanguageData.GR_FireSpit.label: fire spit
LanguageData.GR_GreatHairballProjectile.label: greater hairball
LanguageData.GR_HairballProjectile.label: hairball
LanguageData.GR_IncendiaryMote.label: incendiary bolt
LanguageData.GR_Laser.label: laser beam
LanguageData.GR_PlasmaBurst.label: plasma burst
LanguageData.GR_PoisonBlast.label: poison blast
LanguageData.GR_PoisonBreath.label: poison breath
LanguageData.GR_Proj_ThrownSac.label: thrown sac
LanguageData.GR_RazorProjectile.label: razor scythe
LanguageData.GR_SmokeBomb.label: smokepop shell
LanguageData.GR_SniperFlechette.label: sniper flechette
LanguageData.GR_StunBolt.label: stun bolt
LanguageData.GR_Warhead.label: antigrain warhead
LanguageData.GR_Web.label: web
LanguageData.AA_Bullet_TinyVenomBarb.label: tiny venomous barb
LanguageData.AA_ExplodingWeb.label: exploding web
LanguageData.AA_HugeQuill.label: huge quill
LanguageData.RB_BeeBomb.label: bee shell
LanguageData.RB_Quill.label: quill
LanguageData.GR_HugeBearCatRug.description: A bearcat pelt rug. This thing seems to be quite more exotic than other pelt rugs!
LanguageData.GR_HugeBearCatRug.label: huge bearcat rug
LanguageData.GR_HugeBearRug.description: A bear pelt rug. Taxidermy seems to be in vogue on the Rim!
LanguageData.GR_HugeBearRug.label: huge bear rug
LanguageData.GR_MuffaloRug.description: A muffalo pelt rug. Taxidermy seems to be in vogue on the Rim!
LanguageData.GR_MuffaloRug.label: muffalo pelt rug
LanguageData.GR_WolfRug.description: A wolf pelt rug. Taxidermy seems to be in vogue on the Rim!
LanguageData.GR_WolfRug.label: wolf rug
LanguageData.GR_ScalyBearRug.description: Really, couldn't you decorate with vinyl decals, like a normal, non-insane person? This looks horrendous. Some of the colonists seem to like it, though..
LanguageData.GR_ScalyBearRug.label: scaly bear rug
LanguageData.GR_ArchocentipedeFormer.description:
The archotech version of the Genomorpher, this machine combines the best genetic material of all the animal branches into a single Archocentipede Growthcell. The way it functions is beyond understanding and is impossible to replicate even with the best glitterworld technology.

In order for this machine to function, it requires multiple connecting DNA Storage Banks which each have the complete genetic data from the different animal branches. Each storage bank will be completely drained in order to ensure that the resulting Growthcell is perfect in every single way.

The resulting Archocentipede Growthcell requires its own special Archowomb, as it is impossible to replicate the necessary nurturing environment.

Genomes needed:
LanguageData.GR_ArchocentipedeFormer.label: archocentipede former
LanguageData.GR_ArchoWomb.description: A relatively massive machine designed to simulate the environment of a mother’s womb specifically for the Archocentipede. Once the Growthcell has been inserted, the embryo can start growing all the way to its adult life stage before being released.\n\nCare should be taken before beginning the process, as the resulting energy signature will force any and all hostiles to attack the colony before the monstrosity can be born. The procedure will last 15 days and once finished, the creature will be under the player’s control with peerless combat prowess.
LanguageData.GR_ArchoWomb.label: archowomb
LanguageData.GR_DNAStorageBank.description:
A vital piece of archotechnology that is part of the process in creating an Archocentipede.

This machine is able to extract genetic material from live hybrids and stores it for later use, preventing deterioration. Depending on the base Genoframe, the amount of viable genetic material extracted will vary.

Each storage bank can only store the genetic material from a single animal branch, such as ursine or feline. Inserting a hybrid into the database yields half as much valuable data compared to a paragon. DNA storage banks need to be in line of sight of the archocentipede former to connect to it properly.

Detailed yields for paragons (half for hybrids):

Awful quality: yields 1%
Poor quality: yields 3%
Normal quality: yields 6%
Good quality: yields 12%
Excellent quality: yields 25%
Masterwork quality: yields 50%
Legendary quality: yields 100%
LanguageData.GR_DNAStorageBank.label: DNA storage bank
LanguageData.GR_AncientBioBattery.description: A very efficient battery that has somehow managed to survive for so long without maintenance, purpose-built to power genome storage rooms.
LanguageData.GR_AncientBioBattery.label: ancient microbiobattery
LanguageData.GR_AncientCrate.description: Whatever this crate contained has long ago rotted and turned to dust.
LanguageData.GR_AncientCrate.label: ancient ruined crate
LanguageData.GR_AncientCrateDouble.description: Whatever this crate contained has long ago rotted and turned to dust.
LanguageData.GR_AncientCrateDouble.label: ancient ruined crate
LanguageData.GR_AncientCratePile.description: Whatever these crates contained has long ago rotted and turned to dust.
LanguageData.GR_AncientCratePile.label: pile of ancient ruined crates
LanguageData.GR_AncientCryofreezer.description: A super-charged air-conditioning unit designed for its intended purpose of preventing genomes from breaking down entropically. Unfortunately, that temperature is also beyond the comfort range of almost all organic life but it can no longer maintain the desired output of approaching absolute zero.
LanguageData.GR_AncientCryofreezer.label: ancient cryofreezer
LanguageData.GR_AncientElectrowomb.description: This device has collapsed in on itself, rendering it useless.
LanguageData.GR_AncientElectrowomb.label: ancient electrowomb
LanguageData.GR_AncientElectrowombLarge.description: The exterior shell has completely fallen apart, yet it seems that it exploded instead of imploded.
LanguageData.GR_AncientElectrowombLarge.label: ancient large electrowomb
LanguageData.GR_AncientElectrowombSpawner.description: This device has collapsed in on itself, rendering it useless.
LanguageData.GR_AncientElectrowombSpawner.label: ancient electrowomb
LanguageData.GR_AncientGeneticsTinkeringTable.descrip: Most of the useful and otherwise functional parts are missing, indicating intentional sabotage.
LanguageData.GR_AncientGeneticsTinkeringTable.label: ancient genetics tinkering table
LanguageData.GR_AncientGenomeExtractor.description: Lacking proper maintenance for so long, this machine is rusted shut.
LanguageData.GR_AncientGenomeExtractor.label: ancient genome extractor table
LanguageData.GR_AncientGenomeRecombinator.description: A very old recombinator that somehow possesses an awful stench and excessive mould.
LanguageData.GR_AncientGenomeRecombinator.label: ancient genome recombinator
LanguageData.GR_AncientGenomorpher.description: A Genomorpher that has fallen into complete disrepair, some salvage still remains.
LanguageData.GR_AncientGenomorpher.label: ancient genomorpher
LanguageData.GR_AncientTechCrate.description: Whatever this crate contained has long ago rotted and turned to dust.
LanguageData.GR_AncientTechCrate.label: ancient mechanized crate
LanguageData.GR_AncientTissueGrinder.description: The machinery and electronics are irreparable but could be broken down for scrap.
LanguageData.GR_AncientTissueGrinder.label: ancient tissue grinder
LanguageData.GR_BioLight.description: A metallic capsule filled with some bioluminescent compound. This one seems to still be producing some faint light.
LanguageData.GR_BioLight.label: ancient bioluminescent light
LanguageData.GR_CommsTable.description: This was once a smart table. But now it's dumb.
LanguageData.GR_CommsTable.label: ancient comms table
LanguageData.GR_LabLockerBank.description: An ancient, rusted bank of lockers. All of the lockers have been forced open with nothing valuable left behind.
LanguageData.GR_LabLockerBank.label: ancient lab locker bank
LanguageData.GR_LooseCables.description: This tangle of decayed cables were probably once housed inside the ceiling panels.
LanguageData.GR_LooseCables.label: loose cables
LanguageData.GR_MonitorBank.description: These monitors will never show any information again.
LanguageData.GR_MonitorBank.label: ancient monitor banks
LanguageData.GR_Rack.description: This server rack once housed a lot of genetic information, but all its data has now degraded completely.
LanguageData.GR_Rack.label: ancient server rack
LanguageData.GR_RandomBooster.label: random booster
LanguageData.GR_RandomExcavator.label: random excavator
LanguageData.GR_RandomGenoframe.label: random genoframe
LanguageData.GR_RandomGenome.label: random genome
LanguageData.GR_Ruined_Mechahybrid_Antenna.descriptio: A collapsed antenna that is completely unable of transmitting anything ever again.
LanguageData.GR_Ruined_Mechahybrid_Antenna.label: ancient mechahybrid antenna
LanguageData.GR_Ruined_Mechahybridizer.description: The broken maws of this ancient factory will never be able to close again.
LanguageData.GR_Ruined_Mechahybridizer.label: ancient mechahybridizer
LanguageData.GR_RuinedAncientBioBattery.description: This ancient tank once converted biological matter into an electrical current. It is now completely broken and useless.
LanguageData.GR_RuinedAncientBioBattery.label: ancient ruined bio-battery tank
LanguageData.GR_RuinedAncientGeneTailoringPod.descrip: This ancient structure was once used to inject nanomachines in a subject in an attempt to create the perfect soldier. It now lies rusted and unsalvageable.
LanguageData.GR_RuinedAncientGeneTailoringPod.label: ancient ruined gene-tailoring pod
LanguageData.GR_RuinedAnimalControlHub.description: It seems that this structure was capable of emitting some kind of electromagnetic radiation used to control brain function of certain animals. This was crude and primitive technology prone to failure and instability, and as such it is completely useless now.
LanguageData.GR_RuinedAnimalControlHub.label: ancient animal control hub
LanguageData.GR_RuinedAnimalEnrichmentCenter.descript: Whatever was held inside this dank, rusted metal coffin has long since escaped. It is damaged beyond all repair.
LanguageData.GR_RuinedAnimalEnrichmentCenter.label: ancient animal enrichment center
LanguageData.GR_RuinedArchotechPlatform.description: This deteriorated platform seems to have been used to assemble some kind of machinery. It now lays bare and empty.
LanguageData.GR_RuinedArchotechPlatform.label: ancient construction platform
LanguageData.GR_RuinedBarrelDouble.description: Whatever this barrel held has long ago evaporated.
LanguageData.GR_RuinedBarrelDouble.label: ancient ruined lab barrels
LanguageData.GR_RuinedGeneDuplicator.description: This semi-spherical vat seems to have held some kind of viscuous liquid inside, as evidenced by the rancid-smelling residue coating its insides. It is now completely ruined and only useful as metal scrap.
LanguageData.GR_RuinedGeneDuplicator.label: ancient gene duplicator
LanguageData.GR_RuinedGenePod.description: An ancient genepod that was used to combine genetic material. This machine is obsolete and damaged beyond repair.
LanguageData.GR_RuinedGenePod.label: ancient ruined genepod
LanguageData.GR_RuinedGeneRecombinator.description: A completely obsolete model of gene recombinator, that is also deteriorated and rusted beyond and salvaging.
LanguageData.GR_RuinedGeneRecombinator.label: ancient gene recombinator
LanguageData.GR_RuinedGeneticDatabase.description: This ancient computer was used to storage genetic information and feed genetic dataprints to other machines, but its components have deteriorated so far that all the data it contained has degraded to nothingness.
LanguageData.GR_RuinedGeneticDatabase.label: ancient genetic database
LanguageData.GR_RuinedGeneticExtractionTable.descript: Yet another workstation that has deteriorated beyond all recovery. A foul-smelling vial of stagnant liquid warns you to keep away from it.
LanguageData.GR_RuinedGeneticExtractionTable.label: ancient genetic extraction table
LanguageData.GR_RuinedGeneticStorage.description: This little wheeled table housed a small specialized computer and refrigerated trays to store genetic material, but it is now so damaged it can only be used as scrap.
LanguageData.GR_RuinedGeneticStorage.label: ancient genetic storage
LanguageData.GR_RuinedGenomeSequencer.description: A large centrifuge used in gene sequencing. Its corroded parts will never be of use to anyone again.
LanguageData.GR_RuinedGenomeSequencer.label: ancient genome sequencer
LanguageData.GR_RuinedHexagelPlant.label: ancient hexagel core reactor
LanguageData.GR_RuinedIncubator.description: This small canister has been smashed to pieces and whatever it contained has long evaporated.
LanguageData.GR_RuinedIncubator.label: ancient ruined incubator
LanguageData.GR_RuinedLabBarrel.description: Whatever this barrel held has long ago evaporated.
LanguageData.GR_RuinedLabBarrel.label: ancient ruined lab barrel
LanguageData.GR_RuinedLabShelf.description: Rusted shelving units coated in grime and dust.
LanguageData.GR_RuinedLabShelf.label: ancient lab shelf
LanguageData.GR_RuinedMechanoidTinkeringTable.descrip: This workbench appears to have been used to try and reverse engineer mechanoid components, but it is damaged to the point of uselessness.
LanguageData.GR_RuinedMechanoidTinkeringTable.label: ancient mechanoid tinkering table
LanguageData.GR_RuinedMutaniteCentrifuge.description: A machine used to process mutagenic minerals, now completely broken and unsalvageable.
LanguageData.GR_RuinedMutaniteCentrifuge.label: ancient mutanite centrifuge
LanguageData.GR_RuinedNutrientVat.description: A slurry of liquefied organic pulp filled this reinforced glass vat. It now lays broken and empty.
LanguageData.GR_RuinedNutrientVat.label: ancient nutrient vat
LanguageData.GR_RuinedPulper.description: A weird-looking machine that shredded wood into organic pulp. Broken beyond repair.
LanguageData.GR_RuinedPulper.label: ancient pulper
LanguageData.GR_RuinedPulpRecycler.description: An obsolete, ancient machine that recycled wood, corpses and bones into organic pulp. Broken beyond repair.
LanguageData.GR_RuinedPulpRecycler.label: ancient pulp recycler
LanguageData.GR_RuinedRefrigeratedCoils.description: These structural cryofuel coils have been completely ruined by time and neglect. They can no longer function.
LanguageData.GR_RuinedRefrigeratedCoils.label: ancient refrigerated coils
LanguageData.GR_RuinedTissueGrowingVat.description: A metal sarcophagus used to grow bio-mechanic implants in a controlled manner. Its machinery has been gutted and has later decayed into rusted scrap.
LanguageData.GR_RuinedTissueGrowingVat.label: ancient tissue growing vat
LanguageData.GR_RuinedVials.description: Small canisters that once held some valuable liquids.
LanguageData.GR_RuinedVials.label: ancient ruined vials
LanguageData.GR_SignA.description: A weathered sign advertising a company that has long been forgotten.
LanguageData.GR_SignA.label: ancient sign
LanguageData.GR_SignB.description: A weathered sign advertising a company that has long been forgotten.
LanguageData.GR_SignB.label: ancient sign
LanguageData.GR_SignC.description: A weathered sign advertising a company that has long been forgotten.
LanguageData.GR_SignC.label: ancient sign
LanguageData.GR_SignD.description: A weathered sign advertising a company that has long been forgotten.
LanguageData.GR_SignD.label: ancient sign
LanguageData.GR_SignE.description: A weathered sign advertising a company that has long been forgotten.
LanguageData.GR_SignE.label: ancient sign
LanguageData.GR_SignF.description: A weathered sign advertising a company that has long been forgotten.
LanguageData.GR_SignF.label: ancient sign
LanguageData.GR_SignG.description: A weathered sign advertising a company that has long been forgotten.
LanguageData.GR_SignG.label: ancient sign
LanguageData.GR_SignH.description: A weathered sign advertising a company that has long been forgotten.
LanguageData.GR_SignH.label: ancient sign
LanguageData.GR_Sink.description: Drinking from this will give you more than just a few rads...
LanguageData.GR_Sink.label: ancient sink
LanguageData.GR_SmallBioLight.description: A metallic capsule filled with some bioluminescent compound. This one seems to still be producing some faint light.
LanguageData.GR_SmallBioLight.label: ancient bioluminescent light
LanguageData.GR_Toilet.description: Drinking from this will give you more than just a few rads...
LanguageData.GR_Toilet.label: ancient toilet
LanguageData.GR_BiomechanicalLabBeacon.description: This strange stellated octahedron seems to house an assembly of complex computer chips. It appears to be self-powered, and a faint beeping noise emanates from its core. The radio waves it is constantly emitting are being decoded, but the process seems to have destabilized its power source, and it will blow up soon!
LanguageData.GR_BiomechanicalLabBeacon.label: biomechanical lab beacon
LanguageData.GR_Mechafuse.description:
This piece of technology is unusual in that its design isn’t linked to the original Mechahybridizer, having been developed later on while trying to emulate the system internals. This is due to the fact that the Mechahybridizer broadcasts a signal to a nearby orbital mechanoid hive to protect the process and then recover the potential resulting mechahybrid to be dispatched into assignments. However, this process is extremely expensive and often there would be enough recovery personnel on-site, so this device was developed to reduce costs.

In the event that the Mechahybridizer attempts to send a signal once it begins the operation, this device intercepts the signal before it can extend to a sufficient distance. Upon capture, the device self-destructs to fool the system that the signal has been received and a unit has been dispatched.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> If there are no intact fuses around a mechahybridizer, a mechanoid raid will arrive via drop-pod in 6 hours if the mechahybridization process is attempted.
LanguageData.GR_Mechafuse.label: mechafuse
LanguageData.GR_MechahybridAntenna.description: A large broadcasting server that is specifically tuned to maintain the tameness of mechahybrids. Without this server, the self-replicating mechanites within the mechahybrids will eventually no longer be able to register friendlies as their IFF updates and branches, causing them to go manhunter.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> It is important to note that a single antenna can only broadcast to a limited number of mechahybrids, specifically up to 5 of them regardless of their size or complexity. Any additionally mechahybrid present beyond the initial 5 will no longer receive updates and will eventually lose their "tameness". Part of the broadcast will reach nearby settlements and ruins, attracting maddened mechahybrids, so they will appear from the moment you have a mechahybrid antenna in the periodic roaming monstrosities raids.
LanguageData.GR_MechahybridAntenna.label: mechahybrid antenna
LanguageData.GR_Mechahybridizer.description:
A massive factory designed to improve upon genetically perfect animals by adding doses of mechanites in a controlled fashion and influencing the process to ensure the it is a success and there is no rejection of foreign materials. Once the operation begins on a creature designated as "paragon", the factory closes with hermetic seals and broadcasts a recovery signal to the nearest orbital mechanoid hive.

Originally designed to be part of an automated mechanoid factory placed on animal worlds, the intent was to herd animals into the system, weaponize them, and send them off for their deployment. Due to the invasive nature of the mechanites, the creature absolutely had to lack junk genetic data lest the subject perish and waste materials. Later on, the lack of such paragons resulted in it being installed in facilities with genetic modification equipment on hand and external bypasses to reduce the waste material produced from summoning a retrieval team.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> If there are no intact fuses around a mechahybridizer, a mechanoid raid will arrive via drop-pod in 6 hours if the mechahybridization process is attempted.
LanguageData.GR_Mechahybridizer.label: mechahybridizer
LanguageData.GR_FleshGrowth_Building.description: Something went terribly wrong. A huge and weird growth has spawned within the electrowomb, breaking it completely and spreading all around. This growth is a hatchery for small flies that can devour any living creature to the bone. It seems to be sensitive to daylight, but no one knows how far it can spread at night.
LanguageData.GR_FleshGrowth_Building.label: flesh growth
LanguageData.GR_ElectroWomb.description: A relatively small machine designed to simulate the environment of a mother’s womb. Once a Growthcell has been inserted, the embryo can start growing all the way to its adult life stage before being released. Provides limited growing room for specimens.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay notes:</color> This electrowomb is very cheap to build, but will produce a failure if the inserted creature has a body size bigger than 1.
LanguageData.GR_ElectroWomb.label: electrowomb
LanguageData.GR_GenePod.description:
A machine where the miracle - or horror - of birth happens using automated procedures, a little genetic hand-waving and a great heaping dose of lost sleep. The inserted Genoframe is influenced by two genomes, one dominant and one recessive, and can be modified with optional boosters.

The process isn’t perfect, due to material contamination, missing chromosomes or simple bad luck, and can result in something completely unwanted being developed. For the machine, it makes no difference whether it’s trying to grow an animal or a comically large fruit. The resulting growth cell has a limited lifespan as the embryonic fluid will start breaking down if not allowed to gestate properly.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay notes:</color> If the process to deliver ingredients to the genomorpher is interrupted, the ingredients that have already been inserted in it will deteriorate and be lost.
LanguageData.GR_GenePod.label: genomorpher
LanguageData.GR_GeneRecombinator.description: A specialized extraction module that is equipped to extract the genomes from a subject’s corpse. The material is converted into a soupish-mixture which is then condensed into an extremely complex genome. Completely useless by itself, but functions as a template which can be compared to another genome. The procedure removes any excess genetic information and renders them completely identical.\n\nBear in mind that this machine cannot duplicate some genomes, such as colossal.
LanguageData.GR_GeneRecombinator.label: genome recombinator
LanguageData.GR_GeneticExtractionTable.description: A specialized extraction module that is equipped to extract the genomes from a subject’s corpse. The material is converted into a soupish-mixture which is then condensed into the relevant genome. Processing speed is influenced by intelligence, as it relies on knowing which button to press, when to press it and where to find it under the intimidating number of submenus.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> The genome extractor table will only work for tier 1 genomes. For tier 2 (insectoids, reptiles, equines) and tier 3 (colossals) you will need genome excavators, built on the genetics tinkering table or found on abandoned labs.
LanguageData.GR_GeneticExtractionTable.label: genome extractor table
LanguageData.GR_GeneticsTinkeringTable.description: An advanced and specialized workbench capable of manufacturing medical-grade implants and mixing genetic boosters specifically tuned to Genomorphers. These include but aren’t limited to boosters that improve the safety or stability of hybrids and even micro-controllers that enable direct user-input.
LanguageData.GR_GeneticsTinkeringTable.label: genetics tinkering table
LanguageData.GR_LargeElectroWomb.description: A larger machine designed to simulate the environment of a mother’s womb. Once a Growthcell has been inserted, the embryo can start growing all the way to its adult life stage before being released. Provides more growing room for larger specimens.
LanguageData.GR_LargeElectroWomb.label: large electrowomb
LanguageData.GR_NutrientVat.description: A slurry of liquefied organic pulp fills this reinforced glass vat. This solution is used by genetic experiments, and increases work speed when placed near a Vanilla Genetics Expanded workbench. One workbench can use to up to two nutrient vats.
LanguageData.GR_NutrientVat.label: nutrient vat
LanguageData.GR_TissueGrinder.description:
A workplace accident waiting to happen, the Tissue Grinder takes the corpses of animals and carefully rips and tears them to pieces in order to extract useful genetic data in the form of Genoframes. The hardware is also capable of combining two identical frames into a superior form by comparing and improving upon latent and inferior genetic material.

Workspeed is influenced by someone who actually knows what they’re looking for in order to skip some of the more routine tasks, and possibly shouldn’t be placed into the hands of someone with no concept of ethics, morality or conscience.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay notes:</color> Bear in mind that the cost in corpses effectively doubles for each quality level, making a legendary genoframe prohibitive. The main usefulness of these recipes is to convert two genoframes of a given quality to a higher one, but your main source of genoframes shouldn't be hand crafting them. Go out there and explore abandoned labs!
LanguageData.GR_TissueGrinder.label: tissue grinder
LanguageData.GR_TissueGrowingVat.description: This particular machine can be explained as a simplified and miniaturized version of a Genomorpher, using only a single genome which influences an inserted Genoframe. Instead of trying to create an entire animal, it instead creates very specific limbs or organs. Due to the vastly simplified process and minimal variables, no complications arise during the process and the resulting product is similarly influenced by the quality of the Genoframe.\n\nThe Implant Augmentator can be used to create implants for animals - not just hybrids - with the quality of Genoframe dictating the deficits or bonuses those implants provide. Not all implants are created equal, some being inferior replacements and others providing new capabilities provided the animal in question can support them. Human implants are also a possibility.
LanguageData.GR_TissueGrowingVat.label: implant augmentator
LanguageData.GR_TrapIED_Boom.description: An improvised explosive trap built out of... best if you don't think about it. Cannot be placed adjacent to other traps. Animals can sense these when calm. Less potent than regular high-explosive shell traps.
LanguageData.GR_TrapIED_Boom.label: boom-pustule trap
LanguageData.GR_LabDoor.description: Divides rooms. Simple doors must be manually opened, which slows people down. The amount of slowdown depends on what the door is made of.
LanguageData.GR_LabDoor.label: lab door
LanguageData.GR_MechLabWall.description: An impassable wall. Capable of holding up a roof.
LanguageData.GR_MechLabWall.label: mech laboratory wall
LanguageData.GR_RustedWallA.description: An impassable wall. Capable of holding up a roof.
LanguageData.GR_RustedWallA.label: rusted laboratory wall
LanguageData.GR_RustedWallB.description: An impassable wall. Capable of holding up a roof.
LanguageData.GR_RustedWallB.label: rusted laboratory wall
LanguageData.GR_FleshFliesGas.label: flesh flies
LanguageData.GR_ElectroEgg.description: This diminutive egg seems to be metallic in nature at first, yet it is soft enough to crush it between two fingers. Once thus crushed it can be swallowed normally, and the active mechanites inside have a reinvigorating effect on the consumer. This effect can be slightly addictive, though.
LanguageData.GR_ElectroEgg.label: mechanoid egg
LanguageData.GR_FeatherdustJoint.description: Chickenbear feathers can be gathered and processed into a fine powder, which produces a mild euphoric sensation when burned and inhaled. Featherdust use can produce a dependency.
LanguageData.GR_FeatherdustJoint.label: featherdust joint
LanguageData.GR_Filth_Fuel.label: chemfuel puddle
LanguageData.GR_FilthMucus.label: mucus
LanguageData.GR_Gas_Dust.label: dust
LanguageData.GR_Gas_Ice.label: ice crystals
LanguageData.GR_Hairballs.label: hairballs
LanguageData.GR_InsectCloudMotes.label: insect clouds
LanguageData.GR_Poison_Cloud.label: poison cloud
LanguageData.GR_Poison_Gas.label: poison gas
LanguageData.GR_Insanity_Cloud.label: insanity cloud
LanguageData.RB_RandomGeneticResearch.label: bee research (unfinished)
LanguageData.RB_Bee_Cattle_Drone.label: cattle drone
LanguageData.RB_Bee_Cattle_Queen.label: cattle queen
LanguageData.RB_Bee_Clucking_Drone.label: clucking drone
LanguageData.RB_Bee_Clucking_Queen.label: clucking queen
LanguageData.RB_Bee_Exploding_Drone.label: exploding drone
LanguageData.RB_Bee_Exploding_Queen.label: exploding queen
LanguageData.RB_Bee_Horned_Drone.label: horned drone
LanguageData.RB_Bee_Horned_Queen.label: horned queen
LanguageData.RB_Bee_Hound_Drone.label: hound drone
LanguageData.RB_Bee_Hound_Queen.label: hound queen
LanguageData.RB_Bee_Hybernating_Drone.label: hybernating drone
LanguageData.RB_Bee_Hybernating_Queen.label: hybernating queen
LanguageData.RB_Bee_Megaspidery_Drone.label: megaspidery drone
LanguageData.RB_Bee_Megaspidery_Queen.label: megaspidery queen
LanguageData.RB_Bee_Mousy_Drone.label: mousy drone
LanguageData.RB_Bee_Mousy_Queen.label: mousy queen
LanguageData.RB_Bee_Organic_Drone.label: organic drone
LanguageData.RB_Bee_Organic_Queen.label: organic queen
LanguageData.RB_Bee_Purring_Drone.label: purring drone
LanguageData.RB_Bee_Purring_Queen.label: purring queen
LanguageData.RB_Bee_Scaly_Drone.label: scaly drone
LanguageData.RB_Bee_Scaly_Queen.label: scaly queen
LanguageData.GR_BearLeg.description: A prosthetic animal leg strengthened with bear muscles, and ending in sharp bear claws that add a powerful melee attack.
LanguageData.GR_BearLeg.label: animal implant: bear leg
LanguageData.GR_BearLiver.description: An implant that uses bear genes to improve function of the liver. Specially useful for those alcoholic chickens.
LanguageData.GR_BearLiver.label: animal implant: bear liver
LanguageData.GR_BoomalopeHeart.description: An implant that uses chemfuel to pump blood through the animal's body. This implant is intended for creatures whose heart is failing catastrophically, since it is less efficient than a natural heart.
LanguageData.GR_BoomalopeHeart.label: animal implant: boomalope heart
LanguageData.GR_BoomalopeStomach.description: An implant that uses boomalope genes to improve function of the stomach.
LanguageData.GR_BoomalopeStomach.label: animal implant: boomalope stomach
LanguageData.GR_CanidEye.description: An eye implant that uses genetic splicing with canid genes to give an old (or damaged) eye decent vision. This implant is not meant to replace healthy eyes.
LanguageData.GR_CanidEye.label: animal implant: canid eye
LanguageData.GR_CatTail.description: A tail implant that uses genetic splicing with feline genes to substitute a broken or missing tail. This implant is not meant to replace a healthy tail. Note that only creatures with a natural tail can use this.
LanguageData.GR_CatTail.label: animal implant: cat tail
LanguageData.GR_ChickenBrains.description: An implant that uses chicken brain tissue to patch damaged neurological pathways. This implant is intended for brain-dead creatures.
LanguageData.GR_ChickenBrains.label: animal implant: chicken brains
LanguageData.GR_ChickenKidney.description: An implant that uses chicken genes to improve function of the kidney. Birds have large kidneys. Yep, you learned that today.
LanguageData.GR_ChickenKidney.label: animal implant: chicken kidney
LanguageData.GR_ChitinousHeart.description: A bionic heart implant using a reinforced shell of insectoid chitin for protection. This is a heart designed to take a beating. Pun intended, but it's the last one, I promise.
LanguageData.GR_ChitinousHeart.label: animal implant: chitinous heart
LanguageData.GR_CrocodileSpine.description: A bionic implant adding a sturdy crocodile spine to the creature's skeletal system.
LanguageData.GR_CrocodileSpine.label: animal implant: crocodile spine
LanguageData.GR_InsectileEye.description: A bionic implant which gives the creature compound insectile eyes. Compared with simple eyes, compound eyes possess a very large view angle, and can detect fast movement.
LanguageData.GR_InsectileEye.label: animal implant: insectile eye
LanguageData.GR_InsectMandibles.description: A bionic implant adding insect mandibles to the creature's face. Insect mandibles improve manipulation and eating, while also working as an offensive weapon
LanguageData.GR_InsectMandibles.label: animal implant: insect mandibles
LanguageData.GR_InsectoidPheromones.description: An implant that uses genetic splicing with insectoid genes to give the ability to detect pheromones, thus improving the olfactory system. Can't be installed on birds and (ironically) insects.
LanguageData.GR_InsectoidPheromones.label: animal implant: pheromone receivers
LanguageData.GR_LizardLeg.description: A bionic animal leg reinforced with tough lizard skin.
LanguageData.GR_LizardLeg.label: animal implant: lizard leg
LanguageData.GR_MechaHeart.description: A bionic heart strengthened by the action of active mechanites.
LanguageData.GR_MechaHeart.label: animal implant: reinforced bionic heart
LanguageData.GR_MechaReceptors.description: This elephant auditory receptor can scan a really wide band of frequencies.
LanguageData.GR_MechaReceptors.label: animal implant: elephant auditory receptor
LanguageData.GR_MechaSpine.description: A bionic implant adding a plasteel structure with active mechanites to the creature's skeletal system.
LanguageData.GR_MechaSpine.label: animal implant: reinforced bionic spine
LanguageData.GR_MechaTail.description: An advanced animal tail, even more powerful than a natural one. Note that only creatures with a natural tail can use this.
LanguageData.GR_MechaTail.label: animal implant: reinforced tail
LanguageData.GR_MiniatureThrumboHorn.description: A bionic implant adding a small thrumbo horn where the creature's nose used to be. This implant acts as a nose, allowing the creature to breathe and smell, and can be used by the creature in melee. Birds, insects, and creatures that already have a thrumbo horn don't have enough space on their skull to install this implant.
LanguageData.GR_MiniatureThrumboHorn.label: animal implant: miniature thrumbo horn
LanguageData.GR_MonitorLizardBrain.description: A bionic implant using monitor lizard brain tissue. This highly intelligent reptile has very quick reflexes.
LanguageData.GR_MonitorLizardBrain.label: animal implant: monitor lizard brain
LanguageData.GR_MothTympanalOrgan.description: A bionic implant adding a moth tympanal organ to the creature's ear. Moths can hear sounds of a higher frequency than any bat can produce.
LanguageData.GR_MothTympanalOrgan.label: animal implant: moth tympanal organ
LanguageData.GR_MuffaloLung.description: An implant that uses muffalo genes to increase breathing capacity.
LanguageData.GR_MuffaloLung.label: animal implant: muffalo lung
LanguageData.GR_MuffaloSpine.description: A spine implant that uses genetic splicing with muffalo genes to give an old (or damaged) spine decent functioning. This implant is not meant to replace healthy spines.
LanguageData.GR_MuffaloSpine.label: animal implant: muffalo spine
LanguageData.GR_RodentEar.description: An ear implant that uses genetic splicing with rodent genes to give an old (or damaged) ear decent hearing. This implant is not meant to replace healthy ears.
LanguageData.GR_RodentEar.label: animal implant: rodent ear
LanguageData.GR_RodentNose.description: An implant that uses genetic splicing with rodent genes to give an old (or damaged) nose decent smelling. This implant is not meant to replace healthy noses. Can't be installed on birds and insects.
LanguageData.GR_RodentNose.label: animal implant: rodent nose
LanguageData.GR_ScytherTail.description: A scyther tail created by attaching a jury-rigged scyther blade to an animal's tail. Note that only creatures with a natural tail can use this.
LanguageData.GR_ScytherTail.label: animal implant: scyther tail
LanguageData.GR_ThrumboBrain.description: A bionic brain implant using thrumbo neural tissue. Superb intelligence and reflexes.
LanguageData.GR_ThrumboBrain.label: animal implant: bionic thrumbo brain
LanguageData.GR_ThrumboEye.description: A bionic eye reinforced with specialized photoreceptors from a powerful thrumbo.
LanguageData.GR_ThrumboEye.label: animal implant: bionic thrumbo eye
LanguageData.GR_ThrumboJaws.description: A bionic implant adding thrumbo jaws to the creature's mouth. These creatures devour trees, so you can imagine their jaws are up to the task.
LanguageData.GR_ThrumboJaws.label: animal implant: bionic thrumbo jaws
LanguageData.GR_ThrumboLeg.description: A bionic animal leg reinforced with muscle tissue from a powerful elephant.
LanguageData.GR_ThrumboLeg.label: animal implant: elephant leg
LanguageData.GR_WolfJaws.description: An implant that uses genetic splicing with wolf genes to give an old (or damaged) jaw decent functioning. This implant is not meant to replace healthy jaws.
LanguageData.GR_WolfJaws.label: animal implant: wolf fangs
LanguageData.GR_BearClaws.description: An improvement over the bionic Power Claw, these claws have been vat-grown with a mixture of human and bear DNA, and replace the subject's hand with versatile, non-retractable claws.
LanguageData.GR_BearClaws.label: human implant: bear claws
LanguageData.GR_BearMuscleTissue.description: Grafts of bear muscle tissue on the subject's torso increase overall strength and stamina. These grafts however tend to stimulate the production of an abundance of body hair.
LanguageData.GR_BearMuscleTissue.label: human implant: bear muscle tissue
LanguageData.GR_BoomalopeNodules.description: These skin nodules can be integrated in a human's torso, causing a huge explosion if the subjects dies violently.
LanguageData.GR_BoomalopeNodules.label: human implant: boomalope nodules
LanguageData.GR_BunnyEars.description: This huge leporid ear gives amazing enhanced hearing. Also, super kawaii!
LanguageData.GR_BunnyEars.label: human implant: bunny ear
LanguageData.GR_CatEye.description: This hybrid humanoid / feline eye provides improved night vision, at the expense of a certain short-sightedness outside and during the day. Ideal for nyctophile colonists.
LanguageData.GR_CatEye.label: human implant: cat eye
LanguageData.GR_ChemfuelHeart.description: A reinforced bionic heart that acts like a miniature 4-stroke internal combustion engine powered by chemfuel to pump blood through the body.
LanguageData.GR_ChemfuelHeart.label: human implant: chemfuel heart
LanguageData.GR_ChemfuelStomach.description: A reinforced bionic stomach that uses a combination of gastric acid and chemfuel to efficiently digest food.
LanguageData.GR_ChemfuelStomach.label: human implant: chemfuel stomach
LanguageData.GR_CompoundEye.description: This hybrid humanoid / insectoid eye provides improved vision in a wide range of different wavelengths, including those usually outside the human range, completely modifying the way the subject perceives their surroundings.
LanguageData.GR_CompoundEye.label: human implant: compound eye
LanguageData.GR_DevouringJaws.description: A set of improved teeth designed to rapidly chew and ingest any kind of food. Leaves kind of a mess, though...
LanguageData.GR_DevouringJaws.label: human implant: devouring jaws
LanguageData.GR_DiggingBearClaws.description: Grotesque but powerful hand blades that have been modified to be used in mining operations, increasing both speed and yield.
LanguageData.GR_DiggingBearClaws.label: human implant: digging mole claws
LanguageData.GR_HibernationModule.description: A biochip that modifies the brain to reduce the need for sleep.
LanguageData.GR_HibernationModule.label: human implant: hibernation module
LanguageData.GR_HumanThrumboHorn.description: A genetically modified thrumbo horn that can be implanted in a subject's forehead. Nasty.
LanguageData.GR_HumanThrumboHorn.label: human implant: thrumbo horn
LanguageData.GR_IguanaScales.description: A synthetic skin substitute that is much thicker and a much better insulator than human skin. This provides a measure of defense but, more importantly, makes the subject more resistant to high temperatures.
LanguageData.GR_IguanaScales.label: human implant: iguana scales
LanguageData.GR_InsectoidAntennae.description: These hybrid humanoid / insectoid feeling appendages can be implanted in a subject's head, completely modifying the way the subject "sees" and feels the surrounding world. The experience may be slightly overwhelming due to sensorial overload.
LanguageData.GR_InsectoidAntennae.label: human implant: insectoid antennae
LanguageData.GR_KidneyToxicFilters.description: This is a bioelectronic kidney prosthesis that greatly improves the efficiency of the subject's kidney by using insectoid tissue, providing resistance to toxic substances.
LanguageData.GR_KidneyToxicFilters.label: human implant: kidney toxic filters
LanguageData.GR_MoleratNerveDampener.description: A biochip that reduces pain sensitivity, allowing the subject to survive pain shock.
LanguageData.GR_MoleratNerveDampener.label: human implant: mole-rat nerve dampener
LanguageData.GR_MousePheromones.description: A bionic vomeronasal organ that allows the subject pheromonal communication with other animals, increasing the chance of taming and training them successfully.
LanguageData.GR_MousePheromones.label: human implant: mouse pheromones
LanguageData.GR_MuffaloMammaries.description: By implanting muffalo mammaries into a colonist... Wait, what??? Who came up with this idea????? Anyway... this implant allows your colonist to be milked... What the...
LanguageData.GR_MuffaloMammaries.label: human implant: muffalo mammaries
LanguageData.GR_MuffaloMuscles.description: By weaving muffalo muscle strands with the subjects muscular system, strength, resilience and carrying capacity are greatly increased.
LanguageData.GR_MuffaloMuscles.label: human implant: muffalo strengthened muscles
LanguageData.GR_MuffaloSkin.description: A synthetic skin substitute that is much thicker and a much better insulator than human skin. This provides a measure of defense but, more importantly, makes the subject more resistant to low temperatures.
LanguageData.GR_MuffaloSkin.label: human implant: muffalo skin
LanguageData.GR_NeuronReinforcement.description: A biochip using animal stem cells to reinforce the natural abilities of human neurons, increasing learning and research speed.
LanguageData.GR_NeuronReinforcement.label: human implant: neuron reinforcement
LanguageData.GR_Ovipositor.description: You know you have gone too far in your scientific experiments when you are implanting a human being with an ovipositor. This will enable the colonist to lay chicken eggs twice a day. It hurts as hell, though. Also, yuck.
LanguageData.GR_Ovipositor.label: human implant: ovipositor
LanguageData.GR_PsychicDampeners.description: Artificial hearing aids that block psychic emanations, providing considerable resistance to mechanoids' psychic attacks.
LanguageData.GR_PsychicDampeners.label: human implant: psychic dampeners
LanguageData.GR_ThrumboSkin.description: A synthetic skin substitute that is much thicker and a much better insulator than human skin. This provides a lot of defense and makes the subject more resistant to extremes of temperature.
LanguageData.GR_ThrumboSkin.label: human implant: thrumbo skin
LanguageData.GR_VenomousFangs.description: This hybrid implant substitutes the subject's jaw with a set of venomous fangs. Useful in combat, but not very comfortable.
LanguageData.GR_VenomousFangs.label: human implant: venomous fangs
LanguageData.GR_WolfMuscularFibers.description: The subjects legs can be modified by weaving hybrid human/wolf muscle fibers within the base tissue, giving them a significant strength and speed boost.
LanguageData.GR_WolfMuscularFibers.label: human implant: wolf muscular fibers
LanguageData.RB_Cattle_Honeycomb.description: This is a honeycomb produced by cattle bees. Cattle bees have muffalo genes spliced in their genome. They are covered in a thick white pelt, and produce milk, wool and muffalo genetic material. Honeycombs can be procesed in a Honey Extractor to get milk, wool and muffalo genetic material.
LanguageData.RB_Cattle_Honeycomb.label: cattle honeycomb
LanguageData.RB_Clucking_Honeycomb.description: This is a honeycomb produced by clucking bees. Clucking bees have chicken genes spliced in their genome. They are covered in feathers, and produce eggs and avian genetic material. Honeycombs can be procesed in a Honey Extractor to get bee eggs, genewax and avian genetic material.
LanguageData.RB_Clucking_Honeycomb.label: clucking honeycomb
LanguageData.RB_Exploding_Honeycomb.description: This is a honeycomb produced by exploding bees. Exploding bees have boomalope genes spliced in their genome. This bees produce genetic honey, chemfuel, and boom genetic material. Honeycombs can be procesed in a Honey Extractor to get genetic honey, chemfuel, and booma genetic material.
LanguageData.RB_Exploding_Honeycomb.label: exploding honeycomb
LanguageData.RB_Horned_Honeycomb.description: This is a honeycomb produced by horned bees. Horned bees have thrumbo genes spliced in their genome. They take a very long time to generate honeycombs, but they produce highly sought after colossal genetic material. Honeycombs can be procesed in a Honey Extractor to get genetic honey, genewax, and colossal genetic material.
LanguageData.RB_Horned_Honeycomb.label: horned honeycomb
LanguageData.RB_Hound_Honeycomb.description: This is a honeycomb produced by hound bees. Hound bees have wolf genes spliced in their genome. They produce canine genetic material. Honeycombs can be procesed in a Honey Extractor to get genetic honey, genewax and canine genetic material.
LanguageData.RB_Hound_Honeycomb.label: hound honeycomb
LanguageData.RB_Hybernating_Honeycomb.description: This is a honeycomb produced by hybernating bees. Hybernating bees have grizzly bear genes spliced in their genome. They are covered in a soft fur. Honeycombs can be procesed in a Honey Extractor to get genetic honey, genewax and bear genetic material.
LanguageData.RB_Hybernating_Honeycomb.label: hybernating honeycomb
LanguageData.RB_Megaspidery_Honeycomb.description: This is a honeycomb produced by megaspidery bees. Megaspidery bees have insectoid genes spliced in their genome. They are covered in chitinous plates and produce insect jelly and insectoid genetic materials. Honeycombs can be procesed in a Honey Extractor to get insect jelly, genewax and insectoid genetic material.
LanguageData.RB_Megaspidery_Honeycomb.label: megaspidery honeycomb
LanguageData.RB_Mousy_Honeycomb.description: This is a honeycomb produced by mousy bees. Mousy bees have rodent genes spliced in their genome. They produce rodent genetic materials and many find their constant high-pitched squeaking sounds to be quite distracting. Honeycombs can be procesed in a Honey Extractor to get genetic honey, genewax and rodent genetic material.
LanguageData.RB_Mousy_Honeycomb.label: mousy honeycomb
LanguageData.RB_Organic_Honeycomb.description: This is a honeycomb produced by organic bees. Organic bees are genetically modified bees that extract cellulose fibers from nearby plants and convert it into a slurry of genetic material which can be refined into template genomes. The main use of these bees, however, is to hybridize them with other bees to create genetic bees of different subspecies. Honeycombs can be procesed in a Honey Extractor to get honey, beeswax and template genomes.
LanguageData.RB_Organic_Honeycomb.label: organic honeycomb
LanguageData.RB_Purring_Honeycomb.description: This is a honeycomb produced by purring bees. Purring bees have cat genes spliced in their genome. They produce feline genetic materials and a lot of videos on the internet. Honeycombs can be procesed in a Honey Extractor to get genetic honey, genewax and feline genetic material.
LanguageData.RB_Purring_Honeycomb.label: purring honeycomb
LanguageData.RB_Scaly_Honeycomb.description: TScaly bees have reptile genes spliced in their genome. They are covered in tough looking scales and produce reptilian genetic materials. Honeycombs can be procesed in a Honey Extractor to get genetic honey, genewax and reptile genetic material.
LanguageData.RB_Scaly_Honeycomb.label: scaly honeycomb
LanguageData.RB_GeneticHoney.description: This honey has been extracted from any genetic honeycomb, and has a very strange greenish tone that makes it less appetizing. Decays incredibly fast, but it can be consumed as a potent physically enhancing drug that doesn't cause any side effects. This honey can be consumed raw or cooked with other ingredients, and its properties transfer to the resulting meal.
LanguageData.RB_GeneticHoney.label: genetic honey
LanguageData.GR_CompressedChemfuel.description: A very volatile sludge with a tar-like appearance. Can be used defensively or converted to regular chemfuel in a refinery.
LanguageData.GR_CompressedChemfuel.label: deepchem
LanguageData.GR_EggBomb.description: This huge egg is filled with unstable chemicals. It is going to explode!
LanguageData.GR_EggBomb.label: detonating unstable egg
LanguageData.GR_BoosterController.description: Self-assembling machinery that fuses with the specimen’s nerve centers during the growth process. Effectively allows hybrids to be drafted but increases the chance of failure due to introduction of invasive foreign material.
LanguageData.GR_BoosterController.label: controller
LanguageData.GR_BoosterFertility.description: Compressed medicine that allows hybrids to develop functional sexual organs. Effectively allows hybrids to reproduce with the exact same species.\n\nNote: Insectoid hybrids are always incapable of reproducing.
LanguageData.GR_BoosterFertility.label: fertility unblocker
LanguageData.GR_BoosterHarmono.description: Capsules containing genetic stabilizers that help maintain the dominant-recessive genome relationship. Effectively reduces the chance of the hybrid having the opposite intended result.
LanguageData.GR_BoosterHarmono.label: harmonobooster
LanguageData.GR_BoosterHarmonoGreater.description: Capsules containing genetic stabilizers that help maintain the dominant-recessive genome relationship. Greatly reduces the chance of the hybrid having the opposite intended result, at the expense of more processing time.\n\nThis booster can't be crafted, only scavenged.
LanguageData.GR_BoosterHarmonoGreater.label: improved harmonobooster
LanguageData.GR_BoosterImmuno.description: Capsules containing genetic restorative medicine that helps prevent genomes from breaking down prematurely. Effectively helps mitigate the possibility of the hybrid ending in complete failure.
LanguageData.GR_BoosterImmuno.label: immunobooster
LanguageData.GR_BoosterImmunoGreater.description: Capsules containing genetic restorative medicine that helps prevent genomes from breaking down prematurely. Greatly helps mitigate the possibility of the hybrid ending in complete failure, at the expense of more processing time.\n\nThis booster can't be crafted, only scavenged.
LanguageData.GR_BoosterImmunoGreater.label: improved immunobooster
LanguageData.GR_BoosterStabilizator.description: Compressed medicine that slows the growth of the specimen, increasing both stability and safety. Effectively increases growth time by 50% in exchange for a vastly increased chance of obtaining the intended result.
LanguageData.GR_BoosterStabilizator.label: stabilizator
LanguageData.GR_BoosterTempo.description: Capsules containing growth hormones that help speed up the hybrid’s growth process, halving the time it takes to complete.
LanguageData.GR_BoosterTempo.label: tempobooster
LanguageData.GR_ImprovedController.description: Self-assembling machinery that fuses with the specimen’s nerve centers during the growth process. Effectively allows hybrids to be drafted.\n\nThis booster can't be crafted, only scavenged.
LanguageData.GR_ImprovedController.label: improved controller
LanguageData.GR_MechaniteBooster.description: This highly sought-after booster will result in an almost perfect hybrid: fertile, draftable and genetically stable.\n\nThis booster can't be crafted, only scavenged.
