LanguageData.GR_MechaniteBooster.label: mechanite booster
LanguageData.GR_StabilizedFertilityUnblocker.descript:
Compressed medicine that allows hybrids to develop functional sexual organs. Effectively allows hybrids to reproduce with the exact same species.

This booster can't be crafted, only scavenged.

Note: Insectoid hybrids are always incapable of reproducing.
LanguageData.GR_StabilizedFertilityUnblocker.label: stabilized fertility unblocker
LanguageData.GR_BearBugEggFertilized.description: Fertilized bear bug egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. Actually, it's a beetle egg, it is disgusting either way... If all goes well, it should hatch into a bear bug.
LanguageData.GR_BearBugEggFertilized.label: bear bug egg (fert.)
LanguageData.GR_BearBugEggUnfertilized.description: Unfertilized bear bug egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. Actually, it's a beetle egg, it is disgusting either way...
LanguageData.GR_BearBugEggUnfertilized.label: bear bug egg (unfert.)
LanguageData.GR_EggMeadowLizardFertilized.description: Fertilized meadow lizard egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into a baby meadow lizard. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
LanguageData.GR_EggMeadowLizardFertilized.label: meadow lizard egg (fert.)
LanguageData.GR_EggNighthrumboFertilized.description: Fertilized nighthrumbo egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into a baby nighthrumbo. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
LanguageData.GR_EggNighthrumboFertilized.label: nighthrumbo egg (fert.)
LanguageData.GR_NeedlechickenEggFertilized.descriptio: A fertilized needlechicken egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into a needlechick. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
LanguageData.GR_NeedlechickenEggFertilized.label: needlechicken egg (fert.)
LanguageData.GR_NeedlechickenEggUnfertilized.descript: An unfertilized needlechicken egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
LanguageData.GR_NeedlechickenEggUnfertilized.label: needlechicken egg (unfert.)
LanguageData.GR_GenoframeAwful.description: A stable genetic material consumed by the genomorpher machine that serves as a framework for hybrid experimentation. After the framework is used to create a growthcell, the genoframe is empty and discarded.\n\nAwful genoframe will result in a hybrid with greatly decreased stats over a normal hybrid.
LanguageData.GR_GenoframeAwful.label: genoframe (awful)
LanguageData.GR_GenoframeExcellent.description: A stable genetic material consumed by the genomorpher machine that serves as a framework for hybrid experimentation. After the framework is used to create a growthcell, the genoframe is empty and discarded.\n\nExcellent genoframe will result in a hybrid with significantly improved stats over a normal hybrid.
LanguageData.GR_GenoframeExcellent.label: genoframe (excellent)
LanguageData.GR_GenoframeGood.description: A stable genetic material consumed by the genomorpher machine that serves as a framework for hybrid experimentation. After the framework is used to create a growthcell, the genoframe is empty and discarded.\n\nGood genoframe will result in a hybrid with slightly improved stats over a normal hybrid.
LanguageData.GR_GenoframeGood.label: genoframe (good)
LanguageData.GR_GenoframeLegendary.description: A stable genetic material consumed by the genomorpher machine that serves as a framework for hybrid experimentation. After the framework is used to create a growthcell, the genoframe is empty and discarded.\n\nLegendary genoframe will result in an incredibly powerful hybrid that’s eclipsing normal creatures.
LanguageData.GR_GenoframeLegendary.label: genoframe (legendary)
LanguageData.GR_GenoframeMasterwork.description: A stable genetic material consumed by the genomorpher machine that serves as a framework for hybrid experimentation. After the framework is used to create a growthcell, the genoframe is empty and discarded.\n\nMasterwork genoframe will result in a hybrid with superior stats over a normal hybrid.
LanguageData.GR_GenoframeMasterwork.label: genoframe (masterwork)
LanguageData.GR_GenoframeNormal.description: A stable genetic material consumed by the genomorpher machine that serves as a framework for hybrid experimentation. After the framework is used to create a growthcell, the genoframe is empty and discarded.\n\nNormal genoframe will result in a hybrid with baseline stats.
LanguageData.GR_GenoframeNormal.label: genoframe (normal)
LanguageData.GR_GenoframePoor.description: A stable genetic material consumed by the genomorpher machine that serves as a framework for hybrid experimentation. After the framework is used to create a growthcell, the genoframe is empty and discarded.\n\nPoor genoframe will result in a hybrid with slightly decreased stats over a normal hybrid.
LanguageData.GR_GenoframePoor.label: genoframe (poor)
LanguageData.GR_GenoframeUnfinished.description: An unfinished genoframe of some kind.
LanguageData.GR_GenoframeUnfinished.label: unfinished genoframe
LanguageData.GR_ArchotechGenomeExcavator.description:
An advanced genome extraction device that is capable of self-arming and self-guiding at the touch of a button. The best genetic material can be found in the brain and the automated design instantly compresses this material into a usable genome. Each device holds up to three charges before it becomes useless.

The desired subject needs to be docile and trusting of the user who intends on scooping out their brain in a process that is guaranteed to be painless and instantaneous. It is recommended to anesthetize the creature as any sudden movement on their part will interrupt the whole process. Alternatively, it will also work on a dead body... The device is much better at compressing material and as such can be used on even the largest of organisms.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> This excavator can be used to acquire the following genomes:
LanguageData.GR_ArchotechGenomeExcavator.label: archotech genome excavator
LanguageData.GR_GenomeExcavator.description:
A simplified genome extraction device designed to be usable by even the most simple-minded goon, as such it resembles a caulking gun that works in reverse. The best genetic material can be found in the brain and the automated, single-use design instantly compresses this material into a usable genome.

The desired subject needs to be docile and trusting of the user who intends on scooping out their brain in a process that is not-quite instantaneous. It is recommended to anesthetize the creature as any sudden movement on their part will interrupt the whole process. Alternatively, it will also work on a dead body...

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> This excavator can be used to acquire the following genomes:
LanguageData.GR_GenomeExcavator.label: genome excavator
LanguageData.GR_AlphaGenomeExcavator.description:
A simplified genome extraction device designed to be usable by even the most simple-minded goon, as such it resembles a caulking gun that works in reverse. The best genetic material can be found in the brain and the automated, single-use design instantly compresses this material into a usable genome.

The desired subject needs to be docile and trusting of the user who intends on scooping out their brain in a process that is not-quite instantaneous. It is recommended to anesthetize the creature as any sudden movement on their part will interrupt the whole process. Alternatively, it will also work on a dead body...

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> This excavator can be used to acquire the following genomes:
LanguageData.GR_AlphaGenomeExcavator.label: alpha genome excavator

A simplified genome extraction device designed to be usable by even the most simple-minded goon, as such it resembles a caulking gun that works in reverse. The best genetic material can be found in the brain and the automated, single-use design instantly compresses this material into a usable genome.

The desired subject needs to be docile and trusting of the user who intends on scooping out their brain in a process that is not-quite instantaneous. It is recommended to anesthetize the creature as any sudden movement on their part will interrupt the whole process. Alternatively, it will also work on a dead body...

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> This excavator can be used to acquire the following genomes:
LanguageData.GR_EldritchExcavator.label: eldritch genome excavator

The best genetic material can be found in the brain and the automated, single-use design instantly compresses this material into a usable genome.

The desired subject needs to be docile and trusting of the user who intends on scooping out their brain in a process that is not-quite instantaneous. It is recommended to anesthetize the creature as any sudden movement on their part will interrupt the whole process. Alternatively, it will also work on a dead body...

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> This excavator can be used to acquire the following genomes:
LanguageData.GR_DinoGenomeExcavator.label: dino genome excavator
LanguageData.GR_MegafaunaGenomeExcavator.description:
A simplified genome extraction device designed to be usable by even the most simple-minded goon, as such it resembles a caulking gun that works in reverse. The best genetic material can be found in the brain and the automated, single-use design instantly compresses this material into a usable genome.

The desired subject needs to be docile and trusting of the user who intends on scooping out their brain in a process that is not-quite instantaneous. It is recommended to anesthetize the creature as any sudden movement on their part will interrupt the whole process. Alternatively, it will also work on a dead body...

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> This excavator can be used to acquire the following genomes:
LanguageData.GR_MegafaunaGenomeExcavator.label: megafauna genome excavator
LanguageData.GR_AvianGenetic.description:
A payload of avian genetic material. Can be used at the genomorpher to create a growth cell of a hybrid animal. This tier 1 genome can be used as either a primary or secondary genome.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All primary avian hybrids lay eggs. Combat avian hybrids get increased movement speed.

<color=#E5E54C>Extraction:</color> Can be extracted via the genome extractor table or any genome excavator from:
LanguageData.GR_AvianGenetic.label: avian genome
LanguageData.GR_BoomalopeGenetic.description:
A payload of boom genetic material. Can be used at the genomorpher to create a growth cell of a hybrid animal. This tier 1 genome can be used as either a primary or secondary genome.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All boom hybrids explode on death. Farm hybrids produce chemfuel, while combat hybrids can use exploding abilities.

<color=#E5E54C>Extraction:</color> Can be extracted via the genome extractor table or any genome excavator from:
LanguageData.GR_BoomalopeGenetic.label: boom genome
LanguageData.GR_CanineGenetic.description:
A payload of canine genetic material. Can be used at the genomorpher to create a growth cell of a hybrid animal. This tier 1 genome can be used as either a primary or secondary genome.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All primary canine hybrids have the adrenaline rush ability. All canine hybrids have fast attack speed.

<color=#E5E54C>Extraction:</color> Can be extracted via the genome extractor table or any genome excavator from:
LanguageData.GR_CanineGenetic.label: canine genome
LanguageData.GR_EquineGenetic.description:
A payload of equine genetic material. Can be used at the genomorpher to create a growth cell of a hybrid animal. This tier 2 genome can only be used as primary genome.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All equine hybrids have increased caravaning speed.

<color=#E5E54C>Extraction:</color> Can only be extracted via any genome excavator from:
LanguageData.GR_EquineGenetic.label: equine genome
LanguageData.GR_FelineGenetic.description:
A payload of feline genetic material. Can be used at the genomorpher to create a growth cell of a hybrid animal. This tier 1 genome can be used as either a primary or secondary genome.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All feline hybrids have an inherent dodge chance. Combat feline hybrids get a further increase to dodge chance.

<color=#E5E54C>Extraction:</color> Can be extracted via the genome extractor table or any genome excavator from:
LanguageData.GR_FelineGenetic.label: feline genome
LanguageData.GR_HumanoidGenetic.description:
A payload of humanoid genetic material. Can be used at the genomorpher to create a growth cell of a hybrid animal. This tier 1 genome can be used as either a primary or secondary genome.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All humanoid hybrids have a somewhat decent level of intelligence and can do various tasks around the colony.

<color=#E5E54C>Extraction:</color> Can be extracted via the genome extractor table or any genome excavator from any sentient humanoid.
LanguageData.GR_HumanoidGenetic.label: humanoid genome
LanguageData.GR_InsectoidGenetic.description:
A payload of insectoid genetic material. Can be used at the genomorpher to create a growth cell of a hybrid animal. This tier 2 genome can only be used as primary genome.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All hybrids with insectoid genome get 100% toxic resistance as well as an armor increase. Combat hybrids get a further increase to armor.

<color=#E5E54C>Extraction:</color> Can only be extracted via any genome excavator from:
LanguageData.GR_InsectoidGenetic.label: insectoid genome
LanguageData.GR_MuffaloGenetic.description:
A payload of muffalo genetic material. Can be used at the genomorpher to create a growth cell of a hybrid animal. This tier 1 genome can be used as either a primary or secondary genome.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All muffalo hybrid animals are pack animals, with primary muffalo hybrids getting 50% increased capacity caravaning. All muffalo hybrids have an increase to minimum comfortable temperature.

<color=#E5E54C>Extraction:</color> Can be extracted via the genome extractor table or any genome excavator from:
LanguageData.GR_MuffaloGenetic.label: muffalo genome
LanguageData.GR_ReptileGenetic.description:
A payload of reptile genetic material. Can be used at the genomorpher to create a growth cell of a hybrid animal. This tier 2 genome can only be used as primary genome.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All reptile hybrids have greatly increased minimum and maximum comfortable temperature. They also have melee and ranged venomous attacks.

<color=#E5E54C>Extraction:</color> Can only be extracted via any genome excavator from:
LanguageData.GR_ReptileGenetic.label: reptile genome
LanguageData.GR_RodentGenetic.description:
A payload of rodent genetic material. Can be used at the genomorpher to create a growth cell of a hybrid animal. This tier 1 genome can be used as either a primary or secondary genome.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All rodent hybrids have a reduced body size, but greatly increased reproduction rates (if fertile).

<color=#E5E54C>Extraction:</color> Can be extracted via the genome extractor table or any genome excavator from:
LanguageData.GR_RodentGenetic.label: rodent/leporid genome
LanguageData.GR_TemplateGenetic.description: Absolutely and completely worthless, but when properly processed alongside an actual genome it can be trimmed down to an identical copy.
LanguageData.GR_TemplateGenetic.label: template genome
LanguageData.GR_ThrumboGenetic.description:
A payload of colossal genetic material. Can be used at the genomorpher to create a growth cell of a hybrid animal. This tier 3 genome can only be used as primary genome.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Extreme increase in size, health, damage and market value at the cost of slower attack speed.

<color=#E5E54C>Extraction:</color> Can only be extracted via an archotech genome excavator from:
LanguageData.GR_ThrumboGenetic.label: colossal genome
LanguageData.GR_UrsineGenetic.description:
A payload of ursine genetic material. Can be used at the genomorpher to create a growth cell of a hybrid animal. This tier 1 genome can be used as either a primary or secondary genome.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All primary ursine hybrids have stunning attacks, and high body size.

<color=#E5E54C>Extraction:</color> Can be extracted via the genome extractor table or any genome excavator from:
LanguageData.GR_UrsineGenetic.label: ursine genome
LanguageData.GR_AlphaGenetic.description: A payload of alpha animal genetic material. Can be used at the genomorpher to create a growth cell of a hybrid animal. This tier 2 genome can only be used as primary genome.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Extraction:</color> Can be extracted via an alpha genome excavator from:
LanguageData.GR_AlphaGenetic.label: alpha genome
LanguageData.GR_EldritchGenetic.description: A payload of eldritch genetic material. Can be used at the genomorpher to create a growth cell of a hybrid animal. This tier 2 genome can only be used as primary genome.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Extraction:</color> Can be extracted via an eldritch genome excavator from:
LanguageData.GR_EldritchGenetic.label: eldritch genome
LanguageData.GR_DinoGenetic.description: A payload of dinosaur genetic material. Can be used at the genomorpher to create a growth cell of a hybrid animal. This tier 2 genome can only be used as primary genome.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Extraction:</color> Can be extracted via a dinosaur genome excavator from:
LanguageData.GR_DinoGenetic.label: dinosaur genome
LanguageData.GR_MegafaunaGenetic.description: A payload of megafauna genetic material. Can be used at the genomorpher to create a growth cell of a hybrid animal. This tier 2 genome can only be used as primary genome.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Extraction:</color> Can be extracted via a megafauna genome excavator from:
LanguageData.GR_MegafaunaGenetic.label: megafauna genome
LanguageData.GR_ArchoGrowthcell.description: A portable, archotech-made casing designed to hold an embryo until it is transferred to a relevant Electrowomb, similar to fertilized eggs that require incubation. This particular one is completely stable and contains the embryo of an Archocentipede, which can only grow in an Archowomb.\n\nCare should be taken before beginning the process, as the resulting energy signature will force any and all hostiles to attack the colony before the monstrosity can be born.
LanguageData.GR_ArchoGrowthcell.label: archotech growth cell
LanguageData.GR_GrowthCell.description: A portable, human-made casing designed to hold an embryo until it is transferred to a relevant Electrowomb, similar to fertilized eggs that require incubation. The embryonic fluid is unstable due to the unnatural genetic combination, so it will need to be implanted within 7 days after its creation. This growth cell has to be kept at a temperature between 0 and 50 degrees Celsius.
LanguageData.GR_GrowthCell.label: growth cell
LanguageData.GR_BearChickenEggFertilized.description: Fertilized bearchicken egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into one of these abominations. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
LanguageData.GR_BearChickenEggFertilized.label: bearchicken egg (fert.)
LanguageData.GR_BearChickenEggUnfertilized.descriptio: Unfertilized bearchicken egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
LanguageData.GR_BearChickenEggUnfertilized.label: bearchicken egg (unfert.)
LanguageData.GR_BoomachickenEggFertilized.description: Fertilized Boomachicken egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into one of these abominations. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.

LanguageData.GR_ChickenrabbitEggFertilized.descriptio: Fertilized Chickenrabbit egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into one of these abominations. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked. The colonists swear it was coloured like this when the animal laid it.
LanguageData.GR_ChickenrabbitEggFertilized.label: chickenrabbit egg (fert.)
LanguageData.GR_ChickenrabbitEggUnfertilized.descript: Unfertilized Chickenrabbit egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. The colonists swear it was coloured like this when the animal laid it.
LanguageData.GR_ChickenrabbitEggUnfertilized.label: chickenrabbit egg (unfert.)
LanguageData.GR_ChickenspiderEggUnfertilized.descript: Unfertilized Chickenspider egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. Since insectoid hybrids are always infertile, these eggs can never be fertilized.
LanguageData.GR_ChickenspiderEggUnfertilized.label: chickenspider egg (unfert.)
LanguageData.GR_ChickenwolfEggFertilized.description: Fertilized Chickenwolf egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into one of these abominations. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
LanguageData.GR_ChickenwolfEggFertilized.label: chickenwolf egg (fert.)
LanguageData.GR_ChickenwolfEggUnfertilized.descriptio: Unfertilized Chickenwolf egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
LanguageData.GR_ChickenwolfEggUnfertilized.label: chickenwolf egg (unfert.)
LanguageData.GR_ParagonChickenEggFertilized.descripti: Fertilized avian paragon egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. These eggs are pure, pristine, perfect examples of chicken eggs. Immaculate eggness. If all goes well, it should hatch into an avian paragon.
LanguageData.GR_ParagonChickenEggFertilized.label: avian paragon egg (fert.)
LanguageData.GR_ParagonChickenEggUnfertilized.descrip: Unfertilized avian paragon egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. These eggs are pure, pristine, perfect examples of chicken eggs. Immaculate eggness.
LanguageData.GR_ParagonChickenEggUnfertilized.label: avian paragon egg (unfert.)
LanguageData.GR_ParagonIguanaEggFertilized.descriptio: Fertilized reptile paragon egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. If all goes well, it should hatch into a reptile paragon.
LanguageData.GR_ParagonIguanaEggFertilized.label: reptile paragon egg (fert.)

LanguageData.GR_ParagonIguanaEggUnfertilized.label: reptile paragon egg (unfert.)

LanguageData.GR_EldritchChickenEggFertilized.descript: Fertilized chicken of Mi-go egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. The purple shell is incredibly disconcerting. If all goes well, it should hatch into a chicken of Mi-go.
LanguageData.GR_EldritchChickenEggFertilized.label: chicken of mi-go egg (fert.)
LanguageData.GR_EldritchChickenEggUnfertilized.descri: Unfertilized chicken of Mi-go egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. The purple shell is incredibly disconcerting.
LanguageData.GR_EldritchChickenEggUnfertilized.label: chicken of mi-go egg (unfert.)
LanguageData.GR_EldritchReptileEggFertilized.descript: Fertilized snake of Yig egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. The purple shell is incredibly disconcerting. If all goes well, it should hatch into an snake of Yig egg.
LanguageData.GR_EldritchReptileEggFertilized.label: snake of yig egg (fert.)
LanguageData.GR_EldritchReptileEggUnfertilized.descri: Unfertilized snake of Yig egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. The purple shell is incredibly disconcerting.
LanguageData.GR_EldritchReptileEggUnfertilized.label: snake of yig egg (unfert.)
LanguageData.GR_ChickennathusEggFertilized.descriptio: Fertilized chickennathus egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. If all goes well, it should hatch into a chickennathus.
LanguageData.GR_ChickennathusEggFertilized.label: chickennathus egg (fert.)
LanguageData.GR_ChickennathusEggUnfertilized.descript: Unfertilized chickennathus egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.

LanguageData.GR_ParasaurolopeEggFertilized.label: parasaurolope egg (fert.)

LanguageData.GR_RaptortoiseEggFertilized.description: Fertilized raptortoise egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. If all goes well, it should hatch into a raptortoise.
LanguageData.GR_RaptortoiseEggFertilized.label: raptortoise egg (fert.)

LanguageData.GR_SpinobearEggFertilized.description: Fertilized spinobear egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. If all goes well, it should hatch into an spinobear.
LanguageData.GR_SpinobearEggFertilized.label: spinobear egg (fert.)
LanguageData.GR_SpinobearEggUnfertilized.description: Unfertilized spinobear egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
LanguageData.GR_SpinobearEggUnfertilized.label: spinobear egg (unfert.)
LanguageData.GR_StegothrumboEggFertilized.description: Fertilized stegothrumbo egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. This is insanely nutritive! If all goes well, it should hatch into a stegothrumbo.
LanguageData.GR_StegothrumboEggFertilized.label: stegothrumbo egg (fert.)
LanguageData.GR_StegothrumboEggUnfertilized.descripti: Unfertilized stegothrumbo egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. This is insanely nutritive!
LanguageData.GR_StegothrumboEggUnfertilized.label: stegothrumbo egg (unfert.)
LanguageData.GR_TriceraffaloEggFertilized.description: Fertilized triceraffalo egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. If all goes well, it should hatch into a triceraffalo.
LanguageData.GR_TriceraffaloEggFertilized.label: triceraffalo egg (fert.)
LanguageData.GR_TriceraffaloEggUnfertilized.descripti: Unfertilized triceraffalo egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
LanguageData.GR_TriceraffaloEggUnfertilized.label: triceraffalo egg (unfert.)
LanguageData.GR_VelociwolfEggFertilized.description: Fertilized velociwolf egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. If all goes well, it should hatch into a velociwolf.
LanguageData.GR_VelociwolfEggFertilized.label: velociwolf egg (fert.)
LanguageData.GR_VelociwolfEggUnfertilized.description: Unfertilized velociwolf egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
LanguageData.GR_VelociwolfEggUnfertilized.label: velociwolf egg (unfert.)
LanguageData.GR_ChickenlodonEggFertilized.description: Fertilized chickenlodon egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. If all goes well, it should hatch into a chickenlodon.
LanguageData.GR_ChickenlodonEggFertilized.label: chickenlodon egg (fert.)
LanguageData.GR_ChickenlodonEggUnfertilized.descripti: Unfertilized chickenlodon egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
LanguageData.GR_ChickenlodonEggUnfertilized.label: chickenlodon egg (unfert.)
LanguageData.GR_MantistanisEggFertilized.description: Fertilized mantistanis egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. If all goes well, it should hatch into a mantistanis.
LanguageData.GR_MantistanisEggFertilized.label: mantistanis egg (fert.)
LanguageData.GR_MantistanisEggUnfertilized.descriptio: Unfertilized mantistanis egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
LanguageData.GR_MantistanisEggUnfertilized.label: mantistanis egg (unfert.)
LanguageData.RB_BeeEggsActive.description: Fertilized chickenbee egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. If all goes well, it should hatch into a chickenbee.
LanguageData.RB_BeeEggsActive.label: chickenbee egg (fert.)
LanguageData.GR_BearffaloMilk.description: Milk from a bearffalo. Quite high in fat content, and very nutritive.
LanguageData.GR_BearffaloMilk.label: bearffalo milk
LanguageData.GR_ChickenffaloMilk.description: Milk from a chickenffalo. Looks like watered down milk. Probably not much better than water.
LanguageData.GR_ChickenffaloMilk.label: chickenffalo milk
LanguageData.GR_MechaMilk.description: This metallic, quicksilver-like substance is not edible. It can be processed in a refinery to obtain neutroamine, chemfuel and a bit of silver.
LanguageData.GR_MechaMilk.label: mechanoid milk
LanguageData.GR_MuffalobearMilk.description: A delicious milk with roasted overtones. Very pleasurable to eat, but not very nutritious.
LanguageData.GR_MuffalobearMilk.label: muffalobear milk
LanguageData.GR_MuffalochickenMilk.description: This lumpy milk seems to have concentrated protein chunks, tasting like chicken nuggets. Disgusting, but colonists seem to like it.
LanguageData.GR_MuffalochickenMilk.label: muffalochicken milk
LanguageData.GR_RatMilk.description: This milk has a rich, thick quality to it. It's origins, however, might be a bit dubious... Unless you trust mafia guys called Tony.
LanguageData.GR_RatMilk.label: ratffalo milk
LanguageData.GR_ThrumffaloMilk.description: Milk from a thrumffalo. Very nutritive. Also with a sky-high cholesterol content, but in the Rim nobody judges you.
LanguageData.GR_ThrumffaloMilk.label: thrumffalo milk
LanguageData.GR_WeirdMilk.description: This strange milk has a sour, chemical taste.
LanguageData.GR_WeirdMilk.label: weird milk
LanguageData.GR_WolffaloMilk.description: This milk seems to be filled with strands of grey wool. Disgusting, and quite hard to drink...
LanguageData.GR_WolffaloMilk.label: hairy milk
LanguageData.GR_EldritchMilk.description: This milk is purple coloured, and its smell is indescribable. It whispers to you...
LanguageData.GR_EldritchMilk.label: eldritch milk
LanguageData.GR_DinoMilk.description: Triceramuffalos produce a secretion from various parts of their upper digestive tract. The resulting "milk" is fed to their offspring via regurgitation, and is similar to what mammals produce; it contains similar levels of fat and protein, along with carotenoids and antibodies. Now, why would anyone want to drink this? 'Cause it's the Rim, baby!
LanguageData.GR_DinoMilk.label: dino "milk"
LanguageData.GR_GreenMilk.description: This Lizardochs milk is green coloured, and quite unappetizing.
LanguageData.GR_GreenMilk.label: green milk
LanguageData.GR_ParaMilk.description: Paraceramuffalo milk is rich and creamy. Probably too rich in fat, but certainly tasty.
LanguageData.GR_ParaMilk.label: paraceramuffalo milk
LanguageData.GR_Castoreum.description: Castoreum is a yellowish exudate from the castor sacs of beavers. It is historically used as a food additive and natural flavour, often as a substitute vanilla flavour.
LanguageData.GR_Castoreum.label: beaverwolf castoreum
LanguageData.GR_Chickenbearfeathers.description: Chickenbear feathers can be gathered and processed into a fine powder called featherdust, which produces a mild euphoric sensation when burned and inhaled.
LanguageData.GR_Chickenbearfeathers.label: chickenbear feathers
LanguageData.GR_HugeBearCatPelt.description: This enormous bear pelt is probably valuable as a trade good. Or it can also be repurposed as a huge rug.
LanguageData.GR_HugeBearCatPelt.label: bearcat pelt
LanguageData.GR_HugeBearPelt.description: This enormous bear pelt is probably valuable as a trade good. Or it can also be repurposed as a huge rug.
LanguageData.GR_HugeBearPelt.label: huge bear pelt
LanguageData.GR_MuffaloPelt.description: Cool! You can now make your own Game of Thrones / Rimworld cosplay! Or it can also be repurposed as a comfy rug.
LanguageData.GR_MuffaloPelt.label: muffalo pelt
LanguageData.GR_WolfPelt.description: This wolf pelt can be used as a trade good. It can also be crafted into a decorative wolf rug.
LanguageData.GR_WolfPelt.label: wolf pelt
LanguageData.GR_DarkYoungWoodLog.description: This wood-like material has been extracted from the tentacles of an eldritch creature. Let's make a cupboard out of them!
LanguageData.GR_DarkYoungWoodLog.label: eldritch wood
LanguageData.GR_EldritchInsectJelly.description: Jelly stored and used as food by oversized insects. It is smooth, rich, and brings incredible joy to those who consume it. Also, it quickly erodes their sanity and turns them into insane psychopaths, but it's so tasty! Because of its unique biological properties, it can nourish almost any creature and never rots.
LanguageData.GR_EldritchInsectJelly.label: eldritch insect jelly
LanguageData.GR_DinoInsectJelly.description: Dinobeetles are huge beasts that produce a nutrient rich, amber-like substance. They probably would use it to build complex structures similar to bees, but they don't because... well, because they are a scientific abomination doomed from the start... Because of its unique biological properties, it can nourish almost any creature and never rots.
LanguageData.GR_DinoInsectJelly.label: dino insect jelly
LanguageData.GR_FossilChemfuel.description: Deprecated. No hybrid should produce this now.
LanguageData.GR_FossilChemfuel.label: fossil chemfuel
LanguageData.GR_ScalyBearPelt.description: This enormous bear pelt is part hair and part scales. It is probably valuable as a trade good. Or it can also be repurposed as a huge rug.
LanguageData.GR_ScalyBearPelt.label: scaly bear pelt
LanguageData.GR_HybridChitin.description: Overlapping plates of hardened chitin. Really durable in combat, but provides little protection from weather.
LanguageData.GR_HybridChitin.label: hybrid chitin
LanguageData.GR_HybridScales.description: Light, pliable sheets of scaly skin. Light, strong, and insulates well.
LanguageData.GR_HybridScales.label: hybrid scales
LanguageData.GR_HybridSilk.description: Fabric spun from silk fibers. Resists blunt and sharp damage easily, but provides almost no protection against extreme heat.
LanguageData.GR_HybridSilk.label: hybrid silk
LanguageData.GR_LuxuriantHybridWool.description: This wool is extremely thick and warm. Probably the best wool in the animal kingdom.
LanguageData.GR_LuxuriantHybridWool.label: luxuriant hybrid wool
LanguageData.GR_MechaWool.description: This wool seems to be strengthened by plasteel micro-fibers, and covered with advanced circuitry.
LanguageData.GR_MechaWool.label: mechanoid wool
LanguageData.GR_ThickHybridWool.description: Coarse, strong wool. Provides heat and cold protection, as well as decent defense.
LanguageData.GR_ThickHybridWool.label: thick hybrid wool
LanguageData.GR_ThinHybridWool.description: Brittle, strange smelling wool. Not the best wool to craft apparel with, though it's better than nothing. Provides nice heat insulation.
LanguageData.GR_ThinHybridWool.label: thin hybrid wool
LanguageData.GR_EldritchWool.description: This purple wool seems to be irrigated by pulsing, throbbing purple veins. However, they can only be detected by sight, not by touch.
LanguageData.GR_EldritchWool.label: eldritch wool
LanguageData.GR_DinoFeatherWool.description: This seems to be an amalgamation of feathers, scales and wool, perfectly interweaved. Gives adequate defense against blunt attacks, and is quite warm.
LanguageData.GR_DinoFeatherWool.label: dino featherwool
LanguageData.GR_DinoLeather.description: Thick leather partially covered in scales and dino-fuzz. Harvested from a dinosaur hybrid, this thick skin insulates and protects very well.
LanguageData.GR_DinoLeather.label: dinosaur leather
LanguageData.GR_BeeGenetic.description:
A payload of bee genetic material. Can be used at the genomorpher to create a growth cell of a hybrid animal. This tier 1 genome can be used only as a primary genome.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All bee hybrids hover over terrain.

<color=#E5E54C>Extraction:</color> Can be extracted via the genome extractor table from a combination of 10 bees.
LanguageData.GR_BeeGenetic.label: bee genome
LanguageData.GR_TastyMeat.description: Raw meat from a manchicken. Unbelievably tasty, and unbelievably unethical. Light, nutritive and with no poisoning chance.
LanguageData.GR_TastyMeat.label: crispy meat
LanguageData.GR_MechanoidChip.description: This strange stellated octahedron seems to house an assembly of complex computer chips. It appears to be self-powered, and a faint beeping noise emanates from its core. It is constantly emitting encoded radio waves, and perhaps these could be suitably amplified if the technology behind it could be properly reverse engineered.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Mechanoid chips can only be found in abandoned labs, and can be used to build beomechanical lab beacons, that will allow you to find a biomechanical lab complex, as well as mechahybrid antennas.
LanguageData.GR_MechanoidChip.label: mechanoid chip
LanguageData.GR_Boomthread.description: This silk is as strong as synthread, but it is also an excellent thermal insulator
LanguageData.GR_Boomthread.label: boomthread
LanguageData.GR_Shiftskin.label: formless boomalope shiftskin
LanguageData.RB_Bee_Leather.description: Hard overlapping scales of insect chitin laid over a thick layer of semi-rigid leather. Quite durable in combat, but provides little protection from weather.
LanguageData.RB_Bee_Leather.label: bee leather
LanguageData.GR_ROM_RawCHFood_FormlessMeat.descriptio: Raw meat from a formless spawn of Tsathoggua. Or... or at least a boomalope abomination that *looked* similar. Hard to know with these things.
LanguageData.GR_ROM_RawCHFood_FormlessMeat.label: formless spawn meat
LanguageData.RB_Genewax.description: Genewax is extracted from combs of the Genetic family (any Vanilla Genetics Expanded hybrid bees) and is much less resistant than regular beeswax. Its only practical application is that it can be used to create genoframes, instead of corpses.
LanguageData.RB_Genewax.label: genewax
LanguageData.GR_PoisonAmpoule.description: This tiny vial is filled with a concentrated solution of Muffalokomodo saliva, which acts as a powerful neuromuscular-blocking drug. This effectively paralyses anyone that drinks it, though it is not lethal. It will also stop bleeding rate while active, perhaps allowing a person to avoid bleeding to death if the poison is administered quick enough. Effects wear off after 6 hours.
LanguageData.GR_PoisonAmpoule.label: poison ampoule
LanguageData.GR_AnimusHare.description: The Animus Hare is a small leporid with latent psionic abilities. Quite intelligent for an animal, but not intelligent enough to properly use psionics. It will however quickly bond with human beings and reach with his mind to make happy everyone that treats it well.\n\nUseful abilities: they will often use a psionic nuzzle on colonists, keeping everyone happier. They also breed very fast.
LanguageData.GR_AnimusHare.label: animus hare
LanguageData.GR_AnimusHare.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_AnimusHare.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_AnimusHare.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_BearBug.description: This tardigrade-like creature is basically a huge bed bug infused with bear genes. It consumes a large amount of food, but its paralysing attacks combined with its resilience can be very useful in a combat situation.\n\nUseful abilities: Bear bugs produce a powerful neurotoxin that can paralyse their prey, leaving them completely unable to defend themselves.
LanguageData.GR_BearBug.label: bear bug
LanguageData.GR_BearBug.tools.0.label: mandibles
LanguageData.GR_BearBug.tools.1.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Beetlefleet.description: A chitinous, floating, hydrogen-filled monstruosity. The beetlefleet wanders the land bouncing off objects in its path, seeking prey to consume.\n\nUseful abilities: Beetlefleet produce insect jelly, explode on death violently, and reproduce asexually.
LanguageData.GR_Beetlefleet.label: beetlefleet
LanguageData.GR_Beetlefleet.tools.1.label: tentacles
LanguageData.GR_Boomalisk.description: The boomalisk is a boomalope / dunealisk hybrid that produces a weird spider silk know as boomthread. This silk is as strong as synthread, but it is also an excellent thermal insulator. Boomalisks also catch their prey with exploding webs, which... sounds dangerous.\n\nUseful abilities: Produces chemfuel, boomthread and attacks with explosive webs.
LanguageData.GR_Boomalisk.label: boomalisk
LanguageData.GR_Boomalisk.tools.0.label: left first leg
LanguageData.GR_Boomalisk.tools.1.label: right first leg
LanguageData.GR_Boomalisk.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Boomalisk.verbs.0.label: exploding web
LanguageData.GR_Groundffalo.description: The horned groundffalo is a great pack beast (albeit a bit slow) capable of producing milk and wool, as well as mining for your colony. Compared to a Groundrunner it won't be as efficient on its mining work, but the other characteristics make up for this.\n\nUseful abilities: Produces wool, milk, acts as a pack beast and mines rocks and minerals.
LanguageData.GR_Groundffalo.label: groundffalo
LanguageData.GR_Groundffalo.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Groundffalo.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_Groundffalo.tools.2.label: horns
LanguageData.GR_Groundffalo.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_MeadowLizard.description: The meadow lizard is a beast bred for speed. Even though they are naturally slower than meadow aves, they can be trained to be incredibly fast mounts.\n\nUseful abilities: if properly trained they can be ridden into battle at incredible speeds (this interaction needs the Giddy-Up, combat mounts mod, and it will make the Meadow Lizards move 100% faster when used as mounts). They are also venomous.
LanguageData.GR_MeadowLizard.label: meadow lizard
LanguageData.GR_MeadowLizard.tools.0.label: claws
LanguageData.GR_MeadowLizard.tools.2.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Needlechicken.description: This abomination is part chicken, part sentient cactus. If cactus nuggets weren't a thing, they are now. You can think of them as solar-powered egg layers.\n\nUseful abilities: Needlechicken are decent egg layers. They can defend themselves with tiny poison barb attacks, and they almost don't need sustenance (except for a large amount of sunlight).
LanguageData.GR_Needlechicken.label: needlechicken
LanguageData.GR_Needlechicken.tools.0.label: stings
LanguageData.GR_Needlechicken.tools.2.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Needlechicken.verbs.0.label: tiny venomous barb
LanguageData.GR_Nighthrumbo.description: The nighthrumbo is a fearsome predator. Armed with strong jaws and its horn, as well as ranged quill attacks, it will make quick work of any prey it decides to attack. It is also a nocturnal animal, sleeping during the day and being active at night.\n\nUseful abilities: Ranged quill attacks.
LanguageData.GR_Nighthrumbo.label: nighthrumbo
LanguageData.GR_Nighthrumbo.tools.0.label: horn
LanguageData.GR_Nighthrumbo.tools.1.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Nighthrumbo.tools.2.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_Nighthrumbo.tools.4.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Nighthrumbo.verbs.0.label: huge quill
LanguageData.GR_WolfShrimp.description: A terrifyingly alien creature, the wolf shrimp is keenly intelligent like its wolf forefather, slithering in blinding speed towards its prey.\n\nUseful abilities: Extremely fast regenerative capabilities.
LanguageData.GR_WolfShrimp.label: wolf shrimp
LanguageData.GR_WolfShrimp.tools.0.label: head
LanguageData.GR_WolfShrimp.tools.2.label: left blade
LanguageData.GR_WolfShrimp.tools.3.label: right blade
LanguageData.GR_ArchotechCentipede.description:
The Archocentipede is the end result of the Archotech Project, aiming to provide a "friendly" machine superintelligence with a capable body. Even though this centipede is forcefully loyal to the colonists, and can be somewhat controlled by them, most other inhabitants of the planet will consider its existence to be an abomination, since dabbling in archotech usually signals the end of comprehensible human life.

Useful abilities: What do you mean useful? You have WON the game. The Archocentipede is basically unkillable by normal means, just use it to lay waste to everything on the planet. You don't have enemies now, you have pets. Ooooor... the archotech may kill all your colonists. One of the two.

Based on concept art for the Nydus Worm by Federico Dallocchio from Blizzard Entertainment at the Starcraft wiki, https://starcraft.fandom.com/wiki/Nydus_worm/Development.
LanguageData.GR_ArchotechCentipede.label: archocentipede
LanguageData.GR_ArchotechCentipede.tools.0.label: head
LanguageData.GR_ArchotechCentipede.verbs.0.label: disintegrator
LanguageData.GR_ArchotechCentipede.verbs.1.label: twin-coiled laser
LanguageData.GR_Chickenbear.description:
For something so small, this animal has a hell of an appetite. Lacking in many departments, but the nutrition has to go somewhere and that’s for those looking for a non-vegan friendly option of getting high. The person who thought it would be a good idea to inhale the burning feathers of this plump creature is one stoners everywhere praise while they blaze.

An avian hybrid with recessive ursine genes. Has a considerably larger appetite and inherent wildness, but reproduces quickly.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Can be regularly plucked for chickenbear feathers. When these feathers are turned into powder at a Drug lab, they can be rolled into a joint to be smoked.
LanguageData.GR_Chickenbear.label: chickenbear
LanguageData.GR_Chickenbear.tools.0.label: claws
LanguageData.GR_Chickenbear.tools.2.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Chickencat.description:
Respectably intelligent as well as lethal, these egg-laying, plump beasties love nuzzling their owners and biting their enemies. Sometimes those are different people.

An avian hybrid with recessive feline genes. Quite clean with a low gestation period, they make for an average farm animal and an average combatant with good speed. They still require a pen, so they make for good home defence.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Like all cat hybrids, they have an improved melee dodge ability and a short nuzzle period.
LanguageData.GR_Chickencat.label: chickencat
LanguageData.GR_Chickencat.tools.0.label: claws
LanguageData.GR_Chickencat.tools.2.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Chickenffalo.description: You wouldn’t think something so small could eat so much, but that’s the price you pay for the sake of convenience. That convenience is sporadic, low in nutrition and very thin, an effect commonly described as “nerfing”.\n\nAn avian hybrid with recessive muffalo genes. Loves to wander so it must be securely penned, but they make for a surprising pack beast. Capable of producing the holy trinity of milk, eggs and wool, however they can be quite lacking in substance.
LanguageData.GR_Chickenffalo.label: chickenffalo
LanguageData.GR_Chickenffalo.tools.0.label: claws
LanguageData.GR_Chickenffalo.tools.1.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Chickenlope.description: The ideal companion for those who love the smell of fried eggs and napalm in the morning. Regretfully they’re far too birdbrained to receive any direction, and most owners have been noted saying “I’ve made a huge mistake”.\n\nAn avian hybrid with recessive boomalope genes. Very dangerous if allowed to breed uncontrollably, so consider liberal fireproofing and selling the excess. Produces chemfuel daily as well as an egg every 2 days.
LanguageData.GR_Chickenlope.label: chickenlope
LanguageData.GR_Chickenlope.tools.0.label: claws
LanguageData.GR_Chickenlope.tools.2.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Chickenrabbit.description: One of the few unusual animal hybrids that come with their own warning labels despite being almost entirely harmless. All it took was one absent-minded vegan to cause a single couple of “Breeders” to overpopulate and devastate the ecology of an entire continent. Fairy tales surrounding the fluffy terror have done nothing to help it be taken seriously.\n\nAn avian hybrid with recessive rodent genes. An incredibly low metabolism coupled with an incredibly high reproduction rate, these animals are very sensitive to temperature and are unusually keen on escaping to the wilds.
LanguageData.GR_Chickenrabbit.label: chickenrabbit
LanguageData.GR_Chickenrabbit.tools.0.label: claws
LanguageData.GR_Chickenrabbit.tools.2.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Chickenwolf.description: The Tiny Terror with a disconcertingly large mouth and sharp teeth. These comic flightless web-footed little bastards may finally unwittingly help man to fathom the uncharted depths of just how dangerous they can make a living creature the size of a basketball.\n\nAn avian hybrid with recessive canine genes. Doesn’t produce much in the way of eggs but makes up for it by having a shorter gestation period and a very nasty bite. They’re also incredibly smart and agile, making them ideal for swarm tactics.
LanguageData.GR_Chickenwolf.label: chickenwolf
LanguageData.GR_Chickenwolf.tools.0.label: claws
LanguageData.GR_Chickenwolf.tools.2.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Turkeyman.description:
A cheeky little shit that likes to go around and play with switches, to satisfy their curiosity much like how an ignorant child would. Perhaps a little too smart for their own good. Their behaviour is similar to failed service animals that learn how to operate household items without restraint.

An avian hybrid with recessive humanoid genes. Surprisingly hardy, quick and very intelligent, these creatures make for a particularly filthy and hungry egg-layer. Their little wings are quite dextrous and can be used to flick switches.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Humanoid hybrids will periodically engage other colonists in conversation, talking about a variety of topics. This increases the social recreation needs of those they interact with by 10% (subject to diminishing returns like all joy types). If using Ideology, humanoid hybrids are subject to precepts. Turkeymen periodically flick switches around the colony (any building that can be toggled on / off by a colonist).
LanguageData.GR_Turkeyman.label: turkeyman
LanguageData.GR_Turkeyman.tools.0.label: claws
LanguageData.GR_Turkeyman.tools.1.label: toothed beak
LanguageData.GR_Turkeyman.tools.2.label: head
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Bearbee.description: A Bear / Bee hybrid. This huge bee hybrid is fiercely agressive and territorial. It will act as a very powerful attack animal with venomous attacks if properly trained.\n\nUseful abilities: All bee hybrids slowly produce their appropriate honeycombs every few days. They also have a very nasty venomous sting. Finally, they will ignore terrain movement costs, since they hover over the ground, except over water, where their movement is slightly impeded.
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Bearbee.label: bearbee
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Bearbee.tools.1.label: claws
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Boombee.description: A Boomalope / Bee hybrid. This bee hybrid is covered in chemfuel pustules and it is highly unstable.\n\nUseful abilities: All bee hybrids slowly produce their appropriate honeycombs every few days. The boombee injects chemfuel with its stinger, instead of venom, which is as painful as it sounds. It will also explode in a cloud of bees when it dies, which is incredibly stupid from a scientific standpoint, but it is cool, so meh. Finally, they will ignore terrain movement costs, since they hover over the ground, except over water, where their movement is slightly impeded.
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Boombee.label: boombee
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Boombee.tools.1.label: claws
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Catbee.description: A Cat / Bee hybrid. The agile catbee is an affectionate pet that will also sting your enemies to death.\n\nUseful abilities: All bee hybrids slowly produce their appropriate honeycombs every few days. The catbee has a powerful venomous sting that makes its prey blind for a while. Finally, they will ignore terrain movement costs, since they hover over the ground, except over water, where their movement is slightly impeded.
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Catbee.label: catbee
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Catbee.tools.1.label: claws
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Chickenbee.description: A Chicken / Bee hybrid. As it is often the case with avian hybrids, they are not very strong on their own, but when used in high enough numbers they can be a real nuisance.\n\nUseful abilities: All bee hybrids slowly produce their appropriate honeycombs every few days. The chickenbee is an excellent egg layer. Finally, they will ignore terrain movement costs, since they hover over the ground, except over water, where their movement is slightly impeded.
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Chickenbee.label: chickenbee
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Chickenbee.tools.1.label: claws
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Lizardbee.description: A Lizard / Bee hybrid. This bee hybrid is covered in scales, providing it with a thick natural armor.\n\nUseful abilities: All bee hybrids slowly produce their appropriate honeycombs every few days. Lizardbees have a very potent venom. Finally, they will ignore terrain movement costs, since they hover over the ground, except over water, where their movement is slightly impeded.
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Lizardbee.label: lizardbee
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Lizardbee.tools.1.label: claws
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Mousebee.description: A Mouse / Bee hybrid. These diminutive bee hybrids reproduce really fast.\n\nUseful abilities: All bee hybrids slowly produce their appropriate honeycombs every few days. The mousebee reproduces with blinding speed. Finally, they will ignore terrain movement costs, since they hover over the ground, except over water, where their movement is slightly impeded.
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Mousebee.label: mousebee
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Mousebee.tools.1.label: claws
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Muffalobee.description: A Muffalo / Bee hybrid. This docile bee hybrid is a versatile pack beast, since it ignores terrain movement costs. Apart from its honeycombs, it also produces additional small amounts of milk and wool.\n\nUseful abilities: All bee hybrids slowly produce their appropriate honeycombs every few days. They also have a very nasty venomous sting. Finally, they will ignore terrain movement costs, since they hover over the ground, except over water, where their movement is slightly impeded.
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Muffalobee.label: muffalobee
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Muffalobee.tools.1.label: claws
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Spiderbee.description: A Megaspider / Bee hybrid. A powerful, armoured bee hybrid that cannot reproduce on its own. It has a dangerous ranged attack.\n\nUseful abilities: All bee hybrids slowly produce their appropriate honeycombs every few days. They also have a very nasty venomous sting, and a ranged sting attack. Finally, they will ignore terrain movement costs, since they hover over the ground, except over water, where their movement is slightly impeded.
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Spiderbee.label: spiderbee
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Spiderbee.tools.1.label: claws
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Spiderbee.tools.2.label: slicing mandibles
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Thrumbee.description: A Thrumbo / Bee hybrid. This gigantic bee hybrid is very intelligent, and it can easily be the backbone of a colony's defense if trained to do it.\n\nUseful abilities: All bee hybrids slowly produce their appropriate honeycombs every few days. They also have a very nasty venomous sting. Finally, they will ignore terrain movement costs, since they hover over the ground, except over water, where their movement is slightly impeded.
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Thrumbee.label: thrumbee
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Thrumbee.tools.0.label: horn
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Thrumbee.tools.2.label: claws
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Wolfbee.description: A Wolf / Bee hybrid. A fearsome flying predator. One of the fastest creatures on the Rim, due to its ability to ignore rugged terrain. Perfect for chasing down intruders.\n\nUseful abilities: All bee hybrids slowly produce their appropriate honeycombs every few days. The wolfbee has a powerful venomous sting that paralyzes its prey. Finally, they will ignore terrain movement costs, since they hover over the ground, except over water, where their movement is slightly impeded.
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Wolfbee.label: wolfbee
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Wolfbee.tools.1.label: claws
LanguageData.GR_Boomabear.description: Originally intended as a way to make boomalopes more combat viable, their powerful muscles have instead been focused on compressing its produced chemfuel as a way to store greater amounts of chemfuel without care of how dangerous it is.\n\nA boomalope hybrid with recessive ursine genes. Consumes more than twice as much compared to a boomalope but produces deepchem instead. They’re also capable of defending themselves should any predator be foolish enough to go after them.
LanguageData.GR_Boomabear.label: boomabear
LanguageData.GR_Boomabear.tools.1.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Boomachicken.description: A biological weapon in compact form, these living, breathing, shitting bombs are ideally assembled into a massive horde and sent squawking at the enemy. Allegedly, this particular hybrid has been brought up in certain court cases as the original creator thought it would be funny to create “lethal egg timers”.\n\nA boomalope hybrid with recessive avian genes. Produces edible eggs very slowly, but can lay a clutch of explosive eggs twice a day. They’re both reasonably intelligent and quick on their feet.
LanguageData.GR_Boomachicken.label: boomachicken
LanguageData.GR_Boomachicken.tools.0.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Boomachicken.tools.1.label: bite
LanguageData.GR_Booman.description:
Outright refuses to let anyone else milk them for their chemfuel as they view it as a violation of their humanoid rights. They make for a good conversation partner even if their stance can be a little volatile.

A boomalope hybrid with recessive humanoid genes. Instead of supplying a regular amount of chemfuel, this hybrid will syphon themselves to top up a limited number of machinery powered by chemfuel, such as generators and pod launchers.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Humanoid hybrids will periodically engage other colonists in conversation, talking about a variety of topics. This increases the social recreation needs of those they interact with by 10% (subject to diminishing returns like all joy types). If using Ideology, humanoid hybrids are subject to precepts. Boomen periodically refuel some machines powered by chemfuel in a 10 tile radius (chemfuel generators and pod launchers).
LanguageData.GR_Booman.label: booman
LanguageData.GR_Booman.tools.1.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Boomcat.description:
Combining the frequent nuzzling of a cat and the deepchem production of a primary boomalope hybrid is surely in no way going to end badly. Better not to ignore them should they complain loudly about wanting dinner.

A boomalope hybrid with recessive feline genes. They make for an interesting companion for those who desire chemfuel production but also want to be able to cuddle said creatures. They can also be butchered to retrieve an internal gland filled with deepchem.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Like all cat hybrids, they have an improved melee dodge ability and a short nuzzle period.
LanguageData.GR_Boomcat.label: boomcat
LanguageData.GR_Boomcat.tools.1.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Boomffalo.description: People love efficiency and having one thing to cover all their needs. Well this isn’t it, but it gets damn close. The chemfuel sacs have mixed with milk-producing glands, which make for a milk that smells like pure gasoline and could almost power a vehicle.\n\nA boomalope hybrid with recessive muffalo genes. Has a large appetite due to it producing multiple products, namely deepchem, thin wool and an unusual milk that shares properties with Chemshine.
LanguageData.GR_Boomffalo.label: boomffalo
LanguageData.GR_Boomffalo.tools.0.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Boomsquirrel.description: With adorable horns and long snoots, these squeaky lads are ideal in giving your enemy a stunning surprise. Their chemfuel mixture includes other gases that create a blinding and disorienting flashbang when remotely detonated. They’ll still make a regular boom if killed though.\n\nA boomalope hybrid with recessive rodent genes. Quite fast and intelligent, these hybrids work as excellent swarm attackers. Their rapid reproduction cycle and stunning explosion make for an ideal companion to ranged masters.
LanguageData.GR_Boomsquirrel.label: boomsquirrel
LanguageData.GR_Boomsquirrel.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Boomsquirrel.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_Boomsquirrel.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Boomwolf.description: For those who wished they could equip their canines with explosive vests, the Boomwolf stores pressurised - almost solidified - chemfuel which can be remotely discharged as a non-explosive gas. Thanks to thermodynamics, it is extremely cold upon release and can just as easily hurt the hybrid as well as everything nearby.\n\nA boomalope hybrid with recessive canine genes. Very intelligent, fast and dangerous, they make for an interesting combat animal. One of the few canine-type hybrids that work better solo than as a pack.
LanguageData.GR_Boomwolf.label: boomwolf
LanguageData.GR_Boomwolf.tools.1.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Dogman.description:
Man’s best friend who is also himself? Have you ever thought that the folds on a dog’s skin can act almost as pockets? What if those folds held a knife and the dog could wield it? Well look no further, as this pup will answer those questions with violence.

A canine hybrid with recessive humanoid genes. A smart hybrid capable of holding conversations with their technical superiors and will sometimes obey commands to slaughter other tamed colony animals.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Humanoid hybrids will periodically engage other colonists in conversation, talking about a variety of topics. This increases the social recreation needs of those they interact with by 10% (subject to diminishing returns like all joy types). If using Ideology, humanoid hybrids are subject to precepts. Dogmen will periodically obey commands to slaughter other colony (tamed) animals.
LanguageData.GR_Dogman.label: dogman
LanguageData.GR_Dogman.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Dogman.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_Dogman.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Wolfalope.description:
Some insane madman thought it would be an excellent idea to strap a flamethrower to a dog and send it out into the world to see what happens. Don’t be surprised to find out this unusual hybrid started a massive forest fire trying to hunt a rabbit.

A canine hybrid with recessive boomalope genes. A bit more dim-witted compared to their baseline, due to a large amount of brain processing power dedicated to the complex internal organs that permit it to spit out burning chemfuel. Excellent flankers thanks to their increased speed and ranged ability.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All canine hybrids get the Adrenaline rush ability, which allows them to move at 200% speed for a short time, but be tired and move slower for half a day afterwards.
LanguageData.GR_Wolfalope.label: wolfalope
LanguageData.GR_Wolfalope.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Wolfalope.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_Wolfalope.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Wolfalope.verbs.0.label: fire breath
LanguageData.GR_Wolfbear.description:
A massively muscled boi with strong claws, sharper teeth and a mean attitude. Adorable when it’s trying to jump you to lick your face, less so when those same paws are trying to restrain you so it can bite your head off.

A canine hybrid with recessive ursine genes. Stronger, more aggressive and with a bigger appetite, this hybrid’s particular lethality comes from its tendency to attack the enemy’s head to try and decapitate them.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All canine hybrids get the Adrenaline rush ability, which allows them to move at 200% speed for a short time, but be tired and move slower for half a day afterwards. There is a 25% chance that wolfbear attacks will target its victim's head, possibly decapitating enemies. Fun!
LanguageData.GR_Wolfbear.label: wolfbear
LanguageData.GR_Wolfbear.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Wolfbear.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_Wolfbear.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Wolfbeaver.description:
Probably an excellent example of a precursor to the alphabeaver, this beastie has an insane appetite and constantly growing teeth that it wears down by chewing on trees. Either consider creating a tree grove just for them to eat or moving to a jungle because they’re gonna be procreating a whole bunch.

A canine hybrid with recessive rodent genes. Fast gestation period, highly intelligent, incredibly fast and lethal. The price for these benefits is that it has a constant hunger for trees and shares ancestry with another rapid dendrovore.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All canine hybrids get the Adrenaline rush ability, which allows them to move at 200% speed for a short time, but be tired and move slower for half a day afterwards.
LanguageData.GR_Wolfbeaver.label: wolfbeaver
LanguageData.GR_Wolfbeaver.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Wolfbeaver.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_Wolfbeaver.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Wolfcat.description:
Dogs and cats are domesticated predators who are just the absolute cutest. So someone wanted to see how dangerous they could make something by combining these two proto predators. The results could be imagined but we’ll let them speak for themselves.

A canine hybrid with recessive feline genes. A combination of two predators makes for an incredible loyal killing machine. Quick, lethal, low maintenance and a desire for companionship. If that wasn’t already enough, it can even randomly attack twice by lunging with both claws and teeth.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All canine hybrids get the Adrenaline rush ability, which allows them to move at 200% speed for a short time, but be tired and move slower for half a day afterwards. Like all cat hybrids, they have an improved melee dodge ability and a short nuzzle period. Double slash: 25% of the time its attacks will strike twice.
LanguageData.GR_Wolfcat.label: wolfcat
LanguageData.GR_Wolfcat.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Wolfcat.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_Wolfcat.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Wolfchicken.description:
Not many people are familiar with just how vicious a chicken can be, with them even going so far to pick at their own wounds or actively eating each other if hungry. Combining that with the sleek hunting form of a wolf and making them vegetarian creates a deadly combo. You’d go mad too if you smelled something delicious but couldn’t stomach eating it.

A canine hybrid with recessive avian genes. Very quick on their feet and very filthy, care must be taken as they are far less suited to cold temperatures. The inherent viciousness of chickens has been combined with a proper combat form, vastly boosting their speed after killing its prey.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All canine hybrids get the Adrenaline rush ability, which allows them to move at 200% speed for a short time, but be tired and move slower for half a day afterwards. Blood frenzy ability: after killing prey, the wolfchicken receives 200% speed for an hour.
LanguageData.GR_Wolfchicken.label: wolfchicken
LanguageData.GR_Wolfchicken.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Wolfchicken.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_Wolfchicken.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Wolffalo.description:
While they make for a completely daft canine hybrid, they’re still smart enough not to wander off. Anyone familiar with dogs that shed enough to make another animal will feel right at home with this hybrid. Even the milk it produces comes already laden with hair for your displeasure!

A canine hybrid with recessive muffalo genes. A great producer of thick wool and, regrettably, hairy milk. This allows them to survive in even more extreme colds, but they frequently make a mess, lack most of their intellect and need to eat a lot.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All canine hybrids get the Adrenaline rush ability, which allows them to move at 200% speed for a short time, but be tired and move slower for half a day afterwards.
LanguageData.GR_Wolffalo.label: wolffalo
LanguageData.GR_Wolffalo.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Wolffalo.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_Wolffalo.tools.2.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbalope.description:
Now here’s a creature you absolutely want to keep properly fed, and you’re going to need entire fields to support a single one. The ridiculous amount of chemfuel they produce means that the ceasing of their life functions is more accurately described as “thermonuclear”. So if they are going to blow up, send them as a surprise gift to friends and family who keep trying to kill you.

A colossal hybrid with recessive boomalope genes. The addition of chemfuel production has vastly increased the amount of food they need to survive, but they’re also smart enough to not need a pen. Consider keeping them far away from your growing fields if the local biome can grow vegetation fast enough to support one.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All colossal hybrids have very slow attacks, but hit very hard, and possess natural armor.
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbalope.label: thrumbalope
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbalope.tools.0.label: horn
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbalope.tools.2.label: left foot
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbalope.tools.3.label: right foot
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbalope.tools.4.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbear.description:
If you thought Thrumbos were tough and hit like a tank, may I present to you the answer to all your prayers. If you can feed the thing, this absolute unit would work extremely well as an assault force against any force foolish enough to threaten its feedstock. Plus you get to ride it on long journeys, possibly clearing entire forests on the way.

A colossal hybrid with recessive ursine genes. While proportionately it has a lower metabolism, its 50% increase in size still leaves it with a higher hunger rate than a thrumbo. They are however easier to train, hit much harder and can take even more punishment.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All colossal hybrids have very slow attacks, but hit very hard, and possess natural armor.
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbear.label: thrumbear
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbear.tools.0.label: horn
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbear.tools.2.label: left foot
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbear.tools.3.label: right foot
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbear.tools.4.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbocat.description:
The other half of the super predator duo, along with the Thrumwolf, exchanging some ferocity and durability in exchange for going for the trachea and spitting suffocating hairballs. That might not sound that dangerous but imagine having a fistful of hair pulled from a shower drain smothering your mouth, and you’ll realise that’s still not disgusting enough. But for those that it appreciates, it will often pin them down and snuggle them given the chance.

A colossal hybrid with recessive feline genes. A large predator with an insane appetite with a focus on ferocious attacks that restrict their prey’s breathing. Extremely agile with a soft spot for those it loves, best suited for biomes that support enough wildlife to sate its hunger.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All colossal hybrids have very slow attacks, but hit very hard, and possess natural armor.
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbocat.label: thrumbocat
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbocat.tools.0.label: horn
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbocat.tools.2.label: left foot
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbocat.tools.3.label: right foot
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbocat.tools.4.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbochicken.description:
This is probably the closest chickens will be able to get to recovering their past glory as super predators. They’ll often hunt any other animal for their next meal, multiple times a day, and you haven’t yet lived until you’ve ridden one on a long journey. If they could be further bred to cross mountains and oceans, they’d be almost perfect.

A colossal hybrid with recessive avian genes. The combination of genes has turned this somewhat peaceful herbivore/dendrovore into a full carnivore, which makes it easier to support their large appetite. As an intelligent predator, it can move incredibly quickly for its size and its attacks are incredibly fierce. It also lays massive eggs that can feed entire families.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All colossal hybrids have very slow attacks, but hit very hard, and possess natural armor.
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbochicken.label: thrumbochicken
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbochicken.tools.0.label: horn
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbochicken.tools.2.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbolizard.description:
About as close as anyone has ever gotten in making a recreation of dragons from folklore. Capable of surviving in extreme cold or heat, the only thing really missing is a pair of wings and an innate desire to hoard piles of gold for a nest. That and the fact that it isn’t carnivorous, instead having an appetite for vegetables and wood fibres.

A colossal hybrid with recessive reptile genes. With a mouth dripping with venom and tough, flexible scales covering the body, this hybrid makes for a nasty and incredibly durable combat hybrid. If that wasn’t enough, it can also breath out a fog of noxious fumes and move deceptively quickly.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All colossal hybrids have very slow attacks, but hit very hard, and possess natural armor. All reptile hybrids have good temperature tolerance ranges and venomous attacks.
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbolizard.label: thrumbolizard
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbolizard.tools.0.label: horn
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbolizard.tools.2.label: left foot
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbolizard.tools.3.label: right foot
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbolizard.tools.4.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbolizard.verbs.0.label: poison breath
LanguageData.GR_Thrumboman.description:
Look, we know what you want, a thrumbo that won’t eat your crops before they’ve harvested, thereby wasting entire seasons of growth for your most valuable plants. And we’re here to tell you it doesn’t exist. But we’ve gotten close in that this one will typically harvest your plants before it eats them for worse nutritional gain. Unless it’s trees, in which case we got nothing.

A colossal hybrid with recessive humanoid genes. Considerably easier to train and maintain, this particular hybrid will help around the colony by cutting and harvesting designated plants. Unusually lean for how much it eats, it won’t provide much in butchering.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Humanoid hybrids will periodically engage other colonists in conversation, talking about a variety of topics. This increases the social recreation needs of those they interact with by 10% (subject to diminishing returns like all joy types). If using Ideology, humanoid hybrids are subject to precepts. All colossal hybrids have very slow attacks, but hit very hard, and possess natural armor. Thrumbomen will obey orders to cut and harvest plants around the colony.
LanguageData.GR_Thrumboman.label: thrumboman
LanguageData.GR_Thrumboman.tools.0.label: horn
LanguageData.GR_Thrumboman.tools.2.label: left foot
LanguageData.GR_Thrumboman.tools.3.label: right foot
LanguageData.GR_Thrumboman.tools.4.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Thrumborat.description:
Practically an overgrown and intelligent pest capable of tunnelling underground, so not even a nuke could clear an infestation. And it truly would be an infestation, as it has a gestation period measured in days rather than weeks, with a single breeding pair capable of wiping out an entire agricultural planet.

A colossal hybrid with recessive rodent genes. Incredibly durable and fast breeding, this surprisingly small hybrid can work as a durable screening force. One of the weakest colossal hybrids in terms of attack power but very few can outlast its incredibly robust physique.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All colossal hybrids have very slow attacks, but hit very hard, and possess natural armor. Burrow underground: Thrumborats can burrow underground to make them almost immune to damage for a short while.
LanguageData.GR_Thrumborat.label: thrumborat
LanguageData.GR_Thrumborat.tools.0.label: horn
LanguageData.GR_Thrumborat.tools.2.label: left paw
LanguageData.GR_Thrumborat.tools.3.label: right paw
LanguageData.GR_Thrumborat.tools.4.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbospider.description:
A leftover bioweapon from a now extinct civilisation designed to completely eradicate biomes with dense vegetation. Records indicate that the local indigineous tribes were very resistant to corporate takeover and the megacorp in charge wanted to reduce profit losses. Eventually the planet starved as they were unable to stop these creatures from feasting on farmlands and food reserves.

A colossal hybrid with recessive insectoid genes. A slow moving tank with limited regenerative capabilities, they are almost unkillable if it weren’t for their crippling hunger. As durable as a capital ship, any colony that could possibly support the diet of even a single one would be considered impervious to raids.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All colossal hybrids have very slow attacks, but hit very hard, and possess natural armor. They are always sterile and can't reproduce, even if a fertility booster is used to create them.
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbospider.label: thrumbospider
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbospider.tools.1.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Thrumffalo.description:
One of the largest creatures in existence, dwarfing even the thrumbo and the mighty Gallatross. Capable of transporting a massive amount of material, producing a luxurious and excellent wool as well as thick, nutritious milk, they are almost the perfect farm animal. That is as long as you don’t look at your feed costs and die of a heart attack, but the scientists that designed this hybrid never had to worry about such things. Food is something for the proles to worry about.

A colossal hybrid with recessive muffalo genes. Completely herbivorous and with an appetite large enough to cause even large colonies to starve within a single season. This hybrid can prove worth the food costs as it produces thick, delicious milk every couple of days as well as luxurious wool every couple of seasons. Slightly more dimwitted than its progenitor and will attempt to wander off in search of food if not kept penned up.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All colossal hybrids have very slow attacks, but hit very hard, and possess natural armor.
LanguageData.GR_Thrumffalo.label: thrumffalo
LanguageData.GR_Thrumffalo.tools.0.label: horn
LanguageData.GR_Thrumffalo.tools.2.label: left foot
LanguageData.GR_Thrumffalo.tools.3.label: right foot
LanguageData.GR_Thrumffalo.tools.4.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Thrumhorse.description:
A very nice combination of two highly adaptive baseline creatures which arguably results in the best riding companion man could ask for. While this isn’t apparent in their speed, it definitely is so once a rider experiences the thundering beats of this horsey positively barreling through entire forests and marshland in order to earn its next sugar cube the size of a fist.

A colossal hybrid with recessive equine genes. While they have a lower muscle-to-fat ratio compared to their progenitor, the overall placement and shape of the horn vastly reduces their combat efficiency. Instead, they more than make up for this shortfall with an incredible endurance even compared to paragons. An excellent riding companion that is easier to train and can even carry an acceptable amount of luggage, the majority of which contains feed.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All equine hybrids are good for caravaning. A caravan with one of them needs a shorter resting period, only stopping to rest between 23:30 PM and 4 AM. Always starts as draftable, even if not using a controller booster to create it.
