LanguageData.GR_ParagonIguana.description: Small and venomous, with a deadly fart, not quite what one was expecting for the perfect reptilian form. I mean, you’d think you’d get a tyrannosaurus rex or something but no, it seems that the humble iguana is here to stay. If anything, the shock of something so small moving so fast has got to be worth something.\n\nA perfect reptile animal, paragon of all similar animals with no needless junk genetic data. Besides the various improvements to the baseline animal’s abilities, this paragon is oviparous and can manually rupture neck sacs containing venomous gas.
LanguageData.GR_ParagonIguana.label: reptile paragon
LanguageData.GR_ParagonIguana.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_ParagonIguana.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_ParagonIguana.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_ParagonInsectoid.description:
A terrifying callback to the original Sorne geneline, this mobile hive was originally designed to engage the mechanoid enemy head-on. By carrying an entire colony of insects with hardened mandibles, their ability to practically disassemble the mechanical menace proved just as effective at turning against their human overlords.

A perfect insectoid animal, paragon of all similar animals with no needless junk genetic data. Besides the various improvements to the baseline animal’s abilities, this paragon is extremely intelligent and carries a dangerous brood of warrior flies. A burst of pheromones summons the drones to protect their hive and queen, shredding apart all other nearby creatures.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All insectoid hybrids have good armor and no toxic sensitivity, but they are always sterile and can't reproduce, even if a fertility booster is used to create them. Insect clouds ability: twice a day it can create insect clouds around it that damage any animal crossing them, except insectoid paragons, which are immune.
LanguageData.GR_ParagonInsectoid.label: insectoid paragon
LanguageData.GR_ParagonInsectoid.tools.1.label: head
LanguageData.GR_ParagonMuffalo.description: A return to simpler times, when everyone could have sworn blind that muffalos used to be able to produce milk but were instead fooled by hornless paragons.\n\nA perfect muffalo animal, paragon of all similar animals with no needless junk genetic data. Besides the various improvements to the baseline animal’s abilities, this paragon also produces milk and can be slaughtered for a valuable pelt, which can either be used for decoration or sold.
LanguageData.GR_ParagonMuffalo.label: muffalo paragon
LanguageData.GR_ParagonMuffalo.tools.0.label: head
LanguageData.GR_ParagonRat.description: Look, I know what you’re thinking. Why is the rat the paragon of all rodents? And even if you’re not thinking that, this is rhetorical anyway so there. What other rodent has caused civilization to collapse due to poorly held beliefs and lack of proper hygiene. As such, we give you the King Rat. Or Queen Rat, if you want a whole colony showing up.\n\nA perfect rodent animal, paragon of all similar animals with no needless junk genetic data. Besides the various improvements to the baseline animal’s abilities, this paragon has a very fast gestation period and its attacks transmit the plague.
LanguageData.GR_ParagonRat.label: rodent paragon
LanguageData.GR_ParagonRat.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_ParagonRat.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_ParagonRat.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_ParagonThrumbo.description: The gigantic, graceful creature has somehow been improved even further, proving infallibly that there’s always a taller mountain. Significantly easier to train and with a more efficient digestive system, this creature is possibly how the thrumbo originally was when first bred in a lab before it got tainted through terraforming.\n\nA perfect colossal animal, paragon of all similar animals with no needless junk genetic data. Besides the various improvements to the baseline animal’s abilities, this paragon makes for an excellent mount for caravans and can forcibly rush itself, getting faster and creating dust clouds that impede ranged attacks.
LanguageData.GR_ParagonThrumbo.label: colossal paragon
LanguageData.GR_ParagonThrumbo.tools.0.label: horn
LanguageData.GR_ParagonThrumbo.tools.2.label: left foot
LanguageData.GR_ParagonThrumbo.tools.3.label: right foot
LanguageData.GR_ParagonThrumbo.tools.4.label: head
LanguageData.GR_ParagonWolf.description: An improved version of man’s best friend, easier to train, more assertive and dangerous to those that threaten a household. Sadly, far too proud to admit it really wants a belly rub.\n\nA perfect canine animal, paragon of all similar animals with no needless junk genetic data. Besides the various improvements to the baseline animal’s abilities, this paragon can be slaughtered for a valuable pelt, which can either be used for decoration or sold.
LanguageData.GR_ParagonWolf.label: canine paragon
LanguageData.GR_ParagonWolf.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_ParagonWolf.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_ParagonWolf.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Bearodile.description:
The dinosaur nerds out there should love this hybrid. Got one hell of a nasty bite and even the claws will both knock you down and make you unable to get up. Often bred for hunting by the bored one percenters who want to eat massive drumsticks, caveman style.

A reptile hybrid with recessive ursine genes. Highly intelligent and an exceptional frontline trooper, with a venomous, stunning attack, fast movement speed and high health pool.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All reptile hybrids have good temperature tolerance ranges and venomous attacks.
LanguageData.GR_Bearodile.label: bearodile
LanguageData.GR_Bearodile.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Bearodile.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_Bearodile.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Boomsnake.description:
What happens when you mix a spitting cobra, a rattlesnake and a creature designed to lactate refined fuel? This abomination, that’s what. Drive it into a corner and it’ll hurt itself to release deadly neurotoxins. Try and keep your distance and it’ll spit toxic blood right at you. Clearly someone was a sore loser in playing pretend games as a child, but had either the brains or the money to prove themselves right.

A reptile hybrid with recessive boomalope genes. Decently intelligent with a nasty bite and even nastier ranged attack, both causing toxic buildup. Quite a high metabolism which supports its various combat abilities, best suited for home defence.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All reptile hybrids have good temperature tolerance ranges and venomous attacks.
LanguageData.GR_Boomsnake.label: boomsnake
LanguageData.GR_Boomsnake.tools.1.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Boomsnake.verbs.0.label: poison spit
LanguageData.GR_Chickenlizard.description:
Chickens are basically an evolution of dinosaurs, so someone decided to turn them back. Unfortunately they didn’t exactly bother to check that they should still be avian rather than reptilian, so it resulted in this nasty little thing that reproduces at an insane rate and empties your fridge. But hey, they’ll spit on your enemies and their right to shit everywhere, especially where they eat.

A reptile hybrid with recessive avian genes. This particular farm animal is smart enough that it doesn’t need a pen and breeds faster than other reptiles. A very large appetite for a creature so small, which supports its ability to spit out nasty toxins on their enemies and create messes on the floor. Very easy to train and has a good memory.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All reptile hybrids have good temperature tolerance ranges and venomous attacks.
LanguageData.GR_Chickenlizard.label: chickenlizard
LanguageData.GR_Chickenlizard.tools.0.label: claws
LanguageData.GR_Chickenlizard.tools.2.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Chickenlizard.verbs.0.label: poison spit
LanguageData.GR_Lizardman.description:
It is doubtful that one would meet a crueller or more sadistic creature than this hybrid. It actively experiences joy when slaughtering tame animals and executing prisoners, the latter of which even provides a powerful boost which can be used before battle. Maybe it has something to do with taking pleasure in killing those that made it this way, but that’s a story for another time.

A reptile hybrid with recessive humanoid genes. A very intelligent but combat-limited animal that performs surprisingly poorly against similar sized creatures. They will only passively engage creatures much smaller than itself and takes some twisted, psychotic pleasure in killing those who are completely helpless.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Humanoid hybrids will periodically engage other colonists in conversation, talking about a variety of topics. This increases the social recreation needs of those they interact with by 10% (subject to diminishing returns like all joy types). If using Ideology, humanoid hybrids are subject to precepts. All reptile hybrids have good temperature tolerance ranges and venomous attacks. Lizardmen will obey order to slaughter other colony animals, as well as execute prisoners of the colony.
LanguageData.GR_Lizardman.label: lizardman
LanguageData.GR_Lizardman.tools.1.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Muffalokomodo.description:
Milking snakes for venom in order to create medicine is an extremely dangerous career. While this particular beast of burden proved to be capable in other areas, it was mainly synthesised in order to provide a safer way of sourcing an anaesthetic coagulant to be used in emergencies. Or someone was trying to get high by milking a reptilian space cow, who really knows.

A reptile hybrid with recessive muffalo genes. A strong pack animal with a massive appetite and a proportionate filth rate. Quite durable and will prove a fatal mistake for any animal that attempts to hunt them, as their fangs, horns and claws are all venomous. Can be milked every ten days for a vial of paralysing coagulant, best used for large animals that are suffering from multiple lacerations.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All reptile hybrids have good temperature tolerance ranges and venomous attacks.
LanguageData.GR_Muffalokomodo.label: muffalokomodo
LanguageData.GR_Muffalokomodo.verbs.0.label: poison spit
LanguageData.GR_Snakecat.description:
Possibly one of the weirdest and cutest snakes to be ever created. They have several tiny legs which are used to push its body along, while also having an invitingly soft fur. Quite a lot of people never stop freaking out from something sliding up the backs of their legs only to get softly headbutted by a purring python.

A reptile hybrid with recessive feline genes. An unusually furry snake that enjoys nuzzling while also hunting for prey much weaker than itself. Incredibly intelligent and easy to train, they combine quick reflexes and an incredibly nasty bite and spitting attack.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All reptile hybrids have good temperature tolerance ranges and venomous attacks.
LanguageData.GR_Snakecat.label: snakecat
LanguageData.GR_Snakecat.tools.1.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Snakecat.verbs.0.label: poison spit
LanguageData.GR_Spidersnake.description:
Neutroamine is rather difficult to manufacture due to the sterile environment needed in creating a synthetic precursor chemical. So some insane madman decided to create a living creature that can synthesise its own neutroamine. Granted it’s not a lot, but have enough of these disturbingly powerful and quick cobras and no one will ever try to rob you, or live long enough to get away.

A reptile hybrid with recessive insectoid genes. For an animal so small, it has an incredibly powerful bite and can spit venom. Sadly they can’t be trained but will happily hunt smaller prey that happen to wander into their territory or fight back against any foolish predators. Can be milked once every several days for a vial of neutroamine, so they function well as part of a large drug farm.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All reptile hybrids have good temperature tolerance ranges and venomous attacks.
LanguageData.GR_Spidersnake.label: spidersnake
LanguageData.GR_Spidersnake.tools.1.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Spidersnake.verbs.0.label: poison spit
LanguageData.GR_Wolfsnake.description:
A great doggo that sheds snakeskin instead of fur, so don’t be too alarmed when scratching at an itchy spot makes skin flake off. It’ll shed itself, even when out hunting prey, so be prepared to occasionally go on a long trek to pick up the valuable skin.

A reptile hybrid with recessive canine genes. Fast, intelligent and with a nasty venomous bite, these hybrids make for an excellent hunting hound. They lack any notion of fear so they may end up going after much larger, more dangerous prey and achieving a pyrrhic victory.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All reptile hybrids have good temperature tolerance ranges and venomous attacks.
LanguageData.GR_Wolfsnake.label: wolfsnake
LanguageData.GR_Wolfsnake.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Wolfsnake.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_Wolfsnake.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Beaverwolf.description:
An incredibly quick - mentally and physically - hunter with an overactive castoreum gland, effectively allowing them to actively convert meat and trees into vegetables. Technically vegans should be fine with it since, much like bees, it needs to be harvested and the beaverwolf will absolutely let you know if it doesn’t like you touching it.

A rodent hybrid with recessive canine genes. A very quick but cautious predator, this hybrid is both intelligent and frequently looking for its next meal. Able to freely roam and doesn’t require a pen, they are both carnivorous and dendrovorous while also offering daily harvests of castoreum which functions as a vegetable in dishes.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All rodent hybrids have fast gestation, as long as a fertility unblocker was used when creating them in the genomorpher.
LanguageData.GR_Beaverwolf.label: beaverwolf
LanguageData.GR_Beaverwolf.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Beaverwolf.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_Beaverwolf.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Molebear.description:
Far too independent to be a combat animal but certainly can both dish out and take pain. Works best in the background, minding its own business and digging up metals it finds. No one is really sure why but it probably has to do with those massive whiskers.

A rodent hybrid with recessive ursine genes. An independent predator with a very low metabolism and high toughness. Has an unusual behaviour where it’ll periodically dig for treasures, typically raw metals.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All rodent hybrids have fast gestation, as long as a fertility unblocker was used when creating them in the genomorpher.
LanguageData.GR_Molebear.label: molebear
LanguageData.GR_Molebear.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Molebear.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_Molebear.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Moleman.description:
A hulking monstrosity that is genuinely caring for those around them, taking great joy and actual personal pleasure through working diligently and caring for those it considers friends and family. Evidently, it was bred specifically by megacorporations to work in dangerous mines, because human greed never changes.

A rodent hybrid with recessive humanoid genes. Very low hunger rate and very intelligent, these gentle giants enjoy digging with their hands. They will sometimes work mining orders given the chance but they’re very slow due to their bulk.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Humanoid hybrids will periodically engage other colonists in conversation, talking about a variety of topics. This increases the social recreation needs of those they interact with by 10% (subject to diminishing returns like all joy types). If using Ideology, humanoid hybrids are subject to precepts. Molemen will periodically obey commands to mine. This will also work with the Quarry mod.
LanguageData.GR_Moleman.label: moleman
LanguageData.GR_Moleman.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Moleman.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_Moleman.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Rabbitcat.description:
For a combination of two relatively small animals, the rabbitcat is fucking massive and has a robust physique. It knows no fear and will tackle anything it wants to eat. Some say this was the other attempt to create the Rabbit of Caerbannog, because who says geneticists don’t have a sick sense of humour.

A rodent hybrid with recessive feline genes. Very intelligent considering the source material, these hybrids are fast, lethal and possess a robust physique with rapid natural regenerative capabilities. Quite stubborn so they require frequent, competent training but they aren’t afraid to show their appreciation.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Like all cat hybrids, they have an improved melee dodge ability and a short nuzzle period. All rodent hybrids have fast gestation, as long as a fertility unblocker was used when creating them in the genomorpher.
LanguageData.GR_Rabbitcat.label: rabbitcat
LanguageData.GR_Rabbitcat.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Rabbitcat.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_Rabbitchicken.description:
Legends say of an ancient and vicious monster, a creature so foul, so cruel that no man yet has fought with it and lived. This was a somewhat successful attempt, as its weakness lulls the enemy into a false sense of security so that it can rip their throat out. So do you feel lucky, punk?

A rodent hybrid with recessive avian genes. A tiny, weak, fast-moving pest with an insane appetite for its size. Incredibly fast gestation period and a 1% chance to instantly kill when attacking.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All rodent hybrids have fast gestation, as long as a fertility unblocker was used when creating them in the genomorpher.
LanguageData.GR_Rabbitchicken.label: rabbitchicken
LanguageData.GR_Rabbitchicken.tools.1.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Ratffalo.description:
An interesting pack animal that packs an incredible punch that also pisses and shits everywhere. That ain’t even the worst part, because these rodents of unusual size are also plague carriers with complete immunity to toxicity. Sometimes scientists just can’t leave well enough alone.

A rodent hybrid with recessive muffalo genes. Quite clever, almost absurdly lethal and quick on its feet. While they are herbivores, they will still happily munch on corpses thanks to their large appetite. Not as short a gestation period compared to other primary rodent hybrids, but every bite transmits the plague.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All rodent hybrids have fast gestation, as long as a fertility unblocker was used when creating them in the genomorpher.
LanguageData.GR_Ratffalo.label: ratffalo
LanguageData.GR_Ratffalo.tools.1.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Squirralope.description:
It’s small, it’s cute, it’s a ticking time bomb waiting for some idiot to think it looks delicious. It’s not going to be a big explosion, but that doesn’t matter much when it’s in your mouth.

A rodent hybrid with recessive boomalope genes. Small, weak but fast, this hybrid suits those looking for a fuel production method in a small package. Produces chemfuel much more efficiently compared to a boomalope but it needs to be milked every day.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All rodent hybrids have fast gestation, as long as a fertility unblocker was used when creating them in the genomorpher.
LanguageData.GR_Squirralope.label: squirralope
LanguageData.GR_Squirralope.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Squirralope.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_Squirralope.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Bearalope.description:
An unusual concoction of traits that was favoured by a particular client who was obsessed on making things explode. Earned the nickname Boom-Boom as they would often make requests along the lines of “two more booms, wasn’t paying attention”.

An ursine hybrid with recessive boomalope genes. Lacking intelligence compared to a bear while also missing out on some strength and confidence in hunting. However, they can be commanded to detonate a limited amount of fuel sacks for increased combat versatility in exchange for hurting themselves intentionally.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All primary bear hybrids have stun attacks. The "Detonate chemfuel" ability can be used to cause a powerful "Bomb" damage type explosion surrounding the hybrid. Bear in mind (pun intended) that this will damage the hybrid too!
LanguageData.GR_Bearalope.label: bearalope
LanguageData.GR_Bearalope.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Bearalope.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_Bearalope.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Bearcat.description:
Massive, fluffy murder babies is just one of the many “cute” nicknames made for this animal, their only downside being that they’re unable to purr. Because they’re so large, they often end up knocking over the people they’re trying to show affection to before laying on top of them for warmth.

An ursine hybrid with recessive feline genes. Retaining their intelligence and being faster and more fierce in combat, they are adorable killing machines that love to nuzzle. They prefer easier prey and can be slaughtered for a valuable pelt, you monster.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All primary bear hybrids have stun attacks. Like all cat hybrids, they have an improved melee dodge ability and a short nuzzle period. The pelt can be sold or used in the art workbench to create rugs.
LanguageData.GR_Bearcat.label: bearcat
LanguageData.GR_Bearcat.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Bearcat.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_Bearcat.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Bearchicken.description:
A surprisingly clean animal dubbed the “owl-bear” by those that find them amusing as they attempt to fly by rapidly flapping their stubby vestigial wings. That is until they get their eyes pecked out and flattened by their weight.

An ursine hybrid with recessive avian genes. Slightly dumber but easier to train, they lay eggs every couple of days and have the strength to protect their clutch. Slower than a bear but their ability to hover over terrain means they perform better in harsh terrain.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All primary bear hybrids have stun attacks. Although too heavy to fly, it can use its wings to nullify terrain movement costs.
LanguageData.GR_Bearchicken.label: bearchicken
LanguageData.GR_Bearchicken.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Bearchicken.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_Bearchicken.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Bearffalo.description:
A wonderful animal for those cold winter journeys, very popular as travellers snuggle into their fur at night to keep warm. Fights have even broken out over who gets to sleep with the oversized rug.

An ursine hybrid with recessive muffalo genes. Very filthy but a decently intelligent pack animal that produces good-quality wool and milk. Performs very well in cold environments and is more than capable of defending itself.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All primary bear hybrids have stun attacks.
LanguageData.GR_Bearffalo.label: bearffalo
LanguageData.GR_Bearffalo.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Bearffalo.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_Bearffalo.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Bearman.description:
A terrifying combination of the raw power of a bear and the intellect of a human, resulting in a creature that loves to abuse its prey. Not quite as strong due to the strange hand-paw shapes, but very energetic and eager to get their victims to catch these hands.

An ursine hybrid with recessive humanoid genes. Very high intellect but comparatively fragile compared to similar hybrids. Their bodies contain oversized and overstimulated adrenal glands that are found just under the surface of the skin, which can be regularly extracted for a substance analogous to Go-Juice.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Humanoid hybrids will periodically engage other colonists in conversation, talking about a variety of topics. This increases the social recreation needs of those they interact with by 10% (subject to diminishing returns like all joy types). If using Ideology, humanoid hybrids are subject to precepts.
LanguageData.GR_Bearman.label: bearman
LanguageData.GR_Bearman.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Bearman.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_Bearman.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Bearmole.description:
Not many people get to see a naked bear, and fewer still want a repeat viewing. This oversized mole-rat inspires plenty of fear with its massive teeth, but gets a lot of love and affection from those wanting a large, hairless pet that gnaws on the furniture.

An ursine hybrid with recessive rodent genes. Very slow both mentally and physically along with dietary restrictions, but very fierce in combat and immune to pain. Also they have the added benefit of having a short reproductive cycle, low metabolism and being completely fearless in hunting prey.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All primary bear hybrids have stun attacks. No pain sensitivity. This hybrid won't be knocked down in combat due to pain.
LanguageData.GR_Bearmole.label: bearmole
LanguageData.GR_Bearmole.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Bearmole.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_Bearmole.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Bearwolf.description:
Great care must be taken before considering raising this chonky boy, as they have a metabolism that matches their high ferocity and movement speed. Pretty useful in keeping egg-laying animal populations in check.

An ursine hybrid with recessive canine genes. A very fierce and intelligent combat hybrid with an incredible appetite. As they are almost constantly hungry, they will dig for berries when they’re hungry provided it’s growing season.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All primary bear hybrids have stun attacks.
LanguageData.GR_Bearwolf.label: bearwolf
LanguageData.GR_Bearwolf.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Bearwolf.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_Bearwolf.tools.3.label: head
An ultratech staff threaded with psychic focusing materials. While it is a solid melee weapon, its main purpose is to enhance the wielder's psychic sensitivity and neural heat dissipation, allowing more use of psychic powers. \nThis weapon has an onboard persona that can bond to only a single person. The wielder and intelligent weapon can synchronize their reflexes and attack with frightening speed, accuracy, and creativity. Once bonded to a wielder, the weapon's persona will refuse to be wielded by anyone else.
LanguageData.VPE_Apparel_EltexCape.description: A long cape of mystical design. It hangs over one side of the torso. Its special materials enhance the wearer's psychic sensitivity and dissipate neural heat.
LanguageData.VPE_Apparel_EltexCape.label: eltex cape
LanguageData.VPE_Apparel_EltexMask.description: An artistically impressive mask emanating psychic energy. Hides the face of the wearer but provides little physical protection. Its special materials enhance the wearer's psychic sensitivity and dissipate neural heat.
LanguageData.VPE_Apparel_EltexMask.label: eltex mask
LanguageData.VPE_Apparel_PlateArmorPrestige.descripti: Overlapping solid plates of armor covering the entire body from neck to feet.\n\nExpensive and beautiful, prestige armor like this is usually made for those of high status. Embedded eltex threads also enhance the wearer's psychic sensitivity.
LanguageData.VPE_Apparel_PlateArmorPrestige.label: prestige plate armor
LanguageData.VPE_Psyring.description: An eltex ring created by a skilled technomancer. It holds the knowledge of one particular psycast, and can be used by other psycasters to cast it.
LanguageData.VPE_Psyring.label: psyring
LanguageData.VPE_IceCrystal.description: A small crystal of ice that cools down the temperature in the room. Can help freeze perishable items.
LanguageData.VPE_IceCrystal.label: ice crystal
LanguageData.VPE_IceWall.description: A fragile wall made of ice. It will melt in heat.
LanguageData.VPE_IceWall.label: ice wall
LanguageData.VPE_Skipdoor.description: A psychic entry-point to a portal network.
LanguageData.VPE_Skipdoor.label: skipdoor
LanguageData.VPE_EltexOre.label: eltex ore
LanguageData.VPE_BrambleSpawner.label: emerging
LanguageData.VPE_Darkness.label: darkness
LanguageData.VPE_Decoy.label: decoy
LanguageData.VPE_FireBeam.label: fire beam
LanguageData.VPE_FireTornado.label: fire tornado
LanguageData.VPE_HurricaneMaker.label: hurricane maker
LanguageData.VPE_Shrineshield_Large.label: large shrineshield
LanguageData.VPE_Shrineshield_Small.label: small shrineshield
LanguageData.VPE_SkyChanger.label: sky changer
LanguageData.VPE_TimeSphere.label: time sphere
LanguageData.VPE_TreeSpawner.label: emerging
LanguageData.VPE_Vortex.label: vortex
LanguageData.VPE_WildPlantSpawner.label: emerging
LanguageData.VPE_Filth_Metal.label: metal slag
LanguageData.VPE_Filth_Rock.label: rock rubble
LanguageData.VPE_Eltex.description: An extremely mysterious mineral that is both malleable and serves as a physical conduit for psycasts. As psycasting originates from archotech engines, some speculate that either these minerals were manufactured by the archotechs or that they developed their engines using this strange mineral.
LanguageData.VPE_Eltex.label: eltex
LanguageData.VPE_BallLightning.label: ball lightning
LanguageData.VPE_Bolt.description: A bolt launched by a staticlord
LanguageData.VPE_Bolt.label: bolt
LanguageData.VPE_ChainBolt.label: chain bolt
LanguageData.VPE_EyeBlast.label: eye blast
LanguageData.VPE_Flameball.description: A ball of flame launched by a conflagrator
LanguageData.VPE_Flameball.label: flameball
LanguageData.VPE_FrostRay.label: frost ray
LanguageData.VPE_IceSpike.description: A spike of ice launched by the frostshaper.
LanguageData.VPE_IceSpike.label: ice spike
LanguageData.VPE_RockConstruct.description: A piece of rock chunk, animated by powerful psychic energy. It will do simple work around the colony tirelessly and with maximum dedication.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color>Constructs generate constant heat for the caster that summoned them. Keeping too many constructs alive could prevent the psycaster from casting other abilities.
LanguageData.VPE_RockConstruct.label: rock construct
LanguageData.VPE_RockConstruct.tools.0.label: head
LanguageData.VPE_SteelConstruct.description: A piece of steel slag, animated by powerful psychic energy. It will engage nearby threats and defend the master from any harm that may come to them.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color>Constructs generate constant heat for the caster that summoned them. Keeping too many constructs alive could prevent the psycaster from casting other abilities.
LanguageData.VPE_SteelConstruct.label: steel construct
LanguageData.VPE_SteelConstruct.tools.0.label: head
LanguageData.VPE_SummonedSkeleton.description: A psychically re-animated husk of a deceased individual. Extremely aggressive, but will soon wither away into nothing.
LanguageData.VPE_SummonedSkeleton.label: skeleton
LanguageData.VPE_SummonedSkeleton.tools.0.label: left fist
LanguageData.VPE_SummonedSkeleton.tools.1.label: right fist
LanguageData.VPE_SmallMeteorite_Incoming.label: small meteorite
LanguageData.VPE_SoulFromSky.label: soul
LanguageData.VPE_MeleeWeapon_EltexDagger.description: A medieval dagger threaded with psychic focusing materials. While it enhances the wielder’s psychic sensitivity and neural heat dissipation, it also serves as an excellent melee weapon, as it follows the same idea as any other knife: A handle for holding and a sharp edge for cutting.
LanguageData.VPE_MeleeWeapon_EltexDagger.label: eltex dagger
LanguageData.VPE_MeleeWeapon_EltexMace.description: A medieval mace threaded with psychic focusing materials. While it enhances the wielder’s psychic sensitivity and neural heat dissipation, it also serves as an excellent melee weapon, engineered for efficient swinging and deadly impacts.
LanguageData.VPE_MeleeWeapon_EltexMace.label: eltex mace
LanguageData.VPE_MeleeWeapon_EltexMace.tools.0.label: handle
LanguageData.VPE_MeleeWeapon_EltexMace.tools.1.label: head
LanguageData.VPE_MeleeWeapon_EltexSword.description: A medieval sword threaded with psychic focusing materials. While it enhances the wielder’s psychic sensitivity and neural heat dissipation, it also serves as an excellent melee weapon. Good for stabbing or slashing. It's light and nimble, which sometimes lets it beat bigger, heavier weapons.
LanguageData.VPE_MeleeWeapon_EltexSword.label: eltex sword
LanguageData.VPE_MeleeWeapon_EltexSword.tools.0.label: handle
LanguageData.VPE_MeleeWeapon_EltexSword.tools.1.label: point
LanguageData.VPE_MeleeWeapon_EltexSword.tools.2.label: edge
LanguageData.VPE_HeatPearls.description: Mystical heat pearls created by a technomancer in a process of condensing psychic heat into a physical form. They can be thrown at the other psycasters to unleash an insane amount of psychic heat, disabling their psycasting ability for the short duration.
LanguageData.VPE_HeatPearls.label: heat pearls
LanguageData.VPE_HeatPearls.verbs.0.label: throw heat pearl
LanguageData.VAERoy_SwanNest.description: A beautiful nest of a swan.
LanguageData.VAERoy_SwanNest.label: swan nest
LanguageData.VAERoy_EggCraneFertilized.description: A fertilized crane egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into a baby crane. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
LanguageData.VAERoy_EggCraneFertilized.label: crane egg (fert.)
LanguageData.VAERoy_EggMegaChickenFertilized.descript: A fertilized megachicken egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into a megachicken. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
LanguageData.VAERoy_EggMegaChickenFertilized.label: megachicken egg (fert.)
LanguageData.VAERoy_EggPeacockFertilized.description: A fertilized peacock egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into a peacock. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
LanguageData.VAERoy_EggPeacockFertilized.label: peacock egg (fert.)
LanguageData.VAERoy_EggPheasantFertilized.description: A fertilized pheasant egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into a peacock. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
LanguageData.VAERoy_EggPheasantFertilized.label: pheasant egg (fert.)
LanguageData.VAERoy_EggQuailFertilized.description: A fertilized quail egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into a quail. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
LanguageData.VAERoy_EggQuailFertilized.label: quail egg (fert.)
LanguageData.VAERoy_EggQuailUnfertilized.description: An unfertilized quail egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
LanguageData.VAERoy_EggQuailUnfertilized.label: quail egg (unfert.)
LanguageData.VAERoy_EggSwanFertilized.description: A fertilized swan egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into a baby swan. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
LanguageData.VAERoy_EggSwanFertilized.label: swan egg (fert.)
LanguageData.VAERoy_EggSwanUnfertilized.description: An unfertilized swan egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
LanguageData.VAERoy_EggSwanUnfertilized.label: swan egg (unfert.)
LanguageData.VAERoy_EggPeacockUnfertilized.descriptio: An unfertilized peacock egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
LanguageData.VAERoy_EggPeacockUnfertilized.label: peacock egg (unfert.)
LanguageData.VAERoy_QuailMeat.description: Raw meat from a quail, extremely tasty. Instantly turns any simple or fine meal it’s used in into a lavish meal. It will also turn grills and bakes from Vanilla Cooking expanded into their lavish variations.
LanguageData.VAERoy_QuailMeat.label: royal poultry
LanguageData.VAERoy_AngoraRabbit.description: Small, domestic rabbit that has developed an ability to enhance the neural heat dissipation of anyone they bond with. Unfortunately, as a side effect, it the bonded master of the angora rabbit dies, the rabbit will also die of sadness and loss.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> As long as an Angora Rabbit is bonded to a colonist, this colonist will receive a psychic connection that helps with neural heat dissipation.
LanguageData.VAERoy_AngoraRabbit.label: angora rabbit
LanguageData.VAERoy_AngoraRabbit.tools.1.label: head
LanguageData.VAERoy_Crane.description: A large, long legged bird species, cranes are a symbol of royalty and nobility and often populate royal gardens. It is believed that people of imperial rank will appreciate having plenty of cranes around them.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> If you have at least 5 cranes under your control, any pawn with a nobility rank of any kind receives a +5 mood boost.
LanguageData.VAERoy_Crane.label: crane
LanguageData.VAERoy_Crane.tools.0.label: claws
LanguageData.VAERoy_Crane.tools.1.label: beak
LanguageData.VAERoy_Crane.tools.2.label: head
LanguageData.VAERoy_Megachicken.description: The unusual royal farm bird, the megachicken is raised by spoiled royalty as a pet project. This bird simply never stops growing, and if taken care of, it can reach gargantuan sizes. Royalty is known to compare their megacocks, and usually the one with the biggest specimen wins. Those that lose often argue that it’s not about the size, it’s about what you do with it.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Megachicken have five growth stages, at 0, 3, 8, 12 and 18 years of age. Their abilities get increasingly powerful as they age.
LanguageData.VAERoy_Megachicken.label: megachicken
LanguageData.VAERoy_Megachicken.tools.0.label: claws
LanguageData.VAERoy_Megachicken.tools.1.label: beak
LanguageData.VAERoy_Megachicken.tools.2.label: head
LanguageData.VAERoy_Orangutan.description: A great ape invented and patented on Sophiamunda, orangutans are intelligent and can hold a normal conversation with humans. They are often used as home teachers in the Empire, educating young nobles in history, arts and correct etiquette.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> When holding sophisticated conversations with humans, they boost their social recreation. Furthermore, each conversation with an orangutan gives a little bit of XP to a random skill.
LanguageData.VAERoy_Orangutan.label: orangutan
LanguageData.VAERoy_Orangutan.tools.0.label: left fist
LanguageData.VAERoy_Orangutan.tools.1.label: right fist
LanguageData.VAERoy_Orangutan.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.VAERoy_Peacock.description: Beautiful, majestic royal birds genetically altered to be able to bestow psychic energy onto people they nuzzle.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Peacocks will periodically nuzzle your colonists. This increases Psychic Sensitivity of the nuzzled person by 25% for 12 hours. This stacks up to 8 times, resulting in +200% Psychic sensitivity if nuzzled by 8 peacocks.
LanguageData.VAERoy_Peacock.label: peacock
LanguageData.VAERoy_Peacock.tools.0.label: claws
LanguageData.VAERoy_Peacock.tools.1.label: beak
LanguageData.VAERoy_Peacock.tools.2.label: head
LanguageData.VAERoy_Pheasant.description: Small, noble poultry that was genetically engineered to be an excellent small game animal. When pheasants run away from danger, they emit a psychic invisibility blast, making their allies hidden from sight.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Whenever a pheasant is in danger (it starts fleeing), it emits a psychic shockwave in radius of 6 cells around itself. It only does it once every 24 hours. This shockwave gives all pawns under player control in that radius Psychic Invisibility for a short period of time, making them untargetable by enemies.
LanguageData.VAERoy_Pheasant.label: pheasant
LanguageData.VAERoy_Pheasant.tools.0.label: claws
LanguageData.VAERoy_Pheasant.tools.1.label: beak
LanguageData.VAERoy_Pheasant.tools.2.label: head
LanguageData.VAERoy_Quail.description: Royal poultry bred specifically for human consumption, quail meat is one of a kind, with a taste so succulent it turns any meal into a lavish meal.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Their extremely tasty meat instantly turns any simple or fine meal it’s used in into a lavish meal. It will also turn grills and bakes from Vanilla Cooking expanded into their lavish variations.
LanguageData.VAERoy_Quail.label: quail
LanguageData.VAERoy_Quail.tools.0.label: claws
LanguageData.VAERoy_Quail.tools.1.label: beak
LanguageData.VAERoy_Quail.tools.2.label: head
LanguageData.VAERoy_RoyalTiger.description: A large, genetically engineered sabretooth tiger that evolved in captivity. Nobility often buys them for their spoiled children, as in reality, royal tiger is not dangerous at all, and prefers fleeing at the first sight of danger.
LanguageData.VAERoy_RoyalTiger.label: royal tiger
LanguageData.VAERoy_RoyalTiger.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.VAERoy_RoyalTiger.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.VAERoy_RoyalTiger.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.VAERoy_Swan.description: One of the largest birds capable of flying, swans are often kept by royalty due to the beautiful nests of flowers they build for themselves. These nests have an extremely high beauty, but a swan will never build more than one.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Swans create their own sleeping spots that have extremely high beauty. These nests are auto-asigned to them as sleeping spots on creation.
LanguageData.VAERoy_Swan.label: swan
LanguageData.VAERoy_Swan.tools.0.label: claws
LanguageData.VAERoy_Swan.tools.1.label: beak
LanguageData.VAERoy_Swan.tools.2.label: head
LanguageData.AEXP_BeaverDam.description: Beaver dams or beaver impoundments are dams built by beavers to provide ponds as protection against predators such as coyotes, wolves, and bears, and to provide easy access to food during winter.
LanguageData.AEXP_BeaverDam.label: beaver dam
LanguageData.AEXP_WalrusTusk.description: A walrus's tusk. It is quite valuable, but somewhat unwieldy as a melee weapon.
LanguageData.AEXP_WalrusTusk.label: walrus tusk
LanguageData.AEXP_WalrusTusk.tools.0.label: point
LanguageData.AEXP_WalrusTusk.tools.1.label: base
LanguageData.AEXP_EggAlligatorFertilized.description: A fertilized alligator egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into an alligator. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
LanguageData.AEXP_EggAlligatorFertilized.label: alligator egg (fert.)
LanguageData.AEXP_EggAnacondaFertilized.description: A fertilized anaconda egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into an anaconda. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
LanguageData.AEXP_EggAnacondaFertilized.label: anaconda egg (fert.)
LanguageData.AEXP_EggCrocodileFertilized.description: A fertilized crocodile egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into a crocodile. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
LanguageData.AEXP_EggCrocodileFertilized.label: crocodile egg (fert.)
LanguageData.AEXP_EggCrocodileUnfertilized.descriptio: An unfertilized crocodile egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
LanguageData.AEXP_EggCrocodileUnfertilized.label: crocodile egg (unfert.)
LanguageData.AEXP_EggDesertTortoiseFertilized.descrip: A fertilized desert tortoise egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into a baby desert tortoise. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
LanguageData.AEXP_EggDesertTortoiseFertilized.label: desert tortoise egg (fert.)
LanguageData.AEXP_EggDesertTortoiseUnfertilized.descr: An unfertilized desert tortoise egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
LanguageData.AEXP_EggDesertTortoiseUnfertilized.label: desert tortoise egg (unfert.)
LanguageData.AEXP_EggGilaMonsterFertilized.descriptio: A fertilized gila monster egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into a baby gila monster. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
LanguageData.AEXP_EggGilaMonsterFertilized.label: gila monster egg (fert.)
LanguageData.AEXP_EggGilaMonsterUnfertilized.descript: An unfertilized desert gila monster egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
LanguageData.AEXP_EggGilaMonsterUnfertilized.label: gila monster egg (unfert.)
LanguageData.AEXP_EggPenguinFertilized.description: A fertilized penguin egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into a baby penguin. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
LanguageData.AEXP_EggPenguinFertilized.label: penguin egg (fert.)
LanguageData.AEXP_EggPlatypusFertilized.description: A fertilized platypus egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into a baby platypus. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
LanguageData.AEXP_EggPlatypusFertilized.label: platypus egg (fert.)
LanguageData.AEXP_EggRattlesnakeFertilized.descriptio: A fertilized rattlesnake egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into a rattlesnake. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
LanguageData.AEXP_EggRattlesnakeFertilized.label: rattlesnake egg (fert.)
LanguageData.AEXP_EggRattlesnakeUnfertilized.descript: An unfertilized rattlesnake egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
LanguageData.AEXP_EggRattlesnakeUnfertilized.label: rattlesnake egg (unfert.)
LanguageData.AEXP_EggWildGooseFertilized.description: A fertilized wild goose egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into a baby wild gosling. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
LanguageData.AEXP_EggWildGooseFertilized.label: wild goose egg (fert.)
LanguageData.AEXP_EggWildGooseUnfertilized.descriptio: An unfertilized wild goose egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
LanguageData.AEXP_EggWildGooseUnfertilized.label: wild goose egg (unfert.)
LanguageData.AEXP_EggAlligatorUnfertilized.descriptio: An unfertilized alligator egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
LanguageData.AEXP_EggAlligatorUnfertilized.label: alligator egg (unfert.)
LanguageData.AEXP_EggPenguinUnfertilized.description: An unfertilized penguin egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
LanguageData.AEXP_EggPenguinUnfertilized.label: penguin egg (unfert.)
LanguageData.AEXP_RawFish.description: Raw fish. A resourceful penguin has fished this.
LanguageData.AEXP_RawFish.label: raw fish
LanguageData.AEXP_RawFish.tools.0.label: raw fish
LanguageData.AEXP_Leather_Hippopotamus.description: A thick leather of a hippopotamus. Not only it's extremely durable, but it also provides considerable heat insulation.
LanguageData.AEXP_Leather_Hippopotamus.label: hippopotamus leather
LanguageData.AEXP_Leather_Lion.description: A strong, luxurious leather of the leader of the animal kingdom. Often sought after by urbworld philanthropists.
LanguageData.AEXP_Leather_Lion.label: lion pelt
LanguageData.AEXP_Leather_Pinniped.description: Tanned and dried leather from a Pinniped. It is of average strength but insulates well against heat.
LanguageData.AEXP_Leather_Pinniped.label: pinniped leather
LanguageData.AEXP_Leather_Platypus.description: Platypus fur was considered a valuable commodity in the days of old Terra, a fact that almost drove the Platypus to extinction. Luckily, the animal was protected and thrived again.
LanguageData.AEXP_Leather_Platypus.label: platypus leather
LanguageData.AEXP_Leather_Tiger.description: Tigers were often the target of poachers in old Earth due to their luxurious pelts, a fact that almost drove them to extinction. With the near lawlessness of the Rim, tiger leather is seen as another exotic commodity to trade between settlements.
LanguageData.AEXP_Leather_Tiger.label: tiger leather
LanguageData.AEXP_Leather_Wildebeest.description: A thick but crumbly wildebeest fur and leather. Whilst it provides some degree of damage protection, the overall longevity of the item decreases.
LanguageData.AEXP_Leather_Wildebeest.label: wildebeest pelt
LanguageData.AEXP_MuskOxWool.description: The inner wool of the muskox, originally known in Inuktitut as Qiviuq. Stronger and warmer than sheep's wool, and softer than cashmere wool.
LanguageData.AEXP_MuskOxWool.label: musk ox wool
LanguageData.WoolCamel.description: Camel's wool. It provides good insulation from heat.
LanguageData.WoolCamel.label: camel wool
LanguageData.AEXP_Cheetah.description: These cats are lean and lanky, allowing them to reach incredible speeds. They have tan-colored fur with solid black, round spots, very long legs, a long narrow tail for balance, and a deep chest that contains large lungs.
LanguageData.AEXP_Cheetah.label: cheetah
LanguageData.AEXP_Coyote.description: A praire wolf native to desert and shrubland areas. It's capable of hunting much bigger prey, and is often found in packs, similar to a wolf.
LanguageData.AEXP_Coyote.label: coyote
LanguageData.AEXP_Coyote.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.AEXP_Coyote.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.AEXP_Coyote.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.AEXP_Crocodile.description: A large semi-aquatic reptile, most often found on the river banks. It's predatory insticts combined with an extremely strong jaw allow them to tackle much larger prey. Older crocodiles can grow to monstrous sizes.
LanguageData.AEXP_Crocodile.label: crocodile
LanguageData.AEXP_Giraffe.description: A giraffe is one of the tallest mammals in the galaxy, using it's long neck to reach leaves and branches unavailable for smaller herbivores.
LanguageData.AEXP_Giraffe.label: giraffe
LanguageData.AEXP_Giraffe.tools.0.label: left hoof
LanguageData.AEXP_Giraffe.tools.1.label: right hoof
LanguageData.AEXP_Giraffe.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.AEXP_Hippopotamus.description: A massive semi-aquatic hoofed mammal with thick skin and a very powerful jaw.\n\nExtremely difficult to tame, these monstrous beings often attack on a failed attempt.
LanguageData.AEXP_Hippopotamus.label: hippopotamus
LanguageData.AEXP_Hippopotamus.tools.1.label: left foot
LanguageData.AEXP_Hippopotamus.tools.2.label: right foot
LanguageData.AEXP_Hippopotamus.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.AEXP_Wildebeest.description: Also called Gnu, this wild cow earned it's name with it's menacing horns, pointed beard and large head. These animals are known from their migrations and the size of their herds.\n\nDue to its wildness, it produces milk at much slower rate than a standard cow.
LanguageData.AEXP_Wildebeest.label: wildebeest
LanguageData.AEXP_Wildebeest.tools.1.label: head
LanguageData.AEXP_Wildebeest.tools.2.label: left hoof
LanguageData.AEXP_Wildebeest.tools.3.label: right hoof
LanguageData.AEXP_Zebra.description: A member of the horse family native to arid and desert areas. Whilst it doesn't share the same bodysize as a horse, due to years of evolution it's one of the fastest hooved animals.
LanguageData.AEXP_Zebra.label: zebra
LanguageData.AEXP_Zebra.tools.0.label: head
LanguageData.AEXP_Zebra.tools.1.label: left hoof
LanguageData.AEXP_Zebra.tools.2.label: right hoof
LanguageData.AEXP_Boombat.description: Due to some insane experiment, these cute, fluffy rodents create a powerful fire-starting explosion when killed.
LanguageData.AEXP_Boombat.label: boombat
LanguageData.AEXP_Boombat.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.AEXP_Boombat.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.AEXP_Boombat.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.AEXP_Kangaroo.description: Kangaroo is a large, human-sized marsupial with strong legs and powerful arms. It's favoured by tribal hunters due to its tender meat and heat resistant leather. Kangaroos make for powerful adversaries in melee combat, and can be trained easily to become animal super-soldiers.
LanguageData.AEXP_Kangaroo.label: kangaroo
LanguageData.AEXP_Kangaroo.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.AEXP_Koala.description: Koalas are easily recognizable for their stout body and large, round heads with fluffy ears. They typically inhabit arid and temperate woodlands, but can rarely be seen in tropical areas. Favoured by royal families, koalas are great household animals due to their wonderful nuzzling effect.
LanguageData.AEXP_Koala.label: koala
LanguageData.AEXP_Koala.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.AEXP_Koala.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.AEXP_Koala.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.AEXP_Platypus.description: Platypus is the sole surviving representative of its animal family. They are beaver-tailed, duck billed mammals, known for the fact that they lay eggs. It is an omnivore animal, capable of killing small rodents with it's venomous ankle spurs.
LanguageData.AEXP_Platypus.label: platypus
LanguageData.AEXP_Platypus.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.AEXP_Platypus.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.AEXP_Platypus.tools.4.label: head
LanguageData.AEXP_ArcticCoyote.description: A small canine native to tundra and boreal forests. It's capable of hunting much bigger prey, and is often found in packs, similar to a wolf.
LanguageData.AEXP_ArcticCoyote.label: arctic coyote
LanguageData.AEXP_ArcticCoyote.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.AEXP_ArcticCoyote.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.AEXP_ArcticCoyote.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.AEXP_BlackBear.description: A medium sized omnivorous mammal adapted for cold climates. Bears' thick blubber and fur keeps them warm in winter.\n\nWhile their usual diet consists of fish, berries, honey, and scavenged meat, the black bear is capable of using its massive strength and deadly claws to kill live prey. They are startlingly quick for such lumbering creatures.
LanguageData.AEXP_BlackBear.label: black bear
LanguageData.AEXP_Otter.description: Long, slim, semi-aquatic mammal dieting mostly on fish and small plants. They are extremaly good swimmers and can hold breath under water for a prolonged amount of time.
LanguageData.AEXP_Otter.label: otter
LanguageData.AEXP_Otter.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.AEXP_Otter.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.AEXP_Otter.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.AEXP_CatAbyssinian.description: A slender, fine-boned, medium sized shrubland cat.
LanguageData.AEXP_CatAbyssinian.label: abyssinian cat
LanguageData.AEXP_CatAbyssinian.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.AEXP_CatAbyssinian.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.AEXP_CatAbyssinian.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.AEXP_CatBengal.description: A domesticated hybrid of a domestic cat and tropical leopard.
LanguageData.AEXP_CatBengal.label: bengal cat
LanguageData.AEXP_CatBengal.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.AEXP_CatBengal.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.AEXP_CatBengal.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.AEXP_CatBritishShorthair.description: A pedigreed version of a regular british cat, with a distinctively chunky body, dense coat and a very broad face. While being relatively powerful-looking due to the large chest and massive paws, these cats are absolutely harmless.
LanguageData.AEXP_CatBritishShorthair.label: british shorthair cat
LanguageData.AEXP_CatBritishShorthair.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.AEXP_CatBritishShorthair.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.AEXP_CatBritishShorthair.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.AEXP_CatMaineCoon.description: A largest domesticated cat, it has a distinctive physical appearance and valuable hunting skills. It's a large and sociable cat, hence it has been given a nickname "the gentle giant."
LanguageData.AEXP_CatMaineCoon.label: maine coon
LanguageData.AEXP_CatMaineCoon.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.AEXP_CatMaineCoon.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.AEXP_CatMaineCoon.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.AEXP_CatMunchkin.description: Also known as a sausage cat, this new cat breed has distinctively short legs. Sweet natured, playful and people-oriented, these cats are perfect companions for people with lazy lifestyle.
LanguageData.AEXP_CatMunchkin.label: munchkin cat
LanguageData.AEXP_CatMunchkin.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.AEXP_CatMunchkin.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.AEXP_CatMunchkin.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.AEXP_CatNorwegianForest.description: A cat adapted for very cold climates due to the top coat of glossy, water-shedding hair.
LanguageData.AEXP_CatNorwegianForest.label: norwegian forest cat
LanguageData.AEXP_CatNorwegianForest.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.AEXP_CatNorwegianForest.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.AEXP_CatNorwegianForest.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.AEXP_CatPersian.description: A long haired cat originating from desert wildcats, Persian cats are regal, intelligent and agile, which makes them excellent for hunting small rodents and birds.
LanguageData.AEXP_CatPersian.label: persian cat
LanguageData.AEXP_CatPersian.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.AEXP_CatPersian.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.AEXP_CatPersian.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.AEXP_CatSiamese.description: A cat with distinctively triangular head, blue eyes and long and muscular body, renowned for it's social nature.
LanguageData.AEXP_CatSiamese.label: siamese cat
LanguageData.AEXP_CatSiamese.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.AEXP_CatSiamese.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.AEXP_CatSiamese.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.AEXP_CatSomali.description: A shrubland cat, sharing the same family as the Abyssinian cat. It has a bushy tail and almond shaped eyes.
LanguageData.AEXP_CatSomali.label: somali cat
LanguageData.AEXP_CatSomali.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.AEXP_CatSomali.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.AEXP_CatSomali.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.AEXP_CatSphynx.description: A cat known for it's lack of fur, developed due to selective breeding. Being warm to the touch, they lose body temperature easily which makes it seek out heat sources.
LanguageData.AEXP_CatSphynx.label: sphynx cat
LanguageData.AEXP_CatSphynx.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.AEXP_CatSphynx.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.AEXP_CatSphynx.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.AEXP_DesertTortoise.description: This heavily armored desert-dwelling reptile is known for its slow moving speed and extremely hard, flat domed shell, making it tough to kill. Desert tortoises, despite their small size, are great combat animals and are relatively easy to tame.
LanguageData.AEXP_DesertTortoise.label: desert tortoise
LanguageData.AEXP_DesertTortoise.tools.0.label: beak
LanguageData.AEXP_DesertTortoise.tools.1.label: head
LanguageData.AEXP_GilaMonster.description: A large, slow moving type of lizard found mostly in the desert, known for it's highly venomous bite which inflicts a severe toxic build-up in it's prey. Outlanders to this day believe in tales which state that this lizard is a spawn from hell, and that it's foul breath is lethal.
LanguageData.AEXP_GilaMonster.label: gila monster
LanguageData.AEXP_GilaMonster.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.AEXP_GilaMonster.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.AEXP_GilaMonster.tools.2.label: teeth
LanguageData.AEXP_GilaMonster.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.AEXP_Hyena.description: A large dog-like carnivore most often found in the shrublands and deserts. A common trope in tribal legends, the laughter produced by these animals can sow fear during the long desert nights.
LanguageData.AEXP_Hyena.label: hyena
LanguageData.AEXP_Hyena.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.AEXP_Hyena.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.AEXP_Hyena.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.AEXP_Lion.description: A king of the animal kingdom, a lion is a muscular, deep-chested cat with a short head and a prominent mane. Being the biggest of the cat species, lions are excellent combat animals despite their wildness.
LanguageData.AEXP_Lion.label: lion
LanguageData.AEXP_Lion.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.AEXP_Lion.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.AEXP_Lion.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.AEXP_Beagle.description: A small scent hound developed primarily to hunt hares. It's intelligence and loyalty make it a valuable companion on the hunt.
LanguageData.AEXP_Beagle.label: beagle
LanguageData.AEXP_Beagle.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.AEXP_Beagle.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.AEXP_Beagle.tools.2.label: cute little teeth
LanguageData.AEXP_Beagle.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.AEXP_Chihuahua.description: A smallest dog, just a little bit bigger than a rat. Back in ancient times considered to be reincarnations of different deities and are to this day considered sacred.
LanguageData.AEXP_Chihuahua.label: chihuahua
LanguageData.AEXP_Chihuahua.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.AEXP_Chihuahua.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.AEXP_Chihuahua.tools.2.label: cute little teeth
LanguageData.AEXP_Chihuahua.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.AEXP_Corgi.description: Historically used for cattle herding, Corgis quickly became popular on imperial worlds and are recognized as a symbol of royalty.
LanguageData.AEXP_Corgi.label: corgi
LanguageData.AEXP_Corgi.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.AEXP_Corgi.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.AEXP_Corgi.tools.2.label: cute little teeth
LanguageData.AEXP_Corgi.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.AEXP_FrenchBulldog.description: Originally used for bull-baiting, which is an act of pitting one species of animal against bulls, these bulldogs are fierce opponents and due to their small body-size, they are fast, agile and lethal.
LanguageData.AEXP_FrenchBulldog.label: french bulldog
LanguageData.AEXP_FrenchBulldog.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.AEXP_FrenchBulldog.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.AEXP_FrenchBulldog.tools.2.label: cute little teeth
LanguageData.AEXP_FrenchBulldog.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.AEXP_GermanShepherd.description: A large sized working dog. Easy to train and good at following instructions, they are often seen accompanying glitterworld police forces and rimworld farmers.
LanguageData.AEXP_GermanShepherd.label: german shepherd
LanguageData.AEXP_GermanShepherd.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.AEXP_GermanShepherd.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.AEXP_GermanShepherd.tools.2.label: bite
LanguageData.AEXP_GermanShepherd.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.AEXP_GreatDane.description: One of the largest species of dogs, Great Danes are dignified, elegant and strong.
LanguageData.AEXP_GreatDane.label: great dane
LanguageData.AEXP_GreatDane.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.AEXP_GreatDane.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.AEXP_GreatDane.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.AEXP_Poodle.description: Large, pampered dogs, historically used for bird hunting, now mostly residing in imperial palaces and royal gardens.
LanguageData.AEXP_Poodle.label: poodle
LanguageData.AEXP_Poodle.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.AEXP_Poodle.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.AEXP_Poodle.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.AEXP_Pug.description: A small dog with physically distinctive features of a wrinkly, short-muzzled face, and curled tail. They are known for being extremely social and friendly, however due to years of genetic alterations they have a very short lifespan. Whatever life they have, they will always cheer up people around them.
LanguageData.AEXP_Pug.label: pug
LanguageData.AEXP_Pug.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.AEXP_Pug.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.AEXP_Pug.tools.2.label: cute little teeth
LanguageData.AEXP_Pug.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.AEXP_Rottweiler.description: A large domestic dog and one of the oldest herding breeds. Being one of the most hard working dogs, it is a perfect companions for farmers and cattle herders.
LanguageData.AEXP_Rottweiler.label: rottweiler
LanguageData.AEXP_Rottweiler.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.AEXP_Rottweiler.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.AEXP_Rottweiler.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.AEXP_Shih-Tzu.description: An oriental toy dog, Shih-Tzu have extremely long and soft hair. While they are kind of useless, petting them provides people with great comfort.
LanguageData.AEXP_Shih-Tzu.label: shih-tzu
LanguageData.AEXP_Shih-Tzu.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.AEXP_Shih-Tzu.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.AEXP_Shih-Tzu.tools.2.label: cute little teeth
LanguageData.AEXP_Shih-Tzu.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.AEXP_WelshTerrier.description: A hunting dog, welsh terriers are perfect for small rodents, foxes and badgers. Due to their intelligence they are perfect at way-finding, always able to return home after running into the woods.
LanguageData.AEXP_WelshTerrier.label: welsh terrier
LanguageData.AEXP_WelshTerrier.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.AEXP_WelshTerrier.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.AEXP_WelshTerrier.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.AEXP_Camel.description: A large land mammal adapted to arid environments. Domesticated since ancient times, its wool and leather are both exceptionally good at insulating against the desert heat, and its milk is quite nourishing. It can be used as a pack animal, but two humps make it extremely uncomfortable to ride.\n\nBy storing water in its large humps and reclaiming moisture from its breath as it exhales, it can go weeks without drinking.
LanguageData.AEXP_Camel.label: camel
LanguageData.AEXP_Camel.tools.0.label: left hoof
LanguageData.AEXP_Camel.tools.1.label: right hoof
LanguageData.AEXP_Camel.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.AEXP_Megascorpion.description: A giant, genetically-engineered scorpion. Once created as a perfect assassination tool, it is not often seen returning to it's natural habitat - desert. A very potent poison in it's stinger helps it in tacking even much bigger prey. A eusocial creature, it cannot reproduce individually.
LanguageData.AEXP_Megascorpion.label: megascorpion
LanguageData.AEXP_Megascorpion.tools.0.label: stinger
LanguageData.AEXP_Megascorpion.tools.1.label: mandibles
LanguageData.AEXP_Megascorpion.tools.2.label: head
LanguageData.AEXP_Rattlesnake.description: A highly venomous snake, recieving it's name from a rattle at the end of their tail. The sound generated sounds as a warning to any predators, however in case it doesn't work, rattlesnake's venom-fangs inflict extremely painful toxin.
LanguageData.AEXP_Rattlesnake.label: rattlesnake
LanguageData.AEXP_Rattlesnake.tools.0.label: venom-fangs
LanguageData.AEXP_Rattlesnake.tools.1.label: head
LanguageData.AEXP_Penguin.description: An aquatic, flightless bird, only found in arctic regions. Often seen in massive flocks, penguins are fierce when cornered, and can quickly overrun any hunter incompetent enough to get close to them.
LanguageData.AEXP_Penguin.label: penguin
LanguageData.AEXP_Penguin.tools.0.label: claws
LanguageData.AEXP_Penguin.tools.1.label: beak
LanguageData.AEXP_Penguin.tools.2.label: head
LanguageData.AEXP_Seal.description: A medium sized semi-aquatic mammal, which only comes ashore to eat, mate and escape from predators. Being an aggresive hunter, seals are capable of tackling much bigger prey and are known for cannibalism if the food is scarce.
LanguageData.AEXP_Seal.label: seal
LanguageData.AEXP_Seal.tools.0.label: left fore-flipper
LanguageData.AEXP_Seal.tools.1.label: right fore-flipper
LanguageData.AEXP_Seal.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.AEXP_Walrus.description: A large marine animal only found in the arctic regions of the planet, a walrus has prominent tusks which are a valuable luxury good across the planet.
LanguageData.AEXP_Walrus.label: walrus
LanguageData.AEXP_Walrus.tools.1.label: head
LanguageData.AEXP_Badger.description: Badgers are small nocturnal mammals with flat, wedge-shaped bodies, broad feet with long claws and coarse hair. Badgers have strong limbs and sharp claws that help them dig burrows and find food underground. They are omnivorous, with a diet consisting largely of earthworms, insects, grubs and small animals such as hedgehogs and rabbits.
LanguageData.AEXP_Badger.label: badger
LanguageData.AEXP_Badger.tools.0.label: left paw
LanguageData.AEXP_Badger.tools.1.label: right paw
LanguageData.AEXP_Badger.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.AEXP_Beaver.description: A large semiaquatic rodent with a flat, paddle-shaped tail and webbed hind feet. Beavers are renowned for their innate construction skills, building their homes, or "lodges", out of sticks, twigs, rocks, and mud in lakes, streams, and tidal river deltas. Beavers are well known for building dams across streams and constructing their lodges in the artificial ponds which form.
LanguageData.AEXP_Beaver.label: beaver
LanguageData.AEXP_Beaver.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.AEXP_Beaver.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.AEXP_Beaver.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.AEXP_Hedgehog.description: Hedgehogs are diminutive mammals with cone-shaped faces, short legs and bodies that are covered with porcupine-like quills. When in danger, the hedgehog rolls into a ball so that the quills cover the entire area of their exposed body and protects the animal from predators.
LanguageData.AEXP_Hedgehog.label: hedgehog
LanguageData.AEXP_Hedgehog.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.AEXP_Hedgehog.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.AEXP_Hedgehog.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.AEXP_RedPanda.description: The red panda is a small mammal, slightly larger than a domestic cat, with a bear-like body and thick russet fur. The belly and limbs are black, and there are white markings on the side of the head and above its small eyes. Red pandas are very skillful and acrobatic animals that predominantly stay in trees. It is a solitary animal, mainly active from dusk to dawn, though they can be quite affectionate if properly tamed.
LanguageData.AEXP_RedPanda.label: red panda
LanguageData.AEXP_RedPanda.tools.0.label: left fist
LanguageData.AEXP_RedPanda.tools.1.label: right fist
LanguageData.AEXP_RedPanda.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.AEXP_WildGoose.description: A non-domesticated grey goose. Compared to their domesticated brethren, which have been selected over thousands of years for larger size, wild geese don't lay as many eggs, and they are renowned for their aggressive temperament. Peace was never an option.
LanguageData.AEXP_WildGoose.label: wild goose
LanguageData.AEXP_WildGoose.tools.0.label: claws
LanguageData.AEXP_WildGoose.tools.1.label: beak
LanguageData.AEXP_WildGoose.tools.2.label: head
LanguageData.AEXP_Gorilla.description: These ground-dwelling herbivorous apes are the largest living primates, and they are the next closest living relatives to humans after the chimpanzees and bonobos. Like all the great apes, the arms of the gorilla are longer than its legs. Gorillas are usually surprisingly shy, though they may show aggression by beating their chests and charging if their social group is threatened.
LanguageData.AEXP_Gorilla.label: gorilla
LanguageData.AEXP_Gorilla.tools.0.label: left fist
LanguageData.AEXP_Gorilla.tools.1.label: right fist
LanguageData.AEXP_Gorilla.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.AEXP_Jaguar.description: A large felid species and the only existing member of the Panthera native to the Americas in old Earth. This spotted cat closely resembles the leopard, but is usually larger and sturdier. The jaguar enjoys swimming and is largely a solitary, opportunistic, stalk-and-ambush predator at the top of the food chain.
LanguageData.AEXP_Jaguar.label: jaguar
LanguageData.AEXP_Lemur.description: A small primate, lemurs are arboreal, and usually nocturnal. They have a pointed snout, large eyes, and a long tail. Although they are social creatures, they have traditionally been regarded as being less intelligent than other primates, since they don't exhibit behaviours of tool use in the wild.
LanguageData.AEXP_Lemur.label: lemur
LanguageData.AEXP_Lemur.tools.0.label: left fist
LanguageData.AEXP_Lemur.tools.1.label: right fist
LanguageData.AEXP_Lemur.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.AEXP_Mandrill.description: Mandrills are one of the largest of all monkeys. They are shy and reclusive primates that live only in rain forests. Mandrills are extremely colorful, perhaps more so than any other mammal. They are easily identifiable by the blue and red skin on their faces and their brightly hued rumps.
LanguageData.AEXP_Mandrill.label: mandrill
LanguageData.AEXP_Mandrill.tools.0.label: left fist
LanguageData.AEXP_Mandrill.tools.1.label: right fist
LanguageData.AEXP_Mandrill.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.AEXP_Tapir.description: A large, herbivorous mammal, similar in shape to a pig, with a short nose trunk, tapirs inhabit jungle and forest regions.
LanguageData.AEXP_Tapir.label: tapir
LanguageData.AEXP_Tapir.tools.1.label: head
LanguageData.AEXP_Tiger.description: The tiger is the largest species among the big cats. It is most recognisable for its dark vertical stripes on reddish-orange fur with a lighter underside. It is an apex predator, territorial and generally solitary. In the wild, tigers mostly feed on large and medium-sized mammals.
LanguageData.AEXP_Tiger.label: tiger
LanguageData.AEXP_Tiger.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.AEXP_Tiger.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.AEXP_Tiger.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.AEXP_Alligator.description: The alligator is a large reptile that inhabits freshwater wetlands. They are apex predators and consume fish, amphibians, reptiles, birds, and mammals. Like other crocodilians, healthy alligators may continue to grow throughout their lives.
LanguageData.AEXP_Alligator.label: alligator
LanguageData.AEXP_Anaconda.description: The anaconda is the heaviest and one of the longest known snake species, reaching lengths of 5 meters. They are a primarily nocturnal species that tends to spend most of its life in or around water.
LanguageData.AEXP_Anaconda.label: anaconda
LanguageData.AEXP_Anaconda.tools.1.label: head
LanguageData.AEXP_IndianElephant.description: The Indian elephant is one of the largest unmodified land animals. In general, Indian elephants are smaller, have smaller ears, but relatively broader skulls and larger trunks than African elephants, and have the highest body point on the head.
LanguageData.AEXP_IndianElephant.label: indian elephant
LanguageData.AEXP_IndianElephant.tools.0.label: left foot
LanguageData.AEXP_IndianElephant.tools.1.label: right foot
LanguageData.AEXP_IndianElephant.tools.2.label: head
LanguageData.AEXP_MegaWolverine.description: A giant, genetically-engineered predator with massive paws to rip apart prey. Thick fur allows it to survive the coldest of climates, whilst it strength allows it to hunt even the largest of mammals for food.
LanguageData.AEXP_MegaWolverine.label: megawolverine
LanguageData.AEXP_MegaWolverine.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.AEXP_MegaWolverine.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.AEXP_MegaWolverine.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.AEXP_Moose.description: The largest and heaviest member of the deer family, well-adapted to life in cold climates. Can easily be identified due to the size of the antlers and long, thin legs. Cannot carry packs, but domesticated moose can be milked. Unlike most other deer species, moose do not form herds and are solitary animals. Although generally slow-moving and sedentary, moose can become aggressive and move quickly if angered or startled.
LanguageData.AEXP_Moose.label: moose
LanguageData.AEXP_Moose.tools.0.label: left hoof
LanguageData.AEXP_Moose.tools.1.label: right hoof
LanguageData.AEXP_Moose.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.AEXP_Muskox.description: Musk oxen are massive buffalo-like mammals that roam the tundra areas in search for roots, mosses and lichens to sustain them. Being related to muffalos, musk oxen can be used as pack animals. They are noted for their thick coat and for the strong odor emitted during the seasonal rut by males, from which their name derives.
LanguageData.AEXP_Muskox.label: musk ox
LanguageData.AEXP_Muskox.tools.0.label: head
LanguageData.AEXP_Muskox.tools.1.label: left hoof
LanguageData.AEXP_Muskox.tools.2.label: right hoof
LanguageData.AEXP_Porcupine.description: A large rodent with a coat of sharp quills that protect it against predators.
LanguageData.AEXP_Porcupine.label: porcupine
LanguageData.AEXP_Porcupine.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.AEXP_Porcupine.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.AEXP_Porcupine.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.VHGE_GasFloodlight.description: A gas-powered tall floodlight that lights a massive area. People need light to move and work at full speed. It doesn’t shine bright enough to grow crops. Needs to be connected to the helixien gas network. Can be safely placed outdoors.
LanguageData.VHGE_GasFloodlight.label: gas floodlight
LanguageData.VHGE_GasLamp.description: A gas-powered standing lamp that lights an area. People need light to move and work at full speed. Needs to be connected to the helixien gas network. Can be safely placed outdoors.
LanguageData.VHGE_GasLamp.label: gas lamp
LanguageData.VHGE_GasSunLamp.description: An industrial lamp which lights an area brightly enough to grow crops, but consumes a medium amount of helixien gas. Automatically turns itself off at night.
LanguageData.VHGE_GasSunLamp.label: gas sun lamp
LanguageData.VHGE_GasWallLamp.description: A gas-powered wall light that lights an area. People need light to move and work at full speed. Needs to be connected to the helixien gas network. Can be safely placed outdoors.
LanguageData.VHGE_GasWallLamp.label: gas wall lamp
LanguageData.VHGE_GasOperatedPodLauncher.description: A fueling port for launching one transport pod. Pod launchers can launch as a group - but the launchers must be placed adjacent to each other. Requires connection to a helixien gas system.
LanguageData.VHGE_GasOperatedPodLauncher.label: gas-operated pod launcher
LanguageData.VHGE_GasGeyser.description: A natural source of high-pressure gas. Helixien pumps can be built on top to extract the gas.
LanguageData.VHGE_GasGeyser.label: gas geyser
LanguageData.VHGE_HelixienGenerator.description: Produces power by consuming helixien gas. Must be connected to a gas pipe network.
LanguageData.VHGE_HelixienGenerator.label: gas powered generator
LanguageData.VHGE_IndustrialHelixienGenerator.descrip: Produces a large amount of power by consuming helixien gas. Must be connected to a gas pipe network.
LanguageData.VHGE_IndustrialHelixienGenerator.label: industrial gas powered generator
LanguageData.VHGE_GasPoweredCrematorium.description: A huge stone crematorium which vaporizes corpses with extremely high temperatures. Operates on helixien gas.
LanguageData.VHGE_GasPoweredCrematorium.label: gas-powered crematorium
LanguageData.VHGE_GasPoweredRefinery.description: Refines biological matter like wood or plant matter into chemfuel. Needs to be connected to the helixien gas network.
LanguageData.VHGE_GasPoweredRefinery.label: gas-powered refinery
LanguageData.VHGE_GasPoweredSmelter.description: Extracts usable metal from slag chunks and other mixed metal items. Operates on helixien gas.
LanguageData.VHGE_GasPoweredSmelter.label: gas-powered smelter
LanguageData.VHGE_GasPoweredSmithy.description: A gas-powered station for smithing non-mechanical weapons and tools.
LanguageData.VHGE_GasPoweredSmithy.label: gas-powered smithy
LanguageData.VHGE_GasPoweredStove.description: A gas-powered stove with an attached countertop for preparing meals.
LanguageData.VHGE_GasPoweredStove.label: gas-powered stove
LanguageData.VHGE_GasCooler.description: An air-cooling machine that releases cooled air around itself. Can be used to create a walk-in freezer. Cannot be adjusted and will automatically lower temperature slightly below zero.
LanguageData.VHGE_GasCooler.label: gas cooler
LanguageData.VHGE_GasHeater.description: A device that converts helixien gas into radiated heat. It can automatically turn itself on or off to reach a specific target temperature.
LanguageData.VHGE_GasHeater.label: gas heater
LanguageData.VHGE_Helixien.label: helixien
LanguageData.VHGE_HelixienPipe.description: A pipe that transmits helixien gas.
