LanguageData.VHGE_HelixienPipe.label: gas pipe
LanguageData.VHGE_SubterraneanHelixienPipe.descriptio: A pipe that transmits helixien gas. This pipe is hidden from view and is protected from gas explosions. Much more expensive to build than regular pipes.
LanguageData.VHGE_SubterraneanHelixienPipe.label: subterranean gas pipe
LanguageData.VHGE_HelixienPump.description: An extractor machine mounted on a pocket of helixien gas. This machine pumps out valuable gas and needs to be connected to other gas-based utilities using the pipe network.
LanguageData.VHGE_HelixienPump.label: helixien pump
LanguageData.VHGE_GasTank.description: A large steel tank for gas storage. Will explode if damaged.
LanguageData.VHGE_GasTank.label: gas tank
LanguageData.VHGE_GasPipeValve.label: gas valve
LanguageData.VNPE_NutrientPastePipe.description: Light steel pipes for transporting the sludge known as nutrient paste from grinders and storage vats to dispensers.
LanguageData.VNPE_NutrientPastePipe.label: nutrient paste pipe
LanguageData.VNPE_NutrientPasteValve.label: nutrient paste valve
LanguageData.VNPE_UndergroundNutrientPastePipe.descri: Subterranean steel pipes for transporting the sludge known as nutrient paste from grinders and storage vats to dispensers.
LanguageData.VNPE_UndergroundNutrientPastePipe.label: subterranean nutrient paste pipe
LanguageData.VNPE_NutrientPasteDripper.description: A bedside device that feeds a person lying in bed whenever they are hungry, provided that nutrient paste dripper is connected to a nutrient paste vat. It must be placed directly adjacent to and facing the head of the bed. Placing more than one nutrient paste dripper near the same bed has no effect.
LanguageData.VNPE_NutrientPasteDripper.label: nutrient paste dripper
LanguageData.VNPE_NutrientPasteFeeder.description: A small pipe output that allows colonists and citizens to pick up nutrient paste.
LanguageData.VNPE_NutrientPasteFeeder.label: nutrient paste feeder
LanguageData.VNPE_NutrientPasteGrinder.description: A machine that synthesizes edible nutrient paste from organic feedstocks placed in adjacent hoppers. It consumes less ingredients and time than any other meal production method - but nobody likes eating nutrient paste. Accepts raw food, but not rough plant matter like hay. It does not actually dispense the nutrient paste, but it allows it to be pumped into vats and dispensers in the network.
LanguageData.VNPE_NutrientPasteGrinder.label: nutrient paste grinder
LanguageData.VNPE_NutrientPasteTap.description: A hopper-like storage basin meant to hold dispensed nutrient paste meal from the vats. Can be used by colonists and prisoners. Nutrient paste meal dispensed and ready for pick-up can deteriorate.
LanguageData.VNPE_NutrientPasteTap.label: nutrient paste tap
LanguageData.VNPE_NutrientPasteVat.description: A large tank that can store up to 75 nutrient paste meals in it. Makes a big mess when destroyed.
LanguageData.VNPE_NutrientPasteVat.label: nutrient paste vat
LanguageData.VNPE_Filth_Paste.label: nutrient sludge
LanguageData.VFEE_Aperitif.description: A non-alcoholic, pleasantly light, citrusy and slightly bitter aperitif that owes its flavors and aromas to sweet and bitter oranges, rhubarb, and imperial craftsmanship. Drinking it induces heavy but painless vomiting, completely clearing the stomach and taste buds. Aperitif recipe is kept secret by the Hospitality guilds of the Empire, and noone was quite able to recreate this fine delicacy on the rimworld surface.\n\nImperial nobility loves aperitif for its ability to clear out food from the digestive system, allowing them to consume even more of the finest imperial food.
LanguageData.VFEE_Aperitif.label: aperitif
LanguageData.VFEE_Apparel_AbsolverHelmet.description: A high-tech helmet with a solid plasteel-weave shell and multi-spectrum sensors, which both feed telemetry data to the user and exterior shell. While it provides excellent protection, its primary purpose is to hide the user by using an intricate optical camouflage system while feeding firing solutions using real-time strategic information. Armor like this is only available to the Sophian Empire’s elite assassins due to limited production.
LanguageData.VFEE_Apparel_AbsolverHelmet.label: absolver helmet
LanguageData.VFEE_Apparel_DeserterHelmet.description: A heavily-armored high-tech helmet, with a solid plasteel-weave shell and indirect visual system for non-frontal view angles. It protects against all but the most powerful or luckily-aimed shots.\n\nOriginally a standard cataphract helmet, this has been modified to include a gunlink at the cost of removing the faceplate, sacrificing defense to improve offensive capabilities.
LanguageData.VFEE_Apparel_DeserterHelmet.label: deserter helmet
LanguageData.VFEE_Apparel_JanissaryHelmet.description: A high-tech armored helmet with a built-in status computer and a protective plasteel-weave plate.\n\nIncludes a plasteel-plated gas mask that offers full protection against gas-related attacks, such as toxic environments.
LanguageData.VFEE_Apparel_JanissaryHelmet.label: janissary helmet
LanguageData.VFEE_Apparel_TechfriarCrown.description: An exceedingly complex facemask fitted with advanced technology that is only known to high-ranking Sophian techpriests. Designed to be worn by designated researchers so that they can advance the empire’s needs without rest.\n\nThe inlaid techprint will synchronize and stimulate the wearer’s brain when they are performing research, effectively doubling research speed as well as restoring their needs for rest and recreation.
LanguageData.VFEE_Apparel_TechfriarCrown.label: techfriar crown
LanguageData.VFE_Apparel_StellicRobe.description: A glorious, long and flowing robe embroidered with intricately complex decorative designs.
LanguageData.VFE_Apparel_StellicRobe.label: stellic robe
LanguageData.VFEE_Apparel_ArmorAbsolver.description: A high-tech suit of powered armor with layered plasteel-weave plates and multi-spectrum sensors, which both feed telemetry data to the user and exterior shell. While it provides excellent protection, its primary purpose is to hide the user by using an intricate optical camouflage system while adjusting and assisting their movement. Armor like this is only available to the Sophian Empire’s elite assassins due to limited production.
LanguageData.VFEE_Apparel_ArmorAbsolver.label: absolver armor
LanguageData.VFEE_Apparel_ArmorDeserter.description: A suit of heavy powered armor, built for maximum protection at the cost of mobility. Heavy layered plasteel-weave plates with solid ablative coatings stop all but the most well-aimed or powerful attacks. Neuro-memetic assistors prevent the suit's massive weight from immobilizing the wearer entirely, but the suit is still quite cumbersome.\n\nOriginally an elaborate suit of prestige armor, this has been modified to make greater use of eltex weave in order to improve the performance of psycasters. This comes at a cost of removing interfering armor plating, but enhances psychic sensitivity and neural heat dissipation.
LanguageData.VFEE_Apparel_ArmorDeserter.label: deserter armor
LanguageData.VFEE_Apparel_JanissaryCuirass.descriptio: A set of pauldrons and neck guard that extends down the wearer’s chest made from layered plasteel-weave plates. While it offers high protection for those areas, it is more of a status symbol and is intended to be worn over clothing and under armor without affecting mobility.
LanguageData.VFEE_Apparel_JanissaryCuirass.label: janissary cuirass
LanguageData.VFEE_RoyalSculptureGrand.description: A huge sculpture sized to dominate a room.
LanguageData.VFEE_RoyalSculptureGrand.label: grand imperial sculpture
LanguageData.VFEE_RoyalSculptureLarge.description: A person-sized piece of material sculpted into an artistic form.
LanguageData.VFEE_RoyalSculptureLarge.label: large imperial sculpture
LanguageData.VFEE_RoyalSculptureSmall.description: A torso-sized piece of material sculpted into an artistic form.
LanguageData.VFEE_RoyalSculptureSmall.label: small imperial sculpture
LanguageData.VFEI_ArmoredShuttle.description: A spacer-tech imperial gunship designed for transit between surface and orbit, or between moons of a planetary system. Equipped with a twin-linked autocannon for ground support, these armored shuttles often land in the middle of the battlefield to extract stranded troops.
LanguageData.VFEI_ArmoredShuttle.label: armored shuttle
LanguageData.VFEI_TurretArmoredShuttle.label: double autocannon
LanguageData.VFEE_Candelabra.description: An elaborate, artistic electrical standing lamp that lights an area. People need light to move and work at full speed.
LanguageData.VFEE_Candelabra.label: candelabra
LanguageData.VFEE_GrandChair.description: A comfortable and elegant chair, it is a work of art in itself. Can be used at tables, work stations, and elsewhere.
LanguageData.VFEE_GrandChair.label: grand chair
LanguageData.VFEE_GrandTable.description: A luxurious, beautiful gold-inlaid table fit for the highborn, it is a work of art in itself. People eat off tables when chairs are placed facing them.
LanguageData.VFEE_GrandTable.label: grand table
LanguageData.VFEE_ImperialBanner.description: A decorative strip of cloth hanging from a vertical wooden frame. Appropriate for royal rooms.
LanguageData.VFEE_ImperialBanner.label: imperial banner
LanguageData.VFEE_RoyalMirror.description: A beautiful and elegant mirror. Gives a small comfort bonus to all nearby beds. Placing more than one mirror near the same bed has no effect.
LanguageData.VFEE_RoyalMirror.label: royal mirror
LanguageData.VFEE_RoyalStool.description: A luxurious gold-inlaid stool fit for the nobility. Can be used at tables, work stations, and elsewhere.
LanguageData.VFEE_RoyalStool.label: royal stool
LanguageData.VFEE_StellicThrone.description: A titanic, ornate chair, designed for a dignity-focused psycaster to sit and meditate. Reigning on a throne is a form of meditation that builds up psyfocus. For dignity psycasters, more impressive meditation thrones and thronerooms increase the rate of psyfocus gain.
LanguageData.VFEE_StellicThrone.label: stellic meditation throne
LanguageData.VFEE_Bomb.description: A small, makeshift bomb. Can be easily defused if found on time.
LanguageData.VFEE_Bomb.label: bomb
LanguageData.VFEE_SteleSmall.description: A slender, modest slab with limited engravings on the sides. Steles have been used since ancient times to memorialize individuals, battles, and other important events.
LanguageData.VFEE_SteleSmall.label: small stele
LanguageData.VFEE_PipeOrgan.description: An exceedingly complex stationary musical instrument crafted at a smithy. A series of tuned pipes are played from the keyboard which releases pressurized air to produce the desired pitch, which lasts as long as the key is pressed. The complex and intricate design, along with the unique sound it produces, make it an ideal apparatus for instigating concerts of the nobility as its music reaches far and wide.
LanguageData.VFEE_PipeOrgan.label: organ
LanguageData.VFEE_UnfinishedOrgan.label: unfinished organ
LanguageData.VFEE_Gun_StrikerTurret.description: A powerful turret mounted on the striker drop pod.
LanguageData.VFEE_Gun_StrikerTurret.label: striker gun
LanguageData.VFEE_Turret_StrikerTurret.description: A redesigned cargo pod designed to bury stabilizing legs upon impact and deploy a powerful turret. Has a limited battery and ammunition pool, and will self-destruct if either run out or it is sufficiently damaged.
LanguageData.VFEE_Turret_StrikerTurret.label: striker turret
LanguageData.VFEE_DropPodIncoming_Building.label: drop pod (incoming)
LanguageData.VFEI_ArmoredShuttleIncoming.label: armored shuttle (incoming)
LanguageData.VFEI_ArmoredShuttleLeaving.label: armored shuttle (leaving)
LanguageData.VFEE_GrandRoyalRug.description: A majestic rug with an imperial-style pattern, for decorating a floor.
LanguageData.VFEE_GrandRoyalRug.label: grand royal rug
LanguageData.VFEE_ImperialSlab.description: A stoney bas relief of imperial imagery for decorating a floor.
LanguageData.VFEE_ImperialSlab.label: imperial slab
LanguageData.VFEE_LargeRoyalRug.description: An intricate rug with an imperial-style pattern, for decorating a floor.
LanguageData.VFEE_LargeRoyalRug.label: large royal rug
LanguageData.VFEE_MeleeWeapon_ToxbladeBladelink.descr: A defensive melee persona weapon usually assigned to every Imperial noble of rank Yeoman or above. While armor penetration is comparable to plasteel blades, the primary function is to dispense acidifer fluids and toxic vapors upon inflicted cuts. The effect only lasts a few seconds and is inflicted roughly half of the time due to the limited size of the weapon.
LanguageData.VFEE_MeleeWeapon_ToxbladeBladelink.label: persona toxblade
LanguageData.VFEE_MeleeWeapon_Toxblade.description: A defensive melee persona weapon usually assigned to every Imperial noble of rank Yeoman or above. While armor penetration is comparable to plasteel blades, the primary function is to dispense acidifer fluids and toxic vapors upon inflicted cuts. The effect only lasts a few seconds and is inflicted roughly half of the time due to the limited size of the weapon.
LanguageData.VFEE_MeleeWeapon_Toxblade.label: toxblade
LanguageData.VFEE_MeleeWeapon_Toxblade.tools.0.label: handle
LanguageData.VFEE_MeleeWeapon_Toxblade.tools.1.label: point
LanguageData.VFEE_MeleeWeapon_Toxblade.tools.2.label: edge
LanguageData.VFEE_OrbitalSlicer.label: orbital slicer
LanguageData.VFEE_Poison.label: poison
LanguageData.VEE_Gun_Fletcher.description: An ultra-long range sniper rifle which fires fin-stabilized flechettes with acidifier injectors. Due to their lethality and danger of falling into enemy hands, they are always biocoded and will self-destruct if their owner either dies or is disarmed. Lethal to organics and almost useless against non-organics, such as mechanoids.\n\nTargets hit by these flechettes will be inflicted with an unstable variant of acidifier fluids, designed to dissolve the target from the inside rather than destroy their equipment. The acid will rampage through the target’s body and usually results in vital organs being dissolved, however lethality isn’t guaranteed.
LanguageData.VEE_Gun_Fletcher.label: fletcher
LanguageData.VEE_Gun_Fletcher.tools.0.label: barrel
LanguageData.VFEE_Bullet_ChargeThumper.label: thump charge
LanguageData.VFEE_Bullet_FletcherDart.label: fletcher dart
LanguageData.VFEE_Gun_ChargeThumper.description: A modified charged-shot assault shotgun that incorporates mechanoid technology. Recovered thump cannons have been reversed engineered to produce a portable ranged weapon capable of firing blunt projectiles. Quite powerful but short ranged, with high performance against structures and heavily armored targets.
LanguageData.VFEE_Gun_ChargeThumper.label: charge thumper
LanguageData.VFEE_Gun_ChargeThumper.tools.0.label: stock
LanguageData.VFEE_Gun_ChargeThumper.tools.1.label: barrel
LanguageData.HC_SlotMachineBlue.description: High stake slots, default stake is 6$.
LanguageData.HC_SlotMachineBlue.label: rim of fortune
LanguageData.HC_SlotMachineGreen.description: Medium priced slots, default stake is 4$.
LanguageData.HC_SlotMachineGreen.label: rima moolah
LanguageData.HC_SlotMachineRed.description: Classic slots, default stake is only 2$, perfect for beginners.
LanguageData.HC_SlotMachineRed.label: rim n reels
LanguageData.VAEWaste_AcidBolt.label: acid spit
LanguageData.VAEWaste_EggHydraFertilized.description: A fertilized hydra egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into a baby hydra. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
LanguageData.VAEWaste_EggHydraFertilized.label: hydra egg (fert.)
LanguageData.VAEWaste_EggToxiguanaFertilized.descript: A fertilized toxiguana egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into a baby toxiguana. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
LanguageData.VAEWaste_EggToxiguanaFertilized.label: toxiguana egg (fert.)
LanguageData.VAEWaste_ToxicMeat.description: Raw meat from a toxic animal.\n\nChewy, gelatinous and disgusting.
LanguageData.VAEWaste_ToxicMeat.label: toxic meat
LanguageData.VAEWaste_ToxicLeather.description: A poor, dark-greenish brown coloured leather that flakes quite easily. Very flammable.
LanguageData.VAEWaste_ToxicLeather.label: wasteleather
LanguageData.VAEWaste_Hydra.description: The Hydra is a fearsome creature, mutated by the toxic environment to become a three-headed snake. Living primarily in water, two of its heads remain submerged while only one absorbs oxygen. Quick and vicious in its attacks, the Hydra's bite is lethal, delivering a potent venom with each strike. Its ability to adapt to even the harshest of environments makes it a formidable predator, and one that should be approached with caution. The Hydra's three heads are a symbol of its adaptability and resilience, making it a force to be reckoned with in the polluted biomes.
LanguageData.VAEWaste_Hydra.label: hydra
LanguageData.VAEWaste_Hydra.tools.0.label: venom-fangs
LanguageData.VAEWaste_Hydra.tools.1.label: head
LanguageData.VAEWaste_Megatardi.description: The Megatardi is a massive and curious creature, mutated from its tiny tardigrade origins to become a behemoth of the wasteland. Though slow and blind, it requires very little sustenance to survive and can consume even the most toxic wastepacks. With its six legs, the Megatardi is an impressive sight, and its gentle nature makes it a popular pack animal among those brave enough to venture into the polluted world. Though it is easily tamed, the Megatardi is not without its dangers. Upon its death, it releases a cloud of toxic gas, making it a potential hazard for anyone nearby. Despite this, the Megatardi remains a valuable asset for those willing to take on the challenges of the polluted wasteland.
LanguageData.VAEWaste_Megatardi.label: megatardi
LanguageData.VAEWaste_Megatardi.tools.0.label: mandibles
LanguageData.VAEWaste_Megatardi.tools.1.label: head
LanguageData.VAEWaste_Pestigator.description: The Pestigator is a massive and fearsome predator that reigns supreme in the polluted swamps of the rimworld. With its heavily armored form and powerful jaws, it is capable of inflicting massive damage on any opponent. Though slow on land, it is nimble and nearly invisible in water, making it a formidable adversary in its native habitat. The Pestigator is not to be underestimated, as its powerful bite can rip apart its prey with ease. Its insatiable hunger and aggression make it impossible to tame, making it a constant threat to any unfortunate enough to encounter it.
LanguageData.VAEWaste_Pestigator.label: pestigator
LanguageData.VAEWaste_Toxafox.description: The Toxafox is a highly intelligent fox that has evolved to survive in the harshest of environments. With a keen sense of smell, it is capable of digging up waste packs buried deep underground, making it a persistent pest. Its presence in an area inadvertently generates more pollution, making it a major environmental hazard. Despite its small size, the Toxafox should not be underestimated. Its cunning and adaptability make it a formidable opponent in a world already wracked by toxicity and decay.
LanguageData.VAEWaste_Toxafox.label: toxafox
LanguageData.VAEWaste_Toxafox.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.VAEWaste_Toxafox.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.VAEWaste_Toxafox.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.VAEWaste_Toxbear.description: The Toxbear is a monstrous and deadly creature born from a polluted environment. Standing at an immense size, this mutated bear is a force to be reckoned with. With no natural predators, it roams and hunts at will, leaving destruction in its wake. Its aggressive behavior is fueled by cancerous growth around its head, making it nearly impervious to damage.\n\nAs the growth puts pressure on its brain, the Toxbear becomes increasingly violent, eventually turning completely rabid. Not long after that, toxbears are known to try and scratch their own brains out, resulting in their violent demise.
LanguageData.VAEWaste_Toxbear.label: toxbear
LanguageData.VAEWaste_Toxbear.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.VAEWaste_Toxbear.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.VAEWaste_Toxbear.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.VAEWaste_Toxiguana.description: The Toxiguana is a small, two-legged lizard that is well adapted to highly polluted environments. Standing about the size of a dog, this creature is not to be underestimated. It has a formidable defense mechanism, capable of spitting corrosive acid at any predator that threatens it. When it dies, a chemical reaction causes its body to rapidly decompose, releasing a toxic cloud of gas that can be dangerous to any living thing that comes into contact with it. The Toxiguana is a remarkable example of the resilience of life in even the harshest of environments.
LanguageData.VAEWaste_Toxiguana.label: toxiguana
LanguageData.VAEWaste_Toxiguana.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.VAEWaste_Toxiguana.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.VAEWaste_Toxiguana.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.VAEWaste_Toxiguana.verbs.0.label: acid spit
LanguageData.VAEWaste_Toxlion.description: The toxlion is a sad reflection of what once was a proud and powerful predator. Its toxic environment has left it weak and emaciated, forcing it to survive by feeding on waste. Despite its diminished state, the Toxlion is still a threat. Its bites and claws have a high chance of spreading the toxflu, and it hunts in large packs to make up for its lack of strength and speed.\n\nToxflu is a highly contagious and rapidly striking disease, characterized by fever, cough, fatigue, and difficulty breathing. It spreads quickly through contact with infected bodily fluids. Those who contract Toxflu can experience severe respiratory distress and, in severe cases, death. It goes away nearly as quickly as it appears, however anyone who interacted socially with a person suffering from toxflu has a chance of catching it.
LanguageData.VAEWaste_Toxlion.label: toxlion
LanguageData.VAEWaste_Toxlion.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.VAEWaste_Toxlion.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.VAEWaste_Toxscorpion.description: The Toxscorpion is a massive and fearsome creature, growing as large as a cow. Its thick carapace and powerful claws make it nearly invulnerable, capable of cutting through even metal. With no definite weak spot, the Toxscorpion is a formidable opponent. Mutated from exposure to radiation and toxins, its stinger injects a fast-acting poison that alters the DNA of its victims. This leads to toxic buildup and cancerous growths that must be swiftly removed before they spread, making the Toxscorpion one of the most dangerous creatures in the wasteland.
LanguageData.VAEWaste_Toxscorpion.label: toxscorpion
LanguageData.VAEWaste_Toxscorpion.tools.0.label: left pincer
LanguageData.VAEWaste_Toxscorpion.tools.1.label: right pincer
LanguageData.VAEWaste_Toxscorpion.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.VAEWaste_Wasteboar.description: The Wasteboar is a massive and powerful creature, mutated by the polluted environment it inhabits. With four eyes and two sets of formidable tusks, it is well equipped to defend itself against predators. Despite its intimidating appearance, the Wasteboar is not aggressive. Instead, it spends its time ramming its head into trees in an effort to break them down. While this behavior may seem harmless, it can have devastating consequences for the environment, making the Wasteboar an unwanted and destructive presence. The destruction of resources and the unsightly impact on the surroundings make this creature a problem that should not be taken lightly.
LanguageData.VAEWaste_Wasteboar.label: wasteboar
LanguageData.VAEWaste_Wasteboar.tools.0.label: tusk
LanguageData.VAEWaste_Wasteboar.tools.2.label: head
LanguageData.VAEWaste_Wastedeer.description: The Wastedeer is a unique creature adapted to thrive in the most polluted of environments. With two heads, it is capable of detecting and avoiding danger from all directions. Its mutated form has allowed it to survive in even the harshest of conditions, making it a symbol of resilience in a world ravaged by toxins. Despite its unusual appearance, the wastedeer is a graceful and nimble creature, easily navigating the treacherous terrain of the wasteland. Though it may not pose a direct threat, its presence serves as a reminder of the dangers of a world overrun by pollution.
LanguageData.VAEWaste_Wastedeer.label: wastedeer
LanguageData.VAEWaste_Wastedeer.tools.0.label: left hoof
LanguageData.VAEWaste_Wastedeer.tools.1.label: right hoof
LanguageData.VAEWaste_Wastedeer.tools.2.label: teeth
LanguageData.VAEWaste_Wastedeer.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.VAEWaste_Wasteffalo.description:
A large, mutated, herding herbivore descended from buffalo and adapted for high-toxicity environment. While enraged wastefallo are deadly, they can be tamed and even serve as pack animals.

The mutated secondary head makes them much more deadly, as with 2 sets of eyes and two gigantic jaws they are more than capable of defending themselves from any predators willing to hunt them.

Due to the genetic instability, wastefallo rarely live long enough to be viable farm animals. Their meat is also known to be highly toxic.
LanguageData.VAEWaste_Wasteffalo.label: wasteffalo
LanguageData.VAEWaste_Wasteffalo.tools.0.label: head
LanguageData.VAEWaste_Wasteffalo.tools.1.label: left hoof
LanguageData.VAEWaste_Wasteffalo.tools.2.label: right hoof
LanguageData.VRE_Draincasket.description:
A sinister contraption designed to extract hemogen from an individual while maintaining their life force at the barest minimum. This dark and foreboding casket houses its occupant in a twilight state of existence with their body slowly drained of precious hemogen. Bound to their Draincasket, the occupant's survival depends on being fed regularly, as they can still starve to death.

Produces 2 hemogen packs a day as long as the occupant has food and the casket is connected to a hemogen network. If deprived of food, the casket will kill its occupant in around 2 days.

If you have Vanilla Nutrient Paste Expanded it will draw nutrients from that network, as long as there is more than 1 meal unit available.
LanguageData.VRE_Draincasket.label: draincasket
LanguageData.VRE_HemogenDrain.description: A hopper-like storage basin meant to input hemogen into the network, filling up connected vats.
LanguageData.VRE_HemogenDrain.label: hemogen drain
LanguageData.VRE_HemogenPipe.description: A pipe that’s used to transport hemogen between taps, drains, vats and hemogen-fueled structures.
LanguageData.VRE_HemogenPipe.label: hemogen pipe
LanguageData.VRE_HemogenTap.description: A hopper-like storage basin meant to dispense hemogen for later use.
LanguageData.VRE_HemogenTap.label: hemogen tap
LanguageData.VRE_HemogenValve.label: hemogen valve
LanguageData.VRE_HemogenVat.description: An industrial liquid storage vat meant for storing hemogen delivered via hemogen pipe network. Vats can be designated to be siphoned for usable hemogen as needed.
LanguageData.VRE_HemogenVat.label: hemogen vat
LanguageData.VRE_UndergroundHemogenPipe.description: Subterranean steel pipes designed to transport hemogen between taps, drains, vats and hemogen-fueled structures.
LanguageData.VRE_UndergroundHemogenPipe.label: subterranean hemogen pipe
LanguageData.VRE_HemodynamicAccelerator.description: An advanced device designed to expedite the deathrest process at the cost of blood consumption. By connecting with a deathresting person, this apparatus employs intricate blood analysis and potent sanguine stimulants to maximize the efficiency of the deathrest casket. As it hastens the character's recovery, the Hemodynamic Accelerator demands a continuous supply of hemogen to fuel its powerful operation. To function, it must be connected to a deathrest casket, ensuring the swift rejuvenation of its occupant.
LanguageData.VRE_HemodynamicAccelerator.label: hemodynamic accelerator
LanguageData.VRE_HemogenSolidifier.description: A device specifically engineered to enhance the durability of deathresting characters by manipulating the molecular structure of their skin. Utilizing an array of bioresonant projectors and blood-based nano-agents, the solidifier targets the hemogen within the character's body, fortifying and thickening skin molecules. As the deathresting individual slumbers, the Hemogen Solidifier weaves a dense, protective layer around them, rendering their skin more resilient and damage-resistant. The device must be connected to a deathrest casket to operate, ensuring its occupant emerges from their rest with an augmented defensive capacity.
LanguageData.VRE_HemogenSolidifier.label: hemogen solidifier
LanguageData.VRE_InvocationMatrix.description: A sophisticated device that infuses deathresting people with rare and potent sanguophage abilities, tapping into the very essence of their vampiric nature. Employing a complex system of blood conduits and essence resonators, the matrix connects to a deathrest casket and channels the life force of its occupant to unlock hidden potential within their bloodline. As the person slumbers in their deathrest state, the Invocation Matrix bestows upon them a selected, unique power. This building requires a constant supply of hemogen to operate.
LanguageData.VRE_InvocationMatrix.label: invocation matrix
LanguageData.VRE_SmallHemogenAmplifier.description: This device leaves a deathrester in a hemogen-amplified state so they gain slightly more hemogen from any hemogen source. Using a gland probe and blood analyzer, it links with a deathresting person, stimulating hemogen glands into a more active state. It must be connected to a deathrest casket to function. This building needs to consume hemogen to function.
LanguageData.VRE_SmallHemogenAmplifier.label: small hemogen amplifier
LanguageData.VRE_SmallHemopump.description: This small, blood-refining pump increases the amount of hemogen a deathresting person can store in their body. It must be connected to a deathrest casket to function. This building needs to consume hemogen to function.
LanguageData.VRE_SmallHemopump.label: small hemopump
LanguageData.VRE_PsychofluidWell.description: A specialized apparatus designed to rejuvenate the psyfocus of deathresting psycasters by delicately nurturing their psychic centers. Employing a meticulous balance of sanguine infusions and harmonic neural stimulation, the well works in concert with the psycaster's innate psychic energies. As the individual rests within the deathrest casket, the Psychofluid Well revitalizes their psychic reservoirs, preparing them for future psychic exertions. To operate effectively, the device must be connected to a deathrest casket and requires a steady supply of hemogen to fuel its subtle yet powerful restorative functions.\n\nContrary to other deathrest machines, the psychofluid well regenerates 5% of total Psyfocus to the deathresting occupant every 1 hour, instead of acting all at once when the deathrest ends.
LanguageData.VRE_PsychofluidWell.label: psychofluid well
LanguageData.VRE_FilthGoo.label: viscuous goo
LanguageData.VRE_HemogenPack_Animal.description: Animal hemogen. Increases regeneration and combat capabilities of the user, but greatly decreases the capability to communicate with others.
LanguageData.VRE_HemogenPack_Animal.label: hemogen pack (animal)
LanguageData.VRE_HemogenPack_Corpse.description: Tainted, rotting hemogen from a cadaver. Greatly weakens the carrier of this hemogen type, but increases the blood filtration significantly.
LanguageData.VRE_HemogenPack_Corpse.label: hemogen pack (corpse)
LanguageData.VRE_HemogenPack_Sanguophage.description: Pure, thick hemogen of another bloodfeeder. The most rare of all hemogens, it gives the carrier a powerful boost to all capacities.
LanguageData.VRE_HemogenPack_Sanguophage.label: hemogen pack (sanguophage)
LanguageData.VRE_Proj_Goo.label: goo
LanguageData.VPEH_BloodShard.label: blood shard
LanguageData.VPEH_SanguineSpear.label: sanguine spear
LanguageData.VPE_Bileceps.description: Harvested bileceps. Can be cooked into meals or eaten raw.
LanguageData.VPE_Bileceps.label: raw bileceps
LanguageData.VPE_Blisscap.description: Raw blisscap obtained by harvesting blisscap mushrooms. When eaten, makes people incredibly high, but also incredibly drowsy.
LanguageData.VPE_Blisscap.label: raw blisscap
LanguageData.VPE_Mycothread.description: Cotton-like, edible fabric extracted from mycothread mushrooms. It’s soft to the touch and easy to work with, deteriorates quickly, has an unusual color, and oddly still smells and tastes like mushrooms.
LanguageData.VPE_Mycothread.label: mycothread
LanguageData.VPE_Building_Zapcap.description: A large, bulbous, beige mushroom that is known to produce small amounts of electricity as it grows. Needs to be placed on power conduits in order to use the newly produced energy. In case of an electrical short, zapcaps amplify the released energy, causing significantly more damage.
LanguageData.VPE_Building_Zapcap.label: zapcap
LanguageData.VPE_Plant_Bileceps.description: Bileceps is a peculiar, coral-like mushroom that thrives on the bile of decaying bodies. Despite its unappetizing source, it possesses a meaty flavor and offers a protein-rich harvest. Its distinctive, cancerous appearance does little to deter colonists from utilizing it as an alternate source of meat.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Requires corpse bile on its tile to grow properly. Time to get a Helixien slug!
LanguageData.VPE_Plant_Bileceps.label: bileceps
LanguageData.VPE_Plant_Blisscap.description: A long, light-blue mushroom that offers more than just sustenance. Eating it induces a state of blissful happiness, making the consumer exceptionally content, yet notably drowsy. It is known to greatly enhance the psychic sensitivity of whoever eats it, and is often consumed by hippie communities on various core planets, allowing them to ‘see into the future’.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Contrary to other plants, blisscap ignores light levels for growth. Sunlight won't destroy it. It will still rest during the night period.
LanguageData.VPE_Plant_Blisscap.label: blisscap
LanguageData.VPE_Plant_Foampuffer.description: An ugly, thick mushroom with a cap that smells of muffalo fecal matter. It needs to be grown in flower pots. If it comes into contact with fire once matured, it explodes, ejecting a spray of firefoam over a short distance.
LanguageData.VPE_Plant_Foampuffer.label: foampuffer
LanguageData.VPE_Plant_Glowcap.description: A small blue mushroom typically found in caves. The smell it emits is absolutely repulsive - but it tastes surprisingly good when cooked. It lights up the area around it, but the light is not strong enough to grow crops.
LanguageData.VPE_Plant_Glowcap.label: glowcap
LanguageData.VPE_Plant_Healtruffle.description: Healtruffle is a mushroom that grows slowly by using nutrients from rotstink - it requires a supply of rotstink gas around it in order to grow. Harvesting it gives you herbal medicine. However, it takes a lot of work to plant and collect because it's very delicate. It's been grown for a long time by settlers for its medicinal properties.
LanguageData.VPE_Plant_Healtruffle.label: healtruffle
LanguageData.VPE_Plant_Lullabloom.description:
A special kind of mushroom that emits calming spores, ideal for planting next to a bed. Needs to be planted in a flower pot. Its presence soothes the sleeping individual, creating a more restful slumber and promoting overall comfort. Its gentle, sleep-inducing properties make it a favorite among colonists seeking a peaceful night's rest.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Contrary to other plants, lullabloom ignores light levels for growth. Sunlight won't destroy it. It will still rest during the night period.

Nullifies the "disturbed sleep" thought.

Will link to vanilla and Vanilla Furniture Expanded beds.
LanguageData.VPE_Plant_Lullabloom.label: lullabloom
LanguageData.VPE_Plant_MycothreadMushroom.description: This mushroom has been cultivated for ages by tunneler mining colonies, as it produces a thick but flexible cotton-like fabric which can be used to craft ropes, clothing and furniture. While it takes an incredibly long time to grow, and dies when exposed to any sort of light, for some colonies it is the only reliable way of obtaining good quality textiles.
LanguageData.VPE_Plant_MycothreadMushroom.label: mycothread mushroom
LanguageData.VPE_Plant_Redcap.description: A cultivated mushroom with a beautiful red cap and a somewhat short lifespan. Recaps are beautiful, but may need to be replanted often.
LanguageData.VPE_Plant_Redcap.label: redcap
LanguageData.VPE_Plant_Stinker.description: A small, fast-growing mushroom that forms large patches. It produces rotstink through a chemical process. Despite its unpleasant odor, it can be harvested for edible fungus. It can grow anywhere, however exposure to sunlight will kill it.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Contrary to other mushrooms, stinkers can handle light source up to 50% luminosity, such as torches.
LanguageData.VPE_Plant_Stinker.label: stinker
LanguageData.VPE_Plant_Thermoregulus.description: A rare, large mushroom that needs to be grown in flower pots. Once mature, thermoregulus emits varying temperature depending on the ambient temperature around it. At all times it will aim to bring the temperature of the room it’s in to 16C. \n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Contrary to other mushroom, sunlight won't destroy it.
LanguageData.VPE_Plant_Thermoregulus.label: thermoregulus
LanguageData.VPE_Plant_Timbercap.description: A short, brown mushroom with a hard stem. When harvested, it yields a small amount of wood-like substance.
LanguageData.VPE_Plant_Timbercap.label: timbercap
LanguageData.VPE_Plant_Zapcap.description: A large, bulbous, beige mushroom that is known to produce small amounts of electricity as it grows. Needs to be placed on power conduits in order to use the newly produced energy. In case of an electrical short, zapcaps amplify the released energy, causing significantly more damage.
LanguageData.VPE_Plant_Zapcap.label: zapcap
LanguageData.VPE_Plant_Toxpuff.description: A small, green oval mushroom typically found in highly polluted areas. It puffs out tox gas when anyone steps on it.
LanguageData.VPE_Plant_Toxpuff.label: toxpuff
LanguageData.MedievalVendingMachine.description: A vending machine for guests that requires no power.
LanguageData.MedievalVendingMachine.label: medieval vending machine
LanguageData.VendingMachine.description: A standard vending machine for guests.
LanguageData.VendingMachine.label: vending machine
LanguageData.VVE_GarageCabinet.description: A handy cabinet that contains a whole bunch of extra tools of various sizes and functions. Increases work speed when placed near a garage bench. One bench can use up to two garage cabinets.
LanguageData.VVE_GarageCabinet.label: garage cabinet
LanguageData.VVE_RefuelingPump.description: A refueling pump that will automatically refuel chemfuel-powered vehicles standing on the interaction spot. Needs to be filled with chemfuel.
LanguageData.VVE_RefuelingPump.label: refueling pump
LanguageData.VVE_GarageBench.description: A specialised work station for assembling vehicle parts. For those that are desperate, rusted cars and engine blocks can be salvaged for any remaining working parts.
LanguageData.VVE_GarageBench.label: garage bench
LanguageData.VVE_TankTrap.description: A defensive fortification for stopping vehicles. Provides small amount of cover and can be walked through on foot, but is not traversable by vehicles.
LanguageData.VVE_TankTrap.label: tank trap
LanguageData.VVE_GarageAutoDoor.description: Divides rooms. Electric-powered operation allows the door to open without manual interaction, letting vehicles through.
LanguageData.VVE_GarageAutoDoor.label: auto garage door (1x4)
LanguageData.VVE_GarageAutoDoorLarge.label: auto garage door (1x5)
LanguageData.VVE_GarageDoor.description: Divides rooms. Manual operation allows people to open it, letting vehicles through.
LanguageData.VVE_GarageDoor.label: garage door (1x4)
LanguageData.VVE_GarageDoorLarge.label: garage door (1x5)
LanguageData.Bullet_Bulldog_MainTurret.label: high-explosive shell
LanguageData.VVE_CarAlternator.description: The alternator is an extremely vital engine part for any vehicle that has electrical systems. Some very basic systems can get away and some more complex vehicles can require more than one.
LanguageData.VVE_CarAlternator.label: car alternator
LanguageData.VVE_CarBattery.description: An insulated rechargeable battery and wiring which powers numerous electronic functions. Acts as a surge protector for electronic systems and multiple batteries can augment or even replace a vehicle's engine.
LanguageData.VVE_CarBattery.label: car battery
LanguageData.VVE_CarExhaust.description: A series of pipes, catalysts and fittings that transfer the exhaust from the car engine away from the driver’s cabin. Engines that will be put under a lot of strain require more exhausts.
LanguageData.VVE_CarExhaust.label: car exhaust
LanguageData.VVE_CarFuelTank.description: Carefully manufactured fuel storage and injection systems. Ideal for vehicles that require greater fuel capacity or even increased fuel consumption without increasing the likelihood of gas leaks or engine damage.
LanguageData.VVE_CarFuelTank.label: car fuel tank
LanguageData.VVE_CarRadiator.description: A series of pipes, tubes, fans, pumps, and the radiator itself, ready to be installed in a vehicle that is expected to endure heavy loads. The harder the engine will have to work, the more radiators required to keep it cool.
LanguageData.VVE_CarRadiator.label: car radiator
LanguageData.VVE_CarSuspension.description: A series of transmissions, springs and shock absorbers that redirect or reduce forces transferred through the wheels. Generally, the greater the mass and cargo capacity of the vehicle, the more suspensions are needed.
LanguageData.VVE_CarSuspension.label: car suspension
LanguageData.VVE_CarTransmission.description: A dedicated, automatic transmission system delicately machined from hardened and durable materials. Due to its comparatively complex design, these are required in vehicles that require immense torque or excessive rotational speeds.
LanguageData.VVE_CarTransmission.label: car transmission
LanguageData.VVE_CarWheel.description: One of the most ancient technologies developed by man, the wheel takes the power from the engine and translates it into movement. As wheels have to not only move the vehicle in whichever direction necessary but also withstand the combined weight, larger and heavier vehicles can need several or dozens of them.
LanguageData.VVE_CarWheel.label: car wheel
LanguageData.VVE_CarWiring.description: Reinforced and machined wiring parts to improve electricity conduction. Typically required in vehicles that have a lot of synchronized or delicate electronic systems.
LanguageData.VVE_CarWiring.label: car wiring
LanguageData.VVE_EngineBlock.description: Literally the part that gets a vehicle moving in the first place, no vehicle can be assembled without one. Exceedingly large or heavy vehicles could require more than one.
LanguageData.VVE_EngineBlock.label: engine block
LanguageData.Bullet_Roadkill_MainTurret.label: high-explosive shell
LanguageData.Rocket_Scytheman_MainTurret.label: swarmer rocket
LanguageData.VFED_Apparel_BombPack.description: A single-use man-portable explosive device. Deployed on the ground, it becomes armed and can be detonated with a press of a button. It generates a big explosion thanks to a wired-in antigrain warhead, making it capable of destroying critical infrastructure and personnel alike.
LanguageData.VFED_Apparel_BombPack.label: bomb pack
LanguageData.VFED_Apparel_InvisibilityEngulfer.descri: A multi-use personal invisibility device engineered based on experimental imperial technology. Allows the user to become completely invisible for a brief duration, after which the device requires a short amount of time to recharge.
LanguageData.VFED_Apparel_InvisibilityEngulfer.label: invisibility engulfer
LanguageData.VFED_DeserterDeclassifier.description: A deserter declassifier is a single-use, ultra-tech device consisting of a brain-scan apparatus and a long, thick needle. It is primarily used by imperial deserters for data extraction. By inserting the needle into the skull of a titled nobility, memories can be downloaded from their brain, forming valuable deserter intel. The amount of intel received is determined by the rank of the noble person. The person affected by a Deserter Declassifier dies in the process, and when used on neutral visitors, it is treated as an act of war.
LanguageData.VFED_DeserterDeclassifier.label: deserter declassifier
LanguageData.VFED_DeserterShuttle.description: A spacer-tech shuttle designed for transit between surface and orbit, or between moons of a planetary system.
LanguageData.VFED_DeserterShuttle.label: deserter shuttle
LanguageData.VFED_FlagshipChunk.description: A chunk of an imperial flagship. It seems to have been torn off by some kind of powerful weapon.\n\nHaving landed not long ago, it still contains useful resources which you can acquire by deconstructing it.
LanguageData.VFED_FlagshipChunk.label: flagship chunk
LanguageData.VFED_BombPack_Deployed.description: A deployed bomb pack. It’s armed, and can be detonated with a press of a button.
LanguageData.VFED_BombPack_Deployed.label: deployed bomb pack
LanguageData.VFED_RemoteTrapIED_AntigrainWarhead.desc: A pair of antigrain warheads connected to a remote triggering mechanism. It can be detonated at will, but the fuse time might vary. Since it is hidden in the surrounding terrain, it cannot be placed adjacent to other traps. Animals can sense these when calm.
LanguageData.VFED_RemoteTrapIED_ArmorPiercing.descrip: A pair of armor-piercing shells connected to a remote triggering mechanism. It can be detonated at will, but the fuse time might vary. Since it is hidden in the surrounding terrain, it cannot be placed adjacent to other traps. Animals can sense these when calm.
LanguageData.VFED_RemoteTrapIED_ArmorPiercing.label: remote armor-piercing charge
LanguageData.VFED_RemoteTrapIED_Cluster.description: A pair of cluster shells connected to a remote triggering mechanism. It can be detonated at will, but the fuse time might vary. Since it is hidden in the surrounding terrain, it cannot be placed adjacent to other traps. Animals can sense these when calm.
LanguageData.VFED_RemoteTrapIED_Cluster.label: remote cluster charge
LanguageData.VFED_RemoteTrapIED_EMP.description: A pair of EMP shells connected to a remote triggering mechanism. It can be detonated at will, but the fuse time might vary. Since it is hidden in the surrounding terrain, it cannot be placed adjacent to other traps. Animals can sense these when calm.
LanguageData.VFED_RemoteTrapIED_EMP.label: remote EMP charge
LanguageData.VFED_RemoteTrapIED_Firefoam.description: A pair of firefoam shells connected to a remote triggering mechanism. It can be detonated at will, but the fuse time might vary. Since it is hidden in the surrounding terrain, it cannot be placed adjacent to other traps. Animals can sense these when calm.
LanguageData.VFED_RemoteTrapIED_Firefoam.label: remote firefoam charge
LanguageData.VFED_RemoteTrapIED_HighExplosive.descrip: A pair of high-explosive shells connected to a remote triggering mechanism. It can be detonated at will, but the fuse time might vary. Since it is hidden in the surrounding terrain, it cannot be placed adjacent to other traps. Animals can sense these when calm.
LanguageData.VFED_RemoteTrapIED_HighExplosive.label: remote charge
LanguageData.VFED_RemoteTrapIED_Incendiary.descriptio: A pair of incendiary shells connected to a remote triggering mechanism. It can be detonated at will, but the fuse time might vary. Since it is hidden in the surrounding terrain, it cannot be placed adjacent to other traps. Animals can sense these when calm.
LanguageData.VFED_RemoteTrapIED_Incendiary.label: remote incendiary charge
LanguageData.VFED_RemoteTrapIED_Shrapnel.description: A pair of shrapnel shells connected to a remote triggering mechanism. It can be detonated at will, but the fuse time might vary. Since it is hidden in the surrounding terrain, it cannot be placed adjacent to other traps. Animals can sense these when calm.
LanguageData.VFED_RemoteTrapIED_Shrapnel.label: remote shrapnel charge
LanguageData.VFED_RemoteTrapIED_Smoke.description: A pair of smoke shells connected to a remote triggering mechanism. It can be detonated at will, but the fuse time might vary. Since it is hidden in the surrounding terrain, it cannot be placed adjacent to other traps. Animals can sense these when calm.
LanguageData.VFED_RemoteTrapIED_Smoke.label: remote smoke charge
LanguageData.VFED_TrapIED_ArmorPiercing.description: A pair of armor-piercing shells connected to a trigger which detonates on touch or bullet impact. Since it is hidden in the surrounding terrain, it cannot be placed adjacent to other traps. Animals can sense these when calm.
LanguageData.VFED_TrapIED_ArmorPiercing.label: IED armor-piercing trap
LanguageData.VFED_TrapIED_Cluster.description: A pair of cluster shells connected to a trigger which detonates on touch or bullet impact. Since it is hidden in the surrounding terrain, it cannot be placed adjacent to other traps. Animals can sense these when calm.
LanguageData.VFED_TrapIED_Cluster.label: IED cluster trap
LanguageData.VFED_TrapIED_Shrapnel.description: A pair of shrapnel shells connected to a trigger which detonates on touch or bullet impact. Since it is hidden in the surrounding terrain, it cannot be placed adjacent to other traps. Animals can sense these when calm.
LanguageData.VFED_TrapIED_Shrapnel.label: IED shrapnel trap
LanguageData.VFED_Gun_Kontarion.description: A self-loading uranium slug cannon designed to attach to a turret.
LanguageData.VFED_Gun_Kontarion.label: kontarion cannon
LanguageData.VFED_Gun_Onager.description: A self-loading rocket launcher designed to attach to a turret.
LanguageData.VFED_Gun_Onager.label: onager cannon
LanguageData.VFED_Gun_Palintone.description: A self-loading cannon designed to attach to a turret.
LanguageData.VFED_Gun_Palintone.label: palintone cannon
LanguageData.VFED_Turret_Kontarion.description: A powerful, long-range weapon used by the Empire to take out enemy targets from afar. Kontarion turret fires ultra-dense uranium slugs at incredible speeds, penetrating even the heaviest armor. With its rapid-fire capabilities, tremendous accuracy and infinite range, it’s a force to be reckoned with. It's more accurate at longer ranges, and can't fire at all close up. May explode when damaged.\n\nIt consumes a lot of ammunition, and requires an adjacent crate of uranium slug ammo in order to automatically reload. It cannot be reloaded by hand.
LanguageData.VFED_Turret_Kontarion.label: kontarion
LanguageData.VFED_Turret_Onager.description: A powerful, anti-armored weapon used by the Empire to destroy heavily armored targets with ease. Capable of launching missiles in bursts of 6, the Onager can deal devastating damage to enemy forces in a short amount of time. The Onager turret is most effective at short range, where its missiles can be accurately targeted at enemy positions. However, it cannot fire at enemies that are too close, as the missiles require a certain distance to arm and track their targets. Additionally, due to the sheer number of missiles it fires, the Onager is not as accurate as other turrets, and can sometimes miss its targets. May explode when damaged.\n\nIt consumes a lot of ammunition, and requires an adjacent crate of missile ammo in order to automatically reload. It cannot be reloaded by hand.
LanguageData.VFED_Turret_Onager.label: onager
LanguageData.VFED_Turret_Palintone.description: A powerful, anti-infantry weapon used by the Empire to defend unsafe outposts. Palintone turret fires large-caliber shells at incredible speeds, dealing heavy damage over significant ranges. It fires huge volleys of rounds that can provide suppressive fire and quickly shred lightly armored targets. With its devastating firepower, the Palintone turret is capable of keeping even the largest enemy forces at bay. May explode when damaged.\n\nIt consumes a lot of ammunition, and requires an adjacent crate of autocannon ammo in order to automatically reload. It cannot be reloaded by hand.
LanguageData.VFED_Turret_Palintone.label: palintone
LanguageData.VFED_Turret_StrikerTurret.description: A stationary, powerful turret. Will self-destruct if it is sufficiently damaged.
LanguageData.VFED_Turret_StrikerTurret.label: striker turret
LanguageData.VFFED_Bullet_Onager.label: onager
LanguageData.VFED_Aerodrone.description: Aerodrone is a spacer-tech, unmanned aerial vehicle equipped with advanced sensors and a missile payload. Designed for autonomous long-range operations, it's commonly used by the Empire for reconnaissance and bombardment. Its ability to release powerful missile salvos makes it effective against various targets. This aerodrone is temporarily disabled due to the recharge cycle.
LanguageData.VFED_Aerodrone.label: aerodrone
LanguageData.VFED_AerodroneBatteryPack.description: A large, overcharged aerodrone battery. Will release a strong electromagnetic pulse when damaged, potentially disabling nearby defenses.
LanguageData.VFED_AerodroneBatteryPack.label: aerodrone battery pack
LanguageData.VFED_AerodroneMunitionsCrate.description: A large military crate of aerodrone munitions. Will explode when damaged, sending armed shells flying in random directions.
LanguageData.VFED_AerodroneMunitionsCrate.label: aerodrone munitions crate
LanguageData.VFED_AmmoBox_Autocannon.description: A small-sized box containing autocannon ammunition. Used by Palintone turrets to reload. May explode when damaged.
LanguageData.VFED_AmmoBox_Autocannon.label: autocannon ammo box
LanguageData.VFED_AmmoBox_Missile.description: A small-sized box containing missiles. Used by Onager turrets to reload. May explode when damaged.
LanguageData.VFED_AmmoBox_Missile.label: missile ammo box
LanguageData.VFED_AmmoBox_UraniumSlug.description: A small-sized box containing uranium slug ammunition. Used by Kontarion turrets to reload. May explode when damaged.
LanguageData.VFED_AmmoBox_UraniumSlug.label: uranium slug ammo box
LanguageData.VFED_BiosecuredCrate.description: A small imperial crate containing extremely valuable items. May contain critical intel. Due to the special restrictions, only imperial nobles are capable of opening it.\n\nAny attempt to destroy it will destroy the items as well.
LanguageData.VFED_BiosecuredCrate.label: biosecured crate
LanguageData.VFED_BurnoutIntelScraper.description: A deserter device capable of intercepting imperial communication. When activated, it will begin intel scraping, emitting a pulse in regular intervals. Each pulse has a chance of generating a small amount of intel, but it may also increase your visibility and even alert an imperial strike force. After 10 pulses, the machine burns out and triggers a fiery explosion.
LanguageData.VFED_BurnoutIntelScraper.label: burnout intel scraper
LanguageData.VFED_CrashedAerodrone.label: crashed aerodrone
LanguageData.VFED_FuelTank.description: A large fuel tank used to refuel Imperial shuttles. Will detonate if damaged beyond a certain point, potentially triggering a chain reaction.
LanguageData.VFED_FuelTank.label: fuel tank
LanguageData.VFED_ImperialMaxpack.description: The Imperial maxpack is a formidable armored transport truck, known for its impressive cargo capacity. Its armor is impenetrable, ensuring the safety of its contents throughout transit. Once in motion, the Maxpack’s doors remain sealed until it reaches its predetermined destination. While attempting to loot its cargo is futile due to built-in self-destruct protocols, its robust exterior makes it an ideal shield on the battlefield.
LanguageData.VFED_ImperialMaxpack.label: imperial maxpack
LanguageData.VFED_ImperialMegaHighShield.description: An advanced shielding apparatus, the Imperial mega high-shield projects a vast momentum repulsor field, allowing shots to exit but preventing any from entering. Specifically calibrated for high angles, it's adept at deflecting incoming mortar rounds, aerodrone strikes and even orbital slicer beams. The unit can be temporarily disabled by EMP attacks, either by shocking the projector unit itself, or using EMP munitions on the shield. Draws huge amounts of power.
LanguageData.VFED_ImperialMegaHighShield.label: imperial mega high-shield
LanguageData.VFED_ImperialUnderfarm.description: An underground hydroponics basic used by the empire to feed their subjects. Fully automated and connected to water mains, these farms require no labor to operate.
LanguageData.VFED_ImperialUnderfarm.label: imperial underfarm
LanguageData.VFED_StrikeBuoy.description: A small surveillance buoy usually deployed around temporary convoy stops. Linked directly with a patrolling aerodrone, once triggered, it will send down a small missile salvo on its location.
LanguageData.VFED_StrikeBuoy.label: strike buoy
LanguageData.VFED_SupplyCrate.description: A small imperial crate containing valuables. May contain intel. Opening it requires bypassing several security protocols and takes a short while.\n\nAny attempt to destroy it will destroy the items as well.
LanguageData.VFED_SupplyCrate.label: supply crate
LanguageData.VFED_SurveillanceDisplayBank.description: An imperial computer interface. Linked to the surveillance console, the display bank shows live footage from all surveillance pillars in the sector.
LanguageData.VFED_SurveillanceDisplayBank.label: surveillance display bank
LanguageData.VFED_SurveillancePillar.description: A large pillar with a series of sensors, cameras and motion detectors. Thanks to the sophisticated face recognition system, surveillance pillar can detect the presence of deserters in radius and will increase the visibility of whichever faction has sent them. Can be disabled via electromagnetic pulse, or by simply cutting off the power grid.
LanguageData.VFED_SurveillancePillar.label: surveillance pillar
LanguageData.VFED_SurveillanceStation.description: A large console designed to link to surrounding surveillance pillars. Provides users with all the necessary data related to enemy movements in a given sector.
LanguageData.VFED_SurveillanceStation.label: surveillance console
LanguageData.VFED_TechiteDiscStack.description: A compact stack of discs crafted from the enigmatic element known as techite, produced exclusively by the Empire for knowledge transcribing. Primarily designed for preserving information. When ignited, they release a dense cloud of blinding smoke and sparks, capable of engulfing a vast area in flames.
LanguageData.VFED_TechiteDiscStack.label: techite disc stack
LanguageData.VFED_Techprinter.description: The Techprinter is a marvel of technological innovation, designed to capture and preserve the intricate knowledge residing within a techfriar’s mind. These advanced machines imprint this invaluable information directly onto techite discs. Techprints provide foundational knowledge for researchers, shedding light on innovations from more advanced societies.
LanguageData.VFED_Techprinter.label: techprinter
LanguageData.VFED_WaterMain.description: A large node connecting all the nearby water mains. Required for the underfarms to operate.
LanguageData.VFED_WaterMain.label: water main
LanguageData.VFED_ZeusCannon.description: A relic of ancient Sophiamundan technology, the Zeus Cannon is a closely guarded secret of the Empire. This formidable artillery piece has the unique ability to convert vast quantities of electricity into potent energy blasts. With unmatched range, it can pierce the atmosphere to target and obliterate objects in orbit. A testament to lost engineering brilliance, it stands as both a weapon and a symbol of the Empire's might.\n\nIt has a long charging cycle, after which it will aim at the target and release a devastating energy blast.
LanguageData.VFED_ZeusCannon.label: zeus cannon
LanguageData.VFED_ZeusCannonCapacitorBank.description: A capacitor bank that transforms electricity into raw energy for the Zeus cannon. Will release a large EMP shockwave when destroyed, potentially disabling the Zeus cannon itself.
LanguageData.VFED_ZeusCannonCapacitorBank.label: zeus cannon capacitor bank
LanguageData.VFED_ZeusCannonControlStation.descriptio: This terminal serves as the primary interface for operating the Zeus Cannon batteries. Equipped with sophisticated controls and targeting systems, the Zeus Cannon Control Station ensures precision and coordination when deploying the formidable firepower of the Zeus Cannons. Operators need to be within its radius to control it.
LanguageData.VFED_ZeusCannonControlStation.label: zeus cannon control station
LanguageData.VFED_Filth_Propaganda.label: imperial propaganda
LanguageData.VFED_Biodecoder.description: A biodecoder is a single-use ultratech device that is designed to strip biocoding off any item. It uses a complex process to unlatch the biocoding from the original item, which transfers the biocoding onto the biodecoder, breaking it in the process. Biodecoder can also be used on biosecured crate to open them. This device is often used in sensitive operations where the presence of biocoding could compromise the mission.
LanguageData.VFED_Biodecoder.label: biodecoder
LanguageData.VFED_PsychicAmplifier.description: Decommissioned Imperial technology since the invention of psylink neuroformers, psychic amplifier upgrades a psylink in the user's mind. It cannot form a psylink by itself, and can only be used by colonists with already present psylink. The user presses the device over the eyes, where it links to the brain directly and restructures part of it. Afterwards, the device disintegrates into worthless ash.
LanguageData.VFED_PsychicAmplifier.label: psychic amplifier
LanguageData.VFED_CriticalIntel.description: A dossier containing critical information about the Shattered Empire. In order to protect the information from the Imperial counterintelligence, the information needs to be stored in a physical form. It can be used to plan high-profile assassination attempts, but it can also be exchanged into standard intel via the contraband system.
LanguageData.VFED_CriticalIntel.label: critical intel
LanguageData.VFED_Intel.description: A dossier containing information about the Shattered Empire. In order to protect the information from the Imperial counterintelligence, the information needs to be stored in a physical form. It can be used to obtain contraband items from the Deserters, or to gain access to quests and even assassination missions on Imperial nobility.
LanguageData.VFED_Intel.label: intel
LanguageData.VFED_Bullet_Shell_ArmorPiercing.label: armor-piercing shell
LanguageData.VFED_Bullet_Shell_Cluster.label: cluster shell
LanguageData.VFED_Bullet_Shell_Shrapnel.label: shrapnel shell
LanguageData.VFED_Bullet_Shrapnel.label: shrapnel
LanguageData.VFED_Shell_ArmorPiercing.description: Armor-piercing shells are designed to explode upon impact and destroy anything they hit, completely bypassing the armor of the target. Their explosion radius is almost non-existent, and armored targets need to be hit directly for the shell to destroy them. They are very accurate and travel at extreme speeds.
LanguageData.VFED_Shell_ArmorPiercing.label: armor-piercing shell
LanguageData.VFED_Shell_Cluster.description: Mortar shell that is designed to split mid-air and release three high-explosive shells. Separated shells are ejected in random directions, following the same vector as the cluster shell. Explosions cover a high area but the accuracy of the shells leave a lot to be desired.
LanguageData.VFED_Shell_Cluster.label: cluster shell
LanguageData.VFED_Shell_Shrapnel.description: Mortar shell that is designed to explode mid-air and release a large number of sharp projectiles. These projectiles are specifically designed to cut through light armored enemies, inflicting serious injuries or fatalities. Projectiles are ejected with the same vector as the mortar shell, and may be stopped by obstacles.
LanguageData.VFED_Shell_Shrapnel.label: shrapnel shell
LanguageData.VFED_AerodroneStrikeIncoming.label: aerodrone strike
LanguageData.VFED_Spark.label: spark
LanguageData.VFED_Bullet_ChargedShot.label: charged shot
LanguageData.VFED_Gun_ChargeCycler.description: A modified charged-shot revolver that incorporates mechanoid technology. Recovered thump cannons have been reversed engineered to allow for a powerful charge revolver with two shooting modes to be created. Charged shot mode will eliminate any close targets with a powerful, armor-piercing blast, whilst a Thump shot mode allows users to incapacitate enemies and break any defenses they might be hiding behind. Modes can be changed with a press of a button.
LanguageData.VFED_Gun_ChargeCycler.label: charge cycler
LanguageData.VFED_Gun_ChargeCycler.tools.0.label: handle
LanguageData.VFED_Gun_ChargeCycler.tools.1.label: barrel
LanguageData.VFED_Gun_ChargeCycler.verbs.0.label: single shot (charged shot)
LanguageData.VFED_Gun_ChargeCycler.verbs.1.label: single shot (thump shot)
LanguageData.VFED_Gun_Fletchling.description: A modification to the extremely rare fletcher rifle, fletchling is a shotgun commonly used by Imperial deserters. The mere possession of it can be seen as an act of war against the Shattered Empire. Each fletchling shot releases a number of small acidifier darts that may hit several targets, causing extreme amounts of pain and acid burns. Lethal to organics and almost useless against non-organics, such as mechanoids. \n\nTargets hit by these flechettes will be inflicted with an unstable variant of acidifier fluids, designed to dissolve the target from the inside rather than destroy their equipment. The acid will rampage through the target’s body and usually results in vital organs being dissolved, however lethality isn’t guaranteed.
LanguageData.VFED_Gun_Fletchling.label: fletchling
LanguageData.VFED_Gun_Fletchling.tools.0.label: barrel
LanguageData.VFEE_Bullet_FletchlingDart.label: fletcher dart
LanguageData.VFEE_Bullet_ThumpShot.label: thump shot
LanguageData.VFED_DeserterShuttleIncoming.label: deserter shuttle (incoming)
LanguageData.VFED_DeserterShuttleLeaving.label: deserter shuttle (leaving)
LanguageData.VPEP_Degrade.label: degrade
LanguageData.VPEP_MindJump.label: mind jump
LanguageData.Bullet_Badger_MainTurret.label: anti-infantry shell
LanguageData.VVE_ArtilleryWreck.description: An ancient, broken self propelled artillery. Everything that isn't rusted away was looted long ago. Given enough resources, it can be restored into an Udar.
LanguageData.VVE_ArtilleryWreck.label: artillery wreck
LanguageData.VVE_BattleTankWreck.description: An ancient, broken battle tank. Everything that isn't rusted away was looted long ago. Given enough resources, it can be restored into a Marshal.
LanguageData.VVE_BattleTankWreck.label: battle tank wreck
LanguageData.VVE_CargoHelicopterWreck.description: An ancient, crashed cargo helicopter. Everything that isn't rusted away was looted long ago. Given enough resources, it can be restored into a Cherokee.
LanguageData.VVE_CargoHelicopterWreck.label: cargo helicopter wreck
LanguageData.VVE_ClassicCarWreck.description: An ancient, broken classic car. Everything that isn't rusted away was looted long ago. Given enough resources, it can be restored into a Springbok.
LanguageData.VVE_ClassicCarWreck.label: classical car wreck
LanguageData.VVE_HelicopterWreck.description: An ancient, broken helicopter. Everything that isn't rusted away was looted long ago. Given enough resources, it can be restored into a Louie.
LanguageData.VVE_HelicopterWreck.label: helicopter wreck
LanguageData.VVE_InfantryFightingVehicleWreck.descrip: An ancient, broken infantry fighting vehicle. Everything that isn't rusted away was looted long ago. Given enough resources, it can be restored into a Badger.
LanguageData.VVE_InfantryFightingVehicleWreck.label: infantry fighting vehicle wreck
LanguageData.VVE_LightArmoredVehicleWreck.description: An ancient, broken light armored vehicle. Everything that isn't rusted away was looted long ago. Given enough resources, it can be restored into a Paladin.
LanguageData.VVE_LightArmoredVehicleWreck.label: light armored vehicle wreck
LanguageData.VVE_OffroadJeepWreck.description: An ancient, broken offroad jeep. Everything that isn't rusted away was looted long ago. Given enough resources, it can be restored into a Nomad.
LanguageData.VVE_OffroadJeepWreck.label: offroad jeep wreck
LanguageData.VVE_RefrigeratedTruckWreck.description: An ancient, broken refrigerated truck. Everything that isn't rusted away was looted long ago. Given enough resources, it can be restored into a Hermano.
LanguageData.VVE_RefrigeratedTruckWreck.label: refrigerated truck wreck
LanguageData.VVE_RocketArtilleryWreck.description: An ancient, broken rocket artillery truck. Everything that isn't rusted away was looted long ago. Given enough resources, it can be restored into a Burya.
LanguageData.VVE_RocketArtilleryWreck.label: rocket artillery wreck
LanguageData.VVE_SiegePodWreck.description: An ancient, destroyed siege pod. Everything that isn't rusted away was looted long ago. Given enough resources, it can be restored into a Goliath.
LanguageData.VVE_SiegePodWreck.label: siege pod wreck
LanguageData.VVE_SupercarWreck.description: An ancient, broken supercar. Everything that isn't rusted away was looted long ago. Given enough resources, it can be restored into a Lightning.
LanguageData.VVE_SupercarWreck.label: supercar wreck
LanguageData.VVE_TruckWreck.description: An ancient, broken truck. Everything that isn't rusted away was looted long ago. Given enough resources, it can be restored into a Big Rig.
LanguageData.VVE_TruckWreck.label: truck wreck
LanguageData.Bullet_Marshal_MainTurret.label: high-explosive shell
LanguageData.VFET_Apparel_TribalHeavy.description: A full-body garment crafted using neolithic tools. While it may look primitive, it is very effective at insulating against cold temperatures. Provides slightly more protection than the standard tribalwear.
LanguageData.VFET_Apparel_TribalHeavy.label: heavy tribalwear
LanguageData.VFET_Apparel_TribalLight.description: A torso-only garment crafted using neolithic tools. While it may look primitive, it is very effective at insulating against heat temperatures.
LanguageData.VFET_Apparel_TribalLight.label: light tribalwear
LanguageData.VFET_Apparel_TribalWar.description: A full-body garment crafted using neolithic tools. While it may look primitive, it is very effective at insulating against cold temperatures. Provides slightly more protection than the standard tribalwear.
LanguageData.VFET_Apparel_TribalWar.label: war tribalwear
LanguageData.VFET_FloorPainting.description: A neolithic-style floor painting depicting an artistic form. Increases in beauty as the colony tech level changes.
LanguageData.VFET_FloorPainting.label: floor painting
LanguageData.VFET_SittingSpot.description: A spot on which to sit. Not very comfortable, and only marginally better than nothing. Can be used at tables, work stations, and elsewhere.
LanguageData.VFET_SittingSpot.label: sitting spot
LanguageData.VFET_ResearchSpot.description: A spot to think on, before a simple research bench can be constructed. Researchers work here to discover new things.
LanguageData.VFET_ResearchSpot.label: research spot
LanguageData.VFET_AnimalTrap.description: A large, sharp spike under tension, connected to a touch-activated trigger. If an animal touches the trigger, the spike springs forth and strikes it. Since it is hidden in the surrounding terrain, it cannot be placed adjacent to other traps. Animals do not sense these when calm, but they are quite obvious to humans and will be avoided by them.
LanguageData.VFET_AnimalTrap.label: animal trap
LanguageData.VFET_LargeFire.description: Produces large amounts of heat and smoke, and burns out after a quadrum. The light from a large fire is visible from afar. As long as it’s burning outdoors, there is a higher chance of wild men wandering in, as well as raids. As with all heat sources, it must be placed indoors so it has a closed space to heat. Refuelable. Due to the size and instability of it, it may eject sparks around itself that may start a fire.
LanguageData.VFET_LargeFire.label: large fire
LanguageData.VFET_Chunk.description: A chunk of rock, carefully picked to have the highest smashing potential.
LanguageData.VFET_Chunk.label: chunk
LanguageData.VFET_Chunk.tools.0.label: rock
LanguageData.VFET_Stake.description: A sharpened wooden log. Can deal substantial damage, but is prone to breaking and deteriorates fast. Good at killing sanguophages.
LanguageData.VFET_Stake.label: stake
LanguageData.VFET_Stake.tools.0.label: shaft
LanguageData.VFET_Stake.tools.1.label: point
LanguageData.VFET_LargeFireSpark.label: spark
LanguageData.VFET_Throwspikes.description: Throwspikes are short sticks sharpened on both sides. They take a while to throw, but can do reasonable damage when they hit. This weapon represents a bundle of throwspikes and can be thrown over and over.
LanguageData.VFET_Throwspikes.label: throwspikes
LanguageData.VFET_Throwspikes.tools.0.label: limb
LanguageData.VFET_ThrowspikesThrown.label: throwspikes
LanguageData.VRecyclingE_LargePollutionPump.descripti: A large, groundwater-filtering pump which slowly cleans large swathes of polluted terrain. The extracted pollution is formed into toxic wastepacks which must be hauled away.
LanguageData.VRecyclingE_LargePollutionPump.label: large pollution pump
LanguageData.VRecyclingE_PollutionFastPump.descriptio: A rapidly-working, groundwater-filtering pump which cleans polluted terrain. The extracted pollution is formed into toxic wastepacks which must be hauled away. It requires a large amount of electricity to work, but it works extremely quickly.
LanguageData.VRecyclingE_PollutionFastPump.label: pollution fastpump
LanguageData.VRecyclingE_WasteCrate.description: A hermetically sealed container that prevents toxic waste stored inside from dissolving and leaking into the environment. Can only hold a limited amount of waste. Does nothing to prevent the waste from burning.
LanguageData.VRecyclingE_WasteCrate.label: waste crate
LanguageData.VRecyclingE_AlloypackSplitter.descriptio: A spacertech device that separates stabilized alloypacks into steel, plasteel and gold compounds. This process takes a long time and requires a lot of power.\n\nConsider that the building will need free space behind it to produce its output!
LanguageData.VRecyclingE_AlloypackSplitter.label: alloypack splitter
LanguageData.VRecyclingE_ElectricRecyclingWorkbench.d: A workbench with automated sorters and electric tools, used in recycling wastepacks into other resources. Can work without electricity at 50% of normal speed.
LanguageData.VRecyclingE_GarbageCompactor.description: An industrial-tech device that condenses trash using hydraulic press into trashbricks. While it does not neutralize the trash completely, it allows for a creation of a construction resource out of it. The process requires considerable amounts of power and emits tox gas during operation.
LanguageData.VRecyclingE_GarbageCompactor.label: garbage compactor
LanguageData.VRecyclingE_RecyclingWorkbench.descripti: A workbench for recycling wastepacks into other resources. Works at 50% of the speed of an electric recycling workbench.
LanguageData.VRecyclingE_RecyclingWorkbench.label: recycling bench
LanguageData.VRecyclingE_RadioactiveIsotopack.descrip: A hazardous package of radioactive isotopes, resulting from the recycling of toxic waste. The surface is heat-sealed for safe transport. Radioactive Isotopacks emit high levels of radiation, posing a serious threat to the health of colonists and animals. If damaged or burned, they release a burst of dangerous radiation.
LanguageData.VRecyclingE_RadioactiveIsotopack.label: radioactive isotopack
LanguageData.VRecyclingE_ReactiveChempack.description: A volatile package of highly reactive chemicals, generated as a byproduct of recycling toxic waste. The surface is heat-sealed for safe transport. Reactive Chempacks are extremely dangerous and can cause devastating explosions if not handled carefully. If damaged or burned, they may explode in a big fireball.
LanguageData.VRecyclingE_ReactiveChempack.label: reactive chempack
LanguageData.VRecyclingE_ReclaimedBiopack.description: A repurposed package of nutrient-rich bio-material, recovered from toxic waste through advanced recycling processes. The surface is heat-sealed for safe transport. Reclaimed Biopacks improve soil fertility if they deteriorate on standard soil. If damaged or burned, they emit a small amount of disgusting rot stink.
LanguageData.VRecyclingE_ReclaimedBiopack.label: reclaimed biopack
LanguageData.VRecyclingE_StabilizedAlloypack.descript: An amalgamation of strong and lightweight alloys, synthesized from toxic waste through complex chemical reactions. The surface is heat-sealed for safe transport. Stabilized Alloypacks contain high-quality material for crafting advanced weapons, armor, and components. If damaged or burned, they release a small puff of non-toxic smoke.
LanguageData.VRecyclingE_StabilizedAlloypack.label: stabilized alloypack
LanguageData.VRecyclingE_Trash.description: A package of non-toxic rubbish, carefully sorted and compacted for efficient storage. The surface is heat-sealed for safe transport. Trash poses no risk of pollution or deterioration, making it safe for long-term storage. However, if burned, trash will release a moderate amount of tox gas as a byproduct of combustion.
LanguageData.VRecyclingE_Trash.label: trash
LanguageData.VRecyclingE_VolatileToxpack.description: An unstable package of highly concentrated toxic waste, created as a byproduct in more complex recycling projects. The surface is heat-sealed for safe transport. Volatile toxpacks are extremely dangerous due to their instability and propensity to emit large amounts of tox gas and pollution. If burned or damaged, they release a massive cloud of deadly tox gas.
LanguageData.VRecyclingE_VolatileToxpack.label: volatile toxpack
LanguageData.VRecyclingE_TrashBrick.description: A stack of solid bricks, created by compressing non-toxic trash into a brick form. The surface is heat-sealed for safe transport. Trashbricks are a durable building material for constructing walls and structures. If damaged or burned, they will release a moderate amount of toxic fumes and pollute the area. Trashbricks do not deteriorate.
LanguageData.VRecyclingE_TrashBrick.label: trashbrick
LanguageData.SmallHotSpring.description:
A natural hot spring whose steaming waters act as a meditative joy source, help cleanse the body of illnesses, and assist in the healing of wounds. Built on top of a naturally occuring steam geyser.

 Up to 4 pawns can use it simultaneously.

A large amount of clever excavation and painstakingly assembled stoneworks are required to allow the boiling hot waters deep underground to come to the surface as pure bathing water. Construction of this building requires an exceptionally high amount of labor. Average constructors will need several workdays in optimal conditions to complete this building. (Eight hours of labor at 100% work speed is about 333 work.)
