It performs a variety of biological alterations including age reversal and pleasure-giving. The pod biotunes to its user at the end of a cycle. While biotuned, it cannot be used by anyone else, but cycles will complete 25% more quickly. Biotuning resets if the pod is unused for 80 days. It draws power directly from the grav engine.
LanguageData.VGE_CompactBiosculpterPod.label: compact biosculpter pod
LanguageData.VGE_EngineeringConsole.description: An interface used to monitor the maintenance status of all gravship components. It draws power directly from the grav engine.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay impact:</color> Reduces maintenance sensitivity of all gravship structures on this gravship by 0.5.
LanguageData.VGE_EngineeringConsole.label: engineering console
LanguageData.VGE_Bullet_MassDriver.label: mass driver shot
LanguageData.VGE_Gun_HeavyChargeAnnihilator.descripti: A heavy pulse-charged weapon for mechanoid defense.
LanguageData.VGE_Gun_HeavyChargeAnnihilator.label: heavy charge annihilator
LanguageData.VGE_Gun_MassDriver.label: mass driver
LanguageData.VGE_HeavyChargeAnnihilator.description: A heavy pulse-charged weapon, designed for sustained suppressive fire in defense of mechanoid orbital platforms. Its upgraded capacitors and cooling systems allow it to unleash longer, more devastating bursts than standard charge blasters, saturating entire areas with surprisingly accurate shots.
LanguageData.VGE_HeavyChargeAnnihilator.label: heavy charge annihilator
LanguageData.VGE_LargeMechanoidThruster.description: A massive astrofuel thruster built by the mechanoid core to propel their orbital platforms. Its construction mirrors human design but is reinforced with mechanical precision and efficiency, lined with thick vacstone plating that shrugs off extreme heat and stress. These thrusters allow mechanoid platforms to maneuver and maintain position in orbit. While unusable for human gravships, destroying one will yield a small supply of precious vacstone blocks.
LanguageData.VGE_LargeMechanoidThruster.label: large mechanoid thruster
LanguageData.VGE_MassDriver.description: A triple-barrel electromagnetic artillery system built into mechanoid defense platforms. It fires dense slugs at incredible velocities, tearing through both armored gravships in orbit and ground targets with equal ease. The weapon is fully autonomous, operated by a machine brain that requires no crew. Commonly seen on orbital bombardment platforms.
LanguageData.VGE_MassDriver.label: mass driver
LanguageData.VGE_MechanoidAstrofuelNode.description: A compact fuel reservoir integrated into mechanoid platforms. It stores refined astrofuel for their systems, but when damaged it is highly unstable and prone to violent explosions.
LanguageData.VGE_MechanoidAstrofuelNode.label: astrofuel node
LanguageData.VGE_MechanoidGravEngine.description: The cold, artificial heart of a mechanoid orbital platform. Unlike human-built grav engines, these are constructed by the mechanoid core itself, optimized for ruthless efficiency and unending operation. They generate immense lift and stabilization fields that allow entire platforms to remain suspended in orbit, forming the backbone of mechanoid dominance in space. While largely incompatible with human systems and not usable for gravship construction, they can be destroyed to yield a gravcore, a rare and valuable prize for any who dare to challenge them.
LanguageData.VGE_MechanoidGravEngine.label: mechanoid grav engine
LanguageData.VGE_MechanoidPodLauncher.description: A mechanized launch bay that periodically fires drop pods packed with mechanoids. These pods strike distant targets or nearby ships, allowing the platform to project its forces far beyond its hull.
LanguageData.VGE_MechanoidPodLauncher.label: mechanoid pod launcher
LanguageData.VGE_MechanoidResourceProcessor.descripti: A machine-built structure that sustains mechanoid platforms by breaking down cosmic debris and wreckage into usable resources. It operates at regular intervals, steadily producing metals and fuels needed to keep their orbiting strongholds supplied.
LanguageData.VGE_MechanoidResourceProcessor.label: resource processor
LanguageData.VGE_MechanoidTempShield.description: A compact defense unit that projects a localized shield around itself. The barrier activates only for a short time before collapsing, after which the device must cool down before it can deploy again.
LanguageData.VGE_MechanoidTempShield.label: mechanoid temp shield
LanguageData.VGE_Compressed_Gold.description: Gold-rich strata crushed and recrystallized into a tight, resilient vein. Found only in deep space.
LanguageData.VGE_Compressed_Gold.label: compressed gold ore
LanguageData.VGE_Compressed_Jade.description: Jade-bearing vacstone compacted into a rigid, fine-grained mass. Found only in deep space.
LanguageData.VGE_Compressed_Jade.label: compressed jade
LanguageData.VGE_Compressed_Plasteel.description: Ultra-dense plasteel conglomerate formed in vacuum over generations; brutally tough to mine. Found only in deep space.
LanguageData.VGE_Compressed_Plasteel.label: compressed plasteel
LanguageData.VGE_Compressed_Silver.description: Silver-bearing rock compacted under extreme pressure into a dense, uniform seam. Found only in deep space.
LanguageData.VGE_Compressed_Silver.label: compressed silver ore
LanguageData.VGE_Compressed_Steel.description: Ancient structural mass fused into a single, ultra-dense vacstone-steel deposit. Found only in deep space.
LanguageData.VGE_Compressed_Steel.label: compressed steel
LanguageData.VGE_Compressed_Uranium.description: Uranium-laden rock sintered into a nearly monolithic deposit, difficult to fracture. Found only in deep space.
LanguageData.VGE_Compressed_Uranium.label: compressed uranium ore
LanguageData.VGE_Compressed_Vacstone.description: An ultra-dense mass of asteroid rock and dust, compacted over millennia into a near-indestructible block. Found only in deep space.
LanguageData.VGE_Compressed_Vacstone.label: compressed vacstone
LanguageData.VGE_GravcorePowerCoil.description: A massive gravtech reactor that channels and amplifies gravitational wave energy into immense electrical output. Its reinforced coils can handle extreme flux, making it one of the most potent power sources available for gravships. Adjacent gravcore power cells will multiply the power output by 15% for each gravcore power cell.
LanguageData.VGE_GravcorePowerCoil.label: gravcore power coil
LanguageData.VGE_GravshipCapacitor.description: Stores high amounts of electricity for later use. Constructed in the gravship substructure, gravship capacitors do not obstruct movement.
LanguageData.VGE_GravshipCapacitor.label: gravship capacitor
LanguageData.VGE_SolarPanelling.description: Produces electricity from sunlight. Does not work in the dark or under artificial light. Does not obstruct movement or provide cover, but can only be constructed on the gravship substructure.
LanguageData.VGE_SolarPanelling.label: solar panelling
LanguageData.VGE_CompactMultiAnalyzer.description: A miniaturized version of the multi-analyzer, designed to work aboard a gravship. While taking up less space, it performs the same essential function: increasing research speed when placed near a high-tech research bench and unlocking advanced projects. Each bench can only benefit from one multi-analyzer. It draws power directly from the grav engine.
LanguageData.VGE_CompactMultiAnalyzer.label: compact multi-analyzer
LanguageData.VGE_CompactResearchBench.description: A miniaturized version of the high-tech research bench, designed to fit within the confined spaces of a gravship. Despite its compact size, it features the same computers and electronic equipment, providing the same research capabilities and unlocking advanced projects as its larger counterpart. It draws power directly from the grav engine.
LanguageData.VGE_CompactResearchBench.label: compact research bench
LanguageData.VGE_FlightComputer.description: An advanced computational unit that optimizes data collection during gravship travel. Increases the amount of gravdata gained from each launch.
LanguageData.VGE_FlightComputer.label: flight computer
LanguageData.VGE_GravshipBlackBox.description: An indestructible advanced recorder integrated into the gravship’s systems. It greatly increases gravdata yield, captures any overflow that would normally be wasted, and allows stored gravdata to be converted directly into research points. A gravship can support only one black box.
LanguageData.VGE_GravshipBlackBox.label: gravship black box
LanguageData.VGE_GravtechConsole.description: An interface used to analyze gravdata gathered from gravship launches and travel. It draws power directly from the grav engine.
LanguageData.VGE_GravtechConsole.label: gravtech console
LanguageData.VGE_GravshipScannerCluster.description: An advanced multi-frequency sensor cluster used by researchers to detect resources underground, across the planet, or in orbit. The scanner can be tuned to target specific minerals underground, or over long range, or set to scan for orbital objects. Detection rates depend on the operator's research ability. Buried resources will require deep drills to extract, while distant mineral deposits and orbital objects must be visited to be collected. Building multiple units does not increase the frequency of orbital detections. It draws power directly from the grav engine.
LanguageData.VGE_GravshipScannerCluster.label: gravship scanner cluster
LanguageData.VGE_GravshipStructuralBeam.description: A structural beam around which a gravship can be constructed. It extends the area in which substructure can be built in a 5x5 square. If destroyed, it causes adjacent beams to collapse.
LanguageData.VGE_GravshipStructuralBeam.label: gravship structural beam
LanguageData.VGE_CapacitorHarmonizer.description: A specialized device that balances and synchronizes energy flow between nearby capacitors. It passively boosts their charge and discharge efficiency to peak performance. Requires power to operate.
LanguageData.VGE_CapacitorHarmonizer.label: capacitor harmonizer
LanguageData.VGE_Gravlift.description: A complex vertical launch and stabilization platform integrated into the gravship’s core structure. The gravlift provides the immense thrust required for vertical takeoff from planetary surfaces and stabilizes orbital insertion, allowing the gravship to remain suspended in space. It also softens the crash landing greatly, reducing the damage taken and ensuring substructure does not suffer critical damage.
LanguageData.VGE_Gravlift.label: gravlift
LanguageData.VGE_GunnerSubpersonaCore.description: A low-grade subpersona specialized in gravship combat. It can link with a single gravship artillery piece and control it the same way a crewmember manning a targeting terminal can. It's combat capabilities are beyond that of even the best human gunner. A gravship can support up to two gunner subpersona cores.
LanguageData.VGE_GunnerSubpersonaCore.label: gunner subpersona core
LanguageData.VGE_MaintenanceHub.description: A centralized system that optimizes and automates routine upkeep across the gravship. It passively reduces the maintenance decay rate of all connected machinery. Requires power to work.
LanguageData.VGE_MaintenanceHub.label: maintenance hub
LanguageData.VGE_VacBarrierDouble.label: vac barrier (2x1)
LanguageData.VGE_VacBarrierQuintuple.label: vac barrier (5x1)
LanguageData.VGE_VacBarrierTriple.label: vac barrier (3x1)
LanguageData.VGE_VacCheckpoint.description: A safety checkpoint indicating the entrance to vacuum environment. Colonists will not cross it without required vacuum resistance.
LanguageData.VGE_VacCheckpoint.label: vac checkpoint
LanguageData.VGE_Filth_Astrofuel.label: astrofuel puddle
LanguageData.VGE_Filth_DamagedSubstructure.label: damaged substructure debris
LanguageData.VGE_Astrofuel.description: A high-energy synthetic fuel. Used to power gravship thrusters and boosters, or to generate electricity in specialized systems. Extremely combustible, it burns violently even in low-oxygen environments.
LanguageData.VGE_Astrofuel.label: astrofuel
LanguageData.VGE_OxygenCanister.description: A life-sustaining gas. Used to pressurize rooms, supply breathable air, or fuel specialized equipment and survival gear. Volatile when damaged.
LanguageData.VGE_OxygenCanister.label: oxygen canister
LanguageData.VGE_Oxyalgae.description: A dense mat of harvested aquatic algae, cultivated for use in life support systems. Oxyalgae provides steady oxygen generation but spoils quickly if not processed.
LanguageData.VGE_Oxyalgae.label: oxyalgae
LanguageData.VGE_HiddenOxygenPipe.description: A hidden, reinforced tube for moving oxygen around. While it’s not visible, it is much more expensive to construct.
LanguageData.VGE_HiddenOxygenPipe.label: hidden oxygen pipe
LanguageData.VGE_IndustrialOxygenPump.description: A powerful unit that releases large amounts of oxygen into the surrounding room, allowing enclosed areas to repressurize in space. Essential for long stays in orbit.
LanguageData.VGE_IndustrialOxygenPump.label: industrial oxygen pump
LanguageData.VGE_LargeOxygenTank.description: A large oxygen tank that supplies oxygen to pumps throughout the gravship.
LanguageData.VGE_LargeOxygenTank.label: large oxygen tank
LanguageData.VGE_LifeSupportSystem.description: A vital device that processes harvested oxyalgae into breathable oxygen. Converts biomass into gas and pumps it into the oxygen network to supply pressurized rooms and equipment.
LanguageData.VGE_LifeSupportSystem.label: algae oxygenator
LanguageData.VGE_OxygenCanisterEmptier.description: A hopper-like storage structure meant to input oxygen from canisters into the network, filling up connected tanks.
LanguageData.VGE_OxygenCanisterEmptier.label: oxygen canister emptier
LanguageData.VGE_OxygenCanisterFiller.description: A hopper-like storage device meant to hold dispensed oxygen in canisters for use later.
LanguageData.VGE_OxygenCanisterFiller.label: oxygen canister filler
LanguageData.VGE_OxygenHarvester.description: A device that draws in breathable oxygen from the surrounding atmosphere and stores it in the oxygen network. It functions only on planetary surfaces with an atmosphere and will not operate in vacuum or low-oxygen environments.
LanguageData.VGE_OxygenHarvester.label: oxygen harvester
LanguageData.VGE_OxygenPipe.description: A reinforced tube for moving oxygen around. Can be placed under walls and other buildings.
LanguageData.VGE_OxygenPipe.label: oxygen pipe
LanguageData.VGE_OxygenReclaimer.description: A self-contained atmospheric unit that extracts trace oxygen from surrounding gases. It produces oxygen at a slow, steady rate, but requires no input materials. Needs to be connected to an oxygen tank via oxygen pipes to work.
LanguageData.VGE_OxygenReclaimer.label: oxygen reclaimer
LanguageData.VGE_OxygenValve.description: Switches oxygen flow on and off.
LanguageData.VGE_OxygenValve.label: oxygen valve
LanguageData.VGE_SmallOxygenTank.description: An oxygen tank that supplies oxygen to pumps throughout the gravship.
LanguageData.VGE_SmallOxygenTank.label: small oxygen tank
LanguageData.VGE_OxyalgaePlant.description: A fast-growing aquatic plant cultivated in hydroponics and used to generate oxygen when converted in algae oxygenator. Rots quickly when harvested, making immediate processing essential.
LanguageData.VGE_OxyalgaePlant.label: oxyalgae plant
LanguageData.VGE_Astronaut.description: A medium sized mechanoid designed to perform construction and firefighting tasks in orbit. It can perform blunt melee attacks if necessary. It is not affected by the slow space traversing speeds.
LanguageData.VGE_Astronaut.label: astronaut
LanguageData.VGE_Astronaut.tools.0.label: torso
LanguageData.VGE_Astropede.description: Heavily armored mechanoids adapted for zero-G warfare. Astropedes drift silently through space using gravitic stabilizers, unleashing explosive kinetic barrages from their minified mass lances. Their hovering profile makes them ideal for shipboard assaults and station breaches, though their slow movement speed leaves them vulnerable to concentrated fire.
LanguageData.VGE_Astropede.label: astropede
LanguageData.VGE_Hunter.description: A fast, human-sized combat mechanoid designed for zero-gravity warfare. It drifts effortlessly through space, closing in on the target before unleashing devastating salvos from the twin-linked javelin launcher. While the missiles strike with lethal precision, their short range forces hunters into dangerous proximity with the enemy.
LanguageData.VGE_Hunter.label: hunter
LanguageData.VGE_Hunter.tools.0.label: head
LanguageData.VGE_MassLance.description: A mechanoid-mounted portable mass driver artillery. It fires dense slugs at incredible velocities, capable of destroying even heavily armored gravships.
LanguageData.VGE_MassLance.label: mass lance
LanguageData.VGE_MassLance.tools.0.label: barrel
LanguageData.VGE_RocketswarmPod.description: A mechanoid-mounted, minified, twin-linked rocketswarm launcher. It is very effective against large groups of weaker combatants, but less effective against small numbers of armored targets.
LanguageData.VGE_RocketswarmPod.label: rocketswarm pod
LanguageData.VGE_RocketswarmPod.tools.0.label: barrel
LanguageData.VGE_LargeAsteroid.label: large asteroid
LanguageData.VGE_LargeDebris.label: large debris
LanguageData.VGE_MediumAsteroid.label: medium asteroid
LanguageData.VGE_MediumDebris.label: medium debris
LanguageData.VGE_SmallAsteroid_Projectile.label: small asteroid
LanguageData.VGE_SmallDebris.label: small debris
LanguageData.VGE_Astrofire.label: astrofire
LanguageData.VGE_AstrofireSpark.label: astrofire spark
LanguageData.VGE_LandingStructure.label: landing structure
LanguageData.VQEA_Bullet_HellsphereCannonGun.label: hellsphere cannon shot
LanguageData.VQEA_Apparel_PatientGown.description: Old and rugged patient gown. Provides no protection or insulation, but makes the medical procedures on a person wearing one just a little bit easier.
LanguageData.VQEA_Apparel_PatientGown.label: patient gown
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientLaboratoryCasket.description: A sealed research pod once used to contain human test subjects. Opening it may release whoever or whatever was left inside.
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientLaboratoryCasket.label: ancient laboratory casket
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientLaboratoryCasket_Empty.descr: An empty research pod once used to contain human test subjects.
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientLaboratoryCasket_Empty.label: empty ancient laboratory casket
LanguageData.VQEA_CandidateCryptosleepCasket.descript: A specialized cryptosleep chamber once used to preserve designated ideal test subjects for experiments. It still hums faintly with power, holding something dormant inside.
LanguageData.VQEA_CandidateCryptosleepCasket.label: candidate cryptosleep casket
LanguageData.VQEA_CandidateCryptosleepCasket_Empty.de: A specialized cryptosleep chamber once used to preserve designated ideal test subjects for experiments. It now sits empty.
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientFauxPlant.description: A fake decorative plant used to brighten sterile rooms. It’s covered in dust but still adds a bit of color.
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientFauxPlant.label: ancient faux plant
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientHospitalArmchair.description: A comfortable chair used in old hospital waiting rooms. The padding is worn but surprisingly intact.
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientHospitalArmchair.label: ancient hospital armchair
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientHospitalBench.description: A simple bench used for patients or visitors. It’s old, sturdy, and oddly comfortable.
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientHospitalBench.label: ancient hospital bench
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientHospitalCrib.description: A small metal crib once used in the vault’s nursery ward. The frame is rusted and the bedding long decayed.
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientHospitalCrib.label: ancient hospital crib
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientIncubator.description: An ancient incubation unit used for medical research or premature infants. Its systems are dead, but the shell remains mostly intact.
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientIncubator.label: ancient incubator
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientIVStand.description: A simple stand with long-empty fluid bags still hanging from it. It once delivered medicine or nutrients to patients.
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientIVStand.label: ancient IV stand
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientKitchenette.description: A compact cooking unit used by lab staff. The surfaces are corroded and the appliances no longer work.
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientKitchenette.label: ancient kitchenette
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientMedicalPartition.description: A folding partition used to divide patient areas for privacy. Its fabric is torn and coated in dust.
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientMedicalPartition.label: ancient medical partition
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientOfficeDesk.description: A sturdy desk once belonging to a researcher or administrator. Old data slates and papers still clutter the surface.
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientOfficeDesk.label: ancient office desk
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientOfficePrinter.description: A dusty old office printer. It hums quietly, though it’s unlikely to print anything useful anymore.
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientOfficePrinter.label: ancient office printer
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientRepairBench.description: A heavy workbench used to maintain laboratory equipment. The tools are gone, but the grease stains remain.
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientRepairBench.label: ancient repair desk
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientResearchCountertop.descripti: A long research table covered in scorched metal and broken instruments. It was used for genetic and chemical experiments.
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientResearchCountertop.label: ancient research countertop
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientSink.description: A rust-stained sink once used for washing hands or instruments. Water no longer flows through its cracked pipes.
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientSink.label: ancient small sink
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientStorageHeater.description: A compact electric heater from the ancient labs. It warms the air to a comfortable level when powered.
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientStorageHeater.label: ancient storage heater
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientToyBox.description: A small toy chest from the nursery wing. It’s filled with broken dolls and faded blocks. Toy boxes can be used to play with babies, satisfying their need for play.
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientToyBox.label: ancient toy box
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientTripWarning.description: A faded caution sign warning of hazards ahead. It serves no real purpose now except decoration.
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientTripWarning.label: trip warning sign
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientVitalsMonitor.description: A bedside monitor used to track patient health. Its display is now cracked and it’s broken beyond repair.
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientVitalsMonitor.label: ancient vitals monitor
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientWaterCooler.description: A dusty water dispenser used by ancient lab staff. It no longer functions but remains as a relic of everyday life.
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientWaterCooler.label: ancient water cooler
LanguageData.VQEA_BustedControlPanel.description: A ruined wall console once connected to the vault’s systems. Sparks still flicker from its shattered screen.
LanguageData.VQEA_BustedControlPanel.label: busted control panel
LanguageData.VQEA_CreepyBabyToy.description: A small, broken toy that once comforted children. Something makes it unsettling to look at. Baby decorations increase the rate at which babies fill their play need when an adult plays with them. Place these around your child play areas.
LanguageData.VQEA_CreepyBabyToy.label: creepy baby toy
LanguageData.VQEA_EmptyContainment.description: An empty containment pod. Whatever was inside got out long ago.
LanguageData.VQEA_EmptyContainment.label: empty containment
LanguageData.VQEA_LargeWoodenMilitaryCrate.descriptio: A decaying wooden crate once used for storing military supplies. It’s too rotten to contain anything now.
LanguageData.VQEA_LargeWoodenMilitaryCrate.label: rotten military crate
LanguageData.VQEA_RubbishPile.description: Piles of old plastic waste left from the laboratory staff. They smell foul.
LanguageData.VQEA_RubbishPile.label: trash bags
LanguageData.VQEA_RuinedAberrationRedirector.descript: Once a mutation-control device, now a heap of caved-in panels and ruined internal channels. Only useless scrap is left.
LanguageData.VQEA_RuinedAberrationRedirector.label: ruined aberration redirector
LanguageData.VQEA_RuinedArchitePathingArray.descripti: This routing array is now a crushed frame with its circuitry burned out. Only warped scrap can be taken from it.
LanguageData.VQEA_RuinedArchitePathingArray.label: ruined archite pathing array
LanguageData.VQEA_RuinedArchiteRecycler.description: Previously a device for reclaiming unused archite capsules, it is now crushed beyond recognition. Nothing useful remains besides twisted metal.
LanguageData.VQEA_RuinedArchiteRecycler.label: ruined archite recycler
LanguageData.VQEA_RuinedArchogenInjector.description: Once a functioning archite injection pod, its chamber and internal systems are completely destroyed. Nothing remains but twisted scrap metal.
LanguageData.VQEA_RuinedArchogenInjector.label: ruined archogen injector
LanguageData.VQEA_RuinedCognitiveRecoveryArray.descri: Once used to ease recovery after injections, its circuitry is burned out and the frame is collapsing. All that remains is bent metal and debris.
LanguageData.VQEA_RuinedCognitiveRecoveryArray.label: ruined cognitive recovery array
LanguageData.VQEA_RuinedComplexityHarmonizer.descript: The harmonizer’s cores have collapsed and its chassis is split wide open. Nothing survives except twisted metal fragments.
LanguageData.VQEA_RuinedComplexityHarmonizer.label: ruined complexity harmonizer
LanguageData.VQEA_RuinedGenomicAttenuator.description: The attenuator’s panels are torn open and its internals melted. Only broken, worthless scrap is left behind.
LanguageData.VQEA_RuinedGenomicAttenuator.label: ruined genomic attenuator
LanguageData.VQEA_RuinedMutagenInhibitorCore.descript: A massive suppression engine reduced to buckled plating and destroyed conduits. Nothing remains but heavy twisted scrap.
LanguageData.VQEA_RuinedMutagenInhibitorCore.label: ruined mutagen inhibitor core
LanguageData.VQEA_RuinedNeurostabilizerArray.descript: This stabilizer module once calmed volatile archite reactions, but now its coils and casing are shattered. Only warped scraps are left.
LanguageData.VQEA_RuinedNeurostabilizerArray.label: ruined neurostabilizer array
LanguageData.VQEA_RuinedRapidInfusionPump.description: The infusion pump’s conduits are ruptured and its inner mechanisms destroyed. Only twisted scrap can be collected from it.
LanguageData.VQEA_RuinedRapidInfusionPump.label: ruined rapid infusion pump
LanguageData.VQEA_RuinedRejectionBufferCoil.descripti: Once a shielding coil, it is now bent, split, and entirely inert. Only scraps of twisted metal remain.
LanguageData.VQEA_RuinedRejectionBufferCoil.label: ruined rejection buffer coil
LanguageData.VQEA_RuinedSpliceframeUplink.description: This uplink console has collapsed into a pile of crushed arrays and dead wiring. Only jagged scrap can be recovered.
LanguageData.VQEA_RuinedSpliceframeUplink.label: ruined spliceframe uplink
LanguageData.VQEA_RuinedTraitSelectionPrism.descripti: The crystal housing is shattered and the prism array completely destroyed. Only splintered debris and warped scrap remain.
LanguageData.VQEA_RuinedTraitSelectionPrism.label: ruined trait selection prism
LanguageData.VQEA_RustedHospitalBed.description: An adjustable medical bed with cracked padding and rusted rails. It was once used for surgeries and recovery.
LanguageData.VQEA_RustedHospitalBed.label: rusted hospital bed
LanguageData.VQEA_RustedWheeledTray.description: A rusted metal tray once used for medical instruments. It squeaks when moved and is covered in grime.
LanguageData.VQEA_RustedWheeledTray.label: rusted wheeled tray
LanguageData.VQEA_VaultTerminal.description: An ancient computer terminal used to control vault systems. It’s inactive, but its screen still flickers faintly.
LanguageData.VQEA_VaultTerminal.label: vault terminal
LanguageData.VQEA_VaultTerminalBank.description: A row of old computer terminals used to monitor vault operations. Most are dark, but a few lights still blink faintly.
LanguageData.VQEA_VaultTerminalBank.label: vault terminal bank
LanguageData.VQEA_WarpedMopBucket.description: A melted mop bucket left behind by cleaning staff. The handle has fused to the rim, making it useless.
LanguageData.VQEA_WarpedMopBucket.label: warped mop bucket
LanguageData.VQEA_WheeledBed.description: A simple rolling hospital bed designed to move patients between rooms. The wheels squeal when pushed.
LanguageData.VQEA_WheeledBed.label: wheeled bed
LanguageData.VQEA_WoodenMilitaryCrate.description: A small, rusted box that once held munitions or rations. Now it’s empty and falling apart.
LanguageData.VQEA_WoodenMilitaryCrate.label: rotten military box
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientFoosballTable.description: A relic from better days. Colonists can play table football here to relax and gain recreation.
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientFoosballTable.label: ancient foosball table
LanguageData.VQEA_AberrationRedirector.description:
A heavy redirection module built from stacked mutation siphons and counterphase nodules that reroute unstable gene echoes into controlled outcome channels. The device hums as its internal actuators shift to isolate potential aberrations.

Allows choosing between two different negative side genes on a successful injection.

Up to 1 aberration redirector can link to a single archogen injector.
LanguageData.VQEA_AberrationRedirector.label: aberration redirector
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientWonderdoc.comps.2.cycles.0.d: Initiate the bioregeneration wondercycle. This cycle will heal all fresh wounds, heal all permanent injuries, and restore all missing body parts, including limbs.\n\nIf a malfunction occurs, the patient will receive random illnesses, which may include carcinoma, as well as severe damage to some of their limbs.
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientWonderdoc.comps.2.cycles.1.d: Initiate the instarehab wondercycle. This cycle will cure all addictions, including a luciferium addiction. If an addiction is caused by a genetic dependency, the responsible gene will also be removed.\n\nIf a malfunction occurs, the patient will receive a random genetic drug dependency.
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientWonderdoc.comps.2.cycles.2.d: Initiate the time reversal wondercycle. This cycle will de-age the patient by 10 years.\n\nIf a malfunction occurs, the patient will either become a toddler again, or will become an elderly.
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientWonderdoc.comps.2.cycles.3.d: Initiate the skill reformatting wondercycle. This cycle will redistribute the current skill levels equally across all skills the patient is capable of.\n\nIf a malfunction occurs, the effect will still occur, but the experience will be distributed randomly.
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientWonderdoc.description: A mysterious ancient medical machine capable of performing powerful “wondercycles” that heal, reshape, or alter a patient in dramatic ways. Its long-neglected systems can malfunction, and the risk grows with every successful procedure. The technology is difficult to comprehend.
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientWonderdoc.label: ancient wonderdoc
LanguageData.VQEA_ArchitePathingArray.description:
A heavy tri-spire console built with archite-routing conduits and branching nanoflux matrices that split the injection stream into parallel mutagenic pathways. The array’s central node glows with shifting colors as it maps new routes for rogue archite clusters.

Provides a chance to gain a second random archite gene or a second random offset gene after a successful injection.

Up to 1 archite pathing array can link to a single archogen injector.
LanguageData.VQEA_ArchitePathingArray.label: archite pathing array
LanguageData.VQEA_ArchiteRecycler.description:
A two-chamber nanite reclamation unit fitted with siphon coils and phase-binding spirals that draw unstable archite residue back into a containment loop. Inside, a dense mesh of microfiltration veins reassembles loose nanite clusters into a usable capsule.

Refunds the archite capsule on a rejected injection when linked to an archogen injector.

Up to 1 archite recycler can link to a single archogen injector.
LanguageData.VQEA_ArchiteRecycler.label: archite recycler
LanguageData.VQEA_ArchogenInjector.description: An ancient, hermetically sealed pod designed to induce powerful but chaotic genetic alterations through archovapor immersion. A primary slot seems to feed archite capsules into a destabilization chamber, where they are vaporized and channeled into the host body. Efforts can be made to control the process from there, but perhaps trying to harness this power is what left this machine abandoned in the first place. \n\nUsed to inject a colonist with archite capsules, triggering genetic alteration and producing a random archite gene with possible side effects. Success can be improved by linking it with additional ancient equipment.
LanguageData.VQEA_ArchogenInjector.label: archogen injector
LanguageData.VQEA_BustedAncientWonderdoc.description: Once capable of performing powerful wondercycles, this ancient medical machine has fully broken down after a catastrophic malfunction. Its chambers are cracked, its systems dead, and it can no longer be used. Some valuables may still be salvaged from it.
LanguageData.VQEA_BustedAncientWonderdoc.label: busted ancient wonderdoc
LanguageData.VQEA_CognitiveRecoveryArray.description: A slim neuro-cascade module lined with mnemonic pulse coils and echo-field dampers that cycle through soft, rhythmic memory stabilization waves. Its internal neurogel reservoir circulates thought-restorative pulses that soothe post-archite neural disruption.\n\nReduces the duration of the injection coma by 1 day when linked to an archogen injector.. Up to 6 cognitive recovery arrays can link to a single archogen injector.
LanguageData.VQEA_CognitiveRecoveryArray.label: cognitive recovery array
LanguageData.VQEA_ComplexityHarmonizer.description:
A tall stabilization unit filled with layered geno-harmonic plates and oscillating phase regulators that smooth out chaotic complexity spikes within the archite stream. Its internal synchronizer cores emit low rhythmic pulses as they bring tangled genetic pathways into orderly alignment.

Prevents genetic complexity from reducing success chance, but each point of complexity will increase the injection procedure duration by 1 hour.

Up to 1 complexity harmonizer can link to a single archogen injector.
LanguageData.VQEA_ComplexityHarmonizer.label: complexity harmonizer
LanguageData.VQEA_GenomicAttenuator.description:
A large quad-node stabilizer rig packed with geno-dampening regulators and microphase baffles that mute unstable metabolic signatures. Its internal attenuation plates hum softly as they pare down excessive gene volatility during archite infusion.

Reduces the metabolism strength of the random side-effect gene by 1 when linked to an archogen injector.

Up to 4 genomic attenuators can link to a single archogen injector.
LanguageData.VQEA_GenomicAttenuator.label: genomic attenuator
LanguageData.VQEA_MutagenInhibitorCore.description:
A massive containment engine built around a tri-core genoquench reactor and layered inhibition plates designed to neutralize runaway mutagenic surges. Thick conduits cycle suppressive counterwaves that smother mutation cascades before they can form.

Removes all mutation outcome chances entirely when linked to an Archogen Injector, but greatly increases the rejection chance.

Up to 1 mutagen inhibitor core can link to a single archogen injector.
LanguageData.VQEA_MutagenInhibitorCore.label: mutagen inhibitor core
LanguageData.VQEA_NeurostabilizerArray.description: A compact neural-phase regulator fitted with microtuned synapse coils and a vented cortex lattice that emits a steady hum of stabilizing neurostatic fields. Its core uses drifting neuroflux anchors to calm archite turbulence during injection cycles.\n\nImproves the success chance of archite injections when linked to an archogen injector. Up to 10 neurostabilizer Arrays can link to a single archogen injector.
LanguageData.VQEA_NeurostabilizerArray.label: neurostabilizer array
LanguageData.VQEA_RapidInfusionPump.description:
A high-pressure archite-flow pump fitted with tri-phase accelerators and microchannel conduits that push volatile nanites through the injector lattice at extreme speed. Stabilizing fins vibrate as the pump regulates infusion pressure across the gene chamber.

Shortens the overall injection time by 12 hours when linked to an archogen injector.

Up to 9 rapid infusion pumps can link to a single archogen injector.
LanguageData.VQEA_RapidInfusionPump.label: rapid infusion pump
LanguageData.VQEA_RejectionBufferCoil.description:
An insulated coil wrapped in ionized synapse wire and anchored with anti-reflux stabilizers that shield the injector chamber from archite backlash. When active, it emits a soft oscillation designed to suppress rejection-triggering impulses in the nanite stream.

Decreases the rejection chance of the injection procedure when linked to an archogen injector.

Up to 5 rejection buffer coils can link to a single archogen injector.
LanguageData.VQEA_RejectionBufferCoil.label: rejection buffer coil
LanguageData.VQEA_SpliceframeUplink.description:
A dense uplink pillar fitted with resonance spire arrays and gene-phase telemetry coils that scrape and decode archite fluctuation patterns. Its chroma-linked neural mesh emits faint pulses as it maps unstable outcomes across an ancient spliceframe network.

Reveals all outcome chances in the archogen injector and slightly increases the odds of mutation outcomes.

Up to 1 spliceframe uplink can link to a single archogen injector.
LanguageData.VQEA_SpliceframeUplink.label: spliceframe uplink
LanguageData.VQEA_TraitSelectionPrism.description:
A crystalline multi-lens console powered by phased neuro-optic conduits and reflective geno-beam sequencers that split archite outcomes into selectable possibility streams. Its prism array glows with shifting spectral patterns when a stable result is detected.

Allows choosing between two different archite genes on a successful injection.

Up to 1 trait selection prism can link to a single archogen injector.
LanguageData.VQEA_TraitSelectionPrism.label: trait selection prism
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientSunLamp.description: An old surgical lamp that shines brightly enough to grow crops when powered.
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientSunLamp.label: ancient sun lamp
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientVaultLamp.description: A standing lamp built for the vaults. It emits a steady, cold light.
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientVaultLamp.label: ancient vault lamp
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientGenepackCrate.description: A secure storage crate used to hold genetic data packs. It can be hacked open to recover the genepacks inside.
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientGenepackCrate.label: ancient genepack crate
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientGenepackCrate_Empty.descript: A secure storage crate used to hold genetic data packs. It's empty now.
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientGenepackCrate_Empty.label: ancient genepack crate
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientOrganBox.description: A secure storage box with a built-in cooling system, typically used to preserve organs over extended durations. Can be hacked open to release whatever remains inside.
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientOrganBox.label: ancient organ box
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientOrganBox_Empty.description: An empty storage box with a built-in cooling system, typically used to preserve organs over extended durations.
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientOrganBox_Empty.label: empty ancient organ box
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientPipelineJunction_Off.descrip: A junction of old chemfuel pipes that still leaks faint fumes. It can be tapped to extract a small amount of chemfuel, though no one knows its source.
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientPipelineJunction_Off.label: ancient pipeline junction
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientPipelineJunction_On.descript: A junction of old chemfuel pipes that still leaks faint fumes. It can be tapped to extract a small amount of chemfuel, though no one knows its source. It can be tapped now.
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientPipelineJunction_On.label: ancient pipeline junction
LanguageData.VQEA_ArchiteCapsuleContainment.descripti: A containment unit built to store and release archite capsules for experimentation. It still hums softly when approached. Can be opened to release an archite capsule.
LanguageData.VQEA_ArchiteCapsuleContainment.label: archite capsule containment
LanguageData.VQEA_ArchiteCapsuleContainment_Empty.des: A containment unit built to store and release archite capsules for experimentation. It's empty.
LanguageData.VQEA_StorageShelf_Empty.description: An empty, wobbly storage shelf. Anything of value is long gone.
LanguageData.VQEA_StorageShelf_Empty.label: empty storage shelf
LanguageData.VQEA_StorageShelf_Gowns.description: A wobbly storage shelf with stacks of patient gowns.
LanguageData.VQEA_StorageShelf_Gowns.label: patient gowns shelf
LanguageData.VQEA_StorageShelf_Meals.description: A wobbly storage shelf containing packaged survival meals.
LanguageData.VQEA_StorageShelf_Meals.label: survival meals shelf
LanguageData.VQEA_StorageShelf_Medicine.description: A wobbly storage shelf containing medicine.
LanguageData.VQEA_StorageShelf_Medicine.label: medicine shelf
LanguageData.VQEA_StorageShelf_Neutroamine.descriptio: A wobbly storage shelf containing bottles of neutroamine.
LanguageData.VQEA_StorageShelf_Neutroamine.label: neutroamine shelf
LanguageData.VQEA_PneumaticTubeLaunchPort.description: A large transfer station for the ancient pneumatic tube network. It was used to send supply capsules between distant vaults, moving food, tools, and research materials through sealed underground tunnels. Cargo can still be loaded and launched, and a return capsule may arrive from another vault after some time.
LanguageData.VQEA_PneumaticTubeLaunchPort.label: pneumatic tube launch port
LanguageData.VQEA_PneumaticTubes.description: A network of ancient tubes used to deliver small and medium items between vaults. Some of the capsules remain stuck inside.
LanguageData.VQEA_PneumaticTubes.label: pneumatic tubes
LanguageData.VQEA_PneumaticTubes_Corner.description: A network of ancient tubes used to deliver small and medium items between vaults. Some of the capsules remain stuck inside.
LanguageData.VQEA_PneumaticTubes_Corner.label: pneumatic tubes
LanguageData.VQEA_PneumaticTubes_Long.description: A network of ancient tubes used to deliver small and medium items between vaults. Some of the capsules remain stuck inside.
LanguageData.VQEA_PneumaticTubes_Long.label: pneumatic tubes
LanguageData.VQEA_PneumaticTubes_Terminus.description: The pneumatic tubes network had an exit port here. It is empty.
LanguageData.VQEA_PneumaticTubes_Terminus.label: empty pneumatic tubes terminus
LanguageData.VQEA_PneumaticTubes_Terminus_Closed.desc: The pneumatic tubes network had an exit port here. It can be opened to access its contents.
LanguageData.VQEA_PneumaticTubes_Triple.description: A network of ancient tubes used to deliver small and medium items between vaults. Some of the capsules remain stuck inside.
LanguageData.VQEA_PneumaticTubes_Triple.label: pneumatic tubes
LanguageData.VQEA_PneumaticTubes_Underground.descript: A network of ancient tubes used to deliver small and medium items between vaults. Some of the capsules remain stuck inside.
LanguageData.VQEA_PneumaticTubes_Underground.label: underground pneumatic tubes
LanguageData.VQEA_PneumaticTubes_Wall.description: A network of ancient tubes used to deliver small and medium items between vaults. Some of the capsules remain stuck inside.
LanguageData.VQEA_PneumaticTubes_Wall.label: pneumatic tubes
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientBioBattery.description: An old power vat that dissolves a living creature inside it to generate electricity. The power output varies based on the bodysize of the creature, and the duration depends on the creature’s mass.
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientBioBattery.label: ancient biobattery
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientChemfuelGenerator.descriptio: A bulky chemfuel-powered generator from a bygone era. It produces large amounts of power but needs to be manually “kicked” back into operation when it stalls.
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientChemfuelGenerator.label: ancient chemfuel generator
LanguageData.VqEA_AncientCookingStation.description: An electrically-powered stove with an attached countertop for preparing meals. It can also be used for butchering dead creatures into pieces of raw meat.
LanguageData.VqEA_AncientCookingStation.label: ancient cooking station
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientGreenbed.description: A small but very efficient artificial greenbed for growing plants. The nutrients pumps must work continuously; the plants will die if power is cut.
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientGreenbed.label: ancient greenbed
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientWorkshop.description: An ancient work station for assembling machinery like guns and ammunition, smithing non-mechanical weapons and tools, or breaking down dead mechanoids.
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientWorkshop.label: ancient workshop
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientBroadcastingStation.descript: An old communications console used to transmit encrypted signals between ancient facilities. Can be used to intercept broadcasts and trace their origin.
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientBroadcastingStation.label: ancient broadcasting station
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientBroadcastingStation_Off.desc: An old communications console used to transmit encrypted signals between ancient facilities. Seems that whatever information it stored is long gone.
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientVaultDoor.description: A heavily fortified, nearly indestructible door. The door requires walls on either side to function.
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientVaultDoor.label: ancient vault door
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientVaultDoor_Large.description: A large, heavily fortified, nearly indestructible door. The door requires walls on either side to function.
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientVaultDoor_Large.label: ancient large vault door
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientVaultWall.description: An impassable, indestructible vault wall. Capable of holding up a roof.
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientVaultWall.label: ancient vault wall
LanguageData.VQEA_LockedAncientVaultDoor.comps.0.jobS: Force this locked ancient vault door open
LanguageData.VQEA_LockedAncientVaultDoor.description: A heavily fortified, indestructible door. It needs to be hacked in order to open it. The door requires walls on either side to function.
LanguageData.VQEA_LockedAncientVaultDoor.label: locked ancient vault door
Force this locked ancient vault door open
LanguageData.VQEA_LockedAncientVaultDoor_Large.descri: A heavily fortified, indestructible door. It needs to be hacked in order to open it. The door requires walls on either side to function.
LanguageData.VQEA_LockedAncientVaultDoor_Large.label: locked ancient large vault door
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientEmergencyPurifier.descriptio: An old automatic purifier that releases extreme heat bursts to sterilize contaminated areas. It still activates on a timed cycle.
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientEmergencyPurifier.label: ancient emergency purifier
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientEmergencyPurifier_Off.descri: An old automatic purifier that releases extreme heat bursts to sterilize contaminated areas. It still activates on a timed cycle.
LanguageData.VQEA_AncientEmergencyPurifier_Off.label: ancient emergency purifier
LanguageData.VQEA_SplicefiendContainment.description: A containment pod filled with murky liquid. The glass bursts at the seams due to the large splicefiend inside.
LanguageData.VQEA_SplicefiendContainment.label: splicefiend containment
LanguageData.VQEA_SplicehulkContainment.description: A containment pod filled with murky liquid. A large splicehulk fills up almost the entire tube.
LanguageData.VQEA_SplicehulkContainment.label: splicehulk containment
LanguageData.VQEA_SplicelingContainment.description: A containment pod filled with murky liquid. It seems a sleeping spliceling lies inside.
LanguageData.VQEA_SplicelingContainment.label: spliceling containment
LanguageData.VQEA_SplicetootsContainment.description: A containment pod filled with murky liquid. Several small splicetoots float around in it, restlessly.
LanguageData.VQEA_SplicetootsContainment.label: splicetoots containment
LanguageData.VQEA_LockedVaultDoor.description: A sealed vault entrance leading to an underground laboratory. Still structurally sound, but heavily pressurized. Can be forced open to allow access.
LanguageData.VQEA_LockedVaultDoor.label: sealed vault door
LanguageData.VQEA_VaultExitRamp.description: A heavily reinforced vehicle ramp with personnel access stairway going up, allowing ascending back to the surface.
LanguageData.VQEA_VaultExitRamp.label: vault exit ramp
LanguageData.VQEA_VaultRamp.description: A heavily reinforced vehicle ramp with personnel access stairway going down the middle. Leads deeper underground, into a sealed ancient vault.
LanguageData.VQEA_VaultRamp.label: vault ramp
LanguageData.VQEA_Filth_DriedBlood.label: dried-up blood
LanguageData.VQEA_Filth_OldDocuments.label: old documents
LanguageData.VQEA_Splicefiend.description: A towering mutant angered and prodded by its endless agony. Its proportions are wildly distorted, with its arms dragging along the floor. Its bursting chest is stretched open wide with straining cords of flesh, as it walks with legs thick enough to shatter stone underfoot.\n\nUnlike its sluggish kin, the splicefiend moves with alarming speed and precision, overwhelming prey with violent charges and rending strikes.
LanguageData.VQEA_Splicefiend.label: splicefiend
LanguageData.VQEA_Splicefiend.tools.0.label: left massive fist
LanguageData.VQEA_Splicefiend.tools.1.label: right massive fist
LanguageData.VQEA_Splicehulk.description: A swollen bloated mutant that quivers with every labored step. Its skin splits under its own weight, oozing yellow fluid from raw seams. Vaguely resembling what was once a human, its limbs of mismatched size flail clumsily at anything nearby. Somehow, it’s able to regenerate all wounds at an unnatural speed.\n\nWhile slow and ponderous, its immense bulk crushes obstacles with ease leaving little hope for anything caught in its path.
LanguageData.VQEA_Splicehulk.label: splicehulk
LanguageData.VQEA_Splicehulk.tools.0.label: left swollen arm
LanguageData.VQEA_Splicehulk.tools.1.label: right swollen arm
LanguageData.VQEA_Splicehulk.tools.2.label: body slam
LanguageData.VQEA_Spliceling.description: A bent and malformed mutant, vaguely resembling what was once a human. Its gaunt face is a patchwork of assorted features shifting onto itself without purpose. It moves erratically, seemingly driven by sudden bursts of sharp pain and an urge for violence. Though its thin stretched skin looks decently fragile, its unpredictability makes it a dangerous foe.\n\nIt is unclear whether a broken human consciousness still lingers inside, or if it is nothing more than a hollow puppet of scrambled genes.
LanguageData.VQEA_Spliceling.label: spliceling
LanguageData.VQEA_Spliceling.tools.0.label: left mutated fist
LanguageData.VQEA_Spliceling.tools.1.label: flesh club
LanguageData.VQEA_Spliceling.tools.2.label: head
LanguageData.VQEA_Splicetoot.description: A small, bloated mutant that crawls awkwardly on underdeveloped limbs, resembling a grotesque parody of a toddler. Its enlarged belly sloshes with rotstink gas, swelling and trembling with each jerking movement. Upon reaching its victim, the fragile body ruptures in a violent explosion, releasing a choking cloud of dangerous gas. Whether it was once a child or simply shaped to mock one is a cruelty too dreadful to think about.
LanguageData.VQEA_Splicetoot.label: splicetoot
LanguageData.VQEA_Splicetoot.tools.0.label: bite
LanguageData.VQEA_MeleeWeapon_Scalpel.description: A precision-crafted cutting tool designed for delicate work. Despite its size, the scalpel’s razor‑thin blade allows for incisions far more precise than any knife. The damage it can deal with each stab reflects the medical skill of the user.
LanguageData.VQEA_MeleeWeapon_Scalpel.label: scalpel
LanguageData.VQEA_MeleeWeapon_Scalpel.tools.0.label: blade
LanguageData.VEE_RadioactiveBarrel.description: A rusted barrel leaking discarded nuclear fuel.
LanguageData.VEE_RadioactiveBarrel.label: radioactive barrel
LanguageData.VEE_Cenote.description: A deep, water filled sinkhole carved into the stone. The surface appears calm. Due to its depth, it can't be filled.
LanguageData.VEE_Cenote.label: cenote
LanguageData.VEE_Cenote_Small.description: A deep, water filled sinkhole carved into the stone. The surface appears calm. Due to its depth, it can't be filled.
LanguageData.VEE_Cenote_Small.label: small cenote
LanguageData.VEE_Coral.description: A colorful, calcified colony of tiny marine organisms, forming intricate reef structures teeming with life.
LanguageData.VEE_Coral.label: coral
LanguageData.VEE_DeadlifeVent.description: A sinister geyser that exhales eerie, ash-like dust, capable of reawakening the dead under its influence.
LanguageData.VEE_DeadlifeVent.label: deadlife geyser
LanguageData.VEE_DenseSnow.description: A heavy, compacted layer of snow that resists movement and muffles sound. Can be mined to remove it.
LanguageData.VEE_DenseSnow.label: solid snow
LanguageData.VEE_DriftwoodChunk.description: A sun-bleached fragment of wood, smoothed by waves and time, carried ashore from some distant and unknown origin.
LanguageData.VEE_DriftwoodChunk.label: driftwood
LanguageData.VEE_DryWheat.description: An amber cereal plant that takes almost an entire growing season to come to fruition. This wheat has been subjected to drought conditions for too long and has wilted away.
LanguageData.VEE_DryWheat.label: dry wheat
LanguageData.VEE_InertPitGate.description: A massive, foreboding hole that connects the surface with a dark network of underground caves. It is possible to climb down into the caverns below.
LanguageData.VEE_InertPitGate.label: pit gate
LanguageData.VEE_JadeChunk.description: A chunk of jadeite. It can be deconstructed to acquire some jade.
LanguageData.VEE_JadeChunk.label: jadeite chunk
LanguageData.VEE_MineableComponentSpacer.description: Advanced ancient electronics, compacted over time. Can be mined for useful advanced components.
LanguageData.VEE_MineableComponentSpacer.label: advanced compacted machinery
LanguageData.VEE_MineableJadeite.description: A silicate mineral in the pyroxene family that is one of the two forms of jade.
LanguageData.VEE_MineableJadeite.label: jadeite
LanguageData.VEE_ObsidianChunk.description: A chunk of obsidian. It can be deconstructed to acquire some obsidian.
LanguageData.VEE_ObsidianChunk.label: obsidian chunk
LanguageData.VEE_RotstinkVent.description: A foul geyser that intermittently belches clouds of corpse-born miasma, choking the air with the stench of decay.
LanguageData.VEE_RotstinkVent.label: rotstink geyser
LanguageData.VEE_Sinkhole.description: A sudden collapse in the ground forming a deep, unstable pit, often exposing bare rock beneath the surface.
LanguageData.VEE_Sinkhole.label: sinkhole
LanguageData.VEE_SmokeVent.description: A smoldering fissure that intermittently exhales thick, acrid smoke, reducing visibility and choking the lungs.
LanguageData.VEE_SmokeVent.label: smoke geyser
LanguageData.VEE_SmoothedJadeite.description: A silicate mineral in the pyroxene family that is one of the two forms of jade.
LanguageData.VEE_SmoothedJadeite.label: smoothed jadeite
LanguageData.VEE_SulfurVent.description: A naturally-occurring break in the ground from which sulfuric gases escape. The gas produced on these fissures stinks of rotten eggs.
LanguageData.VEE_SulfurVent.label: sulfur fissure
LanguageData.VEE_ToxicVent.description: A volatile fissure that periodically spews noxious green gas, poisoning the air and sickening anything that breathes it.
LanguageData.VEE_ToxicVent.label: toxic geyser
LanguageData.VEE_Filth_Seashells.label: seashells
LanguageData.VEE_AnimaPearls.description: A smooth, lustrous pearl formed within anima blossoms, faintly humming with psychic resonance. Anima pearls can be consumed to increase psychic sensitivity for some time.
LanguageData.VEE_AnimaPearls.label: anima pearl
LanguageData.VEE_RawDates.description: Raw dates. Nice to eat, even when raw. They are sweet, nutrient-rich fruit harvested from date palms. They keep well in warm climates and provide a reliable source of energy.
LanguageData.VEE_RawDates.label: dates
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_AnimaBell.description: Clumps of vines that burst through the soil to bloom into beautiful flowers. Believed to be part of great collective roots that spread deep under the terrain which occasionally breach the surface to pollinate. The attractive qualities of the flowers encourage humans not to remove them.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_AnimaBell.label: anima bell
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_AnimaBrambles.description: Thorny, fast-growing vines infused with faint anima energy. Their barbs are humming softly to the touch.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_AnimaBrambles.label: anima brambles
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_AnimaBush.description: A short shrub. Does talk, but only when you're not around.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_AnimaBush.label: anima bush
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_AnimaHeartBlossom.description: A delicate, softly glowing flower that blooms near anima trees, resonating faintly with psychic energy. Can be harvested to acquire anima pearls.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_AnimaHeartBlossom.label: anima heart blossom
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_AnimaHusk.description: A withered anima tree whose bark has hardened into a gray, lifeless shell under relentless pollution. Its psychic presence is muted and distorted, yet a stubborn echo of anima still clings to the husk, refusing to fully fade.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_AnimaHusk.label: anima husk
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_AnimaRoot.description: Pale, twisting roots shot through with a soft inner glow, drawn from anima-touched soil. Can be harvested for wood.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_AnimaRoot.label: anima root
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_FakeAnimaTree.description: An anima-touched tree. It lacks the psychic potency of a true anima tree, but still hums faintly with psychic life.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_FakeAnimaTree.label: anima tree
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_GrandAnimaTree.description: A grand anima-touched tree. It lacks the psychic potency of a true anima tree, but still hums faintly with psychic life.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_GrandAnimaTree.label: grand anima tree
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_MysticCap.description: A small, vividly colored mushroom with a faintly iridescent cap. Can be harvested for raw fungus.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_MysticCap.label: mystic cap
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_TreeAnima_Ancient.description:
A huge rare tree with warm, skin-smooth bark and long iridescent leaves. Infused with bioluminescent micro-organisms, ancient anima trees develop a unique form of psychic symbiosis with surrounding lifeforms.

If a person (psycaster or not) meditates near an ancient anima tree, it will grow anima grass around its base. Once enough grass is grown, it becomes possible to carry out a psychic linking ritual with the tree and upgrade a person's psychic powers. Only tribal peoples know the secret of this ritual. Tribal psycasters are also able to draw psyfocus from ancient anima trees while meditating to them.

Ancient anima trees' psychic properties are weakened if artificial structures are placed nearby. They refuse to be caged or studied, and must remain part of nature.

Most tribes believe that ancient anima trees are not simply trees, but are rather the physical extremities of a single world spirit.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_TreeAnima_Ancient.label: ancient anima tree
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_YoungAnimaTree.description: A small anima-touched tree. It lacks the psychic potency of a true anima tree, but still hums faintly with psychic life.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_YoungAnimaTree.label: anima sapling
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_Fern.description: A shade-loving plant with broad fronds that spreads across damp forest floors and sheltered ground.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_Fern.label: fern
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_JuniperBush.description: A hardy, low-growing bush with aromatic needles and dense berries. Its branches twist and spread, enduring harsh winds and exposed conditions.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_JuniperBush.label: juniper bush
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_BarrelCactus.description: A large cactus native to arid Earth environments. Though it is not a tree, it yields a small amount of fibrous material that can be used like wood.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_BarrelCactus.label: barrel cactus
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_BeavertailCactus.description: A large cactus native to arid Earth environments. Though it is not a tree, it yields a small amount of fibrous material that can be used like wood.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_BeavertailCactus.label: beavertail cactus
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_ChollaCactus.description: A large cactus native to arid Earth environments. Though it is not a tree, it yields a small amount of fibrous material that can be used like wood.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_ChollaCactus.label: cholla cactus
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_HedgehogCactus.description: A large cactus native to arid Earth environments. Though it is not a tree, it yields a small amount of fibrous material that can be used like wood.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_HedgehogCactus.label: hedgehog cactus
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_HoodiaCactus.description: A large cactus native to arid Earth environments. Though it is not a tree, it yields a small amount of fibrous material that can be used like wood.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_HoodiaCactus.label: hoodia cactus
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_OrganPipeCactus.description: A large cactus native to arid Earth environments. Though it is not a tree, it yields a small amount of fibrous material that can be used like wood.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_OrganPipeCactus.label: organ pipe cactus
LanguageData.VEE_DayBoomshroom.description: These mushrooms produce volatile chemicals as a defensive measure. They can be harvested for chemfuel. However, this must be done with extreme caution, as the mushrooms can explode if they're destroyed.\n\nGlitterworld researchers have used genetic material from boomshrooms to bioengineer a number of chemfuel-producing animals.
LanguageData.VEE_DayBoomshroom.label: light-resistant boomshroom
LanguageData.VEE_DayPsilocap.description: A fungus that gives off hallucinogenic spores. Standing next to them is enough to produce mild hallucinations. Psilocaps can be harvested and ingested to produce a vivid sensory experience that increases the user's mood.
LanguageData.VEE_DayPsilocap.label: light-resistant psilocap
LanguageData.VEE_DayWillowgill.description: A tall spindly slow-growing mushroom with gills that droop like a willow tree.
LanguageData.VEE_DayWillowgill.label: light-resistant willowgill
LanguageData.VEE_ButtercupFlower.label: buttercup
LanguageData.VEE_ForgetMeNot.label: forget-me-not
LanguageData.VEE_Gorse.description: A spiny, evergreen shrub covered in bright yellow flowers. Its thick, tangled branches provide shelter while resisting wind and harsh conditions.
LanguageData.VEE_Gorse.label: gorse
LanguageData.VEE_Heather.description: A low, flowering shrub with dense clusters of tiny blooms. It carpets the landscape with muted colors and weathers wind and exposure with ease.
LanguageData.VEE_Heather.label: heather
LanguageData.VEE_Knapweed.label: knapweed
LanguageData.VEE_Loosestrife.description: A beautiful herbaceous perennial wild plant.
LanguageData.VEE_Loosestrife.label: loosestrife
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_DatePalm.description: A tall palm thriving along warm coasts, bearing clusters of sweet, energy-rich fruit. Its deep roots and flexible trunk help it withstand salt spray and coastal winds.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_DatePalm.label: date palm
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_Firewood.description: A hardy tree adapted to grow on volcanic ground. It is among the first plants to colonize cooled lava, helping life return to the black stone.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_Firewood.label: firewood tree
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_Firewood_ChoppedStump.descript: A stump left behind after a firewood tree has been felled. The stump can be extracted but yields very little usable wood. Left outdoors, the stump will deteriorate in time.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_Firewood_ChoppedStump.label: chopped stump
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_Firewood_SmashedStump.descript: The remnants of a firewood tree destroyed by damage. It's ugly. The stump can be extracted but yields very little usable wood. Left outdoors, the stump will deteriorate in time.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_Firewood_SmashedStump.label: smashed stump
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_GnarledPine.description: A twisted, weathered pine shaped by wind and poor soil. Its gnarled branches and sparse needles give it a rugged, enduring appearance.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_GnarledPine.label: gnarled pine tree
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_Laurel.description: An evergreen broadleaf tree with dense foliage and aromatic leaves, well adapted to mild, humid climates.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_Laurel.label: laurel tree
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_LaurelTree_ChoppedStump.descri: A stump left behind after a laurel tree has been felled. The stump can be extracted but yields very little usable wood. Left outdoors, the stump will deteriorate in time.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_LaurelTree_ChoppedStump.label: chopped stump
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_LaurelTree_SmashedStump.descri: The remnants of a laurel tree destroyed by damage. It's ugly. The stump can be extracted but yields very little usable wood. Left outdoors, the stump will deteriorate in time.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_LaurelTree_SmashedStump.label: smashed stump
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_ScrewPine.description: A hardy coastal tree with spiraled leaves and exposed roots, adapted to wind, salt, and shifting sand.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_ScrewPine.label: screw pine
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_ScrewPine_ChoppedStump.descrip: A stump left behind after a screw pine has been felled. The stump can be extracted but yields very little usable wood. Left outdoors, the stump will deteriorate in time.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_ScrewPine_ChoppedStump.label: chopped stump
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_ScrewPine_SmashedStump.descrip: The remnants of a screw pine destroyed by damage. It's ugly. The stump can be extracted but yields very little usable wood. Left outdoors, the stump will deteriorate in time.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_ScrewPine_SmashedStump.label: smashed stump
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_TreeBirch_Auburn.description: A temperate-biome tree known for its thin, white, paper-like bark.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_TreeBirch_Auburn.label: auburn birch tree
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_TreeMaple_Auburn.description: A common medium-sized tree, easily identifiable by its distinct three-pointed leaf.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_TreeMaple_Auburn.label: auburn maple tree
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_TreeOak_Auburn.description: A hardwood tree. Oaks take a long time to grow, but their wood is so strong that 'oak' is used as a metaphor for strength across many cultures.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_TreeOak_Auburn.label: auburn oak tree
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_TreePoplar_Auburn.description: A softwood tree that grows very fast. Unfortunately, its wood is weak and so it yields less useful material than hardwood trees.
LanguageData.VEE_Plant_TreePoplar_Auburn.label: auburn poplar tree
LanguageData.VEE_Mangrove.description: A salt-tolerant tree adapted to life in brackish wetlands. Its tangled roots anchor it in shifting mud and provide shelter for aquatic life.
LanguageData.VEE_Mangrove.label: mangrove
LanguageData.VEE_Mangrove_ChoppedStump.description: A stump left behind after a mangrove tree has been felled. The stump can be extracted but yields very little usable wood. Left outdoors, the stump will deteriorate in time.
LanguageData.VEE_Mangrove_ChoppedStump.label: mangrove chopped stump
LanguageData.VEE_Mangrove_SmashedStump.description: The remnants of a mangrove tree destroyed by damage. It's ugly. The stump can be extracted but yields very little usable wood. Left outdoors, the stump will deteriorate in time.
LanguageData.VEE_Mangrove_SmashedStump.label: mangrove smashed stump
LanguageData.VFEFactory_Conveyor.description: A simple conveyor belt capable of moving an item from one place to the other. Due to safety reasons, items on the conveyor belt cannot be interacted with.
LanguageData.VFEFactory_Conveyor.label: conveyor
LanguageData.VFEFactory_UndergroundConveyorEntrance.d: A simple entrance tunnel for the conveyor belt, allowing it to travel a short distance underground. Needs to be linked to a nearby underground conveyor exit to work.
LanguageData.VFEFactory_UndergroundConveyorExit.descr: A simple exit tunnel for the conveyor belt, allowing it to travel a short distance underground. Needs to be linked to a nearby underground conveyor entrance to work.
LanguageData.VFEFactory_UndergroundConveyorExit.label: underground conveyor exit
LanguageData.VFEFactory_Autofarmer.description: A large, automated machine that combines the functions of a sower and harvester, moving back and forth across a designated outdoor zone to harvest mature plants and sow new crops. It deposits harvested goods into attached factory hoppers or drops them on the ground if hoppers are not present, and its operation can be set to automatically cycle once the majority of plants in the zone are fully grown.
LanguageData.VFEFactory_Autofarmer.label: autofarmer
LanguageData.VFEFactory_Autoloom.description: An industrial-grade loom designed for the automatic production of textiles and apparel, it efficiently weaves materials like cloth and leather into various finished goods. It can only output goods of normal quality.\n\nFactory buildings need factory hoppers for input and output. They produce significant heat and consume much power, but can be overclocked for higher efficiency, increasing both heat generation and power consumption. Needs to be constructed on factory floor.
LanguageData.VFEFactory_Autoloom.label: autoloom
LanguageData.VFEFactory_AutomatedAlloyForge.descripti: A colossal industrial furnace built to contain the immense heat and pressure needed to bond base materials into exotic alloys like plasteel.\n\nFactory buildings need factory hoppers for input and output. They produce significant heat and consume much power, but can be overclocked for higher efficiency, increasing both heat generation and power consumption. Needs to be constructed on factory floor.
LanguageData.VFEFactory_AutomatedAlloyForge.label: automated alloy forge
LanguageData.VFEFactory_AutomatedAmmunitionPress.desc: An industrial-grade press capable of automatically manufacturing various artillery shells from raw materials.\n\nFactory buildings need factory hoppers for input and output. They produce significant heat and consume much power, but can be overclocked for higher efficiency, increasing both heat generation and power consumption. Needs to be constructed on factory floor.
LanguageData.VFEFactory_AutomatedAssembler.descriptio: An automated industrial machine for assembling complex items like components and advanced components.\n\nFactory buildings need factory hoppers for input and output. They produce significant heat and consume much power, but can be overclocked for higher efficiency, increasing both heat generation and power consumption. Needs to be constructed on factory floor.
LanguageData.VFEFactory_AutomatedAssembler.label: automated assembler
LanguageData.VFEFactory_AutomatedBiofuelRefinery.desc: Automatically refines biological matter like wood or plant matter into chemfuel.\n\nFactory buildings need factory hoppers for input and output. They produce significant heat and consume much power, but can be overclocked for higher efficiency, increasing both heat generation and power consumption. Needs to be constructed on factory floor.
LanguageData.VFEFactory_AutomatedCrematorium.descript: An industrial-grade furnace designed for the automated destruction of unwanted items, especially corpses and tainted apparel, using extreme heat.\n\nFactory buildings need factory hoppers for input and output. They produce significant heat and consume much power, but can be overclocked for higher efficiency, increasing both heat generation and power consumption. Needs to be constructed on factory floor.
LanguageData.VFEFactory_AutomatedCrematorium.label: conveyor crematorium
LanguageData.VFEFactory_AutomatedDistillery.descripti: An automated industrial distilling system for the mass production of beer and spirits from raw crops, requiring no manual labor.\n\nFactory buildings need factory hoppers for input and output. They produce significant heat and consume much power. Needs to be constructed on factory floor.
LanguageData.VFEFactory_AutomatedDistillery.label: automated distillery
LanguageData.VFEFactory_AutomatedDrillPlatform.descri: An automated deep-crust drilling rig for extracting minerals from deep under the surface. Capable of extracting stone chunks, but also any metal it finds.\n\nFactory buildings need factory hoppers for input and output. They produce significant heat and consume much power, but can be overclocked for higher efficiency, increasing both heat generation and power consumption.
LanguageData.VFEFactory_AutomatedDrillPlatform.label: automated drill platform
LanguageData.VFEFactory_AutomatedMachiningBay.descrip: An automated industrial facility designed for the precision manufacturing of various weapons, armor, and complex equipment. It can only output goods of normal quality.\n\nFactory buildings need factory hoppers for input and output. They produce significant heat and consume much power, but can be overclocked for higher efficiency, increasing both heat generation and power consumption. Needs to be constructed on factory floor.
LanguageData.VFEFactory_AutomatedMachiningBay.label: automated machining bay
LanguageData.VFEFactory_AutomatedMasonrySaw.descripti: An industrial machine capable of cutting stone chunks into usable blocks.\n\nFactory buildings need factory hoppers for input and output. They produce significant heat and consume much power, but can be overclocked for higher efficiency, increasing both heat generation and power consumption. Needs to be constructed on factory floor.
LanguageData.VFEFactory_AutomatedMasonrySaw.label: automated masonry saw
LanguageData.VFEFactory_AutomatedMincer.description: A series of automated saws and mincers capable of processing corpses into processed mince.\n\nFactory buildings need factory hoppers for input and output. They produce significant heat and consume much power, but can be overclocked for higher efficiency, increasing both heat generation and power consumption. Needs to be constructed on factory floor.
LanguageData.VFEFactory_AutomatedMincer.label: automated mincer
LanguageData.VFEFactory_AutomatedSmelter.description: Extracts usable metal from slag chunks, broken-down weapons, apparel, stone chunks, and mechanoid corpses using strong electromagnets and heat.\n\nFactory buildings need factory hoppers for input and output. They produce significant heat and consume much power, but can be overclocked for higher efficiency, increasing both heat generation and power consumption. Needs to be constructed on factory floor.
LanguageData.VFEFactory_AutomatedSmelter.label: automated smelter
LanguageData.VFEFactory_ConveyorOven.description: An industrial oven for the automated mass-production of simple, shelf-stable, and packaged meals.\n\nFactory buildings need factory hoppers for input and output. They produce significant heat and consume much power, but can be overclocked for higher efficiency, increasing both heat generation and power consumption. Needs to be constructed on factory floor.
LanguageData.VFEFactory_ConveyorOven.label: conveyor oven
LanguageData.VFEFactory_MedicineGranulator.descriptio: An advanced pharmaceutical processor for the mass-production of basic medical supplies and drug batches from raw materials. It efficiently grinds, synthesizes, and packages items like medicine, psychite drugs, and smokeleaf joints.\n\nFactory buildings need factory hoppers for input and output. They produce significant heat and consume much power, but can be overclocked for higher efficiency, increasing both heat generation and power consumption. Needs to be constructed on factory floor.
LanguageData.VFEFactory_MedicineGranulator.label: medicine granulator
LanguageData.VFEFactory_NeutroamineSynthesizer.descri: An advanced chemical facility designed for the automated synthesis of neutroamine from organic feedstocks. It operates by converting rendered animal fat and chemfuel into neutroamine.\n\nFactory buildings need factory hoppers for input and output. They produce significant heat and consume much power, but can be overclocked for higher efficiency, increasing both heat generation and power consumption. Needs to be constructed on factory floor.
LanguageData.VFEFactory_NeutroamineSynthesizer.label: neutroamine synthesizer
LanguageData.VFEFactory_AutomatedFishfarm.description: An industrial fish breeding and harvesting facility designed to automatically extract fish from bodies of water with fish populations.\n\nFactory buildings need factory hoppers for input and output. Needs to be constructed in shallow water.
LanguageData.VFEFactory_AutomatedFishfarm.label: automated fishfarm
LanguageData.VFEFactory_AutomatedCannery.description: An automated industrial preserving system for the mass production of canned goods from various raw ingredients, requiring no manual labor.\n\nFactory buildings need factory hoppers for input and output. They produce significant heat and consume much power, but can be overclocked for higher efficiency, increasing both heat generation and power consumption. Needs to be constructed on factory floor.
LanguageData.VFEFactory_AutomatedCannery.label: automated cannery
LanguageData.VFEFactory_FactoryHopper.description: Holds resources for use by factory machines.
LanguageData.VFEFactory_FactoryHopper.label: factory hopper
LanguageData.VFEFactory_Booster.description: A sensitive micro-processor array designed to enhance the speed and efficiency of industrial factory buildings. It links with nearby factory machines to extend their maximum overclocking limit by an additional 100 percent, allowing for drastically increased production speeds. One factory can use up to three factory boosters.
LanguageData.VFEFactory_Booster.label: factory booster
LanguageData.VFEFactory_Heatsink.description: A cooling system designed to draw excess heat away from industrial factory buildings. It links with nearby factory machines to reduce their total heat output by 25%, mitigating the risk of overheating and fire. One factory can use up to four factory heatsinks.
LanguageData.VFEFactory_Heatsink.label: factory heatsink
LanguageData.VFEFactory_MassProducedMeal.description: A mass-produced meal assembled by machine from processed ingredients.
LanguageData.VFEFactory_MassProducedMeal.label: mass-produced meal
LanguageData.VCE_CannedSoup.description: Soup sealed and secured in a steel can effectively extending shelf life indefinitely.
LanguageData.VCE_CannedSoup.label: canned soup
LanguageData.VFEFactory_ProcessedMince.description: A block of heavily processed minced meat, safe to eat but with an unappetizing taste.
LanguageData.VFEFactory_ProcessedMince.label: processed mince
